weapons.name: Wraith & Starfire
weapons.fulldesc: Shiela's twin revolvers are legendary across the Dark West. Wraith is a revolver of polished silver, its barrel etched with the names of the dead she has laid to rest. When she fires it, it does not eject ordinary bullets; instead, it releases the souls of the departed, ghostly projectiles that pass through flesh to strike at the spirit itself. The souls scream as they fly, a sound that chills the blood of the living and terrifies the undead. Starfire is a revolver of deep, royal purple, its surface inlaid with veins of gold that glow with an inner light. Starfire fires concentrated beams of laser energy, each shot a precise, searing line of light that can cut through steel and stone. Together, the two revolvers grant her unlimited ammunition—for the dead are never truly gone, and the light of the Dark West never fully fades. She can fire spectral bullets that track their targets across dimensions, possess the bodies of those who underestimate her, and dodge through the ethereal plane itself, becoming smoke and shadow for the span of a heartbeat.
weapons.look: Left: polished silver with etched names; Right: royal purple with gold inlay, glowing inner light

passive.name: Soul Ammunition
passive.description: Basic attacks with Wraith deal bonus magic damage equal to 3% of the target's current health. Starfire's basic attacks ignore 15% of the target's armor.

passive.name: Ethereal Dodge
passive.description: After using an ability, Shiela becomes intangible for 0.5 seconds, dodging all incoming damage.

active.name: Soul Seeker (Wraith)
active.description: Fires a homing soul projectile that tracks the target for 3 seconds, dealing 150% magic damage. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Laser Line (Starfire)
active.description: Fires a piercing laser beam in a line, dealing 180% physical damage to all enemies in its path. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

ultimate.name: Ghost Dance
ultimate.description: Shiela enters the ethereal plane for 4 seconds, becoming untargetable and gaining 50% movement speed. While ethereal, she can fire both revolvers simultaneously, and her attacks ignore all armor and magic resist. Cooldown: 75 seconds.

special.name: Bounty Hunter's Mark
special.description: Shiela can mark a target as her bounty, gaining 20% movement speed toward them and dealing 15% bonus damage. Only one bounty at a time.

ratings.notes: Hybrid marksman with high mobility, intangibility, and single-target focus.

skills.name: Spirit Pact
skills.description: Shiela's blue aura heals her for 10 health per second and reveals nearby invisible enemies within 8 meters.

skills.name: Smoke Step
skills.description: Shiela turns into smoke and dashes 10 meters, leaving a cloud that blinds enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Possession
skills.description: Shiela possesses an enemy minion or small creature for 5 seconds, controlling its movement and basic attacks. Cooldown: 25 seconds.

skills.name: Brother's Shadow
skills.description: When Clint is an ally, Shiela gains 10% lifesteal. When Clint is an enemy, her abilities deal 10% more damage to him, but she takes 10% more damage from him.

heroes.lore: Shiela Fiesta was a bounty hunter's daughter who lost her father to revenants and her brother Clint to a demonic pact. She made a pact with the spirits of departed bounty hunters, gaining the power to hunt the supernatural. Now she roams the Dark West, collecting bounties and avoiding the brother she cannot bring herself to kill.

relationships.description: Clint made a deal with a demon and became a revenant lord. Shiela hunts him but cannot bring herself to finish the job.
Both have lost siblings to darkness; they share a quiet understanding when their paths cross.

heroes.specialty: Hybrid marksman/assassin with high mobility, intangibility, and single-target execution potential. Excels at dueling and chasing down marked targets.

heroes.name: Malit Dalit
heroes.shortDescription: A fragment of a dying star who once held the cosmos in his palms and now exists as a whisper between galaxies. Malit Dalit is a forgotten astral mage, his body composed of condensed gas and star energy, his eyes containing galaxies. He works in secret alongside Gord, the new Protector, lending his power to vanquish evils that threaten the balance of existence.
heroes.fullDescription: Malit Dalit drifts through the void like a fragment of a dying star, a being who once held the cosmos in his palms and now exists as a whisper between galaxies—a forgotten astral mage whose body is composed of condensed gas and star energy, his features striking and Germanic, yet his face is merely the anchor for something far greater. His face is strong and square-jawed, with high cheekbones and a straight noble nose, but his skin—pale and fair in human tone—is cracked with fissures of pure purple energy that trace the meridians of his body like a cosmic river. Through these cracks, one can glimpse the stars that swirl within him, nebulae and galaxies turning in slow eternal orbits. His eyes are windows into the heart of creation: sclera absolute black, irises a swirling maelstrom of violet, gold, and silver, pupils as black holes that drink in light. His hair is a gravity-defying mane of light violet spikes, crackling with static and trailing stardust, jagged and windswept as if blown by solar winds. His physique is massively muscular, dense and corded, built like a cosmic warrior—broad shoulders, a thick defined chest, heavy arms, a narrow waist, and powerful legs. He wears robes of absolute black woven with threads of starlight and nebula hues, long and flowing, with a mantle of star-map patterns on his shoulders, constellations stitched with captured light. A high collar frames his neck, and silver chains clink at his waist. Two bangles of polished black stone etched with ancient star charts adorn his wrists, and floating obsidian shards orbit him like moons, each one a fragment of a dead world, trailing dark energy. His feet are bare, floating above the ground, and his entire body seems to hum with the power of a billion suns. His weapon is his very form—his body composed of condensed gas and star energy, a physical shell that he can dissolve and reform at will. His fists strike with the force of a collapsing star; his palms unleash blasts of pure cosmic energy—what mortals have come to call a Kamehameha, a focused beam of stellar fire that can erase mountains from existence. He can create gravity wells that crush his enemies into singularities, tear dimensional rifts that swallow armies whole, and nullify the powers of those who dare to challenge him. His mount, Stardust, is an aerial skateboard forged from condensed fire and celestial gas—its surface a shimmering translucent sheet of orange and gold, its edges trailing comet tails of ionized particles, responding to Malit's will, leaving a trail of glittering stardust in its wake. This forgotten protector was born from the collapse of a supernova, a consciousness forged in the heart of destruction. For eons, he drifted through the cosmos, learning the language of gravity, the music of radiation, the poetry of entropy. He became the Protector of the Universe, fighting cosmic horrors that would drive mortal minds insane, sealing rifts that spewed anti-matter into reality. But eons are long, and even the most dedicated guardian grows weary. He retreated to a nebula at the edge of existence and slept for a million years. When he awoke, a new Protector had risen: Gord, a being of similar power but different nature. Malit decided not to challenge him but to become his shadow, his silent partner, the guardian that no one knew existed. Now he drifts through the void between stars—a realm of absolute silence, where nebulae bloom like cosmic flowers and the light of distant galaxies takes millions of years to reach him. He stands on Stardust, his robes billowing, his purple energy cracks glowing, watching over Gord from afar, ready to intervene when the danger is greatest, a relic who will not rest while the universe needs him—the Forgotten Astral Mage, whose vigil has no end.

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the realms of Kretos were torn, there was only the Void and the Stars. Malit Dalit was born from the collapse of a supernova, a consciousness forged in the heart of destruction. For eons, he drifted through the cosmos, learning the language of gravity, the music of radiation, the poetry of entropy. When the first gods emerged from the chaos, they called him 'The Watcher,' for he had seen the birth of a thousand galaxies and the death of a thousand more. He did not seek worship; he sought understanding. And in his understanding, he found purpose: to protect the fragile worlds that bloomed in the darkness, to guard the flame of life against the cold of the void.
story.part2: The title of 'Protector of the Universe' was not given; it was earned. Malit fought cosmic horrors that would drive mortal minds insane, sealed rifts that spewed anti-matter into reality, and guided young civilizations away from self-destruction. He was a silent guardian, never interfering directly, always nudging, always watching. The gods respected him; the demons feared him. But eons are long, and even the most dedicated guardian grows weary. Malit began to fade, not in power, but in purpose. The universe no longer needed him—or so he believed. He retreated to a nebula at the edge of existence, wrapped himself in its gases, and slept for a million years.
story.part3: When he awoke, a new Protector had risen: Gord, a being of similar power but different nature. Gord was active, interventionist, a warrior who fought evil head-on. Malit watched him from the shadows, at first with suspicion, then with respect. Gord was everything Malit had once been, but without the weariness. The old mage made a decision: he would not challenge the new Protector. He would become his shadow, his silent partner, the guardian that no one knew existed. He began to work alongside Gord, appearing only when the danger was greatest, offering his power in ways that could not be traced back to him. Gord never asked who was helping him; he simply accepted the aid, grateful for the cosmic ally he could never seem to catch.
story.part4: The Sundering tore Malit from his comfortable obscurity. The rifts between worlds threatened to unravel the fabric of reality, and even Gord could not be everywhere at once. Malit began to reveal himself more often, appearing to heroes in their darkest moments, offering cryptic advice and devastating cosmic blasts. He became a myth, a legend whispered by the few who had seen him—a man made of stars, his body cracking with purple light, his eyes holding galaxies. He did not seek recognition; he sought results. The rifts began to close. The demons began to retreat. And Malit retreated with them, back into the shadows, back into the silence.
story.part5: Now, Malit Dalit drifts between the stars, Stardust humming beneath his feet, his body pulsing with the light of a billion suns. He knows that the Sundering is not over, that the demons are regrouping, that the realms of Kretos are still in danger. He watches Gord from afar, proud of the younger protector, ready to intervene when needed. He has heard that some mortals have begun to worship him as a god, and he laughs—a sound like the collision of galaxies. He is not a god. He is a relic, a forgotten guardian, a man made of starlight and regret. But he will not rest while the universe needs him. For he is Malit Dalit, the Forgotten Astral Mage, and his vigil has no end.

weapons.name: Stellar Body (Cosmic Fists)
weapons.fulldesc: Malit Dalit's body is composed of condensed gas and star energy, a physical shell that he can dissolve and reform at will. His fists can strike with the force of a collapsing star; his palms can unleash blasts of pure, cosmic energy—what the mortals have come to call a Kamehameha, a focused beam of stellar fire that can erase mountains from existence. He can create gravity wells that crush his enemies into singularities, tear dimensional rifts that swallow armies whole, and nullify the powers of those who dare to challenge him. He is a living weapon, a force of nature given form, and he has not yet found an enemy who could survive the full expression of his power.
weapons.look: Fists glow with purple and gold energy; palms emit stellar beams

passive.name: Cosmic Regeneration
passive.description: Malit regenerates 1% of his max health every second, doubled when out of combat.

passive.name: Void Adaptation
passive.description: Malit takes 25% reduced damage from all sources when in space or cosmic rifts.

active.name: Gravity Well
active.description: Creates a 6-meter zone at target location that pulls enemies toward the center and deals 100 magic damage per second for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Stellar Beam
active.description: Fires a beam of cosmic energy in a line, dealing 200% magic damage to all enemies hit and piercing through them. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Supernova
ultimate.description: Malit detonates his own energy, dealing 500% magic damage in a 25-meter radius and stunning all enemies for 3 seconds. After casting, Malit's body reforms over 5 seconds during which he is invulnerable but cannot act. Cooldown: 120 seconds.

special.name: Cosmic Nullification
special.description: Malit's presence nullifies enemy shields within 15 meters, preventing them from being applied.

ratings.notes: Extremely powerful mage with high sustain, crowd control, and team utility.

skills.name: Eternal Vigil
skills.description: Malit gains 1% magic power for every minute that passes in the match (up to 30%).

skills.name: Dimensional Rift
skills.description: Malit tears a rift at target location, teleporting himself and any allies within 5 meters to the rift's exit after 1 second. Cooldown: 30 seconds.

skills.name: Starfall
skills.description: Calls down 3 stars from the sky at marked locations, each dealing 120% magic damage in a 4-meter radius. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Forgotten Protector
skills.description: When Gord is an ally, Malit's ultimate cooldown is reduced by 20 seconds. When Gord is an enemy, Malit takes 15% less damage from him.

heroes.lore: Malit Dalit was the Protector of the Universe for eons before growing weary and fading into obscurity. When Gord rose as the new Protector, Malit became his silent partner, working from the shadows to help vanquish cosmic threats. He is a forgotten legend, a man made of starlight who still watches over creation.

relationships.description: Malit works alongside Gord from the shadows, never seeking credit or recognition.
They have crossed paths in the cosmic void; she respects his power but finds him too melancholic.

mount.name: Stardust
mount.description: An aerial skateboard forged from condensed fire and celestial gas, surface a shimmering translucent sheet of orange and gold, edges trailing comet tails of ionized particles. Responds to Malit's will, leaving a trail of glittering stardust.
mount.fulldesc: Stardust is an aerial skateboard forged from condensed fire and celestial gas, its surface a shimmering, translucent sheet of orange and gold, its edges trailing comet tails of ionized particles. Malit stands on it with the ease of a surfer on a wave, his robes billowing, his hair streaming behind him, and he moves through the void with a speed that defies physics. Stardust responds to his will, banking and diving, accelerating and stopping on a thought, and it leaves a trail of glittering stardust in its wake—a path of light that fades slowly, like the memory of a shooting star. It is not a living creature, but it is alive in the way that all cosmic constructs are: a piece of Malit's own energy given form, a companion in the endless silence of space.
abilities: Comet Trail – leaves a trail that damages enemies.
Cosmic Speed – increases Malit's movement speed by 40% while riding.
Stardust Burst – creates a cloud of stardust that blinds enemies.

heroes.specialty: Extremely high-damage mage with global presence, crowd control, and team utility. Scales over time and excels in long matches.

heroes.name: Vixyy Joe
heroes.shortDescription: A Spanish temptress who traded her soul for a winning hand and never looked back. Vixyy Joe runs the infernal casino where souls are wagered and fates are sealed, wielding the Ace of Spades—a deck of cursed obsidian cards—and accompanied by a supreme jade owl. She taught Harley everything he knows about magic and deception, and waits for the day when her former student will challenge her.
heroes.fullDescription: Vixyy Joe steps out of the swirling smoke of the infernal casino like a hand dealt from the bottom of the deck—a Spanish temptress whose very existence is a gamble, and who has never lost, her age fixed at twenty yet her eyes holding the cunning of a woman who has cheated death more times than she can count. Her face is a study in sharp aristocratic elegance: a heart-shaped visage with a delicate pointed chin, high prominent cheekbones, a straight slender nose, and full blood-red lips perpetually curved in a knowing smirk. Her skin is very fair, almost porcelain, smooth and luminous, with a faint golden undertone. Her eyes are large and almond-shaped, emerald green that shifts and deepens like a panther's, holding secrets and promises in equal measure. Her hair is a cascade of long black and maroon waves falling past her shoulders, worn loose, luxurious and flowing. Over her left eye, she wears a gold monocle with an emerald-tinted lens—a piece that allows her to see through lies and illusions. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a wasp-thin waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G-cup bust that strains against her tailored coat. She wears a sharp black coat with blood-red lining, tailored and rich, its lapels wide, over a minimal bra-like top that reveals her full cleavage. Her arms are bare beneath the coat, and a black velvet choker with a single emerald gem—the focus of her soul contract—circles her neck. A wide belt of gold links cinches her waist, and a red leather mini skirt slit high covers her hips, exposing her thick creamy thighs. Long black leather boots with high sharp heels and pointed toes cover her feet, and a top hat with a dented crown and wide brim sits on her head, its gold band adorned with a small silver skull. Gold rings set with rubies and emeralds cover her fingers, and her gold teeth flash when she smiles. Her weapon, the Ace of Spades, is a deck of cursed obsidian cards that float around her like a school of deadly fish—each card a thin sliver of obsidian etched with symbols of luck and fate, orbiting her in a constant shifting halo. With a flick of her wrist, she can send them flying like razors, each one capable of stealing luck, draining life, or sealing a soul contract. The Ace of Spades itself—the card that started it all—she keeps tucked into the band of her top hat, its face a grinning skull wreathed in emerald fire. Her pet, the Supreme Jade Owl, is a construct bound to her by a contract older than her own soul—its feathers carved from a single massive piece of polished jade, each plume distinct and sharp-edged, its eyes two glowing emerald orbs that see through all deception, capable of speaking in a voice like rustling leaves, its screech shattering glass and rattling the souls of the living. This queen of the infernal casino was once a street rat in the back alleys of Barcelona, a girl with nothing but quick fingers and a quicker smile, who made a name for herself in underground gambling dens as 'La Viuda Negra'—the Black Widow—because no one who played against her ever walked away with their winnings. A demon found her in a backroom poker game and offered her a wager: her soul against a deck of cards that could never lose. She drew the Ace of Spades—the card of death, of contracts, of souls—and the demon smiled, telling her that every card she played would bind her deeper to the infernal. Vixyy smiled back and said, 'Then I'll make sure the game never ends.' The infernal casino rose from the ashes of a burned-out theater, a monument to her gamble—a realm of crimson velvet, gold trim, and eternal twilight where souls wager years of their lives, their memories, their very existence. She sits at a table of polished obsidian, the Supreme Jade Owl perched on its stand beside her, her cards orbiting her head, her cane tapping a slow rhythm, dealing fate to all who dare to sit across from her—the Dealer of Fate, the House, waiting for the day when her former student Harley will return to challenge her, when the odds will finally be uncertain, and the most exciting gamble of all will begin.

story.part1: Before she was the Queen of the Infernal Casino, Vixyy Joe was a street rat in the back alleys of Barcelona, a girl with nothing but quick fingers and a quicker smile. She learned to pick pockets, to read marks, to stack decks in games where the losers paid with their teeth. By sixteen, she had made a name for herself in the underground gambling dens of Spain—'La Viuda Negra,' the Black Widow, because no one who played against her ever walked away with their winnings. But Vixyy wanted more than gold. She wanted power. She wanted to control the game itself.
story.part2: The demon found her in a backroom poker game, a handsome man with eyes like embers. He offered her a wager: her soul against a deck of cards that could never lose. Vixyy, arrogant and desperate, accepted. The demon dealt. Vixyy drew the Ace of Spades—the card of death, of contracts, of souls. The demon smiled. 'You've won the deck,' he said. 'But the deck comes with a price. Every card you play, every soul you take, binds you deeper to the infernal. In the end, the deck will own you.' Vixyy looked at the Ace of Spades in her hand, felt its power thrumming through her veins, and smiled back. 'Then I'll make sure the game never ends.'
story.part3: The infernal casino rose from the ashes of a burned-out theater, a monument to Vixyy's gamble. She became its queen, dealing cards to souls who wagered years of their lives, their memories, their very existence. She never lost, not because the deck was rigged—though it was—but because she understood that the true game was not in the cards, but in the hearts of her opponents. She learned to read fear, to exploit hope, to turn desperation into profit. The demon who had given her the deck became her occasional partner, occasional rival, but never her master. Vixyy Joe belonged to no one.
story.part4: The child came to her door one night—a scrawny street rat with eyes too old for his face and hands that moved like lightning. Harley was his name, and he had heard of the Queen of the Infernal Casino. He wanted to learn. Vixyy saw herself in him, the same hunger, the same cunning, the same refusal to accept that the game was rigged. She took him in, taught him the art of the deal, the magic of deception, the weight of a soul contract. He was her finest student, her greatest success, and when he left to make his own name, she let him go with a smile and a warning: 'Never gamble what you are not willing to lose.'
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought new players to the infernal casino—demons, angels, heroes, and villains, all seeking advantage in the chaos. Vixyy watches them all, her cards orbiting, her jade owl perched on its stand, her cane tapping a slow rhythm. She has heard that Harley has become a legend, that he has made deals with powers that even she would hesitate to approach. She is proud of him, and a little afraid. Because she knows that one day, her former student will come back. He will want to challenge her, to prove that he has surpassed the master. And when that day comes, Vixyy will deal the cards, and the game will begin. She does not know who will win. For the first time in centuries, the odds are uncertain. And that, she thinks, is the most exciting gamble of all.

weapons.name: Ace of Spades
weapons.fulldesc: The Ace of Spades is a deck of cards that floats around Vixyy like a school of deadly fish, each card a sliver of cursed potential. The cards are not paper; they are thin slivers of obsidian, etched with symbols of luck and fate, and they orbit her in a constant, shifting halo. With a flick of her wrist, she can send them flying like razors, each one capable of stealing luck, draining life, or sealing a soul contract. The Ace of Spades itself—the card that started it all—she keeps tucked into the band of her top hat, its face a grinning skull wreathed in emerald fire. The deck is bound to her soul; as long as she holds the Ace, she cannot be killed—but every card she plays brings her closer to the infernal bargain's final payment.
weapons.look: Obsidian cards orbiting, etched with luck and fate symbols; Ace shows grinning skull with emerald fire

passive.name: House Advantage
passive.description: Vixyy gains 5% critical strike chance and 5% dodge chance for each enemy within 10 meters, up to 25%.

passive.name: Soul Contract
passive.description: When Vixyy kills an enemy, she gains a stack of Soul Debt. At 5 stacks, she can activate a special ability (see ultimate).

active.name: Card Throw
active.description: Throws three obsidian cards in a spread, each dealing 70% physical damage. Enemies hit lose 10% movement speed for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 5 seconds.

active.name: Luck Drain
active.description: Selects an enemy, reducing their critical strike chance by 50% for 4 seconds and granting Vixyy that critical chance for the same duration. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Ace in the Hole
ultimate.description: Vixyy reveals the Ace of Spades from her hat, dealing 300% true damage to a single target. If the target dies, Vixyy's Soul Debt stacks reset and she gains a permanent 5% damage bonus for the rest of the match (stacks up to 3 times). Cooldown: 60 seconds. Requires 5 Soul Debt stacks.

special.name: Stacked Deck
special.description: Vixyy's basic attacks have a 15% chance to fire an additional card, dealing 50% damage.

ratings.notes: Ranged carry with scaling damage, critical manipulation, and execution potential.

skills.name: Lady Luck
skills.description: Vixyy's basic attacks have a 10% chance to deal double damage and refund the attack's cooldown (if any).

skills.name: Wild Card
skills.description: Vixyy draws a random card, gaining a temporary buff: Heart (heal), Diamond (gold), Club (armor), Spade (damage). Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Fold
skills.description: Vixyy becomes untargetable for 1.5 seconds, leaving behind a decoy card that explodes for 100% damage after the duration. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Student's Challenge
skills.description: When Harley is an ally, Vixyy's cooldowns are reduced by 10%. When Harley is an enemy, both take 10% increased damage from each other.

heroes.lore: Vixyy Joe was a street rat who won a cursed deck of cards from a demon, trading her soul for the power to never lose. She runs the infernal casino, teaching deception and magic to those who seek her out. Her finest student was Harley, who left to make his own legend. Vixyy waits for his return, uncertain who will win their inevitable confrontation.

relationships.description: Vixyy taught Harley everything he knows. She is proud of him, but knows he will one day challenge her.
The demon who gave her the deck occasionally visits. Their relationship is a tense standoff of mutual respect and suspicion.

pet.name: Supreme Jade Owl
pet.description: A supreme jade owl, feathers carved from a single massive piece of polished jade, eyes two glowing emerald orbs. Not living but not merely a statue; a construct bound to Vixyy by a contract older than her own. Can speak in a voice like rustling leaves, can fly, its screech shatters glass and rattles souls.
pet.fulldesc: The supreme jade owl is Vixyy's most prized possession, a construct that has been with her since she won the infernal casino. Its feathers are carved from a single, massive piece of polished jade, each plume distinct, each edge sharp. Its eyes are two glowing, emerald orbs that see through all deception—lies, illusions, even the hidden cards in an opponent's hand. The owl is not a living creature, but it is not merely a statue either; it is a familiar, bound to her by a contract older than her own. It can speak, in a voice like rustling leaves, and it offers her counsel when the odds are uncertain. It can fly, its wings spreading wide, its jade feathers catching the light, and when danger threatens, it can unleash a screech that shatters glass and rattles the souls of the living. It is her only confidant, the one being in the universe she trusts with her secrets, and it watches over her with the silent, patient wisdom of a creature that has witnessed the rise and fall of a thousand gamblers.
abilities: All-Seeing Eye – reveals hidden enemies and traps within 15 meters.
Soul Screech – stuns all enemies within 8 meters for 1 second.
Jade Shield – grants Vixyy a shield that absorbs 200 damage.

heroes.specialty: Critical-based marksman with scaling damage, luck manipulation, and execution potential. Excels in extended fights and high-stakes duels.

heroes.name: Axe Blade
heroes.shortDescription: A mountain carved from the heart of a storm, a being of raw primal power tempered by an unbreakable sense of duty. Axe Blade is the orc king who once sought to level all civilization—until he saw the cost. Now he wanders the world righting the wrongs committed by his brother Balmond, waiting for the day when he can bring the destroyer back from the darkness.
heroes.fullDescription: Axe Blade stands like a mountain carved from the heart of a storm, a being of raw primal power tempered by an unbreakable sense of duty—an orc of immense stature, yet where his brother Balmond is a crimson-tanned engine of destruction, Axe Blade is a figure of regal jade-blue majesty. His face is a map of honorable combat: a square jaw, high cheekbones, a broad flat nose, and a network of scars crisscrossing from brow to jaw—each one a record of a battle fought to protect the weak. His skin is rough, textured like the bark of an ancient oak, yet it gleams with a faint metallic sheen, the color of deep sea ice mixed with the pale blue of a winter sky, with tribal scars crisscrossing his chest and arms. His tusks are painted with white war marks that curve upward from his lower jaw, and his hair is long and thick, pure black, pulled into a samurai topknot with a silver phoenix clasp, the rest falling in a dark curtain down his back. His eyes are deep royal blue, burning with quiet fire, the eyes of a warrior who has seen too much and still chooses to fight. His physique is a monument to orcish strength: a barrel chest of dense corded sinew, thick arms like tree trunks, massive shoulders, a narrow waist relative to his bulk, and powerful legs. He wears a white fur loincloth robe over his chest—thick and soft—with Shinto-style silver and royal blue overlapping plate armor lacquered to a mirror finish covering his torso. Silver bracers engraved with the names of the fallen protect his forearms, and silver pauldrons shaped like crane wings guard his shoulders. A necklace of skeletal demon fingers on braided leather hangs around his neck—trophies from the demon lords he has slain—and a silver link belt with leather pouches cinches his waist. His lower body is covered by half-torn pants of deep royal blue with frayed edges, and his feet are clad in heavy boots. His weapon, the Cataclysm, is a giant great battle axe that has become synonymous with his name—taller than he is, its shaft a length of dark petrified wood wrapped in silver wire, its blade a massive crescent-shaped slab of enchanted steel that gleams with pale blue light, wide and sharp, etched with ancient inscriptions—runes of power that flare with brilliant white lightning when he channels his strength. With the Cataclysm, he can split the earth itself, sending tremors that level mountains; he can throw it like a blazing comet, its edge trailing fire that consumes the impure; and he can spin it in a divine whirlwind that cuts through armies like a scythe through wheat. This orc king was once a conqueror alongside his brother Balmond, uniting the orc tribes under a single banner and forging an empire. But Balmond's rage grew, and he began to dream of conquest, of burning cities, of ruling over ash. During the Siege of Aethelgard, Axe saw a child crying over her mother's body, a family huddled in a burning barn, and he understood that his brother had become the very evil they had once sworn to fight. He confronted Balmond on the steps of the celestial temple, fought him, and banished him—but could not bring himself to kill him. The orc empire shattered into warring factions, and Axe walked away from the ruins, his heart heavy with grief. Now he wanders the world as a savior, appearing in times of crisis and vanishing when the danger passes—standing in the ruins of a demon-ravaged village, the Cataclysm planted before him, his jade-blue skin glowing in the fires of salvation, waiting for the day when he can bring his brother back from the darkness, hoping against hope that the destroyer can be redeemed.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering, when the orc tribes of the badlands were scattered and warring, two brothers were born to the chieftain of the Jade Clan. The elder was Balmond—crimson-skinned, hot-tempered, born with a rage that would one day consume him. The younger was Axe Blade—jade-blue, calm, born with a stillness that belied his power. They were inseparable, Balmond the sword, Axe the shield. Together, they united the orc tribes under a single banner, forging an empire that stretched from the eastern badlands to the western sea. But power corrupts, and Balmond began to dream of conquest, of burning cities, of ruling over ash. Axe tried to counsel him, to remind him of the orcish code: 'Strength is for protection, not destruction.' Balmond laughed and called his brother weak.
story.part2: The breaking point came during the Siege of Aethelgard. Balmond led the orc army against the celestial city, burning villages, slaughtering civilians, reveling in the chaos. Axe followed, not as a soldier, but as a conscience. He saw a child crying over her mother's body, a family huddled in a burning barn, a priest trying to shield his congregation. And he understood that his brother had become the very evil they had once sworn to fight. He confronted Balmond on the steps of the celestial temple, and they fought—brother against brother, axe against axe. Axe won, but he could not kill his brother. He banished him instead, and the orc empire shattered into warring factions once more.
story.part3: Axe Blade walked away from the ruins of his empire, his heart heavy with grief. He had failed his brother, failed his people, failed the code. For decades, he wandered the world, saving villages from bandits, protecting caravans from monsters, healing the wounds that his brother's rampages had caused. He never took a kingdom, never sought followers. He was a shadow, a myth, a green-skinned ghost who appeared in times of crisis and vanished when the danger passed. The people of Kretos began to call him 'The World-Savior,' not knowing that he was also the brother of the World-Destroyer.
story.part4: The Cataclysm axe was forged in the heart of a dying volcano, a weapon of such power that it could level mountains. Axe had taken it from Balmond after their duel, hoping to prevent its use. But the axe was not evil; it was a tool, and tools are only as good or bad as the hands that wield them. Axe learned to use the Cataclysm for salvation—splitting the earth to create barriers against floods, throwing it like a comet to destroy demonic portals, spinning it in a divine whirlwind to protect the innocent. The axe sang in his hands, a weapon of destruction turned to creation.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the world, and Balmond has returned, leading demonic legions in a campaign of annihilation. Axe watches from the shadows, tracking his brother's movements, saving those he can, mourning those he cannot. He knows that the final battle is coming, that he will have to face Balmond again, that one of them will not walk away. He does not want to kill his brother; he wants to save him. He dreams of a day when Balmond will lower his axe, will see the cost of his rage, will remember the oath they swore as children. Until that day, Axe Blade walks the world, the Cataclysm heavy on his shoulder, his brother's name a wound that will not heal. He is the World-Savior Orc King, and his greatest battle is not against demons, but against the hope that his brother can still be saved.

weapons.name: Cataclysm
weapons.fulldesc: The Cataclysm is a giant great battle axe that has become synonymous with Axe Blade's name. The axe is taller than he is, its shaft a length of dark, petrified wood wrapped in silver wire for grip, its blade a massive, crescent‑shaped slab of enchanted steel that gleams with a pale, blue light. The blade is wide, its edge sharp enough to split a hair, its back thick and heavy, capable of crushing stone. Etched into the metal are ancient inscriptions, runes of power that flare with brilliant, white lightning when he channels his strength. With the Cataclysm, Axe Blade can split the earth itself, sending tremors that level mountains; he can throw it like a blazing comet, its edge trailing fire that consumes the impure; and he can spin it in a divine whirlwind that cuts through armies like a scythe through wheat. The axe is not merely a weapon; it is an extension of his will, a tool of salvation that he wields with the precision of a surgeon and the force of a god.

heroes.lore: Axe Blade was once a conqueror alongside his brother Balmond, but after seeing the cost of their rampage, he turned against his brother and became a wandering savior. He now protects the innocent from the very destruction his brother wreaks, hoping to one day redeem Balmond.

relationships.description: Axe Blade loves his brother and wants to save him from darkness, but knows he may have to stop him permanently.
They have fought alongside each other against demonic incursions; Axe respects Alucard's dedication to protecting the innocent.

heroes.specialty: Tanky fighter with team protection, crowd control, and anti-demon specialization. Excels at initiating fights and protecting allies.

dialogue.selection: I will save this world, brother or no.

heroes.name: Taurus
heroes.shortDescription: A divine minotaur who guards the labyrinth that contains his twin brother Minotaur. Taurus was chosen by the gods to be a Zodiac guardian, but refused immortality to stay with his brother. Now he walks the endless corridors of his own creation, waiting for the day when his brother might emerge from the darkness.
heroes.fullDescription: Taurus stands at the center of an eternal maze, a divine minotaur whose duty has become his prison, whose love for his brother has become his eternal penance—a being of divine celestial majesty chosen not for destruction but for the sacred task of containing it. His form is massive, a towering edifice of muscle and fur that seems carved from the very stone of the labyrinth he guards. His fur is thick and lush, a deep jade green with a golden undertone that shimmers in the faint light of the maze. His physique is a monument to divine craftsmanship: vast shoulders, a barrel chest of dense muscle, thick arms, a narrow waist, and powerful legs ending in cloven hooves of solid silver. His face is hidden behind a divine helm of gleaming silver and light gold, sculpted to resemble a celestial bull with jade-tipped horns that sweep back from the brow, but his eyes are visible—burning with steady golden light, calm and watchful, the eyes of a guardian who has not slept for centuries. He wears full plate armor of silver and light gold with jade inlay, the plates seamlessly blended and polished to a mirror shine, with labyrinth patterns traced in jade across the surface. Articulated vambraces engraved with the names of souls he has guided safely cover his arms, and massive pauldrons shaped as celestial bull heads guard his shoulders—the bulls' horns sweeping back, their eyes set with glowing jade. A torc of twisted silver and gold in the shape of serpent heads with ruby eyes circles his neck, and silver and gold chains hang from his shoulders. An armored fauld protects his hips, greaves of divine metal cover his legs, and his cloven hooves are clad in sandals of light gold. His weapon, the Labyrinth's Heart, is a warhammer of such immense size that he must grip it with both hands to lift it—its head alone twice the size of his own. The shaft is a length of petrified wood from the first tree that grew in the center of the labyrinth, wrapped in silver wire, carved with the story of his failure and his redemption. The head is a massive block of light gold and silver, its faces etched with the twisting paths of the maze, its edges sharp enough to cleave stone. At its center, a sphere of jade rotates slowly, glowing with soft green light that pulses with the heartbeat of the labyrinth itself. When he strikes the ground, new passages open; when he raises it to the sky, the walls of his domain rise higher. This divine minotaur was once the twin brother of Minotaur, both born to a divine bull and a mortal queen. Minotaur's rage grew as he did, fed by whispers of demons, and he became a monster in truth—not in form but in heart, killing without reason, destroying without purpose. The gods came to Taurus with an offer: become a Zodiac guardian, ascend to the stars, and they would deal with Minotaur. Taurus refused, saying, 'He is my brother. I will not let you kill him.' The gods offered another way: build a labyrinth that would contain Minotaur forever, and Taurus would be its keeper. He built the maze with his own hands, weaving passages that twisted and turned, sealing the walls with magic that not even a god could break. He led his brother into the heart of the labyrinth, and there he left him, weeping, as the walls closed around them both. Now he walks the endless corridors of his own creation—a realm of stone walls that shift and change, of golden light that filters from nowhere, of silence broken only by the distant roar of his imprisoned brother. He stands at the center of the maze, the Labyrinth's Heart in his hands, his golden eyes fixed on the darkness where Minotaur rages, waiting for the day when a hero will come—or when his brother might finally emerge from the darkness into the light.

story.part1: Before the gods raised their champions to the stars, there were two twin brothers born to a divine bull and a mortal queen. The first was Taurus—calm, patient, born with the wisdom of ages. The second was Minotaur—fierce, hungry, born with a rage that would consume him. They were inseparable as children, playing in the fields of their mother's kingdom, learning the ways of the hunt from their father. But Minotaur's rage grew as he did, fed by the whispers of demons who saw in him a weapon. By the time they were adults, Minotaur had become a monster in truth—not in form, but in heart. He killed without reason, destroyed without purpose, and the people cried out for his death.
story.part2: The gods came to Taurus with an offer: become a Zodiac guardian, ascend to the stars, and be remembered for eternity. In exchange, they would deal with Minotaur. Taurus refused. 'He is my brother,' he said. 'I will not let you kill him.' The gods, impressed by his loyalty, offered another way: build a labyrinth that would contain Minotaur forever, and Taurus would be its keeper. Taurus agreed. He built the maze with his own hands, weaving passages that twisted and turned, sealing the walls with magic that not even a god could break. He led his brother into the heart of the labyrinth, and there he left him, weeping, as the walls closed around them both.
story.part3: For centuries, Taurus walked the labyrinth, maintaining its walls, sealing the cracks that time and demonic influence created. He never saw his brother—Minotaur had descended into the deepest levels, where even the light of Taurus's divine armor could not reach. But he felt him there, raging against his chains, his hunger growing with each passing year. The gods came again, offering to raise Taurus to the Zodiac, to give him a place among the celestial guardians. Taurus refused again. 'I will not leave him alone in the dark,' he said. The gods shook their heads and departed, leaving Taurus to his duty.
story.part4: The Sundering shook the labyrinth to its foundations. New passages opened, old ones collapsed, and for the first time in centuries, Minotaur's roar echoed through the halls. Taurus fought to contain the damage, sealing rifts, rebuilding walls, pushing back the demonic forces that sought to free his brother. He has not slept in months, not rested in years. His armor is dented, his warhammer chipped, his divine light flickering. But he does not stop. He cannot stop. If Minotaur escapes, the world will burn.
story.part5: Now, Taurus stands at a crossroads. He has heard whispers that a hero—a mortal with a pure heart—is seeking the labyrinth, hoping to slay Minotaur and end the threat forever. Taurus does not know whether to let them pass or to turn them away. Part of him wants his brother's suffering to end, wants the burden of being the warden to be lifted. But another part—the part that still remembers playing in the fields with his twin—hopes that Minotaur can be saved, not killed. He waits in the center of the maze, the Labyrinth's Heart heavy in his hands, and he prays to the gods who abandoned him: 'Let me find another way. Let me bring my brother home.' For he is Taurus, the Labyrinth Keeper, and his vigil will not end until the stars themselves burn out.

weapons.fulldesc: The Labyrinth's Heart is a warhammer of such immense size that Taurus must grip it with both hands to lift it, its head alone twice the size of his own. The hammer is a masterpiece of divine architecture, its form echoing the great temples and palaces of the celestial realm. The shaft is a length of petrified wood from the first tree that grew in the center of the labyrinth, wrapped in silver wire for grip, its surface carved with the story of his failure and his redemption. The head is massive, a block of light gold and silver, its faces etched with the twisting paths of the maze, its edges sharp enough to cleave stone, its back heavy enough to crush the strongest armor. At its center, a sphere of jade rotates slowly, glowing with a soft, green light that pulses with the heartbeat of the labyrinth itself. When he strikes the ground with the Labyrinth's Heart, new passages open; when he raises it to the sky, the walls of his domain rise higher; when he charges, the hammer trails light, and the very earth trembles at his approach.

passive.description: Taurus takes 15% reduced damage from enemies he has recently attacked.

active.name: Seal Passage
active.description: Taurus slams the hammer, creating a wall of stone in a 6-meter line that lasts 4 seconds. The wall blocks enemy movement and projectiles. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

active.name: Maze Shift
active.description: Taurus teleports to a nearby location, leaving behind a copy of himself that taunts enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Heart of the Labyrinth
ultimate.description: Taurus channels for 1 second, then creates a 20-meter radius maze zone for 8 seconds. Enemies inside have their vision reduced to 5 meters, their movement speed reduced by 40%, and they cannot use dashes or blinks. Allies inside gain 30% movement speed and see through the maze. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Brother's Echo
special.description: When Minotaur is an ally, Taurus's ultimate also reduces damage taken by Minotaur by 30%. When Minotaur is an enemy, Taurus's attacks against him apply a stacking slow (up to 60%).

ratings.notes: Extremely durable tank with zone control, terrain manipulation, and team utility.

skills.name: Guardian's Aura
skills.description: Allies within 8 meters of Taurus gain 15 armor and magic resist.

skills.name: Horn Charge
skills.description: Taurus charges forward 12 meters, impaling the first enemy hit and carrying them for the duration, then stunning them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Labyrinth's Blessing
skills.description: Taurus blesses an ally, granting them 20% damage reduction and 20% movement speed for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Eternal Vigil
skills.description: Taurus cannot be killed by Minotaur's attacks while Minotaur is under 10% health (prevents accidental fratricide).

heroes.lore: Taurus was chosen by the gods to be a Zodiac guardian, but refused immortality to stay with his twin brother Minotaur, whom he sealed in a labyrinth. He now walks the maze as its keeper, waiting for the day his brother might be saved.

relationships.description: Taurus sealed Minotaur in the labyrinth and guards him out of love, not hatred.
The other Zodiac guardians respect Taurus's choice, though they consider him tragic.

heroes.specialty: Tank with zone control, terrain manipulation, and team protection. Excels at controlling team fights and protecting objectives.

heroes.name: Ecanju Ferosta
titles: The Radiant Priestess of Divine Order
Reincarnation of the Goddess of Light
Nemesis of the Queen of Suffering
heroes.shortDescription: She descends upon the world like the first ray of dawn breaking through an endless night—a being of such pure, radiant beauty that even the shadows recoil from her presence. A Japanese nun elevated to the status of a living saint, Ecanju Ferosta is the reincarnation of the goddess of light, the eternal nemesis of Alice, the Queen of Suffering. She has died a hundred times and been reborn a hundred more, her soul burning brighter with each resurrection, her purpose clearer with each passing age.
heroes.fullDescription: Ecanju Ferosta descends upon the world like the first ray of dawn breaking through an endless night—a Japanese nun elevated to the status of a living saint, the reincarnation of the goddess of light, a being of such pure radiant beauty that even the shadows recoil from her presence. Her face is heart-shaped and soft, with a delicate jawline tapering to a gentle point, high cheekbones, a small straight nose, and full lips that curve in a warm eternal smile. Her skin is ethereal, luminous as moonlight on snow, smooth and flawless, with warmth emanating from within. Her eyes are deep liquid gold, swirling with inner light, blazing like twin suns when her power rises. Her hair is silken blonde, long and straight, flowing past her waist with side-swept bangs brushing her brows. Her physique is voluptuous and regal: a slender wasp-thin waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G-cup bust, her body a temple of divine perfection. She wears a sleeveless white robe plunging deep at the chest, held by golden cords, revealing her cleavage and smooth stomach, with a golden breastplate shaped like a blooming lotus overlaid. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, with golden vambraces on her forearms engraved with prayers, and golden pauldrons curving upward like dove's wings, their edges traced with silver filigree. A simple cord of braided silk with a golden ring pendant hangs around her neck, and golden cords tied in intricate bows cinch her waist. White silk undergarments are visible through high slits in her robe, exposing her thick smooth thighs, and her feet are bare, floating slightly above the ground, leaving faint golden footprints where she walks. A white jade charm shaped like a cross with a winged angel hangs at her hip. In her true form, she has four faces arranged in cardinal directions—creation, destruction, mourning, rebirth—but in her mortal form, she has a single face with golden eyes that blaze when she is angered. Her weapon, the ZeinZui, is a holy staff of exquisite craftsmanship—a length of polished white wood that spirals gently, its surface carved with the stories of a thousand holy wars. At its head, the staff blossoms into a crown of golden leaves, each leaf etched with a single luminous rune, cradling a sphere of pure white light—the captured essence of a dying star, purified and sanctified by the goddess's own hand. When she raises the ZeinZui, the sphere blazes with holy fire, and from it pour rays of healing light that can mend the most grievous wounds, resurrect the fallen, and scour the darkness from the souls of the damned. Her mount, GodRyu, is the Celestial Eastern Dragon King—a creature of breathtaking majesty, a hundred lengths of sinuous white-scaled muscle, his horns a crown of golden light, his mane a cascade of silver flame, his eyes twin suns of pure gold. He wears holy divine armor across his chest, shoulders, and tail—plates of gold and silver etched with runes of protection—and can take human form when he wishes, a tall regal man with flowing white hair and golden eyes. This radiant priestess is the eternal nemesis of Alice, the Queen of Suffering—two halves of a single soul, split at the moment of creation. She has died a hundred times and been reborn a hundred more, her soul burning brighter with each resurrection, her purpose clearer with each passing age. She does not hate her nemesis; she pities her, for she knows that the Queen of Suffering was once a being of light, twisted by grief and betrayal. Now she stands in the great cathedral of Aethelgard—a vast space of soaring marble pillars, stained glass windows depicting the history of the celestial order, and golden light streaming from an unseen source—the ZeinZui in her hand, GodRyu coiled around her, waiting for the day when she can offer Alice redemption, or, if redemption is impossible, the peace of final death.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering, when the realms of Kretos were still being shaped from the primordial chaos, a goddess of pure light was born from the first dawn. She had no name then—only purpose. She walked the newborn worlds, blessing the first forests with warmth, teaching the first creatures to bask in the sun, and singing the stars into their eternal orbits. She was the Light, and where she walked, darkness fled. But the Light was not alone. In the shadows of that first dawn, another being stirred—a being of shadow and sorrow, born from the grief of a universe that had not yet learned to heal. That being would one day be called Alice, the Queen of Suffering, and she was the Light's shadow, her twin, her opposite, her eternal adversary. They did not know each other then; they only knew that they were drawn together, light and shadow, two halves of a whole that could never be united. And so began the eternal dance, the endless cycle of death and rebirth, that would span a hundred lifetimes and shape the fate of Kretos itself.
story.part2: The first time Ecanju Ferosta died, she was not yet called by that name. She was a priestess in the Celestial Order, serving in the great cathedral of Aethelgard when the first rift to Nyxara opened. Demons poured through, their shadows blotting out the sun, and the priestess raised her staff to hold them back. She fought for three days and three nights, her holy light burning brighter with each passing hour, until her body could take no more. She fell, her staff clattering on the marble floor, and the demons surged forward—only to recoil as her body dissolved into light, scattering like a thousand fireflies. The priestess was gone, but her soul was not. It rose, carried by the winds of fate, and found a new vessel: a newborn child in a distant village, a girl with golden eyes and a birthmark shaped like a sunburst. The child would grow to be Ecanju Ferosta, the Radiant Priestess, and she would remember, in the depths of her soul, the sacrifice of her first life. The demons were pushed back, the rift was sealed, and the legend of the reincarnating light began.
story.part3: The hundredth death was the hardest. Ecanju had faced Alice before—a dozen times, a score of times—and each time, the Queen of Suffering had been too powerful, too consumed by her own grief to see the light that still flickered within her. But the hundredth battle was different. It took place in the ruins of the Moonlight Kingdom, where Alice had slaughtered an entire dynasty of elves in a single night. Ecanju arrived to find the silver halls stained with blood, the bodies of children still warm in their mothers' arms. Her rage was unlike anything she had felt in her previous lives—not the cold, righteous fury of a goddess, but the hot, desperate anger of a woman who had seen too much suffering. She fought Alice with a ferocity that surprised even herself, her staff blazing with the light of a dying star, her dragon roaring with a fury that cracked the sky. In the end, she lost. Alice's shadow blade pierced her heart, and she fell, her blood pooling on the blood-stained marble. But as she died, she reached out and touched Alice's cheek, and for a single, fleeting moment, the Queen of Suffering's eyes cleared. She saw not an enemy, but a sister. And then Ecanju was gone, her soul once again carried on the winds, and Alice was left alone in the darkness, weeping.
story.part4: The scholars of the Natural Balance have studied Ecanju's reincarnations for centuries, and they have concluded that her power grows with each death. The light that burns within her is not diminished by the destruction of her body; it is refined, purified, made brighter by the sacrifice. Each time she dies, she returns stronger, faster, more radiant than before. Her enemies fear her not because she is invincible, but because she is inevitable. She cannot be killed permanently; she cannot be stopped; she can only be delayed. The demons of Nyxara whisper her name as a curse, for they know that every time they strike her down, she will rise again, her light brighter, her purpose clearer. The angels of Aethelgard sing her name as a prayer, for they know that as long as she exists, there is hope. And Alice, the Queen of Suffering, knows that one day, Ecanju will face her for the final time—and on that day, the cycle will end, one way or another.
story.part5: The truth, known only to the oldest gods of Kretos, is that Ecanju Ferosta and Alice are not merely enemies—they are two halves of a single soul, split at the moment of creation. The Light and the Shadow were never meant to be separate; they were meant to be balanced, to dance together in eternal harmony. But something went wrong in the Sundering—a wound in the fabric of reality that tore the soul in two, creating the goddess of light and the queen of suffering. Ecanju does not know this truth; she only knows that she must fight, must sacrifice, must die and be reborn until the balance is restored. But deep in her soul, in the space between heartbeats, she feels the pull of something she cannot name—a longing, a loss, a love that she cannot explain. It is the shadow calling to the light, the sister reaching for her twin. And when the final battle comes, Ecanju will not raise her staff to destroy Alice. She will raise it to embrace her, to offer her the redemption that she has sought across a hundred lifetimes. For she is the Radiant Priestess of Divine Order, and her purpose is not to kill—it is to heal.

weapons.name: ZeinZui
weapons.fulldesc: The ZeinZui is a holy staff of exquisite craftsmanship, appearing as a length of polished white wood that spirals gently, its surface carved with the stories of a thousand holy wars. At its head, the staff blossoms into a crown of golden leaves, each leaf etched with a single, luminous rune, and cradled within the leaves is a sphere of pure, white light—the captured essence of a dying star, purified and sanctified by the goddess's own hand. The staff is taller than its wielder, yet she carries it with the ease of a shepherd's crook. When Ecanju raises the ZeinZui, the sphere blazes with holy fire, and from it pour rays of healing light that can mend the most grievous wounds, resurrect the fallen, and scour the darkness from the souls of the damned. When she strikes the ground with it, waves of pure energy radiate outward, buffeting her allies with divine strength and sending demons fleeing in terror. The staff hums with a low, resonant note when active—a sound like a choir singing in the distance—and the air around it shimmers with heatless light.
weapons.look: White spiraling wood, golden leaf crown at head, floating sphere of captured starlight within; runes glow with soft, golden light; warm to touch
weapons.aura: Golden and white, with rays of healing light

passive.name: Aura of Radiance
passive.description: Allies within 15 meters gain 10% increased healing received and 5% damage reduction. Against shadow or demon-aligned enemies, the aura also deals 2% of their max health as holy damage per second.

passive.name: Eternal Rebirth
passive.description: Upon taking fatal damage, Ecanju has a 30% chance to instead survive with 1 HP and become invulnerable for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 180 seconds. This effect is guaranteed to trigger once per reincarnation cycle.

active.name: Ray of Purification
active.description: Fires a beam of holy light in a straight line, dealing 150% magical damage to enemies and healing allies for 200% of Ecanju's magic power. Passes through all targets in its path. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Divine Intervention
active.description: Target ally is shielded for 300% of Ecanju's magic power for 5 seconds. If the shield breaks, it explodes, dealing 100% magical damage to nearby enemies. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

ultimate.name: Final Dawn
ultimate.description: Ecanju channels the full power of the ZeinZui for 2 seconds, then unleashes a massive explosion of holy light in a 30-meter radius. Allies within the radius are healed for 500% of her magic power and cleansed of all debuffs. Enemies take 400% magical damage and are stunned for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 120 seconds. Passive: Upon activation, the spirit of her previous reincarnation manifests, granting all allies 20% increased damage for 10 seconds.

special.name: Light of Redemption
special.description: When Ecanju dies, she leaves behind a patch of radiant light. Allies who stand in this light heal for 10% of their max health per second. After 5 seconds, the light coalesces into a beacon—if an ally touches the beacon within 10 seconds, Ecanju is resurrected with 50% HP and mana.

ratings.notes: Overwhelming magical support with unmatched healing and resurrection capabilities. Her reincarnation passive makes her incredibly difficult to permanently eliminate.

skills.name: Divine Radiance
skills.description: Ecanju's basic attacks and abilities apply a stack of Radiance to enemies (max 5 stacks). Each stack reduces the enemy's dark resistance by 5% and deals 2% of her magic power as holy damage per second.

skills.name: Blessing of the Dawn
skills.description: Ecanju blesses a target ally, increasing their damage and healing output by 25% for 8 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Shield of Faith
skills.description: Ecanju creates a barrier of holy light around herself, absorbing 400 damage + 100% of her magic power. While the shield holds, she is immune to crowd control. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Judgment of the Goddess
skills.description: When Ecanju falls below 20% HP, she unleashes a pulse of light that knocks back all enemies within 10 meters and heals all allies within the same radius for 30% of their max HP. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: In the celestial order of Kretos, Ecanju Ferosta is not merely a priestess—she is a living weapon forged by the divine to oppose the darkness that Alice represents. Born from the light of the first dawn, her soul has been reincarnated across a hundred lifetimes, each death a sacrifice to protect the innocent, each rebirth a testament to the unbreakable will of the light...

relationships.description: Two halves of a single soul, split at creation—light and shadow, destined to clash across a hundred lifetimes. Ecanju seeks to redeem Alice, not destroy her.
The Celestial Eastern Dragon King, bound to Ecanju since her first reincarnation. He is her protector, her brother in arms, and the only being who has witnessed all of her deaths.

mount.name: GodRyu
mount.description: A creature of breathtaking majesty, GodRyu is a hundred lengths of sinuous, white-scaled muscle, his horns a crown of golden light, his mane a cascade of silver flame. His eyes are twin suns of pure gold, and when he opens his mouth, the roar that emerges shakes the foundations of the world. He wears holy divine armor across his chest, shoulders, and tail—plates of gold and silver etched with runes of protection. He can summon others of his kind with a thought, calling forth a legion of celestial serpents to fight at his mistress's command.
mount.fulldesc: GodRyu is not merely a mount; he is a guardian, a companion, and a brother in arms to Ecanju Ferosta. He can take human form when he wishes—a tall, regal man with flowing white hair and golden eyes, dressed in robes of silver and white—but he prefers to remain in his dragon form, circling above Ecanju, watching over her with the fierce devotion of a creature who has served the light since before the first human drew breath. When she prays, he coils around her, his massive head resting on the ground beside her, his golden eyes half-closed in contentment. When she fights, he descends like a comet, his claws rending, his breath a torrent of holy flame, his very presence a barrier that the darkness cannot cross. His scales shimmer with an inner light that pulses in time with her heartbeat, and his roar—a sound like thunder and the crash of waves—can shatter the morale of entire armies. He has been with her through all hundred of her deaths, and he will be with her at the final battle, when the light and shadow finally unite.
abilities: Holy Flame Breath – Torrent of white-gold fire that deals 300% magical damage and purifies dark magic.
Celestial Roar – Emits a roar that stuns all enemies within 20 meters for 2 seconds and reduces their armor by 30%.
Legion Call – Summons 5 lesser celestial dragons to fight for 30 seconds (cooldown: 180 seconds).
Wings of Light – Can fly at supersonic speeds, leaving a trail of golden light that heals allies who pass through it.

heroes.specialty: Support / Healer / Resurrection specialist with powerful anti-darkness capabilities and reincarnation mechanics.

dialogue.greeting: The light welcomes you, child. Have you come to seek redemption?
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Let the final dawn break upon you!
dialogue.death: I will return... as I always have...
dialogue.respawn: A hundred deaths, a hundred rebirths. The light endures.

heroes.name: Longwei
heroes.shortDescription: He sits upon a throne of obsidian and gold, a being whose mere presence commands the reverence of empires and the terror of his enemies. Longwei is the Dragon Emperor, the Tyrant of Scales, a man whose features blend celestial beauty with draconic majesty. He is the father of Zilong, though his son does not know it—raised in secrecy, hidden from the machinations of the court, allowed to grow strong on his own. And Longwei watches, proud and patient, guiding his son's path with subtle hands.
heroes.fullDescription: Longwei sits upon a throne of obsidian and gold, a being whose mere presence commands the reverence of empires and the terror of his enemies—the Dragon Emperor, the Tyrant of Scales, a ruler who has held the Celestial Throne for centuries. His face is a study in Han Chinese refinement: a strong square jaw, high prominent cheekbones, a straight aristocratic nose, and full lips that can curve in warmth or flatten in command. His skin is ethereally clear, with a faint golden undertone and a translucent quality that hints at the dragon within. His eyes are deep burnished gold, igniting to poison-green when his anger rises, and two small curved horns rise from his brow, polished to a mirror shine with black jade tips. His hair is long and flowing, a silken cascade of black shot through with golden strands, falling past his shoulders. His physique is lean and defined, dense with corded muscle—broad shoulders, a defined chest, thick powerful arms, a narrow waist, and long strong legs. He wears golden plate armor shaped like dragon scales, overlapping, chased with coiling serpents and phoenixes in black jade inlay. Articulated golden vambraces with dragon-scale patterns cover his arms, and his pauldrons are shaped like snarling dragon heads, their jaws open, fangs bared, eyes set with poison-green gems. A torc of twisted gold with dragon head ends—ruby eyes gleaming—circles his neck, and a belt of interlocking jade plaques carved with scenes of empire cinches his waist. Armored tassets of gold and jade protect his hips, golden greaves with dragon-scale patterns cover his legs, and golden boots with jade inlay encase his feet. A blood-red cape hangs from his shoulders, heavy with the weight of conquered banners. Rings of gold and black jade adorn every finger, bracelets of carved jade inscribed with ancestor names circle his wrists, and his horns serve as his crown. His weapon, the Dragon's Wrath, is a divine mythological spear passed down through his line for a thousand generations—longer than he is tall, its shaft a length of petrified dragonbone wrapped in golden wire, carved with the story of the first dragon who pledged fealty to his ancestor. Its blade is massive, a wicked curve of black jade edged with gold, etched with runes that blaze with poison-green fire when he channels his power. At the base of the blade, a dragon's head is carved, its jaws open, its throat a conduit for the fire that flows from his soul. When he throws it, it flies true, trailing green flame, erupting in a conflagration that can level armies and reduce fortresses to ash. This dragon emperor is the father of Zilong, though his son does not know it—raised in secrecy, hidden from the machinations of the court, allowed to grow strong on his own. Longwei watches from the shadows, proud and patient, guiding his son's path with subtle hands, testing him with trials that will make him stronger, protecting him from threats that would crush a lesser warrior. He sits in his throne room—a vast hall of obsidian pillars and golden mosaics depicting the history of his dynasty, dragon motifs woven into every surface, the air thick with incense and the weight of centuries—his cape pooling around him, the Dragon's Wrath planted before him, waiting for the day when the secret will be revealed, when father and son will stand together, dragon and dragon-blood, and the world will tremble at the joining of their power.

story.part1: Long before the Sundering, when the dragon-blooded clans of Kretos first rose to power, there was a prince named Longwei. He was the third son of the Celestial Emperor, a boy of unremarkable birth and unpromising future, destined to serve his elder brothers and fade into obscurity. But the dragons had other plans. On the night of his sixteenth birthday, a celestial dragon descended from the heavens and marked him—a burning brand on his chest, the sigil of the Dragon Emperor. His brothers, consumed by jealousy, conspired to kill him. They ambushed him in the jade gardens of the palace, their blades gleaming with poison, their hearts black with betrayal. Longwei fought back, but he was outnumbered, outmatched, and as he fell to his knees, the brand on his chest ignited. He transformed—not into the golden dragon he would one day become, but into something far more terrible: a creature of raw, unbridled fury, scales of obsidian and gold, eyes of molten fire. When the slaughter was over, his brothers lay dead, their bodies scattered across the gardens like broken dolls. Longwei wept over them, for he had not wanted this. But the dragon's blood had spoken, and the throne was his.
story.part2: The first decades of Longwei's reign were a bloodbath. The noble houses, fearful of the young Dragon Emperor, rose against him in a rebellion that consumed the eastern provinces. He met them on the fields of the Jade River, his army outnumbered five to one, his generals urging him to retreat. Longwei refused. He walked to the front lines alone, unarmed, and faced the enemy general—a towering warrior clad in black iron, his sword stained with the blood of Longwei's loyalists. The general laughed and raised his blade. Longwei did not move. He did not flinch. He simply looked at the general, and his eyes began to glow. The transformation was instantaneous: scales erupted from his skin, wings tore through his robes, and in the space of a heartbeat, the Dragon Emperor stood before the enemy army—a golden dragon of such terrifying majesty that the horses bolted and the soldiers dropped their weapons. He breathed once, and the general's army turned to ash. He roared once, and the rebel lords fled. From that day forward, no one dared to challenge the Dragon Emperor openly. They schemed, they plotted, they whispered in the shadows—but they did not fight.
story.part3: The greatest secret of Longwei's reign is not political—it is personal. In his youth, before the dragon's blood consumed him, he loved a woman. Her name was Lian, a warrior of the northern tribes, fierce and free, with hair like a raven's wing and eyes like winter stars. They met on the battlefield, fought to a draw, and fell in love in the space between sword strokes. She was his heart, his anchor, the only person who saw the man beneath the dragon. When she gave birth to their son, Zilong, Longwei wept with joy—and with terror. For he knew that his enemies would stop at nothing to destroy the heir to the Celestial Throne. So he made the hardest choice of his life: he sent Lian and Zilong away, into hiding, and he erased every record of their existence. He visited them in secret, watching his son grow from a distance, teaching him through dreams and whispers, guiding him toward the warrior he was meant to become. Lian understood; she never blamed him. But when she died—killed by assassins who had somehow discovered her location—Longwei's grief was absolute. He did not attend her funeral; he could not bear to see her body. Instead, he flew to the highest peak of the Celestial Mountains and roared for three days and three nights, until his voice gave out and the snow turned to steam. To this day, he carries a lock of her hair in a locket over his heart, and he whispers her name every night before he sleeps.
story.part4: The scholars of the Celestial Order have long debated whether Longwei is a tyrant or a savior. His rule has been absolute, his justice swift and merciless—he has executed entire noble houses for treason, burned cities that dared to defy him, and crushed rebellions with a ferocity that has become legend. But he has also protected the realm from threats that the common people will never know about: demons from Nyxara, abyssal dragons from the void, and the machinations of the Shadow Accord. He has spent his centuries on the throne building walls that cannot be breached, forging alliances that cannot be broken, and training his son in secret to be the warrior who will one day take his place. The people fear him, but they also love him, for they know that the Dragon Emperor's wrath is reserved for their enemies, not for them. And when they see the golden dragon circling above the capital, they bow their heads and whisper thanks, for they know that as long as Longwei rules, the realm is safe.
story.part5: The truth, known only to the oldest dragons and the gods themselves, is that Longwei is dying. The dragon's blood that gave him power is consuming him, turning his organs to jade, his bones to obsidian. He has perhaps another century before he transforms fully into a beast—a mindless, raging dragon that will destroy everything he has built. He does not fear death; he has lived too long for that. But he fears what will happen to Zilong, to his people, to the realm he has protected for so long. So he waits, and he watches, and he guides his son from the shadows, preparing him for the day when he must take the throne. He has left letters for Zilong, hidden in the vaults of the palace, to be opened after his death—letters that explain everything: his mother's fate, his father's love, the burden of the dragon's blood. And when Zilong finally learns the truth, Longwei hopes that his son will not hate him, that he will understand why the Dragon Emperor had to be a stranger, a legend, a myth. For Longwei is not a tyrant; he is a father, and he has sacrificed everything—his love, his family, his very humanity—for the son who will one day save the world.
