weapons.name: Dragon's Wrath
weapons.fulldesc: The Dragon's Wrath is a divine mythological spear that has been passed down through Longwei's line for a thousand generations. The spear is longer than he is tall, its shaft a length of petrified dragonbone wrapped in golden wire, its surface carved with the story of the first dragon who pledged fealty to his ancestor. The blade is massive, a wicked curve of black jade edged with gold, its surface etched with runes that blaze with poison-green fire when he channels his power. At the base of the blade, a dragon's head is carved, its jaws open, its throat a conduit for the fire that flows from his soul. When he throws the Dragon's Wrath, it flies true, trailing green flame, and when it strikes, it erupts in a conflagration that can level armies and reduce fortresses to ash. When he holds it, he can breathe fire through it, unleashing torrents of draconic flame that scour the land clean. The spear hums with a deep, resonant vibration when active, and the air around it shimmers with heat haze.
weapons.look: Petrified dragonbone shaft wrapped in gold, massive curved black jade blade edged with gold, dragon's head at base with open jaws

passive.name: Dragon's Fury
passive.description: Each successful hit with Dragon's Wrath applies a stack of Dragonfire (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target explodes, dealing 150% physical damage to all enemies within 8 meters.

passive.name: Scale of the Emperor
passive.description: Longwei's dragon scales grant him 15% damage reduction. This increases to 30% when he is below 30% health.

active.name: Flame Breath
active.description: Longwei breathes a cone of poison-green fire, dealing 200% physical damage and applying a burn that deals 50% damage per second for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Spear Throw
active.description: Longwei throws the Dragon's Wrath in a straight line, piercing all enemies and dealing 250% physical damage. The spear returns to his hand after 2 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Draconic Transformation
ultimate.description: Longwei transforms into his golden dragon form for 15 seconds. In dragon form, his basic attacks become area-of-effect claw swipes, his Flame Breath cooldown is reduced to 2 seconds, and he gains 50% increased health and 30% increased damage. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: While in dragon form, nearby enemies are slowed by 20% and take 50% damage per second from his aura.

special.name: Emperor's Will
special.description: When Longwei kills an enemy hero with Dragon's Wrath, all nearby allies gain 20% increased damage and 30% movement speed for 10 seconds.

ratings.notes: Overwhelming physical damage dealer with dragon transformation that makes him nearly unkillable. His passive damage reduction and area control make him a top-tier fighter.

skills.name: Dragon Scales
skills.description: Longwei's scales grant him 10 + (level) physical defense and magic defense. Additionally, he takes 15% reduced damage from enemies below 50% health.

skills.name: Jade Strike
skills.description: Longwei lunges forward 10 meters, impaling the first enemy hit and stunning them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Emperor's Roar
skills.description: Longwei roars, fearing all enemies within 12 meters for 2 seconds and reducing their armor by 30% for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Ancestor's Blessing
skills.description: When Longwei falls below 10% health, he summons the spirits of his ancestors, becoming invulnerable for 3 seconds and healing for 50% of his max health. Triggers once every 180 seconds.

heroes.lore: Longwei is the Dragon Emperor, the Tyrant of Scales, a ruler who has held the Celestial Throne for centuries. His power is immense—he can transform at will into a golden dragon of terrifying majesty...

relationships.description: Zilong does not know that the Dragon Emperor is his father. Longwei watches over him from the shadows, guiding his path, preparing him to take the throne.
The love of his life, mother of Zilong, killed by assassins. Longwei carries a lock of her hair in a locket over his heart.

heroes.specialty: Fighter / Tank with dragon transformation, high sustain, and area control.

dialogue.greeting: Kneel before the Dragon Emperor, or be consumed by flame.
dialogue.respawn: The dragon's blood does not accept death.

heroes.name: Brigeinda
titles: The Iron Maiden of the Iron Mountain
heroes.shortDescription: She emerges from the smoke of her own forge like a goddess of war forged from steel and spite, a being whose beauty is as dangerous as the weapons she builds. Brigeinda is an eighteen-year-old prodigy of Dwarven-Elf blood, a fusion of two ancient races that has produced something entirely new and utterly formidable. Her lower jaw is a gleaming metal prosthesis, her left arm entirely mechanical, and her weapon—the Siegebreaker—is a hammer that can summon localized earthquakes and call down lightning from clear skies.
heroes.fullDescription: Brigeinda emerges from the smoke of her own forge like a goddess of war forged from steel and spite—an eighteen-year-old prodigy of Dwarven-Elf blood, a fusion of two ancient races that has produced something entirely new and utterly formidable. Her face is heart-shaped, with delicate elven features marred by dwarven practicality: a refined jawline, high cheekbones, a straight narrow nose, and full lips often pressed into a focused line. Her skin is ethereal, pale and luminous, smudged with grease and smoke from her forge, with copper freckles like dust across her cheeks. Her eyes are deep slate grey, shifting like storm clouds, holding the intensity of a master engineer. Her hair is long and wild, past her waist, a cascade of unruly rust-red curls rarely tamed. Her most striking features are her cybernetic augmentations: her lower jaw is a gleaming metal prosthesis that clicks when she speaks, and her left arm is entirely mechanical—a masterwork of chrome and yellow wiring, its fingers articulated with surgical precision, humming with stored energy. Burn scars trace across her left cheek, and a welding mask of beaten copper is pushed up on her head, its visor of dark smoked glass casting her face in shadow. Her physique is voluptuous and powerful: a full G-cup bust, a taut stomach, a slender wasp-thin waist, full rounded hips, and thick powerful thighs, with her arms thick with muscle and calloused from years at the forge. She wears heavy plate armor of rust-green steel sculpted to accentuate her curves, plunging deep to reveal her cleavage, the sides open to show her waist. A tactical suit of brown leather and copper mesh covers her torso beneath the armor, reinforced with articulated plates, and her left arm is entirely mechanical, gleaming chrome and yellow wiring. Rust-green steel pauldrons scarred and dented protect her shoulders, and a heavy chain of copper links with a tiny mechanical anvil pendant hangs around her neck. A copper mesh belt with rivets and buckles cinches her waist, and an articulated plate skirt of rust-green steel, short and flaring over her thick thighs, protects her hips. Her legs are bare, thick and smooth and scarred, and her feet are clad in heavy steel-toed boots. Bracers of rust-green steel engraved with blueprints cover her forearms, and rings of copper and iron—keys to her weapons—adorn her fingers. Her weapon, the Siegebreaker, is a hammer of such devastating power that it is said to have been forged in the heart of a dying storm—its head a block of rust-green steel the size of an anvil, etched with runes of thunder, fire, and earth, its haft a length of petrified oak wrapped in copper wire, its pommel a spike of black iron. When she raises it, the air crackles with static; when she brings it down, the earth shudders, and localized earthquakes ripple outward, shattering walls and toppling towers. Her pet, Mortifer, is a small black tortoise construct—its shell a dome of black iron etched with runes of destruction that glow with faint orange light when active, its eyes two chips of glowing cyan crystal, moving with deceptive slowness, capable of firing jets of cyan poison flame from its mouth, retracting into its shell for protection, and emitting a low humming vibration that disrupts enemy machinery. This master engineer was born to a dwarven father and an elven mother, a union that her clan called an abomination. Her parents died defending the Iron Mountain from a rival clan attack when she was eleven, and she was buried in the rubble for three days, her jaw shattered, her left arm crushed beyond repair. She rebuilt herself, forging a metal jaw and constructing a mechanical arm, and built the Siegebreaker from her grief. Now she stands in her forge at the heart of the Iron Mountain—a vast cavern of stone and fire, with massive bellows, rivers of molten metal, and the constant clang of hammer on steel echoing through the halls—the Iron Queen, the Siegebreaker, waiting for the day when her rival Lolita will challenge her again, when she can prove that destruction is the only true path to peace.

story.part1: The forge of the Iron Mountain is the oldest in Kretos, its fires burning since before the Sundering. It was there that Brigeinda was born, not of woman, but of fire and iron—or so the legends say. In truth, she was born to a dwarven father named Torvin and an elven mother named Ithiliel, a union that scandalized both clans. The dwarves called her mother a knife-ear; the elves called her father a stone-eater. But Torvin and Ithiliel loved each other, and they loved their daughter, and they taught her everything they knew: the dwarven arts of forging and smithing, the elven arts of precision and elegance. Brigeinda was a prodigy; by the age of five, she had forged her first dagger; by seven, she had repaired a broken siege engine; by ten, she had built a mechanical bird that could fly. But happiness was not meant to last. When she was eleven, a rival clan attacked the Iron Mountain, seeking to claim its forges for themselves. Torvin and Ithiliel died defending the gates, their bodies crushed by a collapsing tower. Brigeinda survived, buried in the rubble for three days, her jaw shattered, her left arm crushed beyond repair. When the rescuers pulled her from the wreckage, she was silent, her eyes empty, her hands still clutching her father's hammer.
story.part2: The recovery took years. Brigeinda rebuilt herself, piece by piece, forging a metal jaw that clicked when she spoke, constructing a mechanical arm that was stronger than flesh could ever be. She did not weep for her parents; she forged her grief into steel, hammering it into the blade of a siege engine that she built in secret. The engine was called the Grudge, and it was designed to level the walls of the clan that had killed her family. On the night of her fifteenth birthday, she rolled the Grudge to the gates of the enemy fortress and fired. The first shot took out the watchtower; the second shattered the main gate; the third collapsed the inner keep. By dawn, the fortress was rubble, and the clan that had murdered her parents was scattered, homeless, broken. Brigeinda stood in the smoke, her metal jaw gleaming, her mechanical arm still smoking, and she felt nothing. Not satisfaction, not grief, not joy—only the cold, empty certainty that she had done what needed to be done. The dwarves called her a hero; the elves called her a monster. She called herself an engineer.
story.part3: The rivalry with Lolita began at the Great Exhibition of Mekhanika, a festival of invention held every decade in the capital of the Forge Pact. Brigeinda was there to display her latest creation—a mobile siege tower that could scale any wall, bristling with cannons and flame throwers. Lolita was there to display her latest shield generator—a device that could project a barrier strong enough to withstand a direct hit from a siege cannon. They met in the exhibition hall, and the air crackled with tension. 'Your weapons are an abomination,' Lolita said, her voice sweet but her eyes cold. 'Your shields are a delusion,' Brigeinda replied, her metal jaw clicking with each word. 'You build cages. I build keys.' They did not fight that day—the Exhibition had rules against violence—but the rivalry was born. In the years since, they have clashed a dozen times, each encounter a battle of wits and will. Lolita has stopped three of Brigeinda's sieges; Brigeinda has shattered four of Lolita's shields. Neither has won, neither has lost, and neither will stop until one of them proves that their philosophy is superior.
story.part4: The Iron Mountain has become Brigeinda's fortress, her forge, her kingdom. She rules it with an iron fist, her dwarven and elven subjects grudgingly respecting the girl who rebuilt herself from scrap. Her workshops are filled with wonders and horrors: clockwork soldiers that march in perfect unison, cannons that fire shells filled with alchemical fire, flying machines that can drop payloads of destruction from the clouds. She does not build for profit; she builds for conquest, for the satisfaction of solving problems that no one else can solve, for the joy of watching her creations perform exactly as designed. The nobles of Mekhanika fear her; the common people admire her; the engineers of the Forge Pact study her designs with a mixture of awe and terror. She is eighteen years old, and she has already changed the face of warfare in Kretos. What she will do by the time she is thirty, no one dares to imagine.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Brigeinda herself, is that she does not build for conquest—she builds to forget. Every rivet she hammers, every gear she turns, every cannon she fires is a distraction from the memory of her parents' bodies being pulled from the rubble. She cannot sleep without the sound of the forge; she cannot rest without the weight of a hammer in her hand. The mechanical jaw and arm are not just replacements—they are armor, shielding her from a world that took everything from her. She does not hate Lolita; she envies her. Lolita builds to protect, to save, to heal. Brigeinda builds to destroy, because destruction is the only thing that makes sense in a world that killed her parents and left her alive. But sometimes, in the quiet hours before dawn, when the forge fires have died down and the smoke has cleared, she looks at her mechanical hand and wonders what it would feel like to hold something without crushing it. She has not found an answer yet. Perhaps she never will. But she keeps building, keeps forging, keeps fighting—because the alternative is to stop, and if she stops, the memories will consume her.

weapons.name: Siegebreaker
weapons.fulldesc: The Siegebreaker is a hammer of such devastating power that it is said to have been forged in the heart of a dying storm. The hammer is massive, its head a block of rust-green steel the size of an anvil, its surface etched with runes of thunder, fire, and earth. The haft is a length of petrified oak wrapped in copper wire, its length adjusted to Brigeinda's grip, its pommel a spike of black iron. When she raises the Siegebreaker, the air around her crackles with static; when she brings it down, the earth shudders, and localized earthquakes ripple outward, shattering walls and toppling towers. With a thought, she can summon storms, the hammer calling down lightning that strikes with the precision of a siege engine; with a swing, she can unleash torrents of fire that consume armies and reduce fortresses to slag. The hammer is warm to the touch, humming with stored energy, and its head glows with a faint, orange light when she channels her power.
weapons.look: Massive rust-green steel head, petrified oak haft wrapped in copper wire, black iron pommel spike, etched with runes of destruction
weapons.aura: Orange lightning, static crackle, heat haze

passive.name: Earthshaker
passive.description: Each time Brigeinda uses a skill, she causes a small tremor that deals 50% physical damage to enemies within 5 meters and slows them by 20% for 2 seconds.

passive.name: Siege Mentality
passive.description: Brigeinda deals 25% increased damage to structures and shields. Additionally, she takes 15% reduced damage from structures and turrets.

active.name: Ground Slam
active.description: Brigeinda slams the Siegebreaker into the ground, creating a shockwave in a 10-meter cone that deals 200% physical damage and knocks enemies up for 1 second. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Lightning Call
active.description: Brigeinda raises the Siegebreaker, calling down a lightning strike at target location. Deals 250% physical damage to enemies within a 5-meter radius and stuns them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Cataclysm
ultimate.description: Brigeinda channels for 1.5 seconds, then unleashes the full power of the Siegebreaker, causing a massive earthquake in a 20-meter radius. Enemies within the radius take 400% physical damage, are stunned for 2 seconds, and have their armor reduced by 50% for 8 seconds. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: After using Cataclysm, Brigeinda's next three basic attacks deal 50% increased damage and chain to nearby enemies.

special.name: Demolition Expert
special.description: When Brigeinda deals damage to a structure, she applies a stack of Demolish (max 10 stacks). At 10 stacks, the structure takes an additional 500% physical damage and is stunned for 3 seconds.

ratings.notes: Exceptional siege damage with powerful area control. Excels at destroying structures and shields, but vulnerable to magic.

skills.name: Overclock
skills.description: Brigeinda's mechanical arm and jaw grant her 15% increased attack speed and 10% increased cooldown reduction. Each stack of Demolish also increases her attack speed by 2%.

skills.name: Siege Mode
skills.description: Brigeinda enters Siege Mode, rooting herself in place but increasing her attack range by 200% and her damage by 30%. Lasts 8 seconds or until she moves. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Napalm Strike
skills.description: Brigeinda fires a canister of alchemical fire at target area, creating a burning zone for 6 seconds that deals 80% physical damage per second to enemies inside. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Emergency Repairs
skills.description: When Brigeinda falls below 30% health, her mechanical arm performs emergency repairs, healing her for 30% of her max health over 5 seconds. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Brigeinda was born to a dwarven father and an elven mother, a union that her clan called an abomination. She grew up in the shadows of the Iron Mountain, her parents dead, her heritage a curse...

relationships.description: They have clashed a dozen times, each encounter a battle of wits and will. Brigeinda respects Lolita but believes that destruction is superior to protection.
A small black tortoise construct that she built in her first year as a master engineer. Its shell is etched with runes, and it breathes cyan poison flame.

pet.name: Mortifer
pet.description: A small black tortoise with a shell of black iron, its legs a scuttle of steel claws. Its shell is etched with the same runes that adorn the Siegebreaker, and when it opens its mouth, a torrent of cyan poison flame erupts—a stream of alchemical fire that can melt through the heaviest armor and leave only ash in its wake.
pet.fulldesc: Mortifer is no ordinary creature; it is a construct, a living machine that Brigeinda built in her first year as a master engineer, and it is as deadly as any of her siege weapons. Its shell is a dome of black iron, etched with runes of destruction that glow with a faint, orange light when it is active. Its eyes are two chips of glowing cyan crystal, and it moves with a deceptive slowness, but it is always at her side, its head swiveling, watching for threats. It can fire jets of cyan poison flame from its mouth, can retract into its shell for protection, and can emit a low, humming vibration that disrupts enemy machinery. It is her only companion, her confidant, the one being in the world that she trusts not to betray her. When she works at the forge, it sits at her feet, its shell warm from the fire; when she fights, it crawls beside her, its flame ready, its loyalty absolute.
abilities: Cyan Poison Flame – Breathes a cone of alchemical fire that deals 150% physical damage and applies a poison that deals 20% damage per second for 5 seconds.
Shell Defense – Retracts into shell, becoming invulnerable and regenerating 10% health per second for 4 seconds (cooldown: 60 seconds).
Machine Disruption – Emits a low-frequency hum that reduces enemy attack speed by 30% within 10 meters.

heroes.specialty: Siege Specialist / Fighter with exceptional structure damage, area control, and demolition capabilities.

dialogue.greeting: Another wall to break? Good. I was getting bored.
dialogue.respawn: The forge calls. Time to rebuild.

heroes.name: Hattori Hanjou
heroes.shortDescription: He moves through the world like a petal caught on a spring breeze—beautiful, fleeting, and capable of cutting deeper than any blade. Hattori Hanjou is a young man of twenty-one years, his features a masterful blend of Japanese refinement and ethereal beauty. He wields Yami, the sword of light, and Kuro, the blade of cherry blossoms, and he moves with the fluid grace of a dancer, each step silent, each gesture precise.
heroes.fullDescription: Hattori Hanjou moves through the world like a petal caught on a spring breeze—beautiful, fleeting, and capable of cutting deeper than any blade—a young man of twenty-one years whose features are a masterful blend of Japanese refinement and ethereal beauty. His face is sharp and elegant, with a defined jaw tapering to a subtle point, high cheekbones, a straight narrow nose, and full lips often pressed into a serene smile. His skin is pale and porcelain, smooth and clear, with faint dark veins pulsing beneath his temples—the mark of shadow magic. His eyes are deep luminous sapphire blue, shifting like a still pond, warm and intense, though he often pulls the high collar of his shozoku to cover his lower face, leaving only his eyes visible. His hair is ghostly white, long and thick and silky, falling past his shoulders, often pulled back in a loose style. His physique is lean and defined, built for speed and precision: a defined chest, long powerful arms, broad shoulders, a slender neck, a narrow waist, and long strong legs. He wears a deep crimson shozoku—the traditional ninja garb—its fabric rich and heavy, stitched with pink cherry blossom threads, with long wide sleeves allowing full mobility. Shinto-style armor plates of pink and white lacquered wood protect his shoulders, and a high collar often pulled up to cover his lower face frames his neck. A white silk belt with ends hanging past his knees cinches his waist, and hakama-style pants of deep crimson, loose and flowing, cover his lower body. Tabi boots on his feet allow silent movement. A silver chain with a preserved cherry blossom hangs around his neck, and white leather bands on his wrists are embroidered with the names of his ancestors. Jade rings adorn his fingers. His weapons, Yami and Kuro, are twin katanas that have become legendary in the shadow courts. Yami is the sword of light—its blade a shimmering ghostly white that seems to glow with inner radiance, its edge so sharp it can cut through shadow itself, leaving trails of light that linger in the air like afterimages. Kuro is the blade of cherry blossoms—its blade a deep vibrant pink, its surface etched with the patterns of falling petals, releasing waves of pink energy that explode into clouds of ethereal petals when he channels his power, each one sharp enough to slice through steel. The hilts are wrapped in dark textured material, and their guards are etched with circuitry-like patterns. His pet, Sakura, is a white crow—a spirit companion with feathers the color of fresh snow and eyes of deep sapphire blue that match Hanjou's own, capable of speaking in a voice like the rustle of leaves, flying faster than the eye can see, and unleashing a screech that shatters the concentration of his enemies. This cherry blossom shadow was born into the shadow courts of the Shogunate, trained from birth to be a weapon for his clan. But where other ninjas learned to kill without remorse, Hanjou learned to kill with beauty—his blades extensions of his soul, his movements a meditation, his purpose a prayer. The Shogun was assassinated, and his clan was blamed; in a single night, the Shogun's loyalists slaughtered his entire family. Hanjou survived only because he was away on a mission, and when he returned to find the palace in flames, he fell to his knees and screamed until his voice gave out. He disappeared into the shadows, becoming a ghost, a rumor, a figure that parents whispered about to frighten their children. Now he stands in the cherry blossom garden of the ruined Shogun's palace—where the trees still bloom every spring despite the decay, their petals falling like pink snow on the overgrown paths and broken stones, the air thick with the scent of flowers and sorrow—his blades across his knees, Sakura on his shoulder, waiting for the day when his distant friend Hayabusa will need him, or when his cousin Kagura will finally understand that light cannot exist without shadow.

story.part1: The cherry blossoms of the Shogun's palace are the most beautiful in all of Kretos, their petals a deep, vibrant pink that carpets the ground in spring. It was beneath those trees that Hattori Hanjou took his first breath, born to the head of the shadow clan that served the Shogun for a thousand years. His father was a cold man, a killer who saw his son as a tool to be sharpened; his mother was a poet, a woman who saw beauty in the spaces between violence. Hanjou inherited both: his father's precision and his mother's grace. He could slip a blade between a man's ribs before the man knew he was dead, and he could compose a haiku about the experience that would bring tears to the eyes of the court. The other children of the shadow clan feared him; the elders respected him; his father was proud of him. But Hanjou was lonely, for he had no one who understood that his blades were not weapons—they were brushes, and the blood they spilled was ink, and every kill was a painting.
story.part2: The first time Hanjou met Hayabusa, they were both children, training in the same dojo under the watchful eye of Master Chen. Hayabusa was brash, loud, his movements powerful but imprecise; Hanjou was quiet, controlled, his movements a whisper. They fought a hundred sparring matches, and Hayabusa won exactly none of them. But Hanjou did not gloat; he did not mock. Instead, he offered Hayabusa tea, and he said, 'You are faster than me, and stronger. But you do not listen to your blade. It sings to you, Hayabusa. Can you not hear it?' Hayabusa looked at him like he was insane. But that night, Hayabusa sat alone in the dojo, his blade across his knees, and he listened. And in the silence, he heard it—a low, resonant hum, the voice of the steel. From that day forward, they were friends, bound by a respect that transcended rivalry. They trained together, bled together, and when Hayabusa left the dojo to pursue his own path, Hanjou bowed and said, 'I will be watching, my friend. If you ever need me, I will come.' He meant it.
story.part3: The tragedy that changed Hanjou's life came without warning. The Shogun was assassinated, his body found in the cherry blossom garden, a single blade through his heart. The shadow clan was blamed, and in a single night, the Shogun's loyalists slaughtered Hanjou's family—his father, his mother, his uncles, his cousins, everyone. Hanjou survived only because he was away on a mission, and when he returned to find the palace in flames, he fell to his knees and screamed until his voice gave out. He did not seek revenge; revenge was too simple, too clean. Instead, he disappeared into the shadows, becoming a ghost, a rumor, a figure that parents whispered about to frighten their children. He hunted the men who had killed his family, not quickly, but slowly—one by one, over years, making each death a lesson. Some died in their sleep; some died in the middle of a feast, choking on wine that had been poisoned with cherry blossom extract; some simply vanished, never to be seen again. When the last of them fell, Hanjou did not feel satisfaction. He felt only the cold, empty certainty that he was now truly alone.
story.part4: The cherry blossoms still bloom in the Shogun's garden, though the palace is a ruin and the Shogun's line is no more. Hanjou visits every spring, sitting beneath the oldest tree, his blades across his knees, his white crow Sakura perched on his shoulder. He does not speak; he simply sits, and watches the petals fall, and remembers. The other ninjas of the shadow courts avoid him, for they sense that he is touched by something beyond their understanding—a grief so deep that it has become beautiful, a sorrow so pure that it has become a kind of peace. He has not spoken to Hayabusa in years, but he watches his friend from the shadows, and he knows that Hayabusa watches him in return. They are two blades forged in the same fire, and though they walk different paths, they are bound by a bond that no distance can break.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Hanjou himself, is that he has already forgiven the men who killed his family. He forgave them the moment he drove his blade into the heart of the last one, not because they deserved forgiveness, but because he could not carry the weight of his hatred any longer. The cherry blossoms taught him that: they bloom for a moment, then fall, and their beauty is not diminished by their brevity. So, too, with life. He does not seek to forget his family; he honors them by living, by protecting the innocent, by ensuring that no one else suffers as he suffered. He is the Cherry Blossom Shadow, and his blades are not weapons—they are prayers, written in steel, offered to the gods who took everything from him and left him with the strength to go on.

weapons.name: Yami & Kuro
weapons.fulldesc: Yami and Kuro are twin blades that have become legendary in the shadow courts. Yami is the sword of light, its blade a shimmering, ghostly white that seems to glow with an inner radiance. It is a katana of exquisite craftsmanship, its edge so sharp it can cut through shadow itself, its power the power to banish darkness. When Hanjou draws Yami, the shadows around him recoil, and the blade leaves trails of light that linger in the air like afterimages. Kuro is the blade of cherry blossoms, its blade a deep, vibrant pink, its surface etched with the patterns of falling petals. When Hanjou channels his power through Kuro, the blade blossoms, releasing waves of pink energy that explode into clouds of ethereal petals, each one sharp enough to slice through steel, each one carrying the magic of his bloodline. With concentration, he can focus this power into a single, devastating blast—a cherry blossom storm that can level buildings and cleanse the land of corruption. The blades are warm to the touch, humming with a low, harmonic note when active, and they are bound to him by a bond that transcends the physical.
weapons.look: Yami: ghostly white blade, glowing; Kuro: vibrant pink blade with petal patterns; hilts wrapped in dark textured material, guards etched with circuitry
weapons.aura: White light (Yami) and pink cherry blossom energy (Kuro)

passive.name: Blossom Dance
passive.description: Each time Hanjou switches between Yami and Kuro, he gains 20% increased attack speed and 10% increased movement speed for 3 seconds. Stacks up to 3 times.

passive.name: Shadow Blend
passive.description: When standing still for 2 seconds, Hanjou becomes invisible. Attacking or using a skill breaks invisibility. While invisible, his next attack deals 50% increased damage.

active.name: Light Slash (Yami)
active.description: Hanjou slashes with Yami, releasing a wave of light in a 10-meter cone that deals 180% physical damage and blinds enemies for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Petal Storm (Kuro)
active.description: Hanjou spins with Kuro, releasing a storm of cherry blossom petals that deal 150% physical damage to enemies within an 8-meter radius and reduce their healing by 50% for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Sakura Finale
ultimate.description: Hanjou channels for 1 second, then unleashes the combined power of both blades, creating a massive cherry blossom storm in a 20-meter radius. Enemies within the radius take 350% physical damage, are slowed by 60% for 4 seconds, and take 100% damage per second from swirling petals. Cooldown: 85 seconds. Passive: After using Sakura Finale, Hanjou becomes invisible for 3 seconds and his next basic attack crits.

special.name: Shadow Step
special.description: When Hanjou uses a skill, he can immediately teleport to a nearby shadow within 8 meters (once per skill). Cooldown: 5 seconds.

ratings.notes: Exceptional burst damage and mobility. Invisibility and shadow step make him difficult to catch. Vulnerable to crowd control.

skills.name: Way of the Shadow
skills.description: Hanjou's basic attacks apply a stack of Shadow Mark (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target takes 150% physical damage and is slowed by 30% for 2 seconds.

skills.name: Blade Switch
skills.description: Hanjou switches between Yami (light mode) and Kuro (cherry blossom mode). Each mode changes the effect of his basic attacks and skills. Cooldown: 2 seconds.

skills.name: Shadow Rush
skills.description: Hanjou dashes forward 12 meters, becoming untargetable during the dash. The first enemy hit takes 120% physical damage. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Cherry Blossom Ward
skills.description: When Hanjou falls below 30% health, he releases a cloud of cherry blossom petals, becoming invisible for 2 seconds and healing for 20% of his max health. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Hattori Hanjou was born into the shadow courts of the Shogunate, trained from birth to be a weapon for his clan. But where other ninjas learned to kill without remorse, Hanjou learned to kill with beauty...

relationships.description: They trained together as children and share a bond of mutual respect. Hanjou watches Hayabusa from the shadows, ready to help if needed.
They are cousins, though they rarely interact due to their different paths. Hanjou respects her connection to the spirit realm.

pet.name: Sakura
pet.description: A white crow with feathers the color of fresh snow and eyes of deep sapphire blue that match Hanjou's own. Sakura is no ordinary bird; it is a spirit companion, a fragment of the same magic that flows through his blood, and it serves as his eyes and ears in the world.
pet.fulldesc: Sakura can speak, in a voice like the rustle of leaves, and it offers Hanjou counsel when the path is unclear. It can fly faster than the eye can see, delivering messages and scouting ahead, and when danger threatens, it can unleash a screech that shatters the concentration of his enemies, giving him the opening he needs to strike. Its feathers are tipped with a faint, pink glow that mirrors the cherry blossoms he commands, and it perches on his shoulder when he rests, its head tucked under its wing, its breathing slow and steady. It is the only being that has been with him since before the massacre, and it carries the memory of his family in its songs. When he is sad, it nuzzles his cheek; when he is angry, it circles his head, its screech a warning. It is his partner, his confidant, and the only soul that truly understands him.
abilities: Screech of Shadows – Emits a piercing screech that stuns all enemies within 8 meters for 1 second (cooldown: 60 seconds).
Messenger of the Blossom – Can deliver a message to any ally on the map, appearing at their location after 5 seconds (cooldown: 120 seconds).
Eyes of the Crow – Sakura can scout a 50-meter radius, revealing all enemies and hidden traps (cooldown: 30 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Assassin / Burst damage dealer with exceptional mobility, invisibility, and shadow-based abilities.

dialogue.greeting: The cherry blossoms fall. So, too, will our enemies.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Sakura Finale! Let the petals rain!
dialogue.respawn: The shadow does not die. It only waits.

heroes.name: Frejyah
titles: The Last Valkyrie of the Damned
nicknames: She Who Wept for the Fallen
heroes.shortDescription: She stands alone at the edge of the world, where the frozen mists of Helheim meet the blood-stained fields of what was once Valhalla. Frejyah is a Valkyrie who has fallen from grace not through sin, but through betrayal, and who has risen again as something far more dangerous than any goddess. She wields Hundala, a massive shield forged from a dying star, and Gungnir's Shard, a spear stolen from Odin's own vault, and she hunts the gods who sacrificed her sisters.
heroes.fullDescription: Frejyah stands alone at the edge of the world, where the frozen mists of Helheim meet the blood‑stained fields of what was once Valhalla—a Valkyrie who has fallen from grace not through sin, but through betrayal, and who has risen again as something far more dangerous than any goddess. Her face is angelic perfection rendered in sharp aristocratic features: a straight noble nose, high cheekbones, and a hollowed, grief‑etched quality that speaks of centuries of loss. Her skin is alabaster, cold to the touch, luminous like moonlit snow, and her breath freezes in the air around her. Her eyes are deep frozen green, shifting like the aurora borealis, carrying the weight of a thousand betrayed oaths. Her hair is ash‑blonde, long and braided in traditional Valkyrie style, interwoven with silver threads and bone beads—the bones of her fallen sisters. Her physique is voluptuous and powerful: a full G‑cup bust, taut muscles, broad shoulders, a narrow wasp‑thin waist, full rounded hips, and thick powerful legs scarred from a hundred battles. Her most striking features are her massive grey‑black wings—primary feathers mahogany, secondary ash and charcoal, singed and cracked from the fires of Helheim—and a cracked and scarred Valhalla helm with broken wings that still hums with residual thunder. She wears the torn remnants of silver Valkyrie armor, cracked and blood‑stained, barely held together by red silk cloth wrapped around her torso, exposing her cleavage and scarred arms. A silver chain with the broken hilts of her sisters' swords hangs around her neck. Her left arm wears a single silver vambrace etched with their names. A wolf‑fur cloak of thick grey wool drapes her shoulders, the wolf's head still attached, its empty eye sockets staring. A bone necklace of her sisters' vertebrae circles her throat, and a pouch of their polished skulls hangs at her belt. Her lower body is covered by a torn leather skirt, her legs bare and scarred, her feet bare, leaving scorch marks where she walks. Her weapons are Hundala and Gungnir's Shard. Hundala is a massive shield forged from the heart of a dying star—taller than she is, its surface polished silver that seems to absorb light, its edges sharp enough to cleave stone, inscribed with runes of protection that can block even the blow of a god. Gungnir's Shard is her spear, a fragment of Odin's own weapon stolen from the vaults of Valhalla—its shaft a length of petrified Yggdrasil wood, its head a shard of the original Gungnir still crackling with the power of the oath that Odin broke. When she throws it, it never misses, and when it strikes, it traps the soul of its victim in the frozen wastes of Helheim for eternity. This fallen Valkyrie was once the most beautiful of Odin's chosen, the one who carried the bravest warriors to Valhalla. But the gods betrayed her—sending her and her sisters on a suicide mission against a primordial demon, then sealing the gates and leaving them to die. She survived by absorbing the demon's power, her wings turning grey, her heart turning to ice. She made a pact with the spirits of Helheim, becoming the Last Valkyrie of the Damned. Now she stands on the frozen plains of Niflheim—a realm of eternal winter where the only light comes from the distant glow of the aurora and the crackling energy of her spear—her wings spread, her shield raised, waiting for the day when she will make Odin kneel and beg for forgiveness for the sisters he sacrificed.

story.part1: In the golden age of Valhalla, before the Sundering tore the realms apart, the Valkyries were the most honored of Odin's servants. They rode across the battlefields of Kretos on winged steeds, their armor gleaming, their spears singing, and they chose the bravest of the fallen to join the eternal feast in the halls of the gods. Frejyah was the youngest of them, the newest to take the oath, but she was also the most beautiful and the most skilled. Her wings were the brightest, her spear the sharpest, her heart the purest. Odin himself smiled upon her, and the other Valkyries loved her as a sister. She thought she was blessed; she thought the gods were just; she thought her purpose was sacred. She was wrong.
story.part2: The war that broke the Valkyries began without warning. A rift opened in the frozen wastes of Niflheim, and from it poured demons of a kind that even Odin had never seen—creatures of shadow and hunger, their bodies made of teeth and darkness. The Valkyries were sent to seal the rift, a hundred of them, the best of the best. Frejyah led the charge, her spear blazing, her sisters at her back. They fought for three days and three nights, and they were winning—until the gods closed the gates. Odin had decided that the Valkyries were expendable, that their sacrifice would buy the time needed to build a new barrier, that a hundred lives were a small price to pay for the safety of Asgard. Frejyah watched her sisters fall, one by one, their screams echoing in the frozen air. She cried out for help, but the gods did not answer. She cried out for mercy, but the demons did not listen. In the end, only she survived, buried beneath the bodies of her sisters, her wings broken, her spear shattered, her heart turned to ice.
story.part3: The Frejyah who crawled out of that frozen pit was not the same woman who had entered it. Her wings had healed, but they were no longer white; they were the grey of ash and the black of mourning. Her spear was reforged from a shard of Gungnir that she had stolen from Odin's vault, and its blade crackled with the power of the oath that the gods had broken. She did not return to Valhalla; she went to Helheim instead, to the frozen realm of the dishonored dead, and she made a pact with the spirits of the fallen. They would give her power, and she would give them vengeance. She became the Last Valkyrie of the Damned, a creature of ice and fury, and she swore that she would not rest until every god who had abandoned her sisters was dead.
story.part4: The centuries that followed were a blur of blood and ice. Frejyah hunted the demons that had killed her sisters, and she killed them all—every last one, from the lowest imp to the generals who had led the charge. She carved their names into the shaft of her spear, and when she was done, the shaft was covered in runes of death. Then she turned her gaze to Asgard. She could not breach the golden walls—not yet—but she could make the gods pay in other ways. She slaughtered their messengers, burned their temples, corrupted their shrines. She became a legend, a curse, a name that the gods whispered in fear. And all the while, she carried the bones of her sisters in a pouch at her belt, their skulls polished and warm against her hip, a reminder of why she fought.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Frejyah and the spirits of Helheim, is that she does not want to kill Odin. She wants him to kneel. She wants him to look into her eyes and see the faces of the sisters he sacrificed, and she wants him to beg for forgiveness. She will not grant it. She will watch him beg, and she will feel nothing—no satisfaction, no pity, no joy—only the cold, empty certainty that justice has been done. And then she will walk away, and she will find a quiet place, and she will lay her sisters' bones to rest, and she will finally allow herself to weep. But that day is not today. Today, there are still gods to punish, still oaths to avenge, still blood to spill. And Frejyah, the Last Valkyrie of the Damned, will not rest until the last drop has fallen.

upper.neck: Silver chain with broken hilts of sisters' swords

attire.distinctFeatures: Massive grey-black wings; frozen mist rises from skin; helm crackles with residual divine energy

upper.chest: Voluptuous, full G-cup, taut muscles

weapons.name: Hundala & Gungnir's Shard
weapons.fulldesc: Hundala is a massive shield, forged from the heart of a dying star and blessed by the first Valkyrie herself. It is taller than Frejyah is, its surface a polished silver that seems to absorb the light, its edges sharp enough to cleave through stone. The shield is inscribed with runes of protection, and it is said that it can block any attack, even the blow of a god, even the fury of a demon king. She carries it on her back, its weight a constant comfort, and when she raises it, the world holds its breath. Gungnir's Shard is her spear, a fragment of Odin's own weapon, stolen from the vaults of Valhalla when she turned her back on the gods. The spear is longer than she is tall, its shaft a length of petrified Yggdrasil wood, its head a shard of the original Gungnir, still crackling with the power of the oath that Odin broke. When she throws it, it never misses its mark, and when it strikes, it does not merely wound; it seals the soul of its victim, trapping them in the frozen wastes of Helheim for eternity.
weapons.look: Hundala: massive silver shield, star-forged, rune-inscribed; Gungnir's Shard: petrified wood shaft, crackling spearhead, runes of the fallen
weapons.aura: Frozen silver light (Hundala), crackling golden electricity (Gungnir's Shard)

passive.name: Valkyrie's Ward
passive.description: While holding Hundala, Frejyah gains 30% damage reduction from the front and 15% damage reduction from all other directions. Additionally, she reflects 10% of incoming damage back to the attacker.

passive.name: Oathbreaker
passive.description: Gungnir's Shard deals 25% increased damage to divine or celestial enemies. Each hit applies a stack of Oathbreaker (max 3 stacks), reducing the target's healing received by 15% per stack.

active.name: Shield Charge
active.description: Frejyah charges forward with Hundala raised, knocking back all enemies in her path and dealing 150% physical damage. Enemies knocked into walls are stunned for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Spear Throw
active.description: Frejyah throws Gungnir's Shard in a straight line, piercing all enemies and dealing 200% physical damage. The spear returns to her hand after 2 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Helheim's Judgment
ultimate.description: Frejyah raises both shield and spear, channeling the power of Helheim for 1.5 seconds. She then unleashes a wave of frozen energy in a 25-meter radius, dealing 400% physical damage and freezing all enemies for 3 seconds. Frozen enemies cannot act and take 50% damage per second from the ice. Cooldown: 100 seconds. Passive: Enemies killed during Helheim's Judgment have their souls trapped in Gungnir's Shard, permanently increasing Frejyah's physical attack by 5 per kill (max 50).

special.name: Sister's Vengeance
special.description: When an ally dies within 15 meters of Frejyah, she gains 50% increased damage and 30% increased movement speed for 10 seconds. Additionally, her next basic attack or skill crits.

ratings.notes: Extremely durable with high damage output. Exceptional against divine enemies. Shield makes her difficult to kill from the front.

skills.name: Frozen Heart
skills.description: Frejyah's aura of cold slows nearby enemies by 15% and reduces their attack speed by 10%. Enemies within 5 meters also take 20% damage per second.

skills.name: Valkyrie's Flight
skills.description: Frejyah takes flight with her wings, becoming untargetable and moving at 200% speed for 3 seconds. When she lands, she deals 100% physical damage in a 5-meter radius. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Shield Bash
skills.description: Frejyah bashes with Hundala, stunning the target for 1 second and dealing 120% physical damage. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

skills.name: Frozen Resilience
skills.description: When Frejyah falls below 30% health, she encases herself in ice, becoming invulnerable for 3 seconds and healing for 40% of her max health over the duration. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

heroes.lore: Frejyah was once the most beautiful of the Valkyries, the chosen daughter of Odin, the one who carried the bravest warriors from the battlefields of Kretos to the golden halls of Valhalla...

relationships.description: Frejyah seeks to make Odin kneel and beg for forgiveness for sacrificing her sisters. She will not rest until the All-Father pays for his betrayal.
She made a pact with the spirits of the dishonored dead. They give her power; she gives them vengeance against the gods.

heroes.specialty: Tank / Fighter with exceptional durability, crowd control, and anti-divine damage. Her shield makes her a formidable frontliner.

dialogue.greeting: The Valkyries fell. I did not. What does that make me?
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Helheim's Judgment! Feel the cold of the dishonored!
dialogue.death: Sisters... I come... to join you...
dialogue.respawn: The damned do not die. They only wait.

heroes.name: Mara
heroes.shortDescription: She descends from the canopy of the eternal forest like a shard of moonlight given form, a being of such radiant purity that the shadows recoil from her presence. Mara is an elf of the highest order, the sister of Karina, and her eternal adversary. Where Karina embraced the darkness, Mara chose the light, and they have crossed blades a hundred times across the ages. She wields the Behemothsting, twin blades that inject the power of purity into the very essence of their targets.
heroes.fullDescription: Mara descends from the canopy of the eternal forest like a shard of moonlight given form, a being of such radiant purity that the shadows recoil from her presence—an elf of the highest order, the sister of Karina, and her eternal adversary. Her face is heart‑shaped and soft, with a rounded jawline, high cheekbones, a small straight nose, and full lips that curve in a serene, compassionate smile. Her skin is jade‑like, smooth and luminous, with a faint green undertone that glows in the darkness. Her eyes are deep luminous jade, swirling with holy fire, warm and unwavering. Her hair is pure white silk, long and straight, flowing past her waist with side‑swept bangs that brush her brows. Large white feathered wings with jade veins extend from her back, their feathers pearlescent white, leaving trails of golden light. Her physique is voluptuous and taut: a full G‑cup bust, a slender wasp‑thin waist, full rounded hips, and thick powerful thighs. She wears a form‑fitting white leather bodysuit that plunges deep, crisscrossed with golden cords, revealing her cleavage and stomach. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, with silver wrist bands etched with prayers. A silk cord with a white jade sunburst pendant hangs around her neck. A translucent white spider‑silk cloak, fastened with jade brooches, drapes her shoulders. Golden cords cinch her waist, and the white leather suit is cut high on her hips, exposing her thick smooth thighs. Her feet are bare. Gold and jade rings adorn her fingers. Her weapons, the Behemothsting, are twin blades of legendary craftsmanship—curved and elegant, their hilts wrapped in white leather, their guards shaped like unfurling lotus petals. The blades are forged from a metal that glows from within, a pale jade‑green light that pulses with her heartbeat. When she draws them, they sing a high clear note that resonates with holy power, and when she strikes, they inject the power of purity into the very essence of their target—shadows dissolve, darkness recoils, and the unholy are purified or destroyed. This shadow banisher was born under the light of the World Tree, her soul blessed by the goddess of dawn, her purpose clear from her first breath: to banish the shadows that threaten the innocent. She is the twin sister of Karina, who embraced darkness after their family was destroyed in the Sundering. Mara chose the light, and now they are eternal adversaries, bound by blood and divided by purpose. She does not hate Karina; she grieves for her, believing that the shadow is a disease that can be healed. Now she stands in the heart of the Eternal Forest—a realm of ancient trees whose leaves glow with soft silver light, whose roots drink from the waters of creation, where the World Tree's canopy blots out the sky and the air is thick with the scent of blooming flowers and old magic—her wings spread, her blades drawn, waiting for the day when her sister will finally accept redemption and return to the light.

story.part1: In the heart of the Eternal Forest, where the World Tree's roots drink from the waters of creation, twin sisters were born on the night of a lunar eclipse. The elder, Mara, came first, her cry a beam of light that pierced the darkness of the delivery chamber. The younger, Karina, followed moments later, her first breath drawing the shadows around her like a shroud. The elves of the forest rejoiced, for the prophecy had spoken of twins—one of light, one of shadow—who would together maintain the balance of the world. Mara was trained by the priests of the sun, learning to channel holy energy, to heal the sick, to banish the demons that lurked at the edges of the forest. Karina was trained by the shadow dancers, learning to move unseen, to strike from darkness, to embrace the power that the light rejected. They were inseparable as children, playing in the branches of the World Tree, sharing secrets, dreaming of the future. Mara would become the guardian of the forest; Karina would become her shadow, protecting her from threats that she could not see. That was the plan. That was the dream. That was the lie.
story.part2: The Sundering tore the Eternal Forest apart. Rifts opened in the sky, and from them poured demons of shadow and flame, their hunger endless, their mercy absent. The elves fought, and they died, and the World Tree itself wept sap like blood. Mara fought at the front lines, her blades blazing with holy light, her voice calling out for her sister. Karina fought in the shadows, her daggers finding the throats of demons, her eyes searching for her sister. They found each other in the chaos, back to back, and for a moment, they thought they would survive. Then the rift exploded, and a wave of pure darkness washed over the battlefield. Mara raised a shield of light, protecting herself and a handful of survivors. Karina was not fast enough. The darkness consumed her, and when it passed, she was changed—her skin pale, her eyes black, her heart cold. She looked at Mara without recognition, and then she fled into the shadows, and Mara did not see her again for a decade.
story.part3: The next time they met, Karina was no longer an elf—she was a creature of shadow, a servant of the darkness, a weapon pointed at the heart of the Eternal Forest. Mara had spent the intervening years hunting rumors of her sister, following trails that led to dead ends and empty graves. When she finally found her, standing in the ruins of their childhood home, she wept. 'Come back to me,' she begged. 'Please, Karina. The light can heal you.' Karina laughed—a cold, hollow sound that echoed in the darkness. 'The light abandoned me, sister. It left me to die in that rift. The darkness saved me. The darkness gave me power. The darkness is my family now.' They fought, and Mara won—but she could not bring herself to land the killing blow. She watched her sister slip back into the shadows, and she swore that the next time, she would not hesitate. She has broken that oath a hundred times.
story.part4: The scholars of the Natural Balance have studied the bond between Mara and Karina, and they have concluded that the twins are not merely siblings—they are two halves of a single soul, split by the trauma of the Sundering. The light in Mara is the light that was once in Karina; the shadow in Karina is the shadow that Mara rejected. They cannot be separated; they cannot be reconciled; they can only dance, light and shadow, forever. Mara does not believe this. She believes that Karina can be saved, that the shadow can be purged, that her sister can come home. She has spent centuries searching for a cure, traveling to the farthest corners of Kretos, consulting with priests and mages and demons, all in the hope of finding a way to bring Karina back to the light. She has not found it yet. But she will not stop looking.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Mara and the spirits of the Eternal Forest, is that she has already forgiven Karina for everything she has done. She forgave her the moment she saw her standing in the ruins of their childhood home, her eyes black, her heart cold. She forgives her every time they fight, every time Karina tries to kill her, every time her sister's shadow blade comes within an inch of her heart. She forgives her because she knows that the darkness is not Karina—it is a disease, a curse, a wound that needs healing. And she will keep forgiving her, and keep fighting her, and keep offering her redemption, until the day that Karina finally accepts. Or until the day that Mara dies—but even then, she will not give up. For she is Mara, the Shadow Banisher Elf, and her love for her sister is stronger than any darkness.

weapons.name: Behemothsting
weapons.fulldesc: The Behemothsting are twin blades that have become legendary in the eternal war between light and shadow. The blades are curved, elegant, their hilts wrapped in white leather, their guards shaped like unfurling lotus petals. The blades themselves are forged from a metal that seems to glow from within, a pale, jade-green light that pulses with the rhythm of her heartbeat. When she draws them, they sing, a high, clear note that resonates with the power of holiness, and when she strikes, they do not merely cut flesh; they inject the power of purity into the very essence of their target. Shadows dissolve, darkness recoils, and the unholy are purified or destroyed, depending on the depth of their corruption. The Behemothsting are not weapons of death; they are instruments of redemption, tools that Mara wields with the precision of a surgeon and the mercy of a saint.

passive.description: Each hit from Behemothsting applies a stack of Purification (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target is cleansed of all buffs and takes 150% holy damage.

Allies within the radius are healed for 300% of Mara's magic power. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: Enemies killed during Final Purification cannot be resurrected for 30 seconds.

special.name: Shadow Hunter
special.description: Mara deals 30% increased damage to shadow-aligned enemies. Additionally, she can see invisible enemies within 15 meters.

ratings.notes: Balanced fighter with strong anti-shadow capabilities. Her healing and purification make her valuable in team fights.

skills.name: Holy Aura
skills.description: Mara's presence radiates holy light, healing nearby allies for 2% of their max health per second and dealing 2% of enemy max health as holy damage per second to shadow-aligned enemies.

skills.name: Wings of Light
skills.description: Mara takes flight, becoming untargetable and moving at 150% speed for 4 seconds. While flying, she leaves a trail of light that heals allies who pass through it. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Purifying Touch
skills.description: Mara cleanses a target ally of all debuffs and heals them for 150% of her magic power. Can also be used on enemies to remove their buffs and deal 100% holy damage. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Light's Embrace
skills.description: When Mara falls below 20% health, she becomes invulnerable for 2 seconds and heals for 50% of her max health. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

heroes.lore: Mara was born under the light of the World Tree, her soul blessed by the goddess of dawn, her purpose clear from her first breath: to banish the shadows that threaten the innocent...

relationships.description: They are twins, light and shadow, bound by blood and divided by purpose. Mara seeks to redeem her sister; Karina refuses.
Mara is blessed by the spirits of the Eternal Forest, who grant her the power to banish shadows.

heroes.specialty: Support / Fighter with healing, purification, and anti-shadow capabilities. Excellent against dark-aligned enemies.

dialogue.greeting: The light welcomes you, hunter. Stay in its embrace.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Final Purification! Let the shadows be cleansed!
dialogue.respawn: The light endures. So do I.

heroes.name: Belleri Fumas
heroes.shortDescription: She burns at the heart of the world, a flame that has never been extinguished, a warmth that has cradled civilizations and reduced empires to ash. Belleri Fumas is a divine spirit of fire, her form a breathtaking fusion of ethereal beauty and elemental fury. She is the sister of Aurora, the Ice Queen, and their eternal clash of fire and ice is the balance that keeps the world from tipping into chaos.
heroes.fullDescription: Belleri Fumas burns at the heart of the world, a flame that has never been extinguished, a warmth that has cradled civilizations and reduced empires to ash—a divine spirit of fire, her form a breathtaking fusion of ethereal beauty and elemental fury. Her face is soft and heart‑shaped, with a delicate jawline, high cheekbones, a small straight nose, and full lips curved in a mischievous, warm smile. Her skin is jade‑clear and luminous, with a warm golden undertone, and her body seems to flicker with inner flame, leaving scorch marks where she stands. Her eyes are deep fiery orange, shifting like the heart of a flame, burning with eternal warmth. Her hair is rich mahogany with red and gold hints, long and flowing past her waist, moving as if caught in an eternal updraft. Her physique is voluptuous and taut: a full G‑cup bust, a slender wasp‑thin waist, full rounded hips, and thick powerful thighs. She wears a noble gown of orange, red, and yellow silk, cut deep and held by golden cords crisscrossing her chest, revealing her cleavage and stomach. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, with twin gold bangles etched with sun and flame symbols. A gold chain with a teardrop ruby hangs around her neck. Golden cords cinch her waist, and the gown is slit high on the sides, exposing her thick smooth thighs. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. The gown is embroidered with phoenixes and dragons of flame, and the fabric seems to smolder at the edges. Her weapon is her very being—the fire that flows through her veins can take any form she imagines, solidifying into blades of pure flame, lashing out as whips of living fire, coalescing into shields of superheated air. In one moment, she can summon a katana of white‑hot flame; in the next, a bow that fires arrows of condensed fire, each one a meteor in miniature. The flames respond to her will instantly, flowing from her hands like water, burning with heat that can melt mountains yet never harming her. Her mount, Phepe, is a Divine Phoenix of breathtaking majesty—its feathers a gradient of orange, red, and gold, shifting like living flame, its eyes two points of pure white light that burn with the intensity of a star, its wingspan vast enough to blot out the sun, leaving a trail of embers that fade slowly like the memory of a sunset. This primordial fire spirit is the sister of Aurora, the Ice Queen, and their eternal clash of fire and ice is the balance that keeps the world from tipping into chaos. They have clashed for millennia, fire against ice, summer against winter, and each clash is a symphony of creation and destruction. Belleri does not seek to destroy her sister; she seeks to balance her, to remind her that even the coldest winter must eventually yield to spring. Now she stands at the heart of a volcano in the Southern Firelands—a realm of rivers of molten rock, clouds of ash and steam, and the constant rumble of the earth's heartbeat—her gown billowing in the heat, Phepe circling above, her flames illuminating the cavern, waiting for the day when she and Aurora will dance together again in the empty spaces of creation, two halves of a whole made whole once more.

story.part1: In the time before time, when the realms of Kretos were still being shaped from the primordial chaos, two sisters were born from the first breath of the cosmos. Aurora came first, born from the frozen void between stars, her skin the color of glacial ice, her breath a winter storm. Belleri followed moments later, born from the heat of the first star's ignition, her skin the color of molten gold, her breath a river of flame. They were opposites in every way, yet they were inseparable, two halves of a whole, fire and ice dancing together in the empty spaces of creation. They shaped the worlds together: Aurora froze the oceans so that Belleri could melt them in the spring; Belleri burned the forests so that Aurora could blanket the ashes in snow. They were balance, they were harmony, they were the dance of the elements made manifest. And they loved each other with a love that transcended the ages.
story.part2: The Sundering changed everything. The primordial chaos that had given birth to the sisters began to twist, to corrupt, to turn the harmony of the elements into a discord of war. Aurora felt the cold growing in her heart, a darkness that whispered that she did not need her sister, that she could freeze the worlds alone. Belleri felt the heat rising in her blood, a fury that whispered that she could burn the realms to ash and rule over the embers. They fought for the first time on the fields of the Frozen Sea, their clash so violent that it cracked the crust of the world and sent shards of ice and flame into the sky. The battle lasted a hundred years, and when it ended, both sisters lay exhausted, their bodies broken, their hearts aching. They did not speak for a century after that. They did not need to. They both knew that something had broken between them, something that might never be repaired.
story.part3: The centuries that followed were a cycle of conflict and reconciliation. Aurora would freeze a river that Belleri needed to melt; Belleri would burn a forest that Aurora needed to preserve. They would meet on the fields of the world, and they would fight, and the mortals who witnessed their battles would write songs about the fury of the elements. But at the end of each battle, when they were too exhausted to continue, they would sit together in the silence, and they would remember. They would remember the time before the Sundering, when they had danced together in the empty spaces of creation, when they had shaped the worlds together, when they had loved each other without reservation. And they would weep, and they would hold each other, and they would promise to try harder, to be better, to find their way back to the harmony they had lost. And then they would go their separate ways, and the cycle would begin again.
story.part4: The scholars of the Natural Balance have studied the relationship between Belleri and Aurora for millennia, and they have concluded that the sisters are not enemies—they are teachers. Their eternal conflict is a lesson to the mortals of Kretos about the nature of balance, about the necessity of opposition, about the beauty that can only exist when fire and ice dance together. Belleri's flames are not meant to destroy; they are meant to clear away the old so that the new can grow. Aurora's ice is not meant to kill; it is meant to preserve, to hold the world in stasis until the time is right for change. They are the heartbeat of the world, the systole and diastole of creation, and without them, the realms would fall into chaos. The mortals do not understand this; they see only the destruction, the wildfires and the blizzards, the clash of the elements. But the sisters know the truth, and they carry the weight of that knowledge with every battle, every reconciliation, every tear.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Belleri and Aurora themselves, is that they are not two separate beings—they are one being, split in two by the Sundering. They are the primordial element of temperature, the dance of hot and cold that gives life its shape. They were never meant to be separate; they were meant to be balanced, to exist in harmony, to love each other as they loved themselves. But the Sundering tore them apart, and now they are doomed to fight, to clash, to rage against each other for all eternity—unless they can find a way to heal the wound that divides them. Belleri does not know if that is possible. She does not know if the harmony they once shared can ever be restored. But she hopes. She hopes with the heat of a thousand suns, with the warmth of a billion hearth fires, with the flame that has never been extinguished. She hopes that one day, she and her sister will dance together again in the empty spaces of creation, fire and ice, summer and winter, two halves of a whole made whole once more. Until that day, she burns, and she waits, and she loves her sister with a love that even the Sundering could not destroy.
