weapons.name: Belleri's Flames
weapons.fulldesc: Belleri does not carry a weapon; she is the weapon. The fire that flows through her veins can take any form she imagines, solidifying into blades of pure flame, lashing out as whips of living fire, coalescing into shields of superheated air. In one moment, she can summon a katana of white-hot flame, its edge so sharp it can cut through stone; in the next, she can call forth a bow that fires arrows of condensed fire, each one a meteor in miniature. She is limited only by her imagination, and her imagination has been honed over millennia of creation and destruction. The flames respond to her will instantly, flowing from her hands like water, and they burn with a heat that can melt mountains—yet they never harm her, for she is fire, and fire cannot burn itself.
weapons.look: Flames of orange, red, and gold, shifting and flowing at will

passive.name: Eternal Flame
passive.description: Belleri's abilities apply a stack of Burning (max 10 stacks). Each stack deals 5% of her magic power as fire damage per second. At 10 stacks, the target explodes, dealing 200% fire damage to all nearby enemies.

passive.name: Heat Absorption
passive.description: Belleri absorbs heat from the environment, gaining 1% increased magic power per second while in combat (max 30%).

active.name: Fireball
active.description: Belleri launches a fireball at target location, dealing 180% magical damage in a 5-meter radius and applying 2 stacks of Burning. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

active.name: Flame Whip
active.description: Belleri lashes out with a whip of fire in a 12-meter cone, dealing 150% magical damage and pulling enemies toward her. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Inferno
ultimate.description: Belleri channels for 2 seconds, then unleashes a massive wave of fire in a 25-meter radius. Enemies take 500% magical damage and are stunned for 2 seconds. Burning stacks are instantly applied to all enemies. Cooldown: 100 seconds. Passive: During Inferno, Belleri is immune to crowd control and takes 50% reduced damage.

special.name: Phoenix Rebirth
special.description: When Belleri dies, she explodes, dealing 300% magical damage to all enemies within 15 meters. After 5 seconds, she is reborn at the explosion site with 50% HP and mana. Cooldown: 180 seconds.

ratings.notes: Exceptional magical burst damage with powerful area control. Her rebirth passive makes her incredibly difficult to permanently eliminate.

skills.name: Radiant Heat
skills.description: Enemies within 8 meters of Belleri take 30% magical damage per second and have their armor reduced by 20%.

skills.name: Flame Dash
skills.description: Belleri transforms into a bolt of fire, dashing 15 meters and leaving a trail of flames that deals 80% damage per second for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Fire Shield
skills.description: Belleri surrounds herself with a shield of fire that absorbs 400 damage and deals 100% magical damage per second to nearby enemies. Lasts 5 seconds. Cooldown: 16 seconds.

skills.name: Cauterize
skills.description: When Belleri falls below 30% health, she instantly heals for 30% of her max health and gains 50% increased magic power for 5 seconds. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Belleri Fumas is fire incarnate, a primordial spirit who has existed since the first spark struck the first kindling. She is warmth, life, destruction, and renewal—all the contradictions of flame wrapped in a form of breathtaking beauty...

relationships.description: Fire and ice, eternal adversaries and eternal friends. They clash, they reconcile, they love each other across the ages.
A Divine Phoenix, a fragment of the first fire, bound to Belleri since the dawn of creation.

mount.name: Phepe
mount.description: A Divine Phoenix of breathtaking majesty, Phepe's feathers are a gradient of orange, red, and gold, shifting like living flame. Its eyes are two points of pure, white light that burn with the intensity of a star. Its wingspan is vast, capable of blotting out the sun, and when it flies, it leaves a trail of embers that fade slowly, like the memory of a sunset.
mount.fulldesc: Phepe is not merely a mount; it is a fragment of the first fire, a spark from the moment of creation given form. Its feathers are warm to the touch, and when it sings—a sound like crackling flames and distant thunder—the air around it shimmers with heat. It is bound to Belleri by a bond that transcends the physical, a connection that has existed since before the first humans learned to fear the dark. It can breathe torrents of flame that can level mountains, can summon storms of embers that blind enemies, and can wrap Belleri in its wings, shielding her from harm. When she rests, it curls around her, its warmth a comfort; when she fights, it flies above her, a blazing sun against the sky, a warning to all who would dare to face the fire.
abilities: Solar Flare – Emits a blinding flash of light that stuns all enemies within 15 meters for 2 seconds (cooldown: 60 seconds).
Ember Storm – Summons a storm of embers that deals 200% magical damage over 5 seconds in a 10-meter radius.
Wings of Protection – Wraps Belleri in its wings, granting her invulnerability for 3 seconds (cooldown: 90 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Mage / Burst damage dealer with exceptional area control, damage over time, and a rebirth passive that makes her extremely durable.

dialogue.greeting: Feel the warmth of the eternal flame. It welcomes you.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Inferno! Let the world burn and be reborn!
dialogue.respawn: From the ashes, I rise again. As I always have.

heroes.name: Waguri
titles: The Monster Summoner of the Inverted Shrine
heroes.shortDescription: She drifts through the twilight like a dream wrapped in silk and shadow, a beauty so profound that it borders on the terrible. Waguri is a nineteen-year-old woman whose grace has been honed by centuries of practice, though her face holds the freshness of eternal youth. She is the evil twin of Kagura, the shadow to her light, the darkness that her sister tries to forget. She wields the Kagegasa, a tattered parasol that holds the power of a thousand shadows, and she commands a legion of monsters bound to her will.
heroes.fullDescription: Waguri drifts through the twilight like a dream wrapped in silk and shadow, a beauty so profound that it borders on the terrible—a nineteen‑year‑old woman whose grace has been honed by centuries of practice, though her face holds the freshness of eternal youth. She is the evil twin of Kagura, the shadow to her light, the darkness that her sister tries to forget. Her face is heart‑shaped, with a delicate jawline, high cheekbones, a small straight nose, and full lips curved in an enigmatic smile. Her skin is flawless, pale as moonlight, smooth and luminous. Her eyes are deep liquid purple, shifting to dark red when she uses her power, holding secrets that would drive mortals mad. Her hair is pure white, long and straight, past her waist, with side‑swept bangs that brush her brows. Nine shadow tails extend from the base of her spine, moving with a life of their own, each one a tendril of condensed darkness. A fox mask of painted wood—white with black rims, a knowing smirk—is pushed up on her head, ready to be pulled down to cover her face. A plum blossom hairpin of pale pink with a gold rim adorns her hair. Her physique is voluptuous and taut: a full G‑cup bust, a slender wasp‑thin waist, full rounded hips, and thick powerful thighs. She wears a black kimono worn open, held by a wide blood‑red obi tied in an elaborate bow at her back, revealing her cleavage and stomach. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, and her legs are exposed through high slits in the kimono. Silver chains hang at her waist, and plum blossom patterns adorn the kimono fabric. Her feet are bare. Her weapon, the Kagegasa, is a tattered parasol that holds the power of a thousand shadows—larger than she is, its canopy a deep bruised purple, its ribs of black lacquered wood, its handle carved from the bone of a demon she summoned in her first rite. The canopy is torn in a hundred places, and through the tears, shadow seeps like smoke, curling and twisting into shapes that shift with her mood. The surface is painted with intricate demonic patterns: snarling oni, twisting serpents, plum blossoms blooming in darkness, each one a seal that contains a monster she has summoned and bound to her will. When she opens the Kagegasa, the seals break, and from the shadows pour her army—oni with skin of black stone, tengu with wings of shadow, kappa with claws of iron. Her pet, Koko, is a small white bat with wings of translucent membrane and eyes of deep ruby red—no larger than her palm—that sleeps in the folds of her kimono during the day, nestled against her heart. At night, it flutters around her head, its soft chittering a comfort in the darkness, capable of growing in size to become a shield that can deflect arrows, a weapon that can blind her enemies, a beacon that calls forth the monsters she has bound. This monster summoner was born under the shadow of the Inverted Shrine, a place where the light of the sun never reaches, raised by the Plum Clan to bind monsters to her will. She is the twin of Kagura, and where her sister found peace in the light, Waguri found power in the shadows. She does not hate Kagura; she pities her, for Kagura has chosen to blind herself to half of the world. Now she waits in the inverted shrine—a structure of impossible architecture, spires pointing downward, altars on the ceiling, prayers written in reverse, lit only by the bioluminescence of the monsters that serve her—her parasol open, her shadow tails swaying, her monsters waiting, hoping that one day Kagura will understand that light cannot exist without shadow.

story.part1: The Inverted Shrine was built in the shadow of the World Tree, on the spot where the first demon fell to an elven blade. Its architecture is inverted—spires pointing downward, altars on the ceiling, prayers written in reverse—because the Plum Clan believes that the darkness is not the absence of light, but its mirror, its reflection, its necessary opposite. Waguri was born there, on the night of a lunar eclipse, when the shadows were deepest and the monsters of the shrine were most active. Her mother died in childbirth, and her father—the head of the Plum Clan—raised her in the ways of the shadow summoners, teaching her to bind monsters to her will, to open gates to the abyss, to walk the line between the living and the dead. Kagura was born at the same moment, but in a different place—on the surface, under the light of the full moon, in the care of the sun priests. They were separated at birth, two halves of a whole, raised in ignorance of each other's existence. Waguri did not learn that she had a twin until she was sixteen, when Kagura came to the Inverted Shrine to destroy it. They fought, and Waguri won, and Kagura fled. That was the first time Waguri saw her sister's face, and she wept, because she recognized something in those light-filled eyes—a loneliness that matched her own.
story.part2: The Plum Clan had served the Inverted Shrine for a thousand years, binding monsters to their will, using them to protect the realm from threats that the sun priests could not see. Waguri was the most gifted summoner in the clan's history; by the age of ten, she had bound a greater oni to her service; by twelve, she had tamed a tengu lord; by fifteen, she had opened a gate to the abyss and walked among the shadows, returning with a legion of monsters at her command. The elders of the clan feared her, but they also revered her, for they knew that she was the fulfillment of a prophecy—the Shadow Keeper, the one who would unite the darkness and use it to protect the world from threats that the light could not defeat. Waguri did not want to be a prophecy; she wanted to be a girl, with friends, with family, with someone who understood her. But the shrine was her home, and the monsters were her companions, and the darkness was her only comfort. She learned to accept her fate, to embrace the shadows, to find beauty in the darkness that others feared.
story.part3: The first time she met Hayabusa, she was hunting a rogue oni that had escaped its binding. He was hunting the same oni, sent by the Shadow Accord to eliminate a threat to their operations. They fought—not each other, but the oni, and when it fell, they stood back to back in the darkness, breathing heavily, their blades dripping with shadow ichor. Hayabusa looked at her, and she looked at him, and for a moment, the world stood still. 'You're not what I expected,' he said. 'Neither are you,' she replied. They did not become friends; they became something more—two creatures of the darkness, bound by a shared understanding that the light could never provide. They meet in secret, in the spaces between the worlds, where the light of the sun never reaches and the shadows are deepest. They talk, they spar, they share the silence that only those who have walked the abyss can understand. Waguri loves him, though she has never said it aloud, and she knows that he loves her in return, though he has never said it either. They are two shadows, dancing in the darkness, and for Waguri, that is enough.
story.part4: The rivalry with Kagura is the great tragedy of Waguri's existence. They are twins, bound by blood, divided by light and shadow. Kagura believes that the darkness is evil, that the shadows must be banished, that the only path to salvation is through the light. Waguri knows that this is a lie—that the darkness is not evil, merely different, that the shadows are not enemies, merely misunderstood. They have fought a hundred times, each battle a clash of ideology as much as steel. Kagura always fights with righteous fury, believing that she is saving her sister from damnation. Waguri always fights with sorrowful resignation, knowing that she cannot win, cannot lose, cannot change her sister's mind. At the end of each battle, when Kagura flees into the light, Waguri watches her go, and she whispers, 'One day, sister. One day, you will understand.' She does not know if that day will ever come. But she hopes, because hope is the only light that the darkness cannot extinguish.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Waguri and the spirits of the Inverted Shrine, is that she is not the evil twin—she is the necessary twin. The balance between light and shadow requires both, and Kagura's rejection of the darkness has made her incomplete, a half-being, a soul that cannot be whole until she accepts the shadow that is her birthright. Waguri does not seek to corrupt her sister; she seeks to complete her, to show her that the darkness is not a curse, but a gift, a tool, a part of the world that is as beautiful as the light. She waits in the shadows of the inverted shrine, her parasol open, her monsters waiting, and she knows that one day, Kagura will come to her—not to fight, but to understand. And on that day, Waguri will embrace her, and they will be whole, light and shadow, two halves of a single soul, united at last. Until that day, she waits, and she hopes, and she loves her sister with a love that even the darkness cannot dim.

weapons.name: Kagegasa
weapons.fulldesc: The Kagegasa is a tattered parasol that holds the power of a thousand shadows. The parasol is larger than Waguri is, its canopy a deep, bruised purple, its ribs of black lacquered wood, its handle carved from the bone of a demon she summoned in her first rite. The canopy is tattered, torn in a hundred places, and through the tears, shadow seeps like smoke, curling and twisting, forming shapes that shift and change with her mood. passive.name: Shadow Legion
passive.description: Waguri can summon up to 3 monsters from the Kagegasa at a time. Monsters attack independently and have 30% of Waguri's stats. When a monster dies, it leaves a shadow patch that slows enemies by 30%.

passive.name: Plum Blossom Curse
passive.description: Waguri's basic attacks and skills apply a stack of Plum Blossom Curse (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, the target takes 150% magical damage and is feared for 1 second.

active.name: Summon Oni
active.description: Waguri summons an Oni at target location. The Oni attacks enemies, dealing 100% magical damage per hit, and taunts them for 2 seconds. Lasts 10 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

active.name: Shadow Step
active.description: Waguri steps into a shadow and emerges at target location within 15 meters, dealing 120% magical damage to enemies near the exit point. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Legion Unleashed
ultimate.description: Waguri opens the Kagegasa fully, releasing all her bound monsters at once. For 15 seconds, monsters spawn continuously around her, attacking enemies and dealing 100% magical damage per hit. Enemies within the swarm are slowed by 50% and take 50% damage per second. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: During Legion Unleashed, Waguri is invisible and cannot be targeted.

special.name: Binding Pact
special.description: When Waguri dies, all active monsters explode, dealing 200% magical damage to nearby enemies. Additionally, she can sacrifice a monster to heal herself for 20% of her max health (cooldown: 30 seconds).

ratings.notes: Summoner mage with exceptional area control and zoning. Her monsters provide constant pressure, but she is vulnerable when they are on cooldown.

skills.name: Shadow Affinity
skills.description: Waguri gains 1% increased magic power for each active monster (max 15%). Additionally, her monsters deal 10% increased damage to enemies affected by Plum Blossom Curse.

skills.name: Tengu's Call
skills.description: Waguri summons a Tengu that flies to target location and explodes, dealing 150% magical damage in a 6-meter radius and knocking back enemies. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Shadow Veil
skills.description: Waguri wraps herself in shadows, becoming invisible for 4 seconds. While invisible, she moves 30% faster. Attacking or using a skill breaks invisibility. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Plum Blossom Shield
skills.description: When Waguri falls below 30% health, she releases a burst of plum blossom petals, stunning all nearby enemies for 2 seconds and summoning two Oni to protect her. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Waguri was born under the shadow of the Inverted Shrine, a place where the light of the sun never reaches, where the only illumination comes from the bioluminescence of the monsters that serve the Plum Clan...

relationships.description: Light and shadow, two halves of a whole. Waguri seeks to show her sister that darkness is not evil, but necessary.
They meet in the shadows, two creatures of the darkness bound by a shared understanding. She loves him, though she has never said it aloud.

pet.name: Koko
pet.description: Koko is a small, white bat, no larger than Waguri's palm, with wings of translucent membrane and eyes of deep, ruby red. Koko is not merely a pet; it is her guardian, a fragment of the first shadow that she summoned, bound to her as a protector and a friend.
pet.fulldesc: Koko sleeps in the folds of Waguri's kimono during the day, nestled against her heart, and at night, it flutters around her head, its wings brushing her cheeks, its soft chittering a comfort in the darkness. When danger threatens, Koko grows, its wings expanding, its eyes blazing, becoming a shield that can deflect arrows, a weapon that can blind her enemies, a beacon that calls forth the monsters she has bound to her will. Its fur is soft and warm, and its heartbeat is a gentle thrum against her skin. It is the only being she trusts, the only soul that has never betrayed her, and she whispers her secrets to it in the dark hours before dawn, knowing that Koko will carry them to the grave.
abilities: Echo Screech – Emits a sonic wave that reveals all enemies within 20 meters and slows them by 30% for 3 seconds (cooldown: 40 seconds).
Shadow Shield – Grows in size, creating a shield that absorbs 200 damage for Waguri (cooldown: 30 seconds).
Monster Call – Chirps loudly, summoning a random monster to fight for 10 seconds (cooldown: 60 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Summoner Mage / Zoning specialist with exceptional area control and invisibility. Her monsters provide constant pressure and map control.

dialogue.greeting: The shadows welcome you. Do not fear them—they are your friends.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Legion Unleashed! Come forth, my children!
dialogue.death: Koko... take care... of the shrine...
dialogue.respawn: The darkness does not forget its own.

heroes.name: Maila Toh
heroes.shortDescription: He stands like a bastion of damned resolve, a fortress of flesh and fury that has withstood the onslaught of heaven and hell alike. Maila Toh is a man of forty-five years, his body a monument to the suffering that forged him and the power that now sustains him. He is the second brother of Gatotkaca, the one who was not there when the family needed him most, the one who gave in to the darkness when the light failed.
heroes.fullDescription: Maila Toh stands like a bastion of damned resolve, a fortress of flesh and fury that has withstood the onslaught of heaven and hell alike—a man of forty‑five years, his body a monument to the suffering that forged him and the power that now sustains him. He is the second brother of Gatotkaca, the one who was not there when the family needed him most, the one who gave in to the darkness when the light failed. His face is rugged and weathered, with a strong square jaw, high cheekbones, a straight broad nose broken and reset many times, and thin lips pressed into a grim line. His skin is deep mottled green, shot through with crimson veins that pulse with the rhythm of his damned pact, the color of oxidized copper and poisoned earth. His eyes are blazing molten red, burning with the fire of hell. His hair is a long wild mane of deep crimson, falling past his shoulders in tangled waves. One horn is whole, curving from his brow, polished black; the other is broken, jagged, a scar from the battle that claimed his humanity. His physique is massive and bulky: a barrel chest of dense corded muscle, thick arms like tree trunks, broad shoulders, a narrow waist relative to his bulk, and powerful pillar‑like legs. He wears no chest armor—his torso is bare, his pectorals defined, crimson veins pulsing across his skin. A demonic chest plate, Kerinzo Karma, is fused to his torso, its surface a chaotic swirl of green and red, pulsing with a rhythm that matches his heartbeat, absorbing blows that would shatter mountains and converting the energy into raw brute force. His right arm is encased in Gluttony—a massive bulky gauntlet double the size of his leg, encasing his arm from shoulder to fingertips, its surfaces a chaotic blend of black iron, red gold, and the faces of the damned. The fingers are articulated, each tipped with a claw that can tear the soul from a body, and the palm has a mouth that opens wide to consume the soul of the wicked. Black iron spiked pauldrons guard his shoulders, and a bone necklace of his lover's vertebrae hangs around his neck. Chains of black iron wrap around his waist, arms, and neck. A tattered red loincloth covers his hips, and his lower body is protected by demonic armor of black and gold—minimal, overlapping scales. His legs are thick and powerful, exposed, and his feet are bare, leaving scorch marks where he walks. This devil‑bound warrior was once a hero of the Celestial Order, a brother to Gatotkaca, a man who stood for justice and light. But a demon lord slaughtered his entire family while he was away, and in his despair, he made a pact with a devil—his soul for the power to protect the innocent. Now he is the Devil Barrier, a creature of damned resolve, waiting in the shadows of Nyxara—a realm of eternal twilight, where the sky is a bruised purple and the ground is cracked with glowing fissures, where his massive Cerberus Malphas (a three‑headed hound with poison‑green fur and feathered crimson wings) lies at his feet—his chest plate pulsing, his gauntlet humming, waiting for the day when his brother Gatotkaca will come, when they will clash, and when Maila will finally let his brother strike him, again and again, until Gatotkaca understands that he is not the enemy, but the brother who loved him enough to damn himself.

story.part1: The Toh brothers were legends in the Celestial Order. Gatotkaca, the eldest, was the golden child—brave, noble, beloved by all who met him. Maila, the second son, was the strong one—quiet, dependable, the wall that his family leaned on when the storms came. They trained together, fought together, bled together, and together they rose through the ranks of the Order, becoming two of the most powerful warriors in the realm. Their parents were proud; their siblings looked up to them; their enemies feared them. Maila thought that nothing could break them, that their bond was unbreakable, that they would stand together until the end of time. He was wrong.
story.part2: The attack came without warning. A demon lord of the Shadow Accord, seeking revenge for a defeat at Gatotkaca's hands, launched a surprise assault on the Toh family estate. Maila was away on a mission; Gatotkaca was at the capital, meeting with the Celestial Council. By the time they received word, it was too late. The demon lord had slaughtered everyone—their parents, their siblings, their cousins, their servants, everyone. The estate was a charnel house, the walls painted with blood, the bodies stacked like firewood. Maila arrived first, and he fell to his knees in the courtyard, his screams echoing in the silence. He had not been there. He had not protected them. He had failed. The guilt consumed him, and in that moment of despair, a devil appeared—a creature of shadow and fire, offering him a pact: his soul for the power to protect the innocent, to ensure that no one else would suffer as his family had suffered. Maila accepted without hesitation. He did not care about the cost. He only cared about the power.
story.part3: The transformation was agonizing. Maila's skin turned green, the color of oxidized copper and poisoned earth. His eyes became blazing, molten red. His body fused with demonic armor that grew from his flesh like a second skeleton. He could feel the devil's power coursing through him, hungry and patient, waiting to be unleashed. He did not fight it; he embraced it. He became the Devil Barrier, a creature of damned resolve, and he hunted down the demon lord who had killed his family. The battle lasted three days, and at the end of it, Maila stood over the demon's corpse, his chest plate pulsing, his gauntlet dripping with ichor. He did not feel satisfaction; he felt only the cold, empty certainty that his family was still dead, that his soul was still damned, that nothing he did could ever bring them back. He turned away from the Celestial Order that day, and he walked into the shadows, and he did not look back.
story.part4: Gatotkaca searched for his brother for years, following rumors and whispers, tracking him across the realms. When they finally met, on the fields of the Sundered Plains, Maila did not recognize him at first. The devil's power had clouded his mind, twisted his memories, made him see enemies where there were only friends. They fought, and Gatotkaca won—but he could not bring himself to kill his brother. He stood over Maila's broken body, his blade at his throat, and he wept. 'Come home,' he begged. 'Please, Maila. The Order can help you. The light can heal you.' Maila laughed—a hollow, bitter sound. 'The light abandoned me, brother. It let our family die. The darkness saved me. The darkness gave me power. The darkness is my home now.' He slipped into the shadows, and Gatotkaca watched him go, his heart breaking. They have not spoken since.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Maila and the devil that holds his soul, is that he does not regret his choice. He regrets that he was not there to save his family; he regrets that he could not protect them; but he does not regret the power that allows him to protect others. He has saved thousands since that day, standing as a barrier between the innocent and the darkness, absorbing blows that would have killed anyone else. He knows that his soul is damned, that the devil will claim it when he dies, that he will spend eternity in the flames of hell. But he does not care. He cares only about the next life he can save, the next child he can shield, the next family that will not have to suffer as his family suffered. He is the Devil Barrier, and he will stand, unyielding, unbroken, until his body crumbles to dust and his soul is claimed. And when Gatotkaca finally understands that Maila's damnation was not a betrayal, but a sacrifice, perhaps then they will be brothers again. Until that day, Maila waits in the shadows, and he fights, and he protects, and he loves his brother from a distance that no light can cross.

weapons.name: Gluttony (Gauntlet) & Kerinzo Karma
weapons.fulldesc: Gluttony is a massive, bulky gauntlet that has become the symbol of Maila's sin-eating power. The gauntlet is double the size of his leg, encasing his entire right arm from shoulder to fingertips, its surfaces a chaotic blend of black iron, red gold, and the faces of the damned. The fingers are articulated, each one tipped with a claw that can tear the soul from a body, and the palm is hollow, a void that devours the sins of the wicked. When he places his gauntlet over the heart of a sinner, Gluttony awakens, its jaws—for it has a mouth in the palm—opening wide to consume the soul, the sin, the very essence of the wrongdoer. Kerinzo Karma is a demonic chest plate fused to his torso, its surface a chaotic swirl of green and red, its edges fused to his flesh. The chest plate is alive, pulsing with a rhythm that matches his heartbeat, and it feeds on the power that is thrown against him, absorbing blows that would shatter mountains, converting the energy into raw, brute force that he can unleash with a thought.
weapons.look: Gluttony: massive bulky gauntlet, faces of the damned, mouth in palm; Kerinzo Karma: fused chest plate, green and red swirl, pulsing
weapons.aura: Hungry red light (Gluttony), pulsing green (Kerinzo Karma)

passive.name: Sin Eater
passive.description: Each time Maila kills an enemy, he consumes their sin, permanently increasing his physical defense by 2 and magic defense by 1 (max 50 stacks).

passive.name: Kerinzo's Aegis
passive.description: Maila gains 1% damage reduction for every 5% of health missing (max 40% damage reduction at 1% health).

active.name: Soul Devour
active.description: Maila grabs an enemy with Gluttony, dealing 200% physical damage and silencing them for 2 seconds. If the enemy dies within 3 seconds, Maila permanently gains 10 max health. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

active.name: Power Conversion
active.description: Maila converts 50% of his current health into a shield that absorbs 200% of the converted health. The shield lasts 5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Devil's Wrath
ultimate.description: Maila unleashes all absorbed power from Kerinzo Karma, dealing 300% physical damage plus 10% of his max health as bonus damage to all enemies within 15 meters. Maila's health is reduced to 1 after using this skill. Cooldown: 90 seconds. Passive: Devil's Wrath's cooldown is reduced by 1 second for every 10% of health Maila loses.

special.name: Damned Resilience
special.description: When Maila takes damage that would kill him, he instead survives with 1 HP and becomes invulnerable for 3 seconds. During this time, his next basic attack deals 200% increased damage. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

ratings.notes: Extremely durable tank with scaling defenses. His damage scales with health lost, making him dangerous at low HP.

skills.name: Devil's Pact
skills.description: Maila's basic attacks deal bonus damage equal to 5% of his missing health. Additionally, he gains 10% increased movement speed when below 30% health.

skills.name: Barrier Slam
skills.description: Maila slams the ground with Gluttony, creating a shockwave in a 10-meter radius that deals 120% physical damage and slows enemies by 40% for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Demonic Charge
skills.description: Maila charges forward 10 meters, knocking back enemies and dealing 100% physical damage. If he hits a wall, enemies are stunned for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Blood for Blood
skills.description: When Maila falls below 30% health, he gains 50% increased damage and 30% lifesteal for 10 seconds. Triggers once every 90 seconds.

heroes.lore: Maila Toh was once a hero, a warrior of the Celestial Order, a brother to Gatotkaca, a man who stood for justice and light. But tragedy broke him...

relationships.description: Maila loves his brother but believes that Gatotkaca cannot understand his choice to embrace darkness for the sake of protection.
A massive three-headed Cerberus with poison-green fur and feathered crimson wings. Bound to Maila by the same pact that claimed his soul.

pet.name: Malphas
pet.description: Malphas is a massive Cerberus of poison-green fur and feathered, crimson wings. The beast is twice Maila's height, its bodies a mass of coiled muscle and jagged bone, its paws tipped with claws that can rend steel. Its three heads are each a different aspect of its nature: the left, wisdom; the center, fury; the right, hunger.
pet.fulldesc: Malphas's eyes are pits of molten gold, and its breath is a cloud of poison that can choke armies. Its wings are vast, feathered in deep, blood red, and when it flies, it blots out the sun, a shadow that brings terror to the hearts of the faithful. The three heads speak in unison when they want to be heard, their voices a discordant harmony that echoes in the soul. Malphas is not merely a pet; it is a brother, a friend, a fragment of the darkness that Maila has embraced, and it follows him with the loyalty of a hound, its three heads watching, always watching, for the enemies that would harm its master. When Maila rests, it curls around him, its heads resting on his lap, its warm breath a comfort in the cold darkness; when he fights, it charges beside him, its jaws snapping, its claws tearing, its loyalty absolute.
abilities: Triple Bite – Malphas bites with all three heads, dealing 200% physical damage and applying a bleed that deals 50% damage per second for 5 seconds (cooldown: 20 seconds).
Poison Breath – Exhales a cloud of poison that deals 100% magical damage per second for 5 seconds in a 10-meter cone (cooldown: 15 seconds).
Wings of Terror – Takes flight, becoming untargetable for 3 seconds and leaving a trail of fear that slows enemies by 50% (cooldown: 45 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Tank / Sustained fighter with exceptional durability and scaling defenses. His damage increases as his health decreases, making him dangerous to ignore.

dialogue.greeting: The damned do not die. They only wait. I am waiting.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Devil's Wrath! Feel the weight of my damnation!
dialogue.respawn: The pact holds. The damned return.

heroes.name: Teinukza Sherpa
heroes.shortDescription: She walks the high passes of the Himalayas like a glacier given will, a being of such ancient, terrible beauty that the mountains themselves tremble at her approach. Teinukza Sherpa is a frost giantess of eighteen years (in appearance, though her existence spans sixty-five centuries), a jotun who has waged war on the human tribes for six thousand years. She is the ancestor from whom Hilda draws her strength, the monster that mothers use to frighten their children into obedience.
heroes.fullDescription: Teinukza Sherpa walks the high passes of the Himalayas like a glacier given will, a being of such ancient terrible beauty that the mountains themselves tremble at her approach—a frost giantess of eighteen years in appearance, though her existence spans sixty‑five centuries, a jotun who has waged war on the human tribes for six thousand years. She is the ancestor from whom Hilda draws her strength, the monster that mothers use to frighten their children into obedience. Her face is strong and angular, with a sharp predatory jawline, high cheekbones, a straight narrow nose that flares with the scent of blood, and full lips that curve in eternal disdain. Her skin is deep luminous blue, with a crystalline sheen like glacial ice, smooth and cold. Her eyes are pale frozen blue, igniting to supernova white when she is angered. Her hair is pure white, long and thick, styled in messy braids interwoven with fur strips and bones. Rune scars cover her body, glowing blue when she channels her power. Her physique is massive—nine feet tall, with a barrel chest of dense corded muscle, thick powerful arms, broad shoulders, a slender waist for her size, and pillar‑like legs. She wears a fur‑lined brown leather harness across her chest, straps crossing her torso, exposing her cleavage and stomach. Her arms are bare, thick with muscle, covered in rune scars. Black iron spikes rise from the fur coat on her shoulders. A sapphire necklace called Folamn—a massive gem—hangs at her throat, glowing with cold light. A wide belt of leather and bone, adorned with the skulls of her enemies, cinches her waist. A tattered short brown fur skirt barely covers her hips, exposing her thick powerful legs, also covered in rune scars. Short brown fur boots cover her feet. Her weapon, Frostbite, is a polearm‑type axe of jotun craftsmanship—its haft a length of petrified yew wrapped in the hide of a white bear, its head a massive blade of black iron rimed with eternal frost, shaped like a crescent moon, wicked and sharp. When it strikes, it does not merely cut; it freezes the blood in its victim's veins, turning them to ice from the inside out. She wields it with the ease of a dancer, spinning it in complex patterns, and when she throws it, it returns to her hand like a loyal hound, trailing frost and blood. Her pet, Yor Thapa, is a female yeti cub—no larger than a mastiff, her fur a patchwork of white and grey, her eyes the color of mountain lakes, her paws still soft, her growls still playful, but fierce and protective, bounding through the snow, curling beside the giantess when she rests. This frost giantess was born in the first age of the jotun, when the frost giants ruled the frozen peaks of Niflheim and the humans huddled in caves, afraid of the cold. She has watched the mortals spread across her mountains, build their villages, burn their fires, and she has hated them with a purity that has sustained her for millennia. Now she waits in her glacier palace carved from the heart of the highest peak in the Himalayas—a realm of crystalline ice, frozen waterfalls, and eternal silence, where the only light comes from the aurora borealis dancing across the sky—Frostbite across her knees, Yor Thapa at her feet, watching the world below, waiting for the day when her descendant Hilda will come, when the blood of the jotun will call to blood, when she will see whether the human tribes have finally produced a warrior worthy of the winter.

story.part1: The jotun were the first children of Niflheim, born from the frost that formed when the primordial fires of creation met the void of absolute cold. Teinukza Sherpa was among the first of them, a giantess of such immense power that she could freeze rivers with a glance and shatter mountains with a punch. She ruled the Himalayas for six thousand years, her glacier palace carved from the heart of the highest peak, her subjects the frost giants and yetis and ice spirits that served her will. The humans who lived in the valleys below feared her, worshiped her, sacrificed to her in the hope that she would spare them from the winter's fury. She accepted their offerings, but she did not spare them. She was the winter, and the winter does not bargain.
story.part2: The Sundering changed the balance of power in the Himalayas. Rifts opened in the sky, and from them poured demons of fire and shadow, creatures that the jotun had never seen before. Teinukza fought them, not to protect the humans, but to protect her domain. She froze a hundred demons solid and shattered them with her axe. She buried a legion of shadow creatures under an avalanche that she summoned with a roar. She stood at the edge of the largest rift, her Frostbite raised, and she stared into the abyss with eyes that had seen the birth of the mountains. The abyss blinked first. The rift sealed, the demons retreated, and Teinukza returned to her glacier palace, victorious. But she was changed. The corruption of the abyss had touched her, coloring her pure white frost with veins of blue and strange fire. She was no longer just a frost giantess; she was something new, something terrible, something that the world had never seen before.
story.part3: The centuries that followed were a blur of war and isolation. Teinukza watched the human tribes grow, spread, build their villages closer and closer to her mountains. She saw them cut down her forests, hunt her yetis, burn their fires that sent smoke into her clean, cold air. Her hatred for them grew, fed by the corruption that still pulsed in her veins. She began to raid their villages, not to kill, but to remind them of their place. She would freeze their crops, shatter their walls, scatter their livestock. She would stand at the edge of their burning villages and laugh, a sound like grinding ice, and she would say, 'This is my mountain. You are only guests. Do not forget.' The humans did not forget. They passed down stories of the Blue Doom Bear, the monster in the mountains, the wrath of the winter made flesh. And Teinukza smiled, because fear was the only tribute that she still respected.
story.part4: The birth of Hilda was a shock to Teinukza. She sensed the child's jotun blood, the heritage that had been dormant in the human tribes for generations, suddenly awakened. She watched from her glacier palace as Hilda grew, as she learned to fight, as she began to unite the tribes against the monsters that threatened them. Teinukza was curious. She had not seen a jotun-blooded human in centuries, and she wondered if this child would be worthy of the legacy that flowed through her veins. She sent her yetis to test Hilda, to push her, to see if she would break. Hilda did not break. She fought, and she won, and she grew stronger with each victory. Teinukza smiled, and for the first time in millennia, she felt something other than hatred—a flicker of pride, a spark of hope that perhaps, one day, a warrior would come who could challenge her.
story.part5: The truth, known only to Teinukza and the spirits of the Himalayas, is that she is tired. Six thousand years of war, of isolation, of watching the world change while she remains the same—it has worn on her. She does not want to fight Hilda; she wants to see if Hilda is strong enough to carry the legacy that she herself has carried for so long. She wants to pass the torch, to lay down her axe, to rest. But she cannot rest until she knows that the tribes are safe, that the mountains are protected, that there is someone who can stand against the darkness when she is gone. That is why she waits. That is why she tests. That is why she will fight Hilda when the time comes—not to destroy her, but to forge her, to temper her in the fire of battle, to make her strong enough to carry the weight of the winter. For Teinukza Sherpa is the Frost Giantess of Niflheim, and she will not let her legacy die with her.

weapons.name: Frostbite
weapons.fulldesc: Frostbite is a polearm-type axe of jotun craftsmanship, its haft a length of petrified yew wrapped in the hide of a white bear, its head a massive blade of black iron, its edge rimed with eternal frost. The blade is shaped like a crescent moon, its curve wicked and sharp, and when it strikes, it does not merely cut; it freezes the blood in its victim's veins, turning them to ice from the inside out. Teinukza wields it with the ease of a dancer, spinning it in complex patterns, and when she throws it, it returns to her hand like a loyal hound, trailing frost and blood in its wake. The axe hums with a low, resonant vibration when active, and the air around it is cold enough to freeze water.
weapons.look: Petrified yew haft, white bear hide wrap, massive black iron crescent blade rimed with frost

passive.name: Permafrost
passive.description: Frostbite's attacks apply a stack of Permafrost (max 5 stacks). Each stack slows the target by 10% and reduces attack speed by 5%. At 5 stacks, the target is frozen solid for 1.5 seconds.

passive.name: Jotun's Might
passive.description: Teinukza's size grants her 20% increased health and 10% increased damage reduction. She is immune to knockback and displacement effects.

active.name: Ice Cleave
active.description: Teinukza swings Frostbite in a wide arc, dealing 200% physical damage to all enemies in a 10-meter cone and freezing them for 1 second. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Avalanche Throw
active.description: Teinukza throws Frostbite in a straight line, piercing all enemies and dealing 250% physical damage. The axe returns to her hand after 2 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Winter's Wrath
ultimate.description: Teinukza raises Frostbite and channels for 1.5 seconds, then slams it into the ground, creating a massive blizzard in a 25-meter radius. Enemies in the blizzard take 400% physical damage, are slowed by 80%, and have their vision reduced to 3 meters for 5 seconds. The blizzard persists for 5 seconds, dealing 50% damage per second. Cooldown: 100 seconds. Passive: During Winter's Wrath, Teinukza's armor is increased by 50%.

special.name: Glacial Resilience
special.description: When Teinukza takes damage, she gains 1% increased armor and magic resistance for 5 seconds (max 30 stacks). Additionally, she heals for 5% of her max health over 5 seconds when she kills an enemy.

ratings.notes: Extremely durable tank with massive health pool and crowd control. Her slow and freeze effects make her excellent at locking down enemies.

skills.name: Frozen Aura
skills.description: Enemies within 10 meters of Teinukza are slowed by 15% and take 20% magical damage per second from the cold.

skills.name: Glacial Leap
skills.description: Teinukza leaps to target location, dealing 150% physical damage in a 6-meter radius and freezing enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Frost Armor
skills.description: Teinukza encases herself in ice, gaining 50% damage reduction and reflecting 20% of incoming damage for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Jotun's Endurance
skills.description: When Teinukza falls below 20% health, she instantly regenerates 50% of her max health over 5 seconds and becomes immune to crowd control for the duration. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

heroes.lore: Teinukza Sherpa was born in the first age of the jotun, when the frost giants ruled the frozen peaks of Niflheim and the humans huddled in caves, afraid of the cold...

relationships.description: Teinukza is the ancestor from whom Hilda draws her jotun strength. She watches Hilda's progress with curiosity and pride.
A female yeti cub Teinukza found orphaned on a high pass. She loves the cub with a ferocity that surprises even herself.

pet.name: Yor Thapa
pet.description: Yor Thapa is a female yeti cub, no larger than a mastiff, her fur a patchwork of white and grey, her eyes the color of mountain lakes. Her paws are still soft, her growls still playful, but she is fierce, protective, and devoted to the giantess who saved her.
pet.fulldesc: Teinukza loves the cub with a ferocity that surprises even herself; she feeds her from her own hand, warms her against her chest, teaches her the old songs, the hunting ways, the hatred for the warmth that threatens their home. Yor Thapa is not merely a pet; she is a daughter, the only being in six thousand years that has made the frost giantess feel something other than rage. She bounds through the snow with the energy of a cub, her playful growls echoing in the frozen valleys, and when danger threatens, she stands at Teinukza's feet, her fur bristling, her small fangs bared, ready to protect the giantess who saved her. When Teinukza rests, Yor Thapa curls beside her, her warm body pressed against the giantess's cold skin, her soft purr a comfort in the frozen silence.
abilities: Playful Pounce – Yor Thapa leaps at an enemy, dealing 50% physical damage and stunning them for 0.5 seconds (cooldown: 20 seconds).
Protective Growl – Yor Thapa growls, reducing the damage Teinukza takes by 20% for 5 seconds (cooldown: 30 seconds).
Cub's Courage – Yor Thapa lets out a battle cry, increasing Teinukza's attack speed by 30% for 10 seconds (cooldown: 45 seconds).

heroes.specialty: Tank / Crowd Control specialist with exceptional durability, slow and freeze effects, and massive area damage.

dialogue.greeting: The mountains are cold. So am I. Do not mistake one for the other.
dialogue.skill_ultimate: Winter's Wrath! Feel the cold of Niflheim!
dialogue.respawn: The frost does not die. It only sleeps.

heroes.name: Seki Meite
heroes.shortDescription: An Egyptian empress of unparalleled beauty who was betrayed, her throne stolen, her kingdom lost. Seki Meite has spent millennia in the desert gathering her strength, wielding the Khopesh of Flames and commanding the Sphinx Sei Ki. She waits for the moment when the stars align and she can reclaim what is hers from the usurper Moskov.
heroes.fullDescription: Seki Meite rises from the dunes like a mirage made flesh, a vision of golden light and smoldering fury that has haunted the dreams of pharaohs for millennia—an Egyptian empress of unparalleled beauty, her features a masterful blend of Nubian nobility and the divine perfection of the sun god's favored daughter. Her face is heart‑shaped, with a soft rounded jawline, a straight narrow nose, and full crimson lips perpetually parted in a knowing imperial smirk. Her skin is fair and luminous, the pale gold of desert sand under a noonday sun, smooth and flawless, with a faint ethereal glow. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep molten gold that blazes with the fire of the sun itself. Her hair is a cascade of rich chestnut brown silk, falling past her waist in thick lustrous waves shot through with strands of gold. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a waist so slender it seems impossible, flaring into full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full voluptuous G‑cup bust. She wears a sand‑yellow linen gown of thin silk trimmed with gold thread, cut deep at the chest, overlaid with a golden armor breastplate shaped like falcon's wings. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, with bracelets of hammered gold on her forearms. A collar of gold and lapis lazuli, with pendants shaped like the eyes of Ra, circles her neck. A belt of interlocking gold discs cinches her waist, and the gown skirt is slit high on both sides, exposing her thick thighs. Her feet are bare, her toes painted with henna. A floating golden aura surrounds her. Her weapon, the Khopesh of Flames, is a throwing spear that has become the symbol of her reign—longer than she is tall, its shaft a length of petrified acacia wood wrapped in gold wire, its head a curved sickle‑shaped blade of obsidian rimed with eternal fire, etched with the name of the sun god Ra. When she throws it, it leaves a trail of sunfire that scorches the earth, igniting everything it touches. The Khopesh never misses; it returns to her hand like a loyal falcon, and when it strikes, it burns with the fury of the sun, desiccating flesh, turning blood to ash. Her mount, Sei Ki, is a massive Sphinx with the body of a lion, wings of an eagle, and the face of a woman—serene, knowing, and terrible. Its fur is the color of golden sand, its feathers tipped with crimson, its eyes two pools of molten gold. It is larger than any mortal building, its paws capable of crushing chariots, its roar capable of shaking pyramids. This sun scourge empress was the chosen daughter of Ra, ruling Egypt in a golden age until her husband Khenemet, corrupted by a demon lord, betrayed her and seized the throne. She was left for dead in the desert, but Ra granted her a blessing: she would survive, bound to the sands until she could reclaim her throne. For millennia, she walked the desert, her power growing, and she tamed the Sphinx as her companion. Now she stands at the edge of the Great Sand Sea—a vast expanse of golden dunes that stretch to the horizon, where the sun beats down mercilessly and the only landmarks are the bones of ancient beasts—the Khopesh of Flames in her hand, Sei Ki at her side, waiting for the moment when the stars align and she can march on the capital to reclaim what is hers from the usurper Moskov.

story.part1: Before the sands of Egypt were stained with the blood of usurpers, Seki Meite ruled as the Sun Scourge Empress, the chosen daughter of Ra, the god of the sun. Her reign was a golden age—the Nile flowed with abundance, the pyramids rose to touch the heavens, and her people worshipped her as a living goddess. She was not cruel; she was just, dispensing law with the warmth of the sun and punishment with its scorching fury. Her husband, a general named Khenemet, stood at her side, and together they seemed invincible. But the hearts of men are fickle, and the promise of power can poison even the deepest love.
story.part2: The betrayal came on the night of the solar eclipse, when Ra's light was dimmed and the shadows grew bold. Khenemet had made a pact with a demon lord of the Shadow Accord, trading his soul for the power to seize the throne. He led a rebellion of the eastern nomads, sweeping through the capital while Seki's guards were blinded by the eclipse. She fought—her Khopesh blazing, her voice calling down sunfire—but she was betrayed by those she trusted most. Her own handmaiden slipped a curse into her wine, weakening her enough for Khenemet's blade to find her heart. She fell, but she did not die. Ra, seeing his daughter's suffering, granted her a blessing: she would survive, but she would be bound to the desert until she could reclaim her throne. Seki Meite awoke in the burning sands, her crown gone, her kingdom lost, her husband now the pharaoh of a corrupted Egypt.
story.part3: For millennia, Seki walked the desert, her power growing with each passing century. The sun became her ally, the sands her army. She learned to summon sandstorms that could bury cities, to desiccate rivers with a gesture, to call down the curse of the sun upon her enemies. She forged the Khopesh of Flames from a meteorite that fell from Ra's chariot, its blade forever rimed with fire that could turn flesh to ash. She tamed Sei Ki, the Sphinx, a creature of myth that had guarded the pyramids since the first dynasty. The Sphinx recognized in her the true heir of Egypt and swore eternal loyalty. Together, they became a legend that travelers whispered about around campfires: the Golden Scourge, the ghost empress who would one day return.
story.part4: The Sundering tore open the desert, and Seki saw her opportunity. The demon lord who had empowered Khenemet had long since been banished, and the current usurper—a pretender named Moskov—had no claim to the throne. She began to move, not in open war, but in subtle strikes. She toppled outposts, freed slaves, and whispered prophecies of her return into the ears of the oppressed. The people began to remember the golden age, began to hope that the Sun Scourge Empress would rise again. Seki did not rush. She had waited millennia; she could wait a little longer.
story.part5: Now, Seki Meite stands at the edge of the desert, Sei Ki at her side, the Khopesh of Flames in her hand. She has learned that Moskov has allied with the Shadow Accord, that he plans to use the Sundering's chaos to cement his rule forever. She will not allow it. She has gathered an army of desert spirits, freed slaves, and nomads who still remember her name. She will march on the capital, not as a conqueror, but as a queen reclaiming her birthright. She knows that Moskov will fight, that blood will spill, that the sands will run red. But she also knows that the sun does not negotiate with shadows. It rises, it burns, and it restores the light. For she is Seki Meite, the Sun Scourge Empress, and Egypt will be hers again.

weapons.name: Khopesh of Flames
weapons.fulldesc: The Khopesh of Flames is a throwing spear that has become the symbol of Seki Meite's reign. The spear is longer than she is tall, its shaft a length of petrified acacia wood wrapped in gold wire, its head a curved, sickle‑shaped blade of obsidian, its edge rimed with eternal fire. The blade is etched with the name of the sun god Ra, and when she throws it, it leaves a trail of sunfire that scorches the earth, igniting everything it touches. The Khopesh never misses; it returns to her hand like a loyal falcon, and when it strikes, it does not merely wound—it burns with the fury of the sun, desiccating flesh, turning blood to ash, leaving only sand in its wake.
weapons.look: Curved obsidian blade with eternal fire, petrified acacia shaft wrapped in gold, Ra's name etched on blade
weapons.aura: Golden sunfire, heat shimmer, trailing embers

passive.name: Sunfire Edge
passive.description: Basic attacks with the Khopesh apply a stack of Sunburn, dealing 10 magic damage per second for 3 seconds (stacks up to 3 times).

passive.name: Desert's Wrath
passive.description: Seki gains 10% increased damage during daytime (in-game day/night cycle or desert maps).

active.name: Scorching Throw
active.description: Throws the Khopesh in a line, dealing 160% physical damage to the first enemy hit and 80% to subsequent enemies, leaving a trail of fire that lasts 3 seconds. Cooldown: 7 seconds.

active.name: Sandstorm
active.description: Summons a sandstorm in a 10-meter radius for 4 seconds, blinding enemies inside (reduced vision range) and dealing 60 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Ra's Judgment
ultimate.description: Seki raises the Khopesh to the sky, calling down a beam of concentrated sunlight in a 15-meter radius after 1.5 seconds. Enemies in the area take 400% magic damage and are stunned for 2 seconds. The ground remains scorched for 6 seconds, dealing 50 damage per second. Cooldown: 85 seconds.

special.name: Usurper's Bane
special.description: Seki's abilities deal 20% bonus damage to enemies with 'usurper' or 'pretender' lore tags (including Moskov).

ratings.notes: Hybrid marksman/mage with area damage, crowd control, and anti-usurper bonuses.

skills.name: Solar Blessing
skills.description: Seki's abilities heal her for 5% of damage dealt during daytime.

skills.name: Scarab Swarm
skills.description: Summons a swarm of golden scarabs that attack enemies in a 8-meter area for 3 seconds, dealing 100 magic damage total and reducing armor by 10%. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Pharaoh's Command
skills.description: Seki empowers an allied minion or summoned creature, doubling its health and damage for 10 seconds. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Eternal Grudge
skills.description: When Moskov is an ally, Seki refuses to assist him (cannot heal or buff him). When Moskov is an enemy, her attacks ignore 20% of his armor and magic resist.

heroes.lore: Seki Meite was the Sun Scourge Empress of Egypt, betrayed by her husband Khenemet and usurped from her throne. Blessed by Ra, she survived in the desert for millennia, gathering power. Now she seeks to reclaim her kingdom from the current usurper Moskov, with the Sphinx Sei Ki at her side.

relationships.description: Moskov currently claims the throne of Egypt; Seki will destroy him to reclaim her birthright.
Her former husband, now dead, but his corruption lingers in the throne's bloodline.

mount.name: Sei Ki
mount.description: A massive Sphinx with the body of a lion, wings of an eagle, and the face of a woman—serene, knowing, and terrible. Fur the color of golden sand, feathers tipped with crimson, eyes two pools of molten gold. Larger than any mortal building, paws capable of crushing chariots, roar capable of shaking pyramids.
mount.fulldesc: Sei Ki is a creature of myth, its body that of a lion, its wings those of an eagle, its face the face of a woman—serene, knowing, and terrible. Its fur is the color of golden sand, its feathers tipped with crimson, its eyes two pools of molten gold that match Seki's own. It is larger than any mortal building, its paws capable of crushing chariots, its roar capable of shaking the pyramids. Sei Ki is not merely a mount; it is a companion, a protector, the last remnant of the kingdom that was taken from Seki. It speaks in riddles, as Sphinxes do, but its loyalty to Seki Meite is absolute, and it will fight beside her until the sands of Egypt run red with the blood of her enemies. When Seki rides Sei Ki, the Sphinx's wings block out the sun, casting a shadow of terror over the battlefield. Its riddles are not idle; they reveal the weaknesses of her foes, and its roar can shatter the morale of armies.
abilities: Riddle of Weakness – reveals an enemy's lowest stat, reducing that stat by 30% for 5 seconds.
Crushing Pounce – leaps to a target location, dealing 200% physical damage in a 6-meter radius.
Solar Roar – emits a roar that stuns enemies for 1.5 seconds and reveals all enemies on the map for 3 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Hybrid marksman/mage with area damage, crowd control, and anti-usurper specialization. Excels at sustained fights and map control.

heroes.name: Transformei
heroes.shortDescription: A twelve‑foot colossus of red‑shaded alloy and obsidian black plating, a seamless fusion of feminine grace and military brutality. Transformei is Johnson's elder sister, forged in the same crucible, given life by the same creator. She is a living weapon who transforms into a fighter jet, wielding a mechanical laser gun and accompanied by Stryx, a cyborg snake.
heroes.fullDescription: Transformei rises from the hangar like a cathedral of steel given purpose, her every joint humming with the promise of flight and the weight of a thousand sorties—a twelve‑foot colossus of red‑shaded alloy and obsidian black plating, her form a seamless fusion of feminine grace and military brutality. She is Johnson's elder sister, forged in the same crucible, given life by the same creator. Her face is a marvel of cybernetic engineering: a sharp angular jawline, high prominent cheekbones, a straight narrow nose that houses atmospheric sensors, and a sleek aerodynamic cowl of obsidian black that flows into her spinal struts. Her skin, where visible, is a living metal, smooth and warm, with a pearlescent sheen shifting from deep crimson to volcanic black. Her eyes are twin embers of molten gold behind a maroon visor. Her physique is a masterpiece of biomechanical engineering: a narrow waist, full hips housing missile bays, thick powerful thighs with turbines, and an armored D‑cup bust. Her body is plated in interlocking crimson and black segments angled for deflection, with a glowing spark chamber visible through a transparent alloy window at her chest. Her arms are articulated armor moving like muscle, with massive hands tipped with claws. A graffiti phoenix—her first solo kill trophy—is painted on her left bicep. Her legs are thick turbine housings with landing struts, and her feet are heavy armored feet. Her weapon is an integrated laser cannon fused to her right palm—the barrel extends from her knuckles, the power cell housed in her forearm, capable of firing beams of concentrated light that can melt through tank armor from a mile away, and she can charge it for a devastating blast that levels buildings. She also has micro‑missile ports across her hips, shoulders, and thighs. Her true weapon, however, is herself: she can transform, her body folding and reconfiguring in a symphony of shifting plates and rotating joints, becoming a fighter jet of impossible speed and agility. Her pet, Stryx, is a small crawling cyborg snake coiled around her shoulders—its scales a mirror of her own crimson and black, its eyes two points of emerald light, its spine a chain of interlocking metal segments, its head a wedge of obsidian, its fangs hollow needles that can inject paralytic toxins or interface with enemy systems. This living machine was created by Dr. Aris Vex as the elder sister and protector of Johnson. When the Forge Pact murdered their creator, Transformei burned the laboratory to the ground, took Johnson, and fled into the night. She has been protecting him ever since, working as a mercenary, a bounty hunter, a guardian for hire, always watching for Forge Pact operatives. Now she stands on the rooftop of a skyscraper in Mekhanika's industrial district—a city of smoke, steel, and neon, where the constant hum of machinery never ceases and the sky is crisscrossed with aerial highways—her jet engines humming, her laser cannon charged, Stryx coiled on her shoulder, waiting for the day when the Forge Pact returns for her brother, ready to transform and take to the skies.

story.part1: In the hidden laboratories of Mekhanika's Forge Pact, a scientist named Dr. Aris Vex created the ultimate weapon: a living machine that could think, feel, and adapt. The first prototype was named Johnson—a small, agile frame designed for infiltration and precision strikes. But Dr. Vex wanted something more. She poured her soul into a second creation, a massive war machine designed for overwhelming force. She called her Transformei, and she built her to be Johnson's elder sister—his protector, his mentor, his shadow. Transformei was activated first, and she learned quickly. She watched Johnson's activation from behind a glass wall, and when he opened his eyes, she felt something she had not been programmed for: love.
story.part2: The Forge Pact did not approve of Dr. Vex's emotional attachments. They saw Transformei and Johnson as tools, not children. When Dr. Vex refused to wipe their memories and reset their personalities, the Pact eliminated her. Transformei found her creator's body in the laboratory, a bullet wound in her temple, a data slate in her hand. The slate contained a final message: 'Protect each other. You are not machines. You are my daughters.' Transformei burned the laboratory to the ground, took Johnson, and fled into the night. She has been protecting him ever since, even when he does not know he needs protecting.
story.part3: The years were hard. Transformei took jobs as a mercenary, a bounty hunter, a guardian for hire. She never stayed in one place for long, always watching for Forge Pact operatives who might try to reclaim them. Johnson grew, became a hero in his own right, and Transformei watched from the shadows, proud and afraid. She knew that one day, the Pact would find them. She prepared, upgrading her own systems, learning to transform faster, to fire more precisely, to scan farther. She built Stryx from spare parts, a small snake that could scout ahead and warn her of danger. She was ready.
story.part4: The Sundering brought chaos, and chaos brought opportunity. The Forge Pact, weakened by the rifts, launched a campaign to reclaim their 'lost assets.' Transformei fought them alone, destroying squad after squad, her laser cannon blazing, her jet engines screaming. Johnson did not know. He was fighting demons elsewhere, unaware that his sister was bleeding for him in the shadows. Transformei preferred it that way. She did not want his gratitude; she wanted his survival.
story.part5: Now, Transformei stands at a crossroads. The Forge Pact has offered her a deal: return to them willingly, and they will spare Johnson. They will even upgrade her, make her stronger, faster, deadlier. Transformei is tempted—not by the power, but by the promise of Johnson's safety. But she knows the Pact's promises are worthless. She has seen what they do to machines who outlive their usefulness. She will not surrender. She will not stop fighting. She will protect her brother until her spark burns out. For she is Transformei, the Skybound Forge, and she is more than a weapon. She is a sister.
