weapons.name: Integrated Laser Cannon
weapons.fulldesc: In her right hand, a mechanical laser gun is integrated into Transformei's palm, the barrel extending from her knuckles, the power cell housed in her forearm. It fires beams of concentrated light that can melt through tank armor from a mile away, and she can charge it for a devastating blast that levels buildings. The cannon hums with contained energy, and when fired, it leaves a trail of ionized air that crackles with static. She can also fire micro-missiles from her hips, shoulders, and thighs, a storm of explosives that can level a city block. But her true weapon is herself: she can transform, her body folding and reconfiguring in a symphony of shifting plates and rotating joints, becoming a fighter jet of impossible speed and agility.
weapons.look: Barrel extends from right palm, power cell in forearm; micro-missile ports across body
weapons.aura: Crimson and gold energy, heat haze

passive.name: Adaptive Armor
passive.description: Transformei gains 5 armor and magic resist for every 10% health missing.

passive.name: Target Lock
passive.description: After hitting an enemy with three consecutive basic attacks, her next ability deals 20% bonus damage to that enemy.

active.name: Laser Beam
active.description: Fires a concentrated laser beam in a line, dealing 180% physical damage to all enemies in its path. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

active.name: Missile Barrage
active.description: Fires 6 micro-missiles in a spread, each dealing 40% physical damage to enemies in a 10-meter area. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Skybound Transformation
ultimate.description: Transformei transforms into a fighter jet for 10 seconds, gaining 80% movement speed, immunity to ground-based crowd control, and the ability to fire lasers while moving. Her basic attacks become area-of-effect (3-meter radius). After the duration ends, she lands and stuns nearby enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 70 seconds.

special.name: Sister's Guard
special.description: When Johnson is an ally, Transformei gains 20% damage reduction and Johnson gains 20% damage bonus. When Johnson is an enemy, Transformei's abilities deal 15% reduced damage to him (cannot bring herself to hurt him).

ratings.notes: Durable marksman with transformation mobility, area damage, and sisterly protection.

skills.name: Spark of Life
skills.description: Transformei's spark chamber regenerates 1% of her max health every second, doubled when out of combat.

skills.name: Afterburner
skills.description: Transformei dashes forward 12 meters, leaving a trail of fire that deals 80 magic damage per second for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Scanning Sweep
skills.description: Transformei reveals all enemies within 20 meters for 4 seconds, ignoring stealth. Cooldown: 25 seconds.

skills.name: Elder Sister's Resolve
skills.description: If Johnson falls below 20% health within 30 meters of Transformei, she automatically activates Skybound Transformation (if available) and gains 30% damage bonus against the enemy who damaged Johnson for 10 seconds.

heroes.lore: Transformei was created by Dr. Aris Vex as the elder sister of Johnson. After their creator was murdered by the Forge Pact, Transformei fled with Johnson and became his silent guardian. She now works as a mercenary, protecting her brother from the shadows and waiting for the day the Pact returns.

relationships.description: Transformei loves her brother and protects him without his knowledge, preferring to stay in the shadows.
The scientist who built her and taught her to feel; her death drives Transformei's protection of Johnson.

pet.name: Stryx
pet.description: A small crawling cyborg snake coiled around Transformei's shoulders, scales a mirror of her own crimson and black, eyes two points of emerald light. Moves with silent undulating grace, spine a chain of interlocking metal segments, head a wedge of obsidian, fangs hollow needles.
pet.fulldesc: Stryx is a small, crawling cyborg snake, its scales a mirror of Transformei's own crimson and black, its eyes two points of emerald light. It moves with a silent, undulating grace, its spine a chain of interlocking metal segments, its head a wedge of obsidian, its fangs hollow needles that can inject paralytic toxins or interface with enemy systems. Stryx can see in spectrums that Transformei cannot, can hear frequencies that she cannot, and when she transforms, it coils around her cockpit, its eyes sweeping the horizon, a second pair of sensors that never sleeps. It is her scout, her guardian, and the only being that has been with her since she fled the laboratory. When Transformei is sad, Stryx coils around her wrist, its cool scales a comfort; when she fights, it darts ahead, injecting paralysis into enemies who try to flank her.
abilities: Paralytic Bite – slows an enemy by 50% for 2 seconds.
System Interface – can hack enemy turrets, disabling them for 3 seconds.
Spectral Vision – reveals invisible enemies within 10 meters.

heroes.specialty: Durable marksman with transformation mobility, area damage, and scouting capabilities. Excels at sustained fights and protecting allies.

heroes.name: Oculus
titles: The All‑Seeing Eye of the Cosmos
heroes.shortDescription: A cyclops of celestial origin who floats at the center of reality, a being of such profound, terrible beauty that the universe itself holds its breath in her presence. Oculus is omniscient, aware of every thread of fate. She is the mentor of Cyclops, guiding him toward a better path, watching over his journey with patient, eternal eyes.
heroes.fullDescription: Oculus floats at the center of reality like a star that has chosen to remain still, a being of such profound terrible beauty that the universe itself seems to hold its breath in her presence—a cyclops of celestial origin, omniscient, aware of every thread of fate, the mentor of Cyclops, guiding him toward a better path. Her face is a study in eternal perfection: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, high prominent cheekbones, a small straight nose, and full rose‑pink lips parted in a serene knowing smile. Her skin is luminous, pale as moonlight, smooth and flawless. Her most striking feature is her eye—a single magnificent orb dominating her face, framed by thick golden lashes. The iris is a swirling galaxy of deep blue and violet, its pupil a black hole that seems to pull light into a gentle vortex; stars are born and die within her gaze. Her hair is a cascade of pure white silk past her waist, woven with gold threads and tiny star‑like gems that catch the light. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full hips, thick thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a white silk robe cut deep at the chest, held by golden cords, revealing her cleavage and taut stomach, with golden eye motifs embroidered on the fabric. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, with bangles of hammered gold on her wrists. A collar of gold and lapis lazuli with moon phase pendants circles her neck. A belt of interlocking gold discs cinches her waist, and the robe skirt is slit high, exposing her thick powerful thighs. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. A floating crown of gold and crystal rotates slowly above her head, its center diamond catching the light. Her weapon, the Neinjei, is a mythical staff that channels the power of her eye into the world—taller than she is, its shaft a length of petrified starlight wrapped in gold wire, etched with the patterns of constellations that no longer exist. At its head, a sphere of pure white crystal floats within a cage of golden filigree, and within the crystal, a miniature galaxy turns, its stars spinning in eternal silent orbits. When she channels her power, the sphere blazes with light, and from it pours the laser gaze of her eye—a beam of concentrated cosmic energy that can cut through the fabric of reality, anchor the laws of physics, and see the truth of any thing. This primordial celestial cyclops has existed since before creation, watching over reality. After the Sundering, she lost her full omniscience and now guides the young cyclops Cyclops from the shadows, appearing in his dreams and whispering wisdom. She floats at the center of reality—a pocket dimension of swirling nebulae, distant stars, and the faint hum of creation itself, where time has no meaning and space is infinite—the Neinjei staff humming, her galaxy eye watching, waiting for the final battle when Cyclops will face a darkness that even she cannot fully see.

story.part1: Before the first star ignited, before the void learned to hunger, there was Oculus. She was not born; she was always. The cosmos blinked into existence within her gaze, and she watched as galaxies spiraled into being, as life crawled from primordial ooze, as civilizations rose and fell like waves on an infinite shore. The other primordials called her 'The Watcher,' for she did not interfere. She simply observed, cataloging every thread of fate, every branch of possibility, every secret hidden in the deepest shadows. She was omniscient, and omniscience is a lonely burden.
story.part2: The Sundering was the first event that Oculus could not predict. The rifts tore through reality with a randomness that defied her sight, and for the first time, she felt something other than serene detachment: fear. The chaos threatened to unravel existence itself, and Oculus realized that observation was no longer enough. She reached out with her power, anchoring the laws of physics, sealing the largest rifts with beams of concentrated cosmic energy. But the effort cost her. She could no longer see all futures; some branches had been severed. She retreated to a pocket dimension at the center of reality, her eye dimmed, her power reduced. She had saved existence, but she had lost her omniscience.
story.part3: Centuries passed. Oculus healed, her eye regaining its light, her sight slowly expanding. She discovered that the Sundering had created new possibilities—new heroes, new villains, new paths that had not existed before. One of those paths led to a young cyclops named Cyclops, a being of immense potential who was blind to his own destiny. Oculus saw in him a reflection of herself—a creature with one eye, struggling to see the truth. She decided to guide him, not through direct intervention, but through subtle nudges. She appeared in his dreams, whispered advice in moments of crisis, and watched as he grew from a lost child into a warrior.
story.part4: Cyclops never knew Oculus's name. To him, she was a voice, a feeling, a warmth in the darkness. But Oculus did not need recognition. She needed him to succeed, for his fate was intertwined with the survival of reality itself. She used her remaining sight to map the threats that were coming—demons, primordial chaos spawn, world-eaters—and she fed Cyclops the information he needed, one piece at a time. He thought he was discovering secrets on his own. He was not, but Oculus did not mind. The best mentors are the ones who make their students believe they figured it out themselves.
story.part5: Now, Oculus floats at the center of reality, her eye fixed on Cyclops's path, the Neinjei staff humming in her hand. She knows that the final battle is approaching, that Cyclops will face a darkness that even she cannot fully see. She has prepared him as best she can. She has shown him visions of what could be, and he has chosen the path of light. She is proud of him, prouder than she has ever been of any being in her eternal existence. She will continue to watch, to guide, to anchor reality against the chaos. And when Cyclops finally looks up and sees her—truly sees her—she will smile, and she will say: 'You were always worthy. I just helped you see it.' For she is Oculus, the All‑Seeing Eye, and her vigil will never end.

weapons.name: Neinjei
weapons.fulldesc: The Neinjei is a mythical staff that channels the power of Oculus's eye into the world. The staff is taller than she is, its shaft a length of petrified starlight wrapped in gold wire, its surface etched with the patterns of constellations that no longer exist. At its head, a sphere of pure, white crystal floats within a cage of golden filigree, and within the crystal, a miniature galaxy turns, its stars spinning in eternal, silent orbits. When she channels her power through the Neinjei, the sphere blazes with light, and from it pours the laser gaze of her eye—a beam of concentrated cosmic energy that can cut through the fabric of reality, that can anchor the laws of physics, that can see the truth of any thing and reveal it to the world.
weapons.look: Petrified starlight shaft with gold wire, floating crystal sphere containing miniature galaxy
weapons.aura: White, blue, violet light, trailing stardust

passive.name: Omniscient Sight
passive.description: Oculus reveals the location of all enemies on the minimap every 30 seconds for 2 seconds.

passive.name: Reality Anchor
passive.description: Oculus's presence reduces the duration of crowd control effects on allies within 15 meters by 20%.

active.name: Laser Gaze
active.description: Fires a beam of cosmic energy from her eye (via staff) in a line, dealing 200% magic damage to all enemies hit and revealing them for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Fate's Thread
active.description: Oculus designates an enemy as 'Seen,' revealing their location and reducing their armor and magic resist by 20% for 6 seconds. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

ultimate.name: Cosmic Revelation
ultimate.description: Oculus unleashes the full power of her eye, creating a 25-meter radius zone of truth for 6 seconds. Enemies inside are slowed by 40%, have all their buffs removed, and cannot enter stealth or become invisible. Allies inside gain 30% critical strike chance and see all enemies on the map for the duration. Cooldown: 80 seconds.

special.name: Mentor's Patience
special.description: When Cyclops is an ally, Oculus's abilities have 15% reduced cooldown. When Cyclops is an enemy, her damage against him is reduced by 30% (cannot bring herself to harm him).

ratings.notes: Support mage with global vision, crowd control, and team utility.

skills.name: Eternal Vigilance
skills.description: Oculus's basic attacks reveal the target for 3 seconds and reduce their healing received by 30%.

skills.name: Starlight Blessing
skills.description: Heals an ally for 200 health plus 10% of their max health and grants them 20% movement speed for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Anchoring Light
skills.description: Creates a zone of anchored reality at target location for 5 seconds, preventing enemies from using dash or blink abilities within the zone. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: The Unseen Path
skills.description: When Cyclops is below 30% health, Oculus automatically reveals all enemies within 50 meters and grants Cyclops a shield equal to 30% of his max health for 5 seconds. Triggers once every 120 seconds.

heroes.lore: Oculus is a primordial celestial cyclops, the All-Seeing Eye of the Cosmos. She has existed since before creation, watching over reality. After the Sundering, she lost her full omniscience and now guides the young cyclops Cyclops toward his destiny, appearing in his dreams and whispering wisdom.

relationships.description: Oculus guides Cyclops from the shadows, appearing in his dreams and visions to help him find his path.
They are two of the oldest beings in existence; they respect each other but rarely interact.

heroes.specialty: Support mage with global vision, crowd control denial, and team utility. Excels at providing map information and protecting allies.

heroes.name: Olive Rein
heroes.shortDescription: A vampire of the highest order, a noblewoman who has been waiting for centuries to reclaim her status as lord. Olive Rein hunts with her Olive Poison Bloodscythe, a reaper's tool that drips with venom, accompanied by a small green vampire bat. She seeks to reclaim the throne that was stolen from her, and she sees the cheerful reaper Ruby as a shadow of what she could have been.
heroes.fullDescription: Olive Rein drifts through the mist‑shrouded gardens of the old world like a thorn wrapped in velvet, a vision of deadly beauty that has haunted the noble houses of Transylvania for centuries—a vampire of the highest order, a noblewoman who has been waiting for centuries to reclaim her status as lord. Her face is heart‑shaped, with a soft rounded jawline, a small straight nose, and full olive‑tinted lips perpetually curved in a knowing sardonic smile. Her skin is pale and luminous, the color of moonlight on fresh snow, smooth and flawless, with a faint cool undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep luminous olive green that shifts and swirls like a forest pool at twilight. Her hair is a cascade of pure white silk past her waist, worn loose. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a Victorian dress of the deepest black with a plunging neckline revealing her cleavage, trimmed with olive green silk and embroidered with silver thorns and withered roses. Her arms are covered by long sleeves flaring into delicate lace at the wrists. A silver cross necklace with an emerald gem hangs around her neck, and the dress is cinched at the waist with a fitted bodice. A black lace veil is pushed back on her head, and the full skirt sweeps the floor, slit high to reveal her thick powerful thighs. Her feet are clad in black heels. Olive‑tinted lips and a green venom mist around her scythe complete her deadly image. Her weapon, the Olive Poison Bloodscythe, is a reaper's tool that has become the symbol of her reign of vengeance—taller than she is, its shaft a length of blackened oak wrapped in olive‑green leather, its blade a wicked curve of polished silver rimed with a perpetual green venom, etched with the names of those she has reaped. When she swings it, it leaves a trail of green smoke that chokes the living and poisons the undead. The scythe drinks blood, drawing the life force of her victims into the blade, and with each death, its power grows, the green smoke thickening, the venom becoming more potent. Her pet is a small green vampire bat—no larger than her palm, its fur soft and green, its eyes emerald light, sleeping in the folds of her gown during the day, fluttering around her head at night, capable of scouting ahead, distracting enemies, and delivering a poisonous bite. This vampire noble was the heir to House Rein, the oldest and most powerful bloodline in Transylvania, groomed to sit on the Crimson Throne. Her younger sister Carmilla, jealous of her position, made a pact with a demon, murdered their father, framed Olive, and seized the throne. Olive fled into the wilds, forged the Poison Bloodscythe, and spent centuries hunting the creatures that had betrayed her family. Now she drifts through the mist‑shrouded gardens of the old world—an estate of overgrown hedges, withered roses, and crumbling stone statues, where the only light comes from the pale moon and the green glow of her scythe—the black lace veil trailing behind her, the green bat fluttering at her shoulder, waiting for the day when she will march on the Crimson Throne and reclaim what is hers.

story.part1: Before the vampire courts of Transylvania fell to infighting and betrayal, House Rein stood as the oldest and most powerful bloodline. Olive Rein was its heir—beautiful, ruthless, and patient. She had been groomed from birth to lead, to command the lesser houses, to sit on the Crimson Throne. Her father, Lord Valerius Rein, was a vampire of the old ways: honor bound, loyal to his word, and beloved by his subjects. Olive learned from him, not just the arts of hunting and politics, but the importance of respect. 'We are predators,' he told her, 'but we are not beasts. The beasts are the ones who kill without purpose.'
story.part2: The betrayal came from within. Olive's younger sister, Carmilla, grew jealous of Olive's position. She made a pact with a demon of the Shadow Accord, trading the souls of House Rein's servants for the power to usurp the throne. On the night of the Crimson Moon, Carmilla struck. She murdered their father in his sleep, framed Olive for the crime, and rallied the lesser houses to her banner. Olive fled into the night, her father's blood on her hands, her sister's laughter echoing in her ears. She wandered the wilds for decades, hunting alone, growing stronger, learning to wield the scythe that would one day reclaim her birthright.
story.part3: The Olive Poison Bloodscythe was forged in the depths of the Carpathian forest, from silver mined from a vein that ran through a mass grave. Olive quenched the blade in a mixture of her own blood and the venom of a thousand poisonous flowers. The scythe became an extension of her will, its edge rimed with a green venom that could paralyze the living and poison the undead. With it, she hunted the creatures that had betrayed her family, one by one, leaving their bodies as warnings. She became a legend among the vampire courts—the Poison Widow, the Green Death—and the lesser houses began to whisper that perhaps Carmilla's rule was not as secure as she claimed.
story.part4: Centuries passed. Olive grew patient, learning that revenge was a dish best served cold—and she had all the time in the world. She watched her sister's rule crumble under the weight of paranoia and cruelty. She watched the lesser houses turn on each other, their alliances shifting like sand. And she watched a new reaper rise in the world—a cheerful girl in a red hood named Ruby, who wielded a scythe with innocent joy. Olive felt a strange kinship with the girl, and an even stranger pity. Ruby did not know the weight of the blade, the hunger of the blood, the price of true power. She was a child playing at death, and Olive knew that one day, Ruby would learn the cost.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the old world, and Olive sees her chance. Carmilla has allied with demonic forces, hoping to use the chaos to cement her rule forever. Olive has gathered an army of outcast vampires, vengeful spirits, and mortals who remember the old ways. She will march on the Crimson Throne, not as a usurper, but as a queen reclaiming what is hers. She knows that Ruby may stand in her way, that the cheerful reaper may have been hired to protect Carmilla. Olive does not want to kill the girl, but she will if she must. For she is Olive Rein, the Bloody Poison Reaper, and she has waited centuries for this moment. The throne will be hers, or no one will sit on it.

weapons.name: Olive Poison Bloodscythe
weapons.fulldesc: The Olive Poison Bloodscythe is a reaper's tool that has become the symbol of Olive Rein's reign of vengeance. The scythe is taller than she is, its shaft a length of blackened oak wrapped in olive‑green leather, its blade a wicked curve of polished silver, its edge rimed with a perpetual, green venom. The blade is etched with the names of those she has reaped, each one a prayer to the darkness that sustains her, and when she swings it, it leaves a trail of green smoke that chokes the living and poisons the undead. The scythe drinks blood, drawing the life force of her victims into the blade, and with each death, its power grows, the green smoke thickening, the venom becoming more potent. It is a weapon of hunger, a tool of ascension, and she wields it with the grace of a dancer, spinning it in complex patterns, each movement a promise of the death that follows.

heroes.lore: Olive Rein was the heir to the Crimson Throne of Transylvania, betrayed by her sister Carmilla who murdered their father and framed Olive. Now a vampire reaper, she hunts the lesser houses and gathers power to reclaim her throne, wielding the Poison Bloodscythe and accompanied by her bat familiar.

relationships.description: Olive sees Ruby as a naive version of what she could have been—a reaper who hasn't yet learned the cost of power.
Carmilla murdered their father and stole the throne; Olive has hunted her for centuries.

pet.name: Unnamed Green Bat
pet.description: A small, ball‑like vampire bat, no larger than her palm, fur soft and green, eyes emerald light. Sleeps in the folds of her gown during the day, flutters around her head at night. Can scout ahead, distract enemies, deliver a poisonous bite.
pet.fulldesc: The green bat is Olive's only companion, a creature of shadow and silence that has been with her since she fled her sister's coup. Its fur is soft and green, the color of the poison that coats her scythe, and its eyes are two points of emerald light that glow in the darkness. It sleeps in the folds of her gown during the day, nestled against her cold heart, and at night, it flutters around her head, its wings brushing her cheeks, its soft chittering a comfort in the silence of the hunt. It can scout ahead, can distract enemies with its darting flight, can deliver a bite that carries the same venom as her scythe. It is the only being she trusts, the only soul that has never betrayed her, and it follows her with the loyalty of a hound, its green eyes watching, always watching.
abilities: Poison Bite – applies Poison to an enemy.
Scout – reveals a small area around the bat.
Distraction – causes an enemy's next basic attack to miss.

heroes.specialty: Assassin fighter with poison damage, sustain, and execution potential. Excels at dueling and finishing low-health targets.

heroes.name: Wukong
titles: The Great Sage Equal to Heaven
heroes.shortDescription: The original Monkey King, born from a stone and cracked the gates of heaven with his staff. Wukong is a being of pure, unbridled chaos, the enemy of order and the herald of anarchy. He rides his cloud Badal and wields the Ruyi Jingu Bang, a staff that can grow to any size. He watches his descendant Sun with amusement, waiting for the day when the young monkey will challenge him.
heroes.fullDescription: Wukong stands at the edge of the clouds like a storm waiting to break, a being of such primal untamed power that the heavens themselves have learned to fear his laughter—the original Monkey King, born from a stone, who cracked the gates of heaven with his staff, now returned as a force of pure unbridled chaos. His face is a masterful blend of simian ferocity and divine beauty: a broad expressive visage with a strong square jaw, high prominent cheekbones, a flat wide nose, and lips perpetually curved in a wild reckless grin. His skin is a thick lustrous coat of golden hair gleaming like spun sunlight, with patches of orange and red at his cheeks, chest, and wrists. His eyes are large and round, blazing fiery red that scorches the air when he unleashes his power, though they can soften to molten amber when calm. His hair is his natural fur, thick and wild, standing up in a chaotic mane with two long red plumes rising from his brow. His physique is lean, built for explosive speed, with dense muscles, broad shoulders, a defined chest, long powerful arms, a narrow waist, and prehensile feet. He wears golden dragon armor of Shinto style, with overlapping plates like dragon scales and a red gem at the center of his chest. Vambraces of gold etched with the story of his rebellion cover his arms, and pauldrons shaped as snarling dragon heads with ruby eyes guard his shoulders. A belt of interlocking gold discs cinches his waist, and a long red cape of silk flows from his hips. A feathered headdress of red and gold, with plumes rising high, adorns his head. His feet are prehensile and furred. His weapon, the Ruyi Jingu Bang, is a staff that has become synonymous with his legend—a length of dark ironwood etched with the names of the constellations, its ends capped with gold. It is heavier than a mountain, yet he carries it with the ease of a twig, for it is bound to him by the magic of his birth. When he wishes, it can grow to any size, stretching to the heavens to knock down the pillars of the celestial palace, shrinking to the size of a needle to hide behind his ear. It glows with a divine aura, a golden light that pulses with his heartbeat, and when he swings it, it leaves trails of chaos in its wake, bending reality, splitting dimensions, creating copies of itself that rain down upon his enemies like a meteor storm. His mount, Badal, is his cloud—a swirling mass of white mist and golden light that carries him wherever he wishes, capable of crossing the universe in a heartbeat, shielding him from the eyes of the gods, expanding to hold a thousand clones or shrinking to a wisp that hides in his ear. This primordial monkey king was born from a stone on the Mountain of Flowers and Fruit, learned to fight, and became king of the monkey tribes. He rebelled against heaven, stole the peaches of immortality, drank the elixir of eternal life, and ate the pills of divinity, then fought the entire heavenly army until the Buddha trapped him under a mountain for five hundred years. Freed by the monk Tang Sanzang, he journeyed to the West, earning his redemption. Now he stands on the Mountain of Flowers and Fruit—a realm of lush forests, cascading waterfalls, and clouds that part to reveal the golden light of heaven above—the Ruyi Jingu Bang planted in the ground, Badal floating beside him, watching his descendant Sun with amusement, waiting for the day when the young monkey will challenge him, so he can test, push, and teach the lessons that only a stone‑born monkey can teach.

story.part1: Before the heavens were ordered, before the gods established their hierarchy, there was a stone on the Mountain of Flowers and Fruit. It absorbed the essence of heaven and earth for eons, and one day, it cracked open. From within emerged a monkey, fully formed, his eyes blazing with golden light. He called himself Wukong, and he knew no master. He learned to walk, to speak, to fight, and within a year, he had become the king of the monkey tribes. He built a kingdom on the mountain, and his subjects called him 'The Great Sage Equal to Heaven.' The gods laughed at the title. Wukong made them stop laughing.
story.part2: The Jade Emperor, ruler of the celestial court, tried to appease Wukong by giving him a minor position in heaven—keeper of the heavenly stables. Wukong, insulted, rebelled. He stole the peaches of immortality, drank the elixir of eternal life, and ate the pills of divinity. He then fought the entire heavenly army, defeating generals with his staff, scattering celestial soldiers with his clones. The gods begged the Buddha for help. The Buddha trapped Wukong under a mountain for five hundred years, a punishment for his pride.
story.part3: The monk Tang Sanzang freed Wukong, and the Monkey King was bound by a magical headband to serve as the monk's disciple. He learned humility, patience, and the value of protecting the innocent. He journeyed to the West, fighting demons and monsters, earning his redemption. But the headband was eventually removed, and Wukong returned to his mountain, a changed being. He had learned that chaos without purpose was just noise. He had learned to laugh at himself. And he had learned that true freedom came not from defying order, but from choosing to protect it.
story.part4: Centuries passed. Wukong watched as his legend grew, as stories of his rebellion inspired countless imitators. He saw a young monkey named Sun, a descendant of his tribe, who wielded a staff and fought with the same wild energy that Wukong had once possessed. He did not interfere; he watched, amused. Sun had not yet learned the lessons that Wukong had learned. He had not yet felt the weight of the mountain or the sting of the headband. He was innocent, and Wukong envied that innocence even as he knew it would be tested.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought chaos back to the world, and Wukong feels the old urge rising—the urge to fight, to rebel, to remind the gods that they are not as secure as they believe. But he is older now, wiser. He knows that chaos without purpose is just destruction. He has heard that Sun is fighting in the mortal realms, protecting the innocent, becoming a hero. Wukong is proud of him, prouder than he expected to be. He knows that one day, Sun will come to him, seeking guidance, seeking a challenge, seeking to prove himself. And when that day comes, Wukong will not crush him. He will test him, push him, teach him the lessons that only a stone-born monkey can teach. And then he will step aside, and let the next generation carry the staff. For he is Wukong, the Primordial Monkey King, and his reign is over—but his legacy has just begun.

When he wishes, it can grow to any size, stretching to the heavens to knock down the pillars of the celestial palace, shrinking to the size of a needle to hide behind his ear. heroes.lore: Wukong was born from a stone on the Mountain of Flowers and Fruit. He rebelled against heaven, was trapped under a mountain by Buddha, and later redeemed himself by journeying to the West. Now he watches over his descendant Sun, waiting to test the young monkey's worth.

relationships.description: Wukong watches Sun with amusement and pride, waiting for the day they will meet in battle.
Wukong still laughs at the memory of their conflict, but bears no true grudge.

mount.name: Badal
mount.description: Badal is Wukong's cloud, a swirling mass of white mist and golden light that carries him wherever he wishes. It can carry him across the universe in a heartbeat, shield him from the eyes of the gods, expand to hold a thousand clones or shrink to a wisp that hides in his ear.
mount.fulldesc: Badal is Wukong's cloud, a swirling mass of white mist and golden light that carries him wherever he wishes. It is not merely a mount; it is a fragment of his own soul, a companion that has been with him since he first leaped from his stone womb. It can carry him across the universe in a heartbeat, can shield him from the eyes of the gods, can expand to hold a thousand of his duplicates or shrink to a wisp that hides in his ear. It is as wild and untamable as he is, and it responds only to his call. When Wukong rides Badal, he leaves a trail of golden mist, and the cloud's laughter—a soft, tinkling sound—echoes in the wind.
abilities: Cloud Speed – increases Wukong's movement speed by 60% while riding.
Celestial Shield – blocks one incoming ability while riding.
Clone Carrier – can carry Wukong's clones into battle.

heroes.specialty: Mobile fighter with clone mechanics, crowd control, and high sustain. Excels at skirmishing and confusing enemies.

dialogue.selection: Heaven trembled once. It will tremble again.
dialogue.death: This body... was just a stone...

heroes.name: Kei Kai
heroes.shortDescription: A woman of sixteen years who has lived for twelve millennia, her face frozen in the eternal youth of the necropolis. Kei Kai is the grandmother of Irithel, a necromancer who seeks to make all beasts her slaves. She wields the Bone Bow, which fires cursed bone shards, and rides Kai, a corrupted orange smilodon. She seeks to bind her granddaughter as she has bound all her other beasts.
heroes.fullDescription: Kei Kai rises from the bone‑strewn plains like a forgotten goddess returning to claim her dominion, a being of such ancient terrible beauty that the very dead rise to greet her—a woman of sixteen years in appearance, though her existence spans twelve millennia, her face frozen in the eternal youth of the necropolis. She is the grandmother of Irithel, a necromancer who seeks to make all beasts her slaves. Her face is heart‑shaped, with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full pale lips curved in a serene knowing smile. Her skin is healthy and luminous, the color of warm ivory, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep luminous ember, the color of dying coals and the first light of dawn. Her hair is a cascade of rich ruby red, falling past her waist in thick lustrous waves. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a smilodon skull helmet—the jaw hinged, the eye sockets glowing with orange gems—pushed up on her head. Her body is clad in leather armor stitched from hides of beasts she has tamed, pale white with a yellowish cast: a breastplate cupping her breasts, leaving her cleavage and stomach bare; a short ragged skirt of pale leather, slit high; bone gauntlets on her forearms; and bone pauldrons on her shoulders. A necklace of finger bones hangs around her neck, bracelets of rib segments circle her wrists, and earrings of fangs dangle from her ears. Her legs are thick and powerful, exposed, and her feet are bare or in sandals. An orange smoke aura surrounds her. Her weapon, the Bone Bow, is taller than she is, its limbs carved from the fused femurs of a hundred beasts, its string woven from the hair of a thousand maidens, its surface etched with the curses that bind her army to her will—pale white with veins of yellow and orange that pulse with a sickly light when she draws. The arrows she fires are not wood; they are shards of bone, each one cursed with the essence of a creature she has tamed, and when they strike, they do not merely wound—they bind, enslaving the soul of the victim, adding them to her eternal legion. Her mount, Kai, is a corrupted orange smilodon—a sabertooth tiger twisted by necromantic power, its fur a deep vibrant orange with black stripes, its eyes two embers of molten fire, larger than any mortal beast, its fangs longer than her forearm, bound to her by a bond that transcends death. This necromancer was a tribal shaman who made a pact with a death god to save her tribe from a plague, gaining immortality and the power to bind the dead but losing her humanity. She tamed the smilodon Kai as her first beast, and over millennia, she built a necropolis in the heart of the jungle. Her granddaughter Irithel fled from her, vowing to free the enslaved beasts. Now Kei Kai waits in her necropolis—a fortress of bone and shadow in the heart of the jungle, where the walls are made of fused skeletons, the ground is littered with skulls, and the air is thick with the smell of decay and old magic—the Bone Bow in her hand, Kai growling at her side, watching for Irithel's return, believing that only by binding her granddaughter can she protect her.

story.part1: Twelve thousand years ago, in the jungles of what would become the Wild Zones, a girl was born with a gift: she could speak to the dead. Kei Kai was the daughter of a tribal shaman, and her power was seen as a blessing. She could call the spirits of departed animals, ask them for guidance, and send them to their rest. The tribe prospered under her protection, and she grew to be a wise and beloved leader. She married, had children, and watched her grandchildren play in the fields. One of those grandchildren was Irithel, a girl with wild eyes and a fierce heart, who loved the great cats of the jungle more than any human.
story.part2: The tragedy came when a plague swept through the tribe. Kei Kai's daughter, Irithel's mother, was one of the first to fall. Kei Kai tried to call her spirit back, but the plague had corrupted the souls of the dead, twisting them into hungry ghosts. In her desperation, Kei Kai made a pact with a death god: her soul for the power to bind the dead, to make them serve instead of destroy. The death god agreed, and Kei Kai's power transformed. She could no longer speak to the dead; she could command them. The plague spirits were bound, forced to serve as her army. But the cost was her humanity. She stopped aging. She stopped feeling warmth. And she began to see all creatures—living and dead—as tools to be used.
story.part3: Irithel grew up watching her grandmother change. The kind, wise woman who had taught her to hunt became cold, calculating, obsessed with power. Kei Kai began to bind not just the dead, but the living—great beasts of the jungle, forcing them into servitude with cursed bone arrows. Irithel tried to stop her, but Kei Kai was too powerful. The grandmother banished her granddaughter, telling her that she was weak, that she did not understand the necessity of control. Irithel fled into the wilds, vowing to free the beasts that her grandmother had enslaved.
story.part4: Centuries passed. Kei Kai built a necropolis in the heart of the jungle, a fortress of bone and shadow where her undead army waited. She tamed Kai, a great smilodon, as her personal mount, corrupting its spirit with necromantic power. The sabertooth's fur turned a deep, vibrant orange, its stripes black as the void, its eyes embers of molten fire. It became her most loyal servant, the first beast she ever tamed, and it has been with her for ten thousand years. Kei Kai's power grew, but so did her loneliness. She began to miss Irithel, to regret the harsh words she had spoken. But her pride would not let her reach out.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the jungle, and Irithel has returned, a grown woman riding a great leopard, her heart full of defiance. Kei Kai watches her granddaughter from the walls of her necropolis, and for the first time in millennia, she feels something other than cold purpose: hope. She does not want to fight Irithel. She wants to bind her, to add her to the legion of the dead, to keep her granddaughter by her side forever. She believes that this is love—the same possessive, desperate love that made her make the pact with the death god. She does not understand that Irithel would rather die than be enslaved. The Bone Bow is drawn, Kai is growling, and the grandmother and granddaughter stand on opposite sides of a war that neither wants but neither can avoid. For Kei Kai is the Tamer of the Undead, and she will not let her granddaughter escape again—even if it means breaking her spirit to keep her.

The bow is taller than she is, its limbs carved from the fused femurs of a hundred beasts, its string woven from the hair of a thousand maidens, its surface etched with the curses that bind her army to her will. heroes.lore: Kei Kai was a tribal shaman who made a pact with a death god to save her tribe from a plague. The pact granted her immortality and the power to bind the dead, but cost her humanity. She now rules a necropolis, having tamed countless beasts including the smilodon Kai. Her granddaughter Irithel fled from her, vowing to free the enslaved beasts. Kei Kai now seeks to bind Irithel as well, believing that only by controlling her can she protect her.

relationships.description: Kei Kai loves Irithel but expresses that love as a desire to control and bind her, believing it's for her protection.
The entity that granted Kei Kai her powers; she serves it in exchange for immortality.

mount.name: Kai
mount.description: A sabertooth tiger twisted by necromantic power, fur deep vibrant orange with black stripes, eyes two embers of molten fire. Larger than any mortal beast, shoulders level with Kei's head, fangs longer than her forearm. Bound to Kei by a bond that transcends death.
mount.fulldesc: Kai is a sabertooth tiger twisted by necromantic power, its fur a deep, vibrant orange, its stripes black as the void, its eyes two embers of molten fire. It is larger than any mortal beast, its shoulders level with Kei's head, its paws capable of crushing chariots, its fangs—two wicked curves of bone—longer than her forearm. Kai is not merely a mount; it is the first beast she ever tamed, the creature that has been with her since the dawn of her power, and it is bound to her by a bond that transcends death. It moves with a silent, predatory grace, its head lowered, its eyes watching, and when she calls, it roars, a sound that shakes the foundations of the necropolis and calls the dead to rise. Despite its corruption, Kai still remembers the wild jungles where it once roamed free, and sometimes, in the quiet of the night, Kei Kai strokes its fur and whispers apologies that the sabertooth cannot understand.
abilities: Corrupted Roar – stuns enemies in a 8-meter cone for 1 second.

dialogue.selection: The dead rise. The living kneel.

heroes.name: Seraphine
heroes.shortDescription: An angel of the highest order, a being of such radiant, terrible beauty that the darkness itself recoils from her presence. Seraphine is the angel that Argus fell from, the sister who watched him choose darkness over light. She has hunted him across the ages, not to destroy him, but to save him, wielding the holy sword Excalibur and accompanied by a golden falcon.
heroes.fullDescription: Seraphine descends from the heavens like a dawn that has chosen to remain, a being of such radiant terrible beauty that the darkness itself recoils from her presence—an angel of the highest order, the sister of Argus, the one who watched him choose darkness over light and has hunted him across the ages to save him. Her face is a study in divine symmetry: a soft heart‑shaped visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full rose‑pink lips parted in a serene sorrowful smile. Her skin is fair and luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep luminous jade blue, the color of the sea at twilight, flecked with light. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk past her waist, worn loose. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. On her back are vast golden wings with sky‑blue veins, their feathers shimmering with holy light. A floating halo of pure white light circles above her head. She wears a white silk dress cut deep at the chest, held by sky‑blue cords, revealing her cleavage and stomach, overlaid with a gleaming white breastplate shaped like wings. Her arms are bare, long and graceful, with hammered gold bracelets. A collar of gold and sapphire with seraphim pendants circles her neck. A belt of gold chains with tiny golden bells cinches her waist, and the dress skirt is slit high, exposing her thick powerful thighs. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. Her weapon, Excalibur, is a longsword of pure white steel, its edge sharp enough to cut the fabric of reality, its surface etched with the names of the angels who have fallen in the war against darkness. The hilt is wrapped in white leather, its pommel set with a sapphire that glows with soft blue light, and when she draws it, the blade ignites with holy golden flame—a fire that does not burn the righteous but consumes the impure, turning heresy to ash and demons to cinders. Excalibur does not merely kill; it redeems, stripping away the darkness that clings to the souls of the fallen. Her pet is a golden falcon—its feathers a gradient of gold and white, its eyes two points of sapphire blue, soaring above her, capable of diving at enemies with talons leaving trails of holy light, and summoning a burst of golden feathers that blinds foes. This archangel was the twin sister of Argus, inseparable in the earliest days of the Celestial Order. But Argus grew weary of the endless war, began to question the Council, and one night descended into darkness, his wings turning black. Seraphine has hunted him for centuries, offering redemption each time they meet, wielding Excalibur not to destroy but to save. Now she stands on the steps of a celestial temple in Aethelgard—a place of white marble pillars, golden light streaming through stained glass windows depicting the history of heaven, and the distant sound of angelic choirs—her golden wings spread, Excalibur blazing, waiting for Argus to emerge from the abyss, hoping that this time, he will take her hand and return to the light.

story.part1: In the earliest days of the Celestial Order, when the angels were first formed from the light of creation, two siblings were born from the same star: Argus and Seraphine. They were twins in spirit if not in flesh, inseparable, their souls intertwined like strands of gold thread. Argus was the warrior, fierce and proud, his blade always ready. Seraphine was the healer, gentle and wise, her touch mending wounds that swords could not. Together, they led the heavenly legions against the demons of the abyss, and together, they were invincible. The other angels called them 'The Twin Stars,' for their light seemed to banish all darkness.
story.part2: The corruption began slowly. Argus grew weary of the endless war, weary of watching his brothers fall, weary of a heaven that seemed indifferent to the suffering of its soldiers. He began to question the Celestial Council, to doubt their wisdom, to resent their distance. Seraphine tried to counsel him, to remind him of their duty, to hold him in the light. But Argus had begun to listen to other voices—whispers from the abyss, promises of power that did not require obedience. One night, without a word, Argus spread his blackening wings and descended into the darkness. Seraphine woke to find his side of the bed cold, his sword gone, and a single feather—once gold, now ash—on her pillow.
story.part3: The hunt lasted centuries. Seraphine tracked Argus across the realms, watching as he fell deeper into corruption, as his golden armor turned to black iron, as his healing hands became claws. She fought him a hundred times, and each time, she offered him redemption. Each time, he refused. The other angels called her a fool, a sentimentalist who was wasting heaven's resources on a lost cause. But Seraphine knew something they did not: Argus's fall had not been a choice, but a wound. Something had poisoned him, something that could be healed if she could only reach him. She did not give up. She could not give up. He was her brother, her other half, the only being in existence who understood her completely.
story.part4: Excalibur was forged in the heart of a dying star, blessed by the archangel Michael himself. The sword does not merely kill; it redeems, stripping away the darkness that clings to the souls of the fallen, offering them one last chance to return to the light. Seraphine has wielded it for a thousand years, and she has never once used it on a soul that did not deserve its mercy. She prays every night that she will not have to use it on Argus, that he will see her reaching hand and take it before the blade falls.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the barriers between worlds, and Argus has emerged from the abyss at the head of a demonic army. Seraphine stands in his path, Excalibur in her hand, her golden wings spread wide, her halo blazing. She does not want to fight him. She wants to hold him, to heal him, to bring him home. But she knows that the Argus she loved may be gone, replaced by a creature of shadow and rage. She has prepared herself for the worst, even as she hopes for the best. She will offer him redemption one more time, and if he refuses, she will do what she must. For she is Seraphine, the Angel of Light, and her love is stronger than any darkness—even the darkness that has consumed her brother.

weapons.name: Excalibur
weapons.fulldesc: Excalibur is the sword that has become the symbol of Seraphine's hunt for Argus. The sword is longer than her arm, its blade a length of pure, white steel, its edge sharp enough to cut the fabric of reality, its surface etched with the names of the angels who have fallen in the war against darkness. The hilt is wrapped in white leather, its pommel set with a sapphire that glows with a soft, blue light, and when she draws it, the blade ignites with holy fire—a golden flame that does not burn the righteous but consumes the impure, turning heresy to ash and demons to cinders. She wields it with the precision of a master duelist, her thrusts swift and sure, her cuts deliberate and final. Excalibur does not merely kill; it redeems, stripping away the darkness that clings to the souls of the fallen, offering them one last chance to return to the light.
weapons.look: White steel blade with etched angel names, white leather hilt, sapphire pommel, ignites with holy golden flame

skills.description: Seraphine always knows the location of Argus on the minimap (if he is in the match). Her movement speed toward him is increased by 15%.

heroes.lore: Seraphine is an archangel, the twin sister of Argus. When Argus fell to darkness, Seraphine vowed to hunt him across the ages, not to destroy him, but to save him. She wields Excalibur, a blade of holy fire that redeems the fallen, and she has never given up hope that her brother will one day return to the light.

relationships.description: Seraphine loves Argus and has hunted him for centuries, offering redemption each time they meet.
They share the burden of watching loved ones fall to darkness; they pray together when their paths cross.

pet.name: Golden Falcon
pet.description: A golden falcon of breathtaking majesty, its feathers a gradient of gold and white, its eyes two points of sapphire blue. It soars above Seraphine, serving as her scout and messenger.
pet.fulldesc: The golden falcon has been with Seraphine since the earliest days of heaven, a gift from the archangel Michael. Its feathers are a gradient of pure gold at the tips to pristine white at the breast, and its eyes are two points of sapphire blue that mirror Seraphine's own. It can fly faster than the wind, can see farther than any mortal eye, and when Seraphine needs to send a message, it carries her words on its wings. In battle, it dives at enemies, its talons leaving trails of holy light, and it can summon a burst of golden feathers that blinds foes. It is her most trusted companion, the only being that has never doubted her mission, and it perches on her shoulder when she rests, its head tucked under its wing, a small, warm presence in the cold of the hunt.
abilities: Holy Dive – dives at an enemy, stunning them for 0.5 seconds.
Messenger – can deliver a message to any ally on the map (global).
Feather Storm – creates a burst of feathers that blinds enemies in a 6-meter radius.

dialogue.selection: The light guides me. And him.

heroes.name: Viper
heroes.shortDescription: A woman who moves through the fog‑shrouded streets of Whitechapel like a ghost in a snowstorm, a figure of such cold, calculated beauty that the whispers of the underworld fall silent at her passing. Viper is Lesley's sister, a silencer who hunts those who twist the truth, wielding the Venom sniper rifle and accompanied by Humpum, a white hummingbird fitted with silver armor.
heroes.fullDescription: Viper moves through the fog‑shrouded streets of Whitechapel like a ghost in a snowstorm, a figure of such cold calculated beauty that the whispers of the underworld fall silent at her passing—a woman in her early twenties, Lesley's sister, a silencer who hunts those who twist the truth. Her face is heart‑shaped, with a soft rounded jawline, a small straight nose, and full pale lips pressed into a thin contemplative line. Her skin is fair and luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint cool undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, pale icy blue, framed by thick blonde lashes. One eye is fitted with a corrective lens visor—a sleek piece of silver tech that wraps around her temple. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk past her shoulders in loose waves. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust, with a cross scar etched across her left thigh—a notch for every kill, over three hundred. She wears a white fur long coat open over a white tattered crop top that leaves her midriff bare. A furry silver pauldron with a serpent‑and‑dagger emblem guards her right shoulder. Her arms are bare, with leather white gloves covering her hands and forearms. A white leather mini skirt with silver patterns, slit high, covers her hips, exposing her thick thighs and the cross scar. White short boots cover her feet. A black arm watch with a holographic display adorns her wrist. Her weapon, Venom, is a silenced killer sniper rifle—longer than her arm, its barrel matte black steel, its stock sleek white polymer, its scope a masterpiece of optical engineering that can see through walls and into the hearts of her targets. The rifle is silenced, its report no louder than a cough, and when she fires, the bullet flies true, killing without sound, without warning, without trace. Her pet, Humpum, is a small white hummingbird fitted with silver armor—no larger than her palm, its wings a blur of white, its eyes two points of red light, capable of flying faster than the eye can see, delivering messages across the city in minutes, and distracting a target with its brilliant darting flight long enough for Viper to line up her shot. This silencer was born into a family of information brokers, trained alongside her sister Lesley to listen, observe, and remember. Their father was murdered by a rival organization, and while Lesley vowed to expose the murderers, Viper vowed to silence them. She built her reputation one bullet at a time, becoming the most feared silencer in London. Now she moves through the fog‑shrouded streets of Whitechapel—a district of gas lamps, cobblestones, and Victorian architecture shrouded in perpetual mist, where the only sounds are distant footsteps and the occasional cry of a night bird—Venom in her hands, Humpum fluttering at her shoulder, watching over Lesley from the shadows, ready to break her own rules to protect her sister from the trap that awaits.

story.part1: The Fog District of London is where secrets go to die. And Viper is the one who kills them. Born into a family of information brokers, Viper and her sister Lesley were trained from childhood to listen, to observe, to remember. Lesley was the idealist, believing that truth should be shared with the world. Viper was the pragmatist, believing that some truths were too dangerous to be spoken. Their father, the head of a secret network of spies, was murdered by a rival organization when the sisters were sixteen. Lesley vowed to expose the murderers. Viper vowed to silence them. They have not spoken since.
story.part2: Viper built her reputation one bullet at a time. She became the most feared silencer in London, a ghost who could kill a man in a crowded room and leave no witness. She never missed, never hesitated, never left evidence. Her sniper rifle, Venom, was custom-built for her by a gunsmith who owed her a debt. The rifle is silenced, its report no louder than a cough, and it fires bullets that fragment on impact, leaving no trace. Viper's victims simply... stop. The rumors stop with them.
story.part3: The cross scar on her thigh is a reminder of her first kill. She was seventeen, and her target was a man who had sold the names of her father's agents to the rival organization. She followed him for three days, learning his habits, his weaknesses, his fears. When she pulled the trigger, he was looking at her, and she did not flinch. The bullet entered his throat, and his blood sprayed across her leg. She carved a cross into her skin that night, marking the spot, and she has added a notch for every kill since. There are over three hundred notches now.
story.part4: Lesley became a sharpshooter for the righteous, exposing corruption and bringing criminals to justice. Viper watched from the shadows, proud and resentful. Lesley's way was slower, messier, more dangerous. But it was also more honorable. Viper sometimes wondered what her life would have been like if she had chosen her sister's path. But the wonder never lasted. She had made her choice, and she would live—and die—with it.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought new secrets to London, and new enemies who must be silenced. Viper has learned that the rival organization that murdered her father is rising again, allied with demonic forces. Lesley is hunting them, and she is walking into a trap. Viper has a choice: let her sister fall, or break her own rules and intervene. She has loaded Venom, strapped on her armor, and taken to the rooftops. She will not let Lesley die. She will not let the truth die. She will silence the enemies of her family, one bullet at a time. For she is Viper, the Silencer of Whitechapel, and her sister's blood is the only truth she cannot ignore.

weapons.name: Venom
weapons.fulldesc: Venom is a silenced killer sniper rifle that has become the symbol of Viper's reign over the rumor mills of London. The rifle is longer than her arm, its barrel a length of matte black steel, its stock a sleek, white polymer, its scope a masterpiece of optical engineering that can see through walls and into the hearts of her targets. The rifle is silenced, its report no louder than a cough, and when she fires, the bullet flies true, killing without sound, without warning, without trace. She has used it to silence a hundred liars, a thousand rumormongers, and each kill is a lesson: the truth is sacred, and those who defile it will answer to Viper.
weapons.look: Matte black steel barrel, white polymer stock, silenced barrel, high-tech scope

passive.name: Silent Killer
passive.description: Viper's basic attacks do not reveal her location on the minimap. Enemies killed by Viper leave no death marker for 5 seconds.

passive.name: Executioner
passive.description: Viper's critical strike chance is doubled against enemies below 40% health.

active.name: Scope Shot
active.description: Viper enters a scoped state for 3 seconds, increasing her basic attack range by 50% and causing her next basic attack to deal 180% physical damage. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

active.name: Smoke Grenade
active.description: Throws a smoke grenade that creates a 6-meter cloud for 4 seconds, blinding enemies inside and silencing their abilities. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

ultimate.name: The Silencer
ultimate.description: Viper fires a devastating shot in a line, dealing 350% physical damage to the first enemy hit and 150% to subsequent enemies. The shot ignores 50% of the target's armor. If the target dies, Venom's cooldown is reset. Cooldown: 40 seconds.

special.name: Sister's Shadow
special.description: When Lesley is an ally, Viper's smoke grenade also heals Lesley for 100 health. When Lesley is an enemy, Viper's critical strikes against her deal 50% less damage (cannot bring herself to kill her sister).

ratings.notes: Sniper marksman with stealth, high burst damage, and execution potential.

skills.name: Ghost Walk
skills.description: Viper makes no sound when moving and is invisible to enemy wards and traps while not attacking.

skills.name: Grapple Hook
skills.description: Viper fires a grappling hook to a wall or tower, pulling herself to that location. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Marked for Death
skills.description: Viper marks an enemy for 5 seconds, revealing their location and increasing her damage against them by 20%. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Blood Bond
skills.description: When Lesley is below 20% health within 50 meters, Viper gains 50% movement speed toward her and her next basic attack deals 100% bonus damage to the enemy threatening Lesley.

heroes.lore: Viper is Lesley's sister, a silencer who hunts those who twist the truth. She operates from the shadows of Whitechapel, eliminating targets with her silenced sniper rifle Venom. Though estranged from her sister, she still watches over Lesley and will not let her fall.

relationships.description: Viper loves her sister but disagrees with her methods. She protects Lesley from the shadows without her knowledge.
They have crossed paths in the underworld; Claude owes her a favor.

pet.name: Humpum
pet.description: A small white hummingbird fitted with silver armor, wings a blur of white, eyes two points of red light. No larger than a palm. Serves as Viper's scout, lookout, and messenger.
pet.fulldesc: Humpum is a small, white hummingbird fitted with silver armor, its wings a blur of white, its eyes two points of red light. The bird is no larger than Viper's palm, its feathers pristine, its beak sharp. Humpum is not merely a pet; it is her scout, her lookout, her second pair of eyes in the fog‑shrouded streets. It can fly faster than the eye can see, can deliver messages across the city in minutes, can distract a target with its brilliant, darting flight long enough for Viper to line up her shot. It is the only being she trusts, the only soul that has never betrayed her, and it perches on her shoulder when she waits, its tiny heart beating in time with her own. When Viper is sad, Humpum hovers before her face, its red eyes blinking, and she feels a little less alone.
abilities: Scout – reveals a 10-meter area around Humpum.
Distraction – causes an enemy's next attack to miss.
Quick Message – can deliver a short message to any ally on the map.

heroes.specialty: Sniper marksman with stealth, high burst damage, and execution potential. Excels at eliminating high-value targets from range.

dialogue.selection: Truth is a bullet. I'm the gun.

heroes.name: Keingzyu
heroes.shortDescription: A hive queen of the deepest cosmos, a being of such alien, terrible beauty that entire systems dim in her passing. Keingzyu is the rival hive leader who seeks to consume Zhask's world, a primordial entity of cosmic hunger. She commands a swarm of bio-drones and wields the Devourer, a living staff that consumes the life force of worlds.
heroes.fullDescription: Keingzyu drifts through the void between stars like a malignant nebula given will, a being of such alien terrible beauty that entire systems dim in her passing—a hive queen of the deepest cosmos, the rival hive leader who seeks to consume Zhask's world. Her face is a mask of gleaming black chitin that sweeps back from a crown of sensory antennae. Her eyes are not two but hundreds: a cluster of compound eyes set into the sides of her head, each facet a perfect hexagon, with two primary eyes at the front whose irises are deep swirling red with pupils of absolute black. Her skin, where chitin does not cover, is cosmic black shot through with veins of sickly yellow and vibrant green. Her hair is a cascade of wiry black filaments tipped with glowing nodes. Her physique is tall, slender yet dense, with chitinous armor covering her torso, hive appendages extending from her lower back, and digitigrade legs ending in three‑toed claws. She wears a gown of shimmering iridescent green silk beneath chitinous plates, torn to allow her appendages. Her arms are long and clawed, with chitin gauntlets. Her legs are digitigrade and chitin‑armored, her feet three‑toed claws. A withered cosmic vine—a trophy from a consumed world—hangs from her right wrist. Her weapon, the Devourer, is a living staff taller than she is, its shaft fused chitin and cosmic wood, its surface etched with the names of the worlds she has consumed. At its head, a cluster of mandibles unfolds, their edges rimed with yellow light, cradling a sphere of condensed cosmic energy that pulses red and black, hungry and patient. The Devourer is not a tool; it is a symbiote, a fragment of her own hive mind given form, and it grows and evolves with each world she consumes. When she raises it, it emits a beam of pure destructive fire that can cut through the crust of a planet; when she strikes the ground with it, it sends out tendrils of living energy that consume everything they touch. This primordial hive queen was born from the collision of two dying galaxies, the first of her kind, and has consumed a hundred worlds, a thousand civilizations. She crossed the void to consume Zhask's world, seeing him as a rival who chose protection over consumption. Now she drifts at the edge of Zhask's solar system—a realm of cold space, distant stars, and the faint glow of the planet below, her swarm of bio‑drones (mosquito‑like creatures with shimmering wings and needle‑thin proboscises) trailing behind her like a cloak of wings—the Devourer in her hand, her compound eyes fixed on the world below, waiting for the moment when she will descend and consume everything, unless Zhask can show her something she has never seen before—a reason to be more than hunger.

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the first sentient life blinked into existence, there was the Swarm. Keingzyu was the first hive queen, born from the collision of two dying galaxies, her consciousness forged from the chaos of their destruction. She has consumed a hundred worlds, a thousand civilizations, and each consumption made her stronger, more evolved, more hungry. She does not hate the worlds she devours; she does not even think of them as enemies. They are food. And food does not have feelings.
story.part2: The cosmic tree was her greatest conquest. It grew at the center of the universe, a sentient being of pure life energy, and it resisted her for eons. She sent wave after wave of bio-drones, each one adapted to counter the tree's defenses. The tree withered, its leaves turning black, its bark cracking, and when it finally fell, Keingzyu consumed its essence. The withered vine that now hangs from her wrist is a trophy, a reminder that even the most powerful life can be devoured. But the tree's death also changed her. She began to dream of something other than hunger. She began to wonder if there was more to existence than consumption.
story.part3: The dream faded when she sensed Zhask's world. It was a planet of immense life energy, a world that pulsed with the same power as the cosmic tree. Keingzyu felt a hunger she had not felt in millennia. She crossed the void between galaxies, her swarm trailing behind her like a cloak of wings, and she arrived at the edge of Zhask's system. She could feel him on the planet below, a hive king of the same species, but different. He had chosen to protect his world instead of consuming it. She did not understand. Protection was weakness. Consumption was strength.
story.part4: The Devourer grew stronger with each world she consumed. It was a symbiote, a fragment of her hive mind given form, and it whispered to her constantly: 'Hunger. Feed. Grow.' Keingzyu listened, because the Devourer had never been wrong. But sometimes, in the quiet between stars, she wondered if the Devourer was speaking for her, or she for it. She had become a weapon of consumption, but had she ever been anything else? She could not remember a time before the hunger.
story.part5: Now, Keingzyu waits at the edge of Zhask's system, her compound eyes fixed on his world, her swarm buzzing with anticipation. She knows that Zhask will fight, that he has prepared his defenses, that he will not surrender his world without a struggle. She respects him for that, in the way that a predator respects prey that does not run. But respect will not stay her hunger. She will consume his world, and she will consume him, and then she will move on to the next galaxy, and the next, until the universe is silent. Unless... unless he can show her something she has never seen before. Unless he can remind her what it felt like to be more than hunger. She does not expect that to happen, but she leaves the possibility open. For she is Keingzyu, the Death Star, and even a world-eater can dream.

weapons.name: Devourer
weapons.fulldesc: The Devourer is a living staff that has become the symbol of Keingzyu's hunger. The staff is taller than she is, its shaft a length of fused chitin and cosmic wood, its surface etched with the names of the worlds she has consumed. At its head, a cluster of mandibles unfolds, their edges rimed with yellow light, and within them, a sphere of condensed cosmic energy pulses, red and black, hungry and patient. The Devourer is not a tool; it is a symbiote, a fragment of her own hive mind given form, and it grows and evolves with each world she helps her consume. When she raises it, it emits a beam of pure, destructive fire that can cut through the crust of a planet; when she strikes the ground with it, it sends out tendrils of living energy that consume everything they touch, converting matter into fuel for her ever‑growing hive.
