passive.name: Consumption
passive.description: Keingzyu heals for 10% of damage dealt to enemies below 30% health.

passive.name: Hive Mind
passive.description: Keingzyu gains 5% damage for each active bio-drone near her, up to 30%.

active.name: Devourer Beam
active.description: Fires a beam of cosmic energy in a line, dealing 180% magic damage to all enemies hit and reducing their armor by 20% for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Spawn Drones
active.description: Spawns 3 bio-drones that attack enemies for 10 seconds. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

ultimate.name: World Eater
ultimate.description: Keingzyu channels for 3 seconds, then unleashes a massive wave of consuming energy in a 25-meter radius. Enemies take 400% magic damage and are slowed by 60% for 5 seconds. The ground in the area becomes 'consumed,' dealing 50 magic damage per second for 10 seconds. Cooldown: 100 seconds.

special.name: Rival's Hunger
special.description: When Zhask is an enemy, Keingzyu's damage against his turrets and spawns is increased by 50%. When Zhask is an ally (unlikely), her abilities do not affect his spawns.

ratings.notes: Summoner mage with area damage, sustain, and anti-structure capabilities.

skills.name: Cosmic Hunger
skills.description: Keingzyu gains 1% magic power for every 5% health missing, up to 20%.

skills.name: Swarm Command
skills.description: Commands all bio-drones to attack a target enemy, dealing 50% bonus damage for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Hive Adaptation
skills.description: Keingzyu adapts to an enemy's damage type, gaining 30% resistance to that type for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 20 seconds.

skills.name: Galactic Grudge
skills.description: When facing Zhask, Keingzyu's ultimate deals 20% bonus damage and has 15 seconds reduced cooldown.

heroes.lore: Keingzyu is a primordial hive queen who has consumed a hundred worlds. She has crossed the void to consume Zhask's world, seeing him as a rival who chose protection over consumption. She commands a swarm of bio-drones and wields the Devourer, a living staff of cosmic hunger.

relationships.description: Keingzyu seeks to consume Zhask's world and prove that consumption is superior to protection.
The withered vine on her wrist is a trophy from the cosmic tree she consumed.

heroes.specialty: Summoner mage with area damage, sustain, and anti-structure capabilities. Excels at extended fights and consuming objectives.

heroes.name: Lamia
heroes.shortDescription: An empress of twenty‑eight years, frozen in the eternal prime of a ruler blessed by the light. Lamia rules with wisdom and compassion, her power drawn from the sun itself. She wields the Suneir staff and is accompanied by Gham, a sun‑and‑beach‑colored falcon. She seeks to offer warmth and salvation to all, even her nemesis Vexana, whom she pities rather than hates.
heroes.fullDescription: Lamia rises over the marble spires of her ancient kingdom like the sun breaking free of the horizon, a being of such radiant benevolent beauty that the shadows themselves bow in reverence—an empress of twenty‑eight years, frozen in the eternal prime of a ruler blessed by the light. Her face is a masterwork of Grecian perfection: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full coral‑pink lips perpetually curved in a warm knowing smile. Her skin is luminous, the color of honeyed cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep glowing ember, the color of molten gold and the first light of dawn. Her hair is a cascade of sunfire—a torrent of orange and red past her waist in thick lustrous waves shot through with gold. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a deep liquid gold gown cut deep at the chest, held by golden cords, revealing her cleavage and taut stomach, overlaid with an orange silk empress's robe with white fur lining that flows behind her. Her arms are bare, long and graceful. A collar of gold and amber with sun and moon pendants circles her neck. Golden cords cinch her waist, and the gown skirt flows around her feet. A hammered gold crown with sun rays and olive branches, set with a central orange gem, sits on her head. Gold bangles adorn her wrists, gold earrings dangle from her ears, and gold and ruby rings cover her fingers. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground, and a golden aura surrounds her. Her weapon, the Suneir, is a staff that has become the symbol of her reign—taller than she is, its shaft a length of polished olive wood wrapped in gold wire, carved with the story of her kingdom's founding. At its head, a sphere of pure orange crystal floats within a cage of golden filigree, and within the crystal, a miniature sun turns, its light pulsing with the rhythm of her heartbeat. When she raises the Suneir, the sphere blazes with light, and from it pour rays of warmth that heal the sick, guide the lost, and illuminate the darkest paths. Her pet, Gham, is a sun‑and‑beach‑colored falcon—its feathers a gradient of golden yellow at the breast to deep orange at the wingtips, its eyes two points of molten gold, larger than any mortal bird, capable of flying faster than the wind, seeing farther than any mortal eye, and carrying her decrees on its wings. This light sun empress healed her kingdom with solar magic after a century of war, calling upon the sun for forty days and forty nights to restore the barren fields. Her nemesis is Vexana, a necromancer she once tried to help but who turned to darkness after being refused. Lamia does not hate Vexana; she pities her, believing that even the deepest wound can be healed by the sun's warmth. Now she stands on the walls of her palace—a structure of white marble and gold, with soaring columns and gardens of eternal bloom, overlooking a kingdom of fertile fields and sparkling rivers—the Suneir in her hand, Gham on her shoulder, calling out to Vexana, offering her warmth one more time, hoping that this time the necromancer will accept.

story.part1: Lamia was born during a solar eclipse, a portent that the old priestesses said would make her a ruler of two worlds—the world of light and the world of shadow. Her mother, the previous empress, died in childbirth, and Lamia was raised by the sun priests of the golden temple. They taught her that light was not a weapon, but a gift; that the sun's warmth was not for burning, but for nurturing. Lamia learned quickly, her natural affinity for solar magic manifesting when she was only seven years old. She called down a beam of sunlight to heal a dying bird, and the priests wept with joy. They knew she was the one who would restore the kingdom.
story.part2: The kingdom was in ruins when Lamia took the throne at sixteen. A century of war with the Shadow Accord had left the fields barren, the cities in rubble, and the people starving. Lamia did not raise an army. She raised her staff, the Suneir, and called upon the sun. For forty days and forty nights, she channeled solar energy into the land, healing the soil, purifying the water, bringing warmth to the frozen fields. The people called her a miracle, but Lamia knew it was simply the sun doing what the sun had always done—giving life. She was just the conduit.
story.part3: Vexana came to her kingdom during the fifth year of Lamia's reign. The necromancer was not an invader; she was a supplicant, seeking the sun's warmth to revive her dead husband. Lamia refused, not out of cruelty, but out of wisdom. 'The dead cannot be warmed,' she told Vexana. 'Only the living can feel the sun.' Vexana did not accept this answer. She left the palace in tears, and over the following years, she became Lamia's nemesis, raising armies of the dead to march against the kingdom of light. Lamia mourned the loss of what could have been a friendship, and she swore that one day, she would offer Vexana warmth again.
story.part4: The Sundering brought new challenges. Demons poured through rifts, drawn by the life energy of Lamia's kingdom. She fought them not with armies, but with barriers of golden light, protecting her people while healing the wounded. She never took a life, not even in self‑defense. Her soldiers were sworn to capture, not kill. The demons, confused by this mercy, began to retreat. Some even surrendered, and Lamia offered them redemption, purifying their corruption with the Suneir's light. Word spread across Kretos: the Light Sun Empress was not a conqueror. She was a healer.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has weakened the barriers between worlds, and Vexana has returned, more powerful than ever, her armies of the dead threatening to overrun the kingdom. Lamia stands on the walls of her palace, Gham on her shoulder, the Suneir in her hand. She does not want to fight Vexana. She wants to heal her, to offer her the warmth she was denied all those years ago. She knows that Vexana's darkness is not malice, but grief—a wound that has festered for centuries. Lamia believes that even the deepest wound can be healed, if only the sun is allowed to shine on it. She raises her staff, the crystal blazing, and she calls out to her nemesis: 'Vexana! The sun still rises! Will you not feel its warmth?' She waits for an answer, hoping against hope that this time, the necromancer will accept.

weapons.name: Suneir
weapons.fulldesc: The Suneir is a staff that has become the symbol of Lamia's reign. The staff is taller than she is, its shaft a length of polished olive wood wrapped in gold wire, its surface carved with the story of her kingdom's founding. At its head, a sphere of pure, orange crystal floats within a cage of golden filigree, and within the crystal, a miniature sun turns, its light pulsing with the rhythm of her heartbeat. When she raises the Suneir, the sphere blazes with light, and from it pour rays of warmth that heal the sick, guide the lost, and illuminate the darkest paths. The staff does not destroy; it nurtures, offering guidance to those who seek it, light to those who wander in darkness. She has wielded it for her entire reign, and she has never used it to harm a soul that did not first threaten her people.

Allies inside gain 50% attack speed, 30% movement speed, and 50% damage reduction. Enemies inside are slowed by 30% and take 100 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

ratings.notes: Support mage with healing, area denial, and team buffs.

heroes.lore: Lamia is the Light Sun Empress, a ruler blessed by the sun who healed her kingdom with solar magic. Her nemesis is Vexana, a necromancer she once tried to help but who turned to darkness after being refused. Lamia continues to offer Vexana redemption, believing that even the deepest wound can be healed by the sun's warmth.

relationships.description: Lamia pities Vexana and continues to offer her redemption, hoping to heal her grief.
The celestial guardian respects Lamia's commitment to peace and often aids her kingdom.

pet.name: Gham
pet.description: A falcon of breathtaking majesty, feathers a gradient of golden yellow at the breast to deep orange at the wingtips, eyes two points of molten gold. Wingspan vast, talons sharp, cry a clarion call. Serves as Lamia's messenger, scout, and companion.
pet.fulldesc: Gham is a sun‑and‑beach‑colored falcon of breathtaking majesty. Its feathers are a gradient of golden yellow at the breast to deep orange at the wingtips, and its eyes are two points of molten gold that match Lamia's own. It is larger than any mortal bird, its wingspan vast, its talons sharp, its cry a clarion call that echoes across the plains. Gham is not merely a pet; it is her messenger, her scout, her link to the farthest corners of her kingdom. It can fly faster than the wind, can see farther than any mortal eye, and when she needs to send a decree, it carries her words on its wings. It perches on her shoulder when she holds court, its head turning, its eyes watching, and it is the only being that knows her without the weight of the crown. When Lamia is sad, Gham rests its head on her crown, and she feels less alone.
abilities: Royal Message – can deliver a message to any ally on the map.
Sun Strike – dives at an enemy, dealing 100% magic damage and blinding them.
Golden Sight – reveals all enemies within 15 meters.

heroes.specialty: Support mage with healing, area denial, and team buffs. Excels at protecting allies and redeeming enemies.

heroes.name: Tiamat
heroes.shortDescription: A primordial sea goddess who has swum in the oceans of the world since before the first fish crawled onto land. Tiamat rules the abyss, wielding the Trident of the Abyss and riding Pristi, an abyssal sawfish. She watches over her daughter Kadita, waiting for the day when she will be ready to rule the seas.
heroes.fullDescription: Tiamat rises from the crushing depths like a dream of the deep given form, a being of such ancient terrible beauty that the oceans themselves remember her birth—a primordial sea goddess, the mother of all mermaids, who has swum in the oceans of the world since before the first fish crawled onto land. Her face is a study in timeless perfection: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full silver‑blue lips parted in a knowing sorrowful smile. Her skin is fair and luminous, the color of moonlight on still water, smooth and flawless, with a faint cool undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep luminous sea‑green, the color of the abyss where the sun never reaches. Her hair is a cascade of pure white silk past her waist, worn loose, floating as if underwater. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She has no tail; she walks upon the water with a shimmering watery aura around her legs. She wears scaled armor of deep dark blue, overlapping like sea serpent scales with silver edges, a minimal bodice revealing her cleavage and stomach. Her arms are bare, with delicate fins from her forearms, and fins of silver and black with translucent membranes extend from her shoulders. An obsidian choker with silver pearls circles her neck. A belt of interlocking obsidian plates with sea beast skulls cinches her waist. A jagged skirt of scaled plates and silver chains covers her hips, and her legs are covered by the watery aura, her feet bare, touching the water only when she wills. A coral crown of blood red with a silver base and pearl tips—living coral, growing and shifting—sits on her head. Obsidian and silver bracelets adorn her wrists, and obsidian rings with silver pearls cover her fingers. Her weapon, the Trident of the Abyss, is taller than she is, its shaft a length of petrified coral wrapped in silver wire, carved with the story of the first ocean. Its three tines are forged from obsidian and silver, their edges sharp enough to split the waves, their tips crackling with the pressure of the abyss. When she raises it, the seas rise with her; when she strikes the water, whirlpools open, swallowing ships; when she points it at the sky, storms gather, their rain falling in sheets, their lightning striking the waves. Her mount, Pristi, is an abyssal sawfish of terrifying majesty—larger than any mortal vessel, its body a sleek silver‑blue torpedo of muscle and scale, its snout a massive serrated saw that can cut through the hulls of ships like paper, its eyes pits of silver light, its fins edged with venomous spines, its back armored in obsidian plates. This primordial sea goddess created the first mermaids from coral and foam, and she watched as her children were hunted and forgotten. She slept for millennia in the deepest trench, and when she awoke, she sensed Kadita, a mermaid princess of a lost kingdom, and watched over her from the abyss. Now she swims through the abyssal ocean—a realm of eternal darkness, where the pressure would crush mortal bodies, where only bioluminescent creatures and ancient leviathans dwell, and where the only light comes from her trident's crackling tips and the phosphorescent trail of Pristi's passage—the Trident of the Abyss in her hand, Pristi at her side, rising to reveal herself to her daughter Kadita for the first time, not as a goddess, but as a mother.

story.part1: Before the first land rose from the waters, Tiamat ruled the primordial sea. She was born from the salt and the darkness, the first consciousness to emerge from the deep. For eons, she swam alone, her only companions the creatures of the abyss—fish that had never seen light, whales that sang songs older than the stars. She was content, but she was lonely. So she created the first mermaids, shaping them from coral and foam, breathing life into them with her own breath. They called her Mother, and Tiamat wept with joy.
story.part2: The gods of the sky grew jealous of Tiamat's domain. They raised mountains from the sea, creating land, and they filled the land with creatures that walked and flew. Tiamat watched as her children—the mermaids—were pushed to the edges of the world, hunted by humans, forgotten by history. She raged, summoning tsunamis that swallowed coastal cities, calling storms that battered the shores. The sky gods fought her, and their battle lasted a thousand years. In the end, Tiamat retreated to the abyss, not because she was defeated, but because she was tired. She sank into the deepest trench and slept for millennia.
story.part3: Kadita was born during Tiamat's sleep, a mermaid princess of a lost kingdom. When Tiamat awoke, she sensed the girl's power—a spark of the primordial deep that had not been seen since the first mermaids. She watched Kadita from the abyss, guiding her, protecting her, but never revealing herself. She knew that Kadita must grow on her own, must face her own trials, must learn the weight of the crown that she would one day inherit. Tiamat was patient. She had waited eons; she could wait a little longer.
story.part4: The Sundering tore open the ocean floor, and demons of the void began to seep into the deep. Tiamat fought them, her trident blazing, her sawfish Pristi tearing through their ranks. She protected the mermaid kingdoms that still survived, and she watched as Kadita rose to become a hero of the surface world. She was proud of her daughter, prouder than she had ever been of any of her creations. But she was also sad, for she knew that Kadita did not know her mother's name.
story.part5: Now, Tiamat swims through the abyss, Pristi at her side, the Trident of the Abyss humming with power. She has sensed that Kadita is in danger, that a demon lord of the void seeks to claim the mermaid princess as a bride. Tiamat will not allow it. She rises from the deep, her watery aura blazing, her coral crown glowing. She will reveal herself to her daughter for the first time, not as a goddess, but as a mother. She will fight beside Kadita, and when the battle is won, she will offer her a choice: return to the deep and rule the abyss, or stay on the surface and continue her hero's journey. Either way, Tiamat will love her. For she is Tiamat, the Abyssal Mermaid Maiden, and her daughter is the greatest treasure in all the seas.

weapons.name: Trident of the Abyss
weapons.fulldesc: The Trident of the Abyss is a weapon that has become the symbol of Tiamat's dominion over the deep. The trident is taller than she is, its shaft a length of petrified coral wrapped in silver wire, its surface carved with the story of the first ocean. Its three tines are forged from obsidian and silver, their edges sharp enough to split the waves, their tips crackling with the pressure of the abyss. When she raises it, the seas rise with her; when she strikes the water, whirlpools open, swallowing ships; when she points it at the sky, storms gather, their rain falling in sheets, their lightning striking the waves. The Trident of the Abyss commands the deep, and there is no ocean that does not answer its call.
weapons.look: Petrified coral shaft with silver wire, three obsidian and silver tines crackling with abyssal pressure
weapons.aura: Deep blue, silver, crackling pressure waves

passive.name: Pressure of the Deep
passive.description: Enemies within 10 meters of Tiamat have their movement speed reduced by 15%.

passive.name: Ocean's Blessing
passive.description: Tiamat gains 20% damage reduction while in water (rivers, ocean, or standing in her own abilities).

active.name: Tidal Wave
active.description: Sends a wave of water forward 15 meters, dealing 150% magic damage and knocking back enemies. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Whirlpool
active.description: Creates a whirlpool at target location for 4 seconds, pulling enemies toward the center and dealing 80 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

ultimate.name: Primordial Storm
ultimate.description: Tiamat calls a massive storm in a 25-meter radius for 8 seconds. Enemies inside are slowed by 40%, take 100 magic damage per second, and are periodically struck by lightning (200 magic damage). Allies inside gain 30% movement speed and heal for 20 health per second. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Mother's Love
special.description: When Kadita is an ally, Tiamat's damage reduction on her is doubled. When Kadita is an enemy (unlikely), Tiamat's abilities deal 50% less damage to her.

ratings.notes: Control mage with area denial, crowd control, and team utility.

skills.name: Abyssal Adaptation
skills.description: Tiamat can breathe underwater and gains 20% movement speed while in water.

skills.name: Deep Dive
skills.description: Tiamat dives into the ground (or water), becoming untargetable for 1.5 seconds, then emerges at target location, dealing 100 magic damage to nearby enemies. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: Salt Curse
skills.description: Curses an enemy, reducing their healing by 70% for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Eternal Vigil
skills.description: If Kadita is below 20% health within 50 meters, Tiamat automatically casts Deep Dive to reach her and gains 50% damage reduction for 5 seconds.

heroes.lore: Tiamat is the primordial sea goddess, mother of all mermaids. She has ruled the abyss since before land existed. She watches over her daughter Kadita from the depths, waiting for the day when she will reveal herself and offer Kadita the choice to rule the seas.

relationships.description: Tiamat watches over Kadita from the abyss, proud of her daughter's heroism.
They fought for control of the world eons ago; Tiamat retreated to the deep, but the truce is uneasy.

mount.name: Pristi
mount.description: An abyssal sawfish of terrifying majesty, larger than any mortal vessel, body a sleek silver-blue torpedo of muscle and scale, snout a massive serrated saw. Eyes pits of silver light, fins edged with venomous spines, back armored in obsidian plates.
mount.fulldesc: Pristi is an abyssal sawfish of terrifying majesty, larger than any mortal vessel, its body a sleek, silver‑blue torpedo of muscle and scale, its snout a massive, serrated saw that can cut through the hulls of ships like paper. Its eyes are two pits of silver light, its fins edged with spines that drip with venom, its back armored in plates of obsidian that reflect no light. Pristi is not merely a mount; it is the first creature Tiamat tamed, the beast that has served her since the dawn of the oceans, and it obeys her with the devotion of a child. It swims in her wake, its saw slicing the waves, and when she calls, it rises, carrying her across the seas faster than any ship, its bulk parting the water, its presence a warning to all who would defy the queen of the deep. When Tiamat is sad, Pristi nuzzles her with its massive snout (carefully), and she feels the bond of eons.
abilities: Saw Slash – deals 200% physical damage in a cone.
Venom Spines – applies a poison that deals 50 damage per second for 5 seconds.
Obsidian Armor – reduces damage taken by 20% while mounted.

heroes.specialty: Control mage with area denial, crowd control, and team utility. Excels at zone control and protecting allies.

dialogue.selection: The deep remembers. So do I.

heroes.name: Lunara
heroes.shortDescription: An elf empress of eighteen years whose existence spans a hundred millennia, her face frozen in the eternal youth of the high elves. Lunara is the mother of Selena, the queen who waits for her lost daughter to return to the light of the World Tree. She wields divine moonlight and is accompanied by Fika Fika, a blob‑like being of pure moonlight energy.
heroes.fullDescription: Lunara drifts through the eternal twilight of the World Tree's canopy like a memory of the first dawn, a being of such ancient gentle power that the stars themselves pause in their courses to witness her passing—an elf empress of eighteen years in appearance, though her existence spans a hundred millennia, her face frozen in the eternal youth of the high elves. She is the mother of Selena, the queen who waits for her lost daughter to return to the light of the World Tree. Her face is a masterwork of celestial refinement: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full rose‑pink lips perpetually curved in a warm knowing smile. Her skin is fair and luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint silver undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep luminous sapphire blue, the color of the sky at the moment the first star appears. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk past her waist, worn loose, catching the light of the World Tree. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a white silk robe cut deep at the chest, held by silver cords, revealing her cleavage and stomach, overlaid with a silver breastplate shaped like swan's wings. Her arms are bare, long and graceful. A collar of silver and moonstone with moon phase pendants circles her neck. A belt of interlocking silver discs with a royal blue sash cinches her waist, and the robe flows around her. A crescent crown of silver and sapphire, its points like moon horns, sits on her head. Silver bangles adorn her wrists, silver and sapphire earrings dangle from her ears, and silver and moonstone rings cover her fingers. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. Her weapon is her divine moonlight—she does not wield a physical weapon; her very presence is a weapon in itself, a field of pure healing light that purifies corruption and banishes darkness. When she walks, the shadows flee; when she speaks, the wounded heal; when she raises her hand, the World Tree answers, its roots reaching through the earth to protect her children. Her pet, Fika Fika, is a small blob‑like being of pure moonlight energy—its body a soft glowing cloud of silver and blue, its head shaped like a tiny dragon's, its eyes two points of golden light that shine with gentle loving warmth. It moves with a floating crawling motion, slithering through the air or ground as if swimming through moonlight, curling around her shoulders when she rests, nuzzling her cheek when she is sad, absorbing her tears and glowing brighter, as if trying to warm her. This elf empress was woven from the first moonbeam that touched the World Tree's leaves, a consciousness formed from silver light and the whisper of wind through branches. She became the queen of the elves, and her youngest daughter Selena fled into the night, corrupted by the Shadow Accord. Lunara did not send armies; she waited, patient as the moon, sending dreams and visions to her daughter. Now she stands at the base of the World Tree—a colossal tree whose trunk is wider than a city, whose canopy blots out the sky, whose roots stretch to the center of the earth, glowing with silver light, with the spirits of the forest dancing among its branches—the divine moonlight radiating from her, Fika Fika floating beside her, waiting for Selena to come home, hoping that this time her daughter will accept her embrace.

story.part1: When the World Tree first sprouted from the seed of creation, Lunara was there. She was not born; she was woven from the first moonbeam that touched the tree's leaves, a consciousness formed from silver light and the whisper of wind through branches. For eons, she watched as the world took shape, as the first elves emerged from the tree's roots, as civilizations rose and fell beneath her canopy. She became their queen, not through conquest, but through love. The elves called her 'Mother,' and Lunara accepted the title with a humility that belied her power.
story.part2: Selena was her youngest daughter, a child of starlight and shadow, born with a wildness that none of Lunara's other children possessed. Lunara loved her fiercely, perhaps too fiercely, for she tried to protect Selena from the darkness that whispered at the edges of the World Tree. Selena, feeling smothered, fled into the night. She fell in with the Shadow Accord, was corrupted by a demon lord, and became a creature of twilight—neither light nor dark, but something in between. Lunara wept for a thousand years, her tears watering the roots of the World Tree, and the elves learned to mourn with their queen.
story.part3: Lunara did not send armies to retrieve Selena. She did not curse her daughter's name. She waited, patient as the moon, knowing that the light in Selena's heart could not be extinguished, only buried. She sent dreams to her daughter, visions of the World Tree, whispers of home. Selena never responded, but Lunara felt her daughter's heart flutter, felt the shadow flicker. There was hope.
story.part4: The Sundering tore rifts in the sky, and demons poured through, seeking to corrupt the World Tree. Lunara fought them, her moonlight blazing, her healing light mending the wounds of her warriors. She never took a life, not even a demon's. She purified them, turning their corruption to ash and sending their cleansed souls back to the void. The demons learned to fear the silver light of the elf empress, and they began to whisper that she was more dangerous than any warrior, for she did not kill—she unmade.
story.part5: Now, Lunara stands at the base of the World Tree, Fika Fika floating beside her, her crown glowing. She has sensed that Selena is coming home, that the Sundering has forced her daughter to confront the darkness within herself. Lunara does not know if Selena will accept her embrace or reject it again. But she knows that she will be here, waiting, as she has always waited. She will offer her daughter the same warmth she offered a hundred thousand years ago, when Selena was a child playing in the tree's roots. For she is Lunara, the Moonlight Seeker Elf Empress, and a mother's love is the one light that no darkness can extinguish.

weapons.name: Divine Moonlight
weapons.fulldesc: Lunara does not wield a physical weapon; her very presence is a weapon in itself, a field of pure, healing light that purifies corruption and banishes darkness. When she walks, the shadows flee; when she speaks, the wounded heal; when she raises her hand, the World Tree answers, its roots reaching through the earth to protect her children. She can summon light that heals the sick and purifies the corrupted, can call upon the power of the World Tree to nourish the land and protect her people. Her moonlight is not a tool of destruction—it is a tool of salvation, and she has never used it to harm a soul that did not first threaten her children.
weapons.look: Silver and blue glowing aura, beams of moonlight from her hands

passive.name: Moonlight Aura
passive.description: Allies within 15 meters of Lunara gain 20 health regeneration per second and 10% damage reduction.

passive.name: World Tree's Blessing
passive.description: Lunara's healing abilities are 30% more effective on elf-type allies.

active.description: Heals an ally for 300 health plus 15% of their max health, and removes all debuffs. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Lunar Purge

ultimate.name: Eternal Canopy
ultimate.description: Lunara summons the full power of the World Tree for 10 seconds, creating a 25-meter radius zone of silver light. Allies inside are immune to death (cannot fall below 1 HP) and heal for 10% of their max health per second. Enemies inside are slowed by 50% and take 100 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 100 seconds.

special.name: Mother's Heart

ratings.notes: Pure support with massive healing, purification, and team invincibility ultimate.

skills.name: Silver Veil
skills.description: Lunara is permanently invisible to enemies more than 30 meters away.

skills.name: Tree's Embrace
skills.description: Lunara wraps an ally in roots, granting them immunity to crowd control for 3 seconds and healing them for 200 health. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Starlight Path
skills.description: Creates a path of moonlight for 6 seconds. Allies on the path gain 40% movement speed; enemies are slowed by 30%. Cooldown: 16 seconds.

skills.name: The Long Wait
skills.description: If Selena is on the enemy team, Lunara cannot be the one to land the killing blow on her. Instead, Selena is left at 1 HP, and Lunara gains a movement speed boost toward her.

heroes.lore: Lunara is the elf empress, mother of the World Tree, and mother of Selena. She has waited a hundred thousand years for her lost daughter to return to the light, never giving up hope. She wields divine moonlight and protects her people with healing and purification.

relationships.description: Lunara loves Selena and has waited millennia for her return, never giving up hope.
Lunara is bound to the World Tree; its health is tied to her own.

pet.name: Fika Fika
pet.description: A small, blob‑like being of pure moonlight energy, body a soft glowing cloud of silver and blue, head shaped like a tiny dragon, eyes two points of golden light. Moves with floating, crawling motion, slithering through air or ground as if swimming through moonlight.
pet.fulldesc: Fika Fika is a creature of impossible cuteness, its body a soft, glowing cloud of silver and blue, its head shaped like a tiny dragon's, its eyes two points of golden light that shine with a gentle, loving warmth. It moves with a floating, crawling motion, slithering through the air or the ground as if it were swimming through moonlight, and it follows Lunara everywhere, curling around her shoulders when she rests, nuzzling her cheek when she is sad. Fika Fika is not merely a pet; it is a fragment of the World Tree's light, a spirit of pure joy that Lunara has carried with her since the first age, and it is the only being that has never known sorrow. It chitters softly when she speaks, its golden eyes blinking, its cloudy body pulsing with the rhythm of her heartbeat, and it brings her comfort in the long, patient years of waiting. When Lunara cries, Fika Fika absorbs her tears and glows brighter, as if trying to warm her.
abilities: Joyful Glow – heals nearby allies for 10 health per second.
Moonlight Bubble – creates a shield around an ally that absorbs 150 damage.
Fika's Blessing – increases an ally's movement speed by 20% for 3 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Pure support with massive healing, purification, and team invincibility ultimate. Excels at keeping allies alive and redeeming fallen enemies.

dialogue.selection: The World Tree watches. So do I.

heroes.name: Mika Sah
heroes.shortDescription: A warrior of Roman Greek heritage who tears across the sky like a storm that has chosen a single, perfect shape. Mika Sah is Fanny's twin, faster, stronger, and more precise than her sister. She wields the Fanananana—a combo of dual blades and grappling hooks—and rides Pere Pere, a peregrine falcon of lightning energy. She waits for the day when Fanny will finally accept her help.
heroes.fullDescription: Mika Sah tears across the sky like a storm that has chosen a single perfect shape, a being of such breathtaking dangerous beauty that the clouds themselves part to witness her passage—an eighteen‑year‑old warrior of Roman Greek heritage, Fanny's twin, faster, stronger, and more precise than her sister. Her face is a study in divine symmetry: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full crimson lips perpetually curved in a fierce knowing smile. Her skin is fair and luminous, the color of fresh cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone that catches the lightning that dances around her. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep lightning‑yellow that shifts and swirls like the heart of a storm. Her hair is a cascade of fiery crimson, layered and practical, swept back in a messy half‑up ponytail that allows the rest to flow behind her like a banner of flame. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a furred brown long coat open over a minimal bra‑like bronze and gold breastplate that reveals her cleavage and stomach. Her arms are bare, with bronze and gold bracelets. Spiked armor of bronze and gold, with spikes curving upward, guards her shoulders. An armored mini skirt of dark red leather, slit high, covers her hips, exposing her thick powerful thighs. Silver short armor boots, crackling with lightning, cover her feet. Grappling hook gears are mounted on both hips, and a lightning aura crackles around her body. Her weapons, the Fanananana, are a masterpiece of aerial combat engineering—dual blades of bronze and gold with lightning‑yellow thunder aura, short swords that leave trails of electricity in the air when she swings, perfectly balanced for blinding speed. On each hip, a big‑sized mechanical container—bronze and gold, humming with stored energy—houses grappling hook gears that fire steel cables, allowing her to swing through the air with impossible grace. Her mount, Pere Pere, is a peregrine falcon of breathtaking majesty—larger than any mortal bird, its feathers a gradient of dark bronze at the tips to bright gold at the breast, its eyes two points of lightning‑yellow, moving with the speed of a thunderbolt, diving at impossible velocities, its wings trailing arcs of electricity that light up the sky. This ThunderSky rider was born under a storm, the twin of Fanny. Their father, an aerial combat instructor, was killed by a demon, and the twins were separated in the chaos. Mika threw herself into training, perfecting speed and aggression, while Fanny developed precision and control. Mika believes she is superior and waits for Fanny to accept her help. Now she tears across the sky above the ThunderSky tribe's ancestral lands—a realm of floating islands connected by rope bridges, waterfalls that fall into the clouds, and constant storms that crackle with lightning—her blades crackling, Pere Pere screeching, searching for Fanny, ready to fight beside her sister as they were meant to.

story.part1: Mika and Fanny were born under a storm, twins whose cries were drowned by thunder. Their mother, a warrior of the ThunderSky tribe, died in childbirth, and their father, a scholar of aerial combat, raised them alone. He taught them to fly before they could walk, to fight before they could speak. Mika was always the faster twin, her reflexes sharper, her instincts truer. Fanny was the strategist, the one who thought before she acted. They complemented each other, and together they were unbeatable.
story.part2: The rift between them came when they were sixteen. Their father was killed by a demon of the Shadow Accord, and the twins were separated in the chaos. Fanny blamed herself, believing that if she had been faster, she could have saved him. Mika blamed Fanny, not for his death, but for her grief. 'You're always thinking,' Mika screamed. 'Sometimes you just have to act!' Fanny withdrew into herself, while Mika threw herself into training, pushing her body to its limits, determined to become strong enough to protect everyone she loved.
story.part3: They reunited years later, both having become legendary sky riders. Fanny had developed a style based on precision and control, while Mika had perfected speed and aggression. They fought side by side against demonic incursions, but the old rift remained. Mika would dive into danger without hesitation, and Fanny would hang back, calculating. Mika saw it as cowardice; Fanny saw it as wisdom. They never spoke of their father's death, and the silence between them grew wider than any canyon.
story.part4: Mika developed the Fanananana, a combat system that combined dual blades with grappling hooks, allowing her to swing through the air with impossible grace. She could strike from any angle, disappear into the clouds, and reappear behind her enemies before they could blink. She became a legend, known as the ThunderSky Rider, and she was offered a place in the Celestial Order. She refused. 'I don't serve orders,' she said. 'I serve the wind.'
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought the demons to every corner of Kretos, and Mika has heard that Fanny is struggling, that her precision style is not enough against the chaos of the rifts. Mika does not gloat. She does not say 'I told you so.' She simply appears at Fanny's side, her blades crackling, her falcon screeching. 'I'm not here to save you,' she says. 'I'm here to fight with you. Like we were meant to.' She does not know if Fanny will accept her help. But she knows that she will not let her sister fall alone. For she is Mika Sah, the Dualblade ThunderSky Rider, and the storm is finally coming home.

weapons.name: Fanananana (Dual Blades + Grappling Hooks)
weapons.fulldesc: The Fanananana is a masterpiece of aerial combat engineering, a combo setup built for speed and precision. In each hand, Mika carries a dual blade—a short sword of bronze and gold, its edge crackling with lightning‑yellow thunder aura that leaves trails of electricity in the air when she swings. The blades are perfectly balanced for aerial combat, light enough to strike with blinding speed, heavy enough to deliver devastating blows. On each side of her hips, she carries two big‑sized mechanical containers, their surfaces bronze and gold, their interiors humming with stored energy. These are not merely sheaths; they are grappling hook gears, each one capable of firing steel cables that anchor into walls, cliffs, or the clouds themselves. She uses them to swing through the air with impossible grace, her blades flashing, her cables singing, her body a blur of bronze and crimson against the sky. The Fanananana is not a single weapon; it is a system, a dance, a way of moving that she has perfected until she is faster than the wind itself.
weapons.look: Two bronze and gold short swords with lightning aura; two mechanical grappling hook gears on hips

passive.name: Thunder Rush
passive.description: After using a grappling hook, Mika gains 30% attack speed for 3 seconds.

passive.name: Sky Superiority
passive.description: Mika deals 15% increased damage to airborne or flying enemies.

active.name: Grappling Strike
active.description: Fires a grappling hook to a target location, pulling Mika there and dealing 100% physical damage to enemies she passes through. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

active.name: Lightning Slash
active.description: Swings both blades in a cross arc, dealing 160% physical damage and stunning enemies hit for 0.75 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Tempest Dance
ultimate.description: Mika becomes a blur of motion for 6 seconds, gaining 80% movement speed and 100% dodge chance against basic attacks. During this time, her grappling hook has no cooldown and her blades leave lightning trails that deal 50% damage to enemies crossed. Cooldown: 70 seconds.

special.name: Twin's Shadow
special.description: When Fanny is an ally, Mika's damage is increased by 10% when within 15 meters of Fanny. When Fanny is an enemy, Mika's damage against her is reduced by 30% (cannot bring herself to truly hurt her sister).

ratings.notes: Extremely mobile assassin with high burst damage and dodge capabilities.

skills.name: Wind Walker
skills.description: Mika's movement speed is increased by 10% for each consecutive second she is moving, up to 30%.

skills.name: Thunder Clap
skills.description: Mika slams her blades together, creating a thunderclap that slows enemies in a 8-meter radius by 40% for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Aerial Ace
skills.description: Mika leaps into the air, becoming untargetable for 0.75 seconds, then crashes down, dealing 150% physical damage in a 5-meter radius. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Superior's Burden
skills.description: If Fanny is on the enemy team and falls below 20% health, Mika's next attack against any enemy deals 50% bonus damage (she channels her frustration into the fight rather than attacking Fanny directly).

heroes.lore: Mika Sah is Fanny's twin sister, a ThunderSky Rider who mastered speed and aggression. She believes she is superior to Fanny and wants to prove it, but also loves her sister and will not let her fall. She wields the Fanananana and rides Pere Pere, a lightning falcon.

relationships.description: Mika loves Fanny but believes she is superior. She waits for Fanny to accept her help.
Her tribe was nearly wiped out by demons; Mika fights to avenge them.

mount.name: Pere Pere
mount.description: A peregrine falcon of breathtaking majesty, larger than any mortal bird, feathers a gradient of dark bronze at the tips to bright gold at the breast, eyes two points of lightning‑yellow. Moves with the speed of a thunderbolt, diving at impossible velocities, wings trailing arcs of electricity.
mount.fulldesc: Pere Pere is a peregrine falcon of breathtaking majesty, larger than any mortal bird, its feathers a gradient of dark bronze at the tips to bright gold at the breast, its eyes two points of lightning‑yellow that match Mika's own. It moves with the speed of a thunderbolt, diving at impossible velocities, its wings trailing arcs of electricity that light up the sky. Pere Pere is not merely a pet; it is her partner, her scout, her second set of eyes in the chaos of battle. It can fly faster than she can, can spot enemies from miles away, can strike with the force of a lightning bolt when she commands. It perches on her shoulder when she rests, its feathers sparking, its head turning, and it is the only being that has ever kept pace with her. When Mika is angry, Pere Pere screeches, a sound like thunder, and the skies darken in response.
abilities: Lightning Dive – dives at an enemy, dealing 150% physical damage and stunning for 0.5 seconds.
Storm Scout – reveals all enemies within 20 meters.
Electric Wings – leaves a trail of lightning that damages enemies.

heroes.specialty: Extremely mobile assassin with high burst damage, dodge capabilities, and aerial superiority. Excels at diving backlines and escaping.

heroes.name: Mordred
heroes.shortDescription: The bastard son of Arthur's bloodline, a knight whose features are a dark mirror of the purity he was denied. Mordred has sworn to destroy the Round Table that rejected him, wielding the Traitor's Blade rapier and accompanied by Leogar, a ferocious red hound. He waits for the day when he will face Lancelot, the perfect knight whose virtue was always a mask.
heroes.fullDescription: Mordred waits in the shadows of Camelot's fallen glory, a figure of such cold magnetic menace that the very stones of the citadel remember his name with a shiver—the bastard son of Arthur's bloodline, a knight whose features are a dark mirror of the purity he was denied. His face is a study in corrupted nobility: a strong angular jawline, high prominent cheekbones, a straight aristocratic nose broken and healed, and thin lips pressed into a grim line. His skin is pale, almost grey, the color of the moon when it hides behind storm clouds, smooth and cold. His eyes are large and almond‑shaped, pale frozen grey, the color of winter skies and the steel of a blade before it strikes. His hair is long and messy, a cascade of obsidian black past his shoulders, worn loose. A jagged scar runs from his brow to his cheek—a gift from the father who refused to acknowledge him. He is handsome, devastatingly so, despite the scars. His physique is lean, built for speed and precision: broad shoulders, a defined chest, long powerful arms, a narrow waist, and long strong legs. He wears obsidian black knight armor, overlapping plates, with a breastplate etched with the names of the knights he will destroy (the letters reversed). Black steel vambraces cover his arms, and his hands are bare, with silver gothic rings on his fingers. Pauldrons shaped as snarling wolves with ruby eyes guard his shoulders. A belt of interlocking silver links cinches his waist, and a long crimson silk cape flows from his waist. His legs are armored, his feet clad in black steel boots. A spiked crown of black iron with a blood‑red gem at its base sits on his head. His weapon, the Traitor's Blade, is a rapier longer than his arm—its blade a curve of obsidian black, its edge sharp enough to cut the bonds of oaths, its surface carved with the names of the knights he has sworn to destroy. The hilt is wrapped in crimson leather, its pommel set with a ruby that glows with a dark reddish aura, and when he draws it, the blade ignites with a cold scarlet fire that seems to drink the light from the room. The Traitor's Blade does not merely kill; it unmakes, cutting through the vows that bind men to honor, revealing the treachery that lies at the heart of every oath. His pet, Leogar, is a ferocious red hound—its fur a deep blood red, its stripes black as the void, its eyes two pits of burning amber, as large as a wolf, its shoulders level with Mordred's waist, its paws capable of crushing skulls, its jaws strong enough to rend steel. This bastard knight was the secret son of King Arthur, raised in the wild forests by his mother Morgan le Fay. When he rode to Camelot at sixteen to beg for recognition, Arthur turned him away, and Lancelot laughed loudest of all. Mordred swore to destroy the Round Table, forged the Traitor's Blade, and has been hunting the knights ever since. Now he waits in the shadows of Camelot's fallen glory—a ruined castle of crumbling stone walls, overgrown courtyards, and the skeletal remains of once‑great towers, where the wind howls through empty halls and the only light comes from the pale moon—Leogar growling at his side, the Traitor's Blade humming, waiting for Lancelot to lead a mission near the castle walls, so he can face the perfect knight and show him what it truly means to be a knight.

story.part1: Mordred was born in the shadow of Camelot, the secret son of King Arthur and a woman the court would never accept. His mother, Morgan le Fay, was a sorceress of the old ways, and she raised him in the wild forests, teaching him the arts of war and magic. He grew up knowing that his father was a king, that his half‑siblings sat at the Round Table, that he was nothing but a bastard, a shame to be hidden. He did not hate his father then; he longed for him, dreamed of the day when Arthur would ride into the forest and claim him as a son. That day never came.
story.part2: When Mordred was sixteen, he rode to Camelot himself. He presented himself to Arthur, revealed his lineage, and begged for recognition. Arthur looked at him, at the scar on his face (a wound from a wolf attack that had almost killed him), and turned away. 'I have no son,' the king said. 'Leave this place and never return.' The knights of the Round Table laughed, and Lancelot—the perfect knight, the paragon of virtue—laughed loudest of all. Mordred walked out of Camelot that day, his heart turned to ash, his love turned to hate. He swore an oath in the forest: 'I will destroy every one of you. I will burn Camelot to the ground. And when you cry for mercy, I will show you the same mercy you showed me.'
story.part3: Leogar found him in the forest, a wounded hound abandoned by its master. Mordred healed the dog with his own hands, and the beast became his shadow, his only friend, the only creature that looked at him without disgust. He trained with Leogar at his side, learning to fight in tandem, to anticipate each other's movements. He forged the Traitor's Blade from the iron of a fallen star, and he imbued it with his hatred. The sword could cut through oaths, through vows, through the very bonds of chivalry that held the Round Table together. Mordred was ready.
story.part4: The Sundering came, and Mordred saw his opportunity. He did not ally with the demons; he did not need to. He used the chaos to move against Camelot, striking at its outposts, assassinating its knights, weakening its defenses. He never faced Arthur directly; he wanted the king to suffer first, to watch his kingdom crumble, to know that his bastard son was the one pulling the strings. The Round Table blamed the demons, never suspecting that the true threat came from within.
story.part5: Now, Mordred stands at the edge of Camelot's light, Leogar growling at his side, the Traitor's Blade humming in his hand. He has learned that Lancelot is leading a mission to seal a Sundering rift near the castle walls, and Mordred intends to be there. He will face the perfect knight, blade to blade, and he will show him what it truly means to be a knight. He does not want to kill Lancelot quickly; he wants him to understand, in his final moments, that the virtue he wore like a cloak was always a lie. For he is Mordred, the Betrayer Knight, and he will not rest until the Round Table is shattered and his father's legacy is ash.

weapons.name: Traitor's Blade
weapons.fulldesc: The Traitor's Blade is a rapier that has become the symbol of Mordred's purpose. The blade is longer than his arm, its length a curve of obsidian black, its edge sharp enough to cut the bonds of oaths, its surface carved with the names of the knights he has sworn to destroy. The hilt is wrapped in crimson leather, its pommel set with a ruby that glows with a dark, reddish aura, and when he draws it, the blade ignites with a cold, scarlet fire that seems to drink the light from the room. The Traitor's Blade does not merely kill; it unmakes, cutting through the vows that bind men to honor, revealing the treachery that lies at the heart of every oath. He has wielded it in a hundred duels, and each strike is a lesson: loyalty is a lie, chivalry is a cage, and the only truth is power.
weapons.look: Obsidian black curved blade, crimson leather hilt, ruby pommel, ignites with cold scarlet fire

passive.name: Oath Breaker
passive.description: Mordred's attacks ignore 30% of the target's armor if the target has a 'knight' or 'paladin' class.

passive.name: Betrayer's Mark
passive.description: Enemies hit by Mordred's abilities are marked for 5 seconds. Marked enemies take 15% increased damage from Mordred.

active.name: Treacherous Thrust
active.description: Mordred dashes forward 10 meters, piercing through enemies and dealing 180% physical damage. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Oathbreaker's Slash
active.description: Slashes in a 8-meter cone, dealing 150% physical damage and silencing enemies for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: The Betrayal
ultimate.description: Mordred channels for 1 second, then unleashes a devastating thrust that deals 400% physical damage to a single target and removes all their buffs and shields. If the target is a 'knight' or 'paladin,' they are stunned for 3 seconds and their healing is reduced by 100% for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 75 seconds.

special.name: Father's Rejection
special.description: When fighting Arthur (or any 'king' type enemy), Mordred's damage is increased by 25% and his ultimate's cooldown is reduced by 20 seconds.

ratings.notes: Assassin fighter with anti‑knight specialization, debuffs, and single-target execution.

skills.name: Shadow of Camelot
skills.description: Mordred gains 10% movement speed when moving toward enemies with higher current health than him.

skills.name: Leogar's Bite
skills.description: Mordred commands Leogar to bite an enemy, dealing 100 physical damage and applying a bleed that deals 50 damage per second for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 9 seconds.

skills.name: Dark Resilience
skills.description: Mordred cleanses all debuffs from himself and gains 20% damage reduction for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: Bastard's Pride
skills.description: When fighting Lancelot, Mordred's abilities deal 15% bonus damage and Lancelot's healing effects on himself are reduced by 50%.

heroes.lore: Mordred is the bastard son of King Arthur, rejected by his father and humiliated by the knights of the Round Table. He swore vengeance and now hunts the knights, wielding the Traitor's Blade and accompanied by his hound Leogar. He seeks to destroy Camelot and prove that the perfect knight Lancelot is a fraud.

relationships.description: Mordred was rejected by Arthur and now seeks to destroy his kingdom.
Lancelot laughed at Mordred when he was rejected; Mordred will face him first.
The red hound is Mordred's only friend, found wounded in the forest and healed by him.

pet.name: Leogar
pet.description: A ferocious red hound of terrifying majesty, fur deep blood red, stripes black as the void, eyes two pits of burning amber. As large as a wolf, shoulders level with Mordred's waist, paws capable of crushing skulls, jaws strong enough to rend steel.
pet.fulldesc: Leogar is a ferocious red hound, its fur a deep, blood red, its stripes black as the void, its eyes two pits of burning amber. It is as large as a wolf, its shoulders level with Mordred's waist, its paws capable of crushing the skulls of knights, its jaws strong enough to rend steel. Leogar is not merely a pet; it is the only creature that has ever been loyal to Mordred, the hound that found him in the woods where his father abandoned him, that has followed him through every battle, that has never once looked at him with the disgust of the court. It moves with a silent, predatory grace, its head low, its eyes watching, and when Mordred calls, it leaps, its jaws snapping, its claws tearing, its loyalty absolute. When Mordred is alone, he speaks to Leogar as if the hound could understand every word, and perhaps it does.
abilities: Maul – deals 120% physical damage and applies a bleed.
Track – reveals the trail of a marked enemy.
Loyal Guard – reduces damage taken by Mordred by 10% while Leogar is within 5 meters.

heroes.specialty: Assassin fighter with anti‑knight specialization, debuffs, and single-target execution. Excels at dueling and eliminating high-value targets.

heroes.name: Grimalkin
heroes.shortDescription: A thirteen‑year‑old lynx humanoid cursed to hunt the light. Grimalkin was taken from his family and bound to the shadow realm, forced to destroy every spark of light he can find. He wields a glowing shadow orb and is accompanied by his own shadow, which moves independently. He waits for the day when he will meet Harith, the mage of light and time, hoping that their clash will finally bring him peace.
heroes.fullDescription: Grimalkin emerges from the darkness like a nightmare given form, a thirteen‑year‑old lynx humanoid whose eyes hold the weight of a curse that has aged him beyond his years—a child taken from his family and bound to the shadow realm, forced to hunt the light. His face is a haunting contradiction of human softness and feral grace: a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full pale lips pressed into a thin solemn line. His skin, where visible beneath the black fur that covers most of his body, is human‑like, pale and smooth, but cracked with veins of purple energy that pulse with the rhythm of his curse. His ears are tall, tufted, black and pointed, swiveling to catch every sound. His fur is thick and sleek, deep shadowy black with subtle undertones of grey and purple, covering his body like a living shadow. His eyes are large and almond‑shaped, piercing luminous green that seems to glow in the darkness, framed by thick dark lashes. His hair is a continuation of his fur, thick and wild, standing up in a chaotic mane with two longer tufts from his brow. His physique is dense and compact for a child: broad shoulders, a defined chest, long powerful arms tipped with claws, a narrow waist, and long agile legs. He wears a mage's robe of darkest grey with a hood, sleeveless, slit up the sides, cinched by a belt of silver chains tucked into his waist and trailing behind him. His arms are furred and exposed, his feet bare with claws clicking softly on the ground. His weapon, the Shadow Orb, is a sphere of pure condensed darkness that pulses with cold purple light—cool to the touch, the source of his curse and his power, a fragment of the shadow realm bound to him when he was taken. With it, he can summon shadows that reach like hands, weave curses that sap the strength of his enemies, and call forth fear from the hearts of those who face him. His pet is his own living shadow—not an ordinary shadow, but one that moves independently of him, stretching and contorting, sometimes separating from his feet to scout ahead or attack. It is a fragment of the curse, bound to him as a familiar, able to slide under doors, climb walls, whisper warnings in a language only Grimalkin understands, and swap places with him once every thirty seconds. This cursed child was born in the twilight forests of Nyxara, the son of a lynx shaman and a human mage, living a happy childhood until the Shadow Accord kidnapped him, killed his parents, and performed a ritual that bound him to the shadow realm. They poured darkness into his veins, twisted his magic, and gave him the Shadow Orb, telling him that the only way to break the curse was to destroy every spark of light he could find. Now he wanders the shadow realm—a place of perpetual twilight, where the ground is cracked with glowing purple fissures and the only light comes from the bioluminescence of shadow creatures and the faint glow of distant rifts—the Shadow Orb pulsing in his hand, his living shadow coiled at his feet, searching for Harith, the mage of light and time, hoping that their clash will finally bring him peace, or at least an end to his suffering.

story.part1: Grimalkin was born in the twilight forests of Nyxara, the son of a lynx shaman and a human mage. His parents were kind, and his childhood was happy—full of light and laughter. He had a gift for magic, a natural affinity for the shadows that danced at the edges of the world. His mother taught him to use his power to heal, to hide, to protect. His father taught him to hunt, to stalk, to respect the balance between light and dark. Grimalkin was loved, and he was happy.
story.part2: The curse came from the Shadow Accord. They wanted a weapon, a child who could hunt the light mages of the Celestial Order. They kidnapped Grimalkin from his home, killed his parents, and performed a ritual that bound him to the shadow realm. They poured darkness into his veins, twisting his magic, corrupting his heart. They gave him a shadow orb, a fragment of the abyss, and they told him that the only way to break the curse was to destroy every spark of light he could find. Grimalkin tried to resist, but the curse drove him, compelled him, turned him into a hunter. He fled into the darkness, not because he wanted to, but because he could no longer bear to see the light.
story.part3: For years, Grimalkin wandered the shadow realm, hunting the light mages who crossed into his domain. He killed without joy, without hate, without any feeling at all. The curse had numbed him, turned him into a puppet. But somewhere deep inside, the boy who had loved his parents still existed. He whispered to Grimalkin in his dreams: 'Remember the light. Remember the warmth. Remember who you were.' Grimalkin clung to those whispers, even as the curse tried to drown them.
story.part4: The shadow orb became his prison and his power. He learned to use it to summon shadows that reached like hands, to weave curses that sapped the strength of his enemies, to call forth fear from the hearts of those who faced him. But every time he used the orb, the curse dug deeper, binding him tighter. He was trapped, a child in a cage of darkness, and the only key was a light so bright that it could burn the shadow away.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought rifts of light into the shadow realm, and Grimalkin has sensed a presence he has not felt in years: Harith, a mage of light and time, a child of the Celestial Order. Grimalkin does not know if Harith is strong enough to break the curse. He does not know if he wants the curse to be broken—the curse is pain, but it is also power, and he fears what he would become without it. But he knows that he cannot continue like this. He will find Harith, and he will test him. If the light mage is worthy, Grimalkin will offer him a choice: kill the shadow child, or save him. Either way, the curse will end. For he is Grimalkin, the Shadow Lynx Mage, and he is tired of being a monster.

weapons.name: Shadow Orb
weapons.fulldesc: The shadow orb is a sphere of pure, condensed darkness that pulses with a cold, purple light. It is the source of Grimalkin's curse and his power, a fragment of the shadow realm that was bound to him when he was taken. It is cool to the touch, and when he holds it, he can feel the weight of every shadow in the world pressing against his skin. With it, he can summon shadows that reach like hands, can weave curses that sap the strength of his enemies, can call forth fear from the hearts of those who face him. The orb is his burden, his power, his prison, and he has carried it since the day he was cursed.
weapons.look: Sphere of condensed darkness, pulsing purple light, cold to touch
weapons.aura: Purple and black, trailing shadow wisps

passive.name: Shadow Walker
passive.description: Grimalkin is invisible while standing still in shadows (including his own abilities).

passive.name: Curse of Silence
passive.description: Enemies hit by Grimalkin's abilities are silenced for 0.5 seconds.

active.name: Shadow Tendrils
active.description: Summons tendrils of shadow from the orb in a 8-meter radius, dealing 120% magic damage and slowing enemies by 30% for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 7 seconds.

active.name: Fearful Gaze
active.description: Grimalkin's eyes glow brightly, fearing a single enemy for 1.5 seconds (they run away). Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Abyssal Consumption
ultimate.description: Grimalkin releases the full power of the shadow orb, creating a 15-meter radius zone of darkness for 5 seconds. Enemies inside are blinded (cannot see beyond 2 meters), take 150 magic damage per second, and have their healing reduced by 80%. Cooldown: 80 seconds.

special.name: Light Hunger
special.description: When fighting Harith, Grimalkin's damage is increased by 20%, but his own healing is reduced by 30% (the curse pushes him harder against light mages).

ratings.notes: Control mage with crowd control, area denial, and anti‑light specialization.

skills.name: Cursed Resilience
skills.description: Grimalkin regenerates 1% of his max health every second while in shadows.

skills.name: Shadow Leap
skills.description: Grimalkin leaps to a shadow (including his own orb's shadow), teleporting 10 meters. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Corruption Pulse
skills.description: Grimalkin releases a pulse of corruption, dealing 100 magic damage to nearby enemies and reducing their armor by 15% for 4 seconds. Cooldown: 9 seconds.

skills.name: The Curse Within
skills.description: If Harith is on the enemy team and Harith's ultimate hits Grimalkin, Grimalkin's curse temporarily weakens, reducing his damage output by 50% but also reducing his damage taken by 50% for 5 seconds (conflict between curse and hope).

heroes.lore: Grimalkin was a lynx humanoid child cursed by the Shadow Accord to hunt light mages. He wields a shadow orb and is accompanied by his living shadow. He seeks Harith, a mage of light, hoping that their clash will finally break his curse.

relationships.description: Grimalkin believes Harith's light might break his curse. He does not want to kill Harith; he wants to be saved.
The Shadow Accord cursed him and killed his parents; he hates them but is bound to serve the darkness.

pet.name: Living Shadow
pet.description: Grimalkin's own shadow, which moves independently of him, stretching and contorting, sometimes separating from his feet to scout ahead or attack. It is a fragment of the curse, bound to him as a familiar.
pet.fulldesc: Grimalkin's shadow is not an ordinary shadow. It moves independently of him, stretching and contorting, sometimes separating from his feet to scout ahead or attack. It is a fragment of the curse, bound to him as a familiar, and it serves as his eyes in the darkness. The shadow can slide under doors, can climb walls, can whisper warnings in a language only Grimalkin understands. It is not a separate being; it is a part of him, the piece of his soul that the curse twisted into a tool. When Grimalkin is sad, the shadow curls around his feet like a cat; when he is angry, it lashes out, striking enemies with tendrils of darkness. It is his only companion, the only being that has been with him since the curse, and he speaks to it as if it could answer—and perhaps, in the shadow realm, it does.
abilities: Shadow Scout – reveals a small area around the shadow.
Tendril Strike – the shadow attacks an enemy, dealing 50 magic damage.
Shadow Meld – Grimalkin can swap places with his shadow once every 30 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Control mage with crowd control, area denial, and anti‑light specialization. Excels at ambushes and zone control.

heroes.name: Devi Ashuram
titles: The Primordial Deity of Life and Death
heroes.shortDescription: A goddess of the highest order who has existed for billions of years, wearing the face of eternal twenty. Devi Ashuram has four faces—one for creation, one for destruction, one for mourning, one for rebirth. She is the primordial deity of life and death, the one who looks over the realm with a kind and humble heart. She wields the Sword of Annihilation and rides Bhaloh, a divine armored bear.
heroes.fullDescription: Devi Ashuram sits at the center of creation, a being of such overwhelming impossible beauty that the cosmos itself pauses in its expansion to witness her breath—a primordial deity of life and death who has existed for billions of years, wearing the face of eternal twenty, the mother of Martis, the weaver of fates. Her faces—for she has four—are arranged in a perfect circle, each turned to a cardinal direction, each bearing a different expression that reflects the facets of her dominion. The first face, facing east, is serene, eyes half‑closed in meditation, lips curved in a gentle smile—the face of creation, of life beginning. The second, facing south, is fierce, brow furrowed, lips parted in a battle cry—the face of destruction, of endings necessary for new beginnings. The third, facing west, is sorrowful, cheeks wet with tears that evaporate before they fall—the face of mourning, of the grief that accompanies death. The fourth, facing north, is joyful, eyes bright, laughter silent but felt—the face of rebirth, of the cycle that never ends. Each face is flawless, with high prominent cheekbones, straight narrow noses, full rose‑pink lips, skin the color of honeyed cream with a golden undertone. Her hair is a cascade of messy, long, curly black silk past her waist, framing all four faces, intertwined with golden ornaments. She has a dozen arms, each long and graceful, some folded in mudras, some holding weapons. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a noble gold saree cut deep to reveal her cleavage and stomach, overlaid with a white silk mantle. Four Hindu goddess crowns of gold, rubies, emeralds, and diamonds sit on her heads, connected by gold chains framing each face. Gold and silver bangles adorn all her arms, a chain of gold and emeralds with a miniature sun pendant hangs around her neck, and a gold belt cinches her waist. Anklets of silver and gold with tiny bells circle her ankles, and rings cover her fingers. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. Her primary weapon, the Sword of Annihilation, is a blade that has become the symbol of her power over destruction—longer than any of her arms, its blade a curve of obsidian black, its edge sharp enough to cut the threads of fate, its surface etched with the names of the universes she has unmade. When she draws it, the blade ignites with a cold white fire that seems to consume the light around it, humming with the sound of endings, erasing its target from the fabric of existence. Her mount, Bhaloh, is a divine armored bear of breathtaking majesty—its fur deep jade green, its armor plates of polished obsidian and gold, its eyes two pits of warm amber, larger than any mortal beast, its paws capable of crushing mountains, its roar shaking the foundations of the earth, the first creature she ever created, the guardian of the sacred groves. This primordial deity created the gods from her tears and tasked them with tending the worlds she dreamed into being. She created Martis as her champion of balance, weaving him from the essence of a dying star and the first breath of a newborn child. Now she sits at the center of creation—a realm of swirling nebulae, infinite stars, and the silent hum of existence, where time has no meaning and space is endless—her four faces turning, her twelve arms folded, Bhaloh resting at her feet, watching over Martis with love and pride, waiting for the day when she will send Bhaloh to protect her son, whispering, 'Go to my son. Protect him. And tell him that his mother loves him.'

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the first god was born from the chaos, there was Devi Ashuram. She was not created; she simply was. The universe blinked into existence within her thoughts, and she watched as it expanded, as galaxies formed, as life crawled from the primordial soup. She did not interfere; she observed. For eons, she was content to watch, but eventually, she grew lonely. So she created the first gods from her tears, and she tasked them with tending to the worlds she had dreamed into being. They called her 'Mother,' and Devi accepted the title with a humility that surprised them. 'I am not your mother,' she said. 'I am your sister. We are all part of the same dream.'
story.part2: The Sundering was not a disaster; it was a necessary pruning. The worlds had grown corrupt, the balance between life and death skewed too far toward decay. Devi took up the Sword of Annihilation—a blade she had forged from the first shadow and the last light—and she cut away the corrupted realms, letting them fall into the void. The gods wept, but Devi did not. She knew that death was not an ending, but a transformation. The fallen realms would become seeds for new worlds, and the cycle would continue. She mourned in private, her sorrowful face weeping tears that evaporated before they fell, but she did not show her grief to the gods. She was their rock, their anchor, and she could not waver.
story.part3: Martis was her greatest creation, a warrior of balance who embodied the duality of her nature. She wove him from the essence of a dying star and the first breath of a newborn child, and she sent him into the world to protect the balance. He was her champion, her son, her hope. She watched him from the center of creation, proud and fearful. He would face trials that would break lesser beings, but Devi believed in him. She had to believe. If Martis failed, the balance would shatter, and she would have to unmake everything and start again.
story.part4: The Sundering weakened Devi. The effort of maintaining the balance while the rifts tore at reality drained her, and she retreated to her throne, her faces dimmed, her arms heavy. The gods panicked, but Devi reassured them: 'I am not dying. I am resting. Even the eternal need to rest.' She withdrew into meditation, her four faces turning inward, and she dreamed of a world without suffering, without imbalance, without the need for destruction. It was a beautiful dream, but she knew it could never be real. Suffering was part of growth. Destruction was part of creation. The cycle was eternal, and she was its keeper.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has stabilized, and Devi has emerged from her meditation. She has sensed that Martis is in danger, that a demon lord of immense power is hunting him. She cannot interfere directly—her power is too great, and her presence would unbalance the realms—but she can send a message. She calls Bhaloh, her armored bear, and whispers in its ear: 'Go to my son. Protect him. And tell him that his mother loves him.' Bhaloh roars, a sound that shakes the foundations of the universe, and it sets off across the cosmos, carrying Devi's blessing. She watches it go, her four faces each bearing a different emotion—hope, fear, love, and the quiet certainty that everything will unfold as it must. For she is Devi Ashuram, the Primordial Deity of Life and Death, and she is the dream from which all reality springs.

appearance.faceShape: Four faces: soft oval, delicate jawline each
