weapons.name: Gluttony
weapons.fulldesc: Gluttony is a massive, bulky gauntlet that has become the symbol of Halal's sin‑eating power. The gauntlet is double the size of his leg, encasing his entire right arm from shoulder to fingertips, its surfaces a chaotic blend of black iron, red gold, and the faces of the damned. The fingers are articulated, each one tipped with a claw that can tear the soul from a body, and the palm is hollow, a void that devours the sins of the wicked. When he places his gauntlet over the heart of a sinner, the Gluttony awakens, its jaws—for it has a mouth in the palm—opening wide to consume the soul, the sin, the very essence of the wrongdoer. The gauntlet pulses with a hungry, red light when it feeds, and with each soul, it grows stronger, more powerful, more terrible. Halal has wielded the Gluttony for centuries, and it has never been sated.
weapons.look: Massive black iron and red gold gauntlet, faces of the damned etched on surface, mouth in palm, clawed fingers

passive.name: Sin Eater
passive.description: Halal gains 5% damage bonus for each stack of Sin he has consumed. Max 10 stacks (50%). Stacks reset on death.

passive.name: Devouring Palm
passive.description: Halal's basic attacks with the Gluttony heal him for 10% of damage dealt.

active.name: Soul Devour
active.description: Halal grabs an enemy with the Gluttony, dealing 200% physical damage and healing for 30% of damage dealt. If the target dies, Halal gains a stack of Sin. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Flame Burst
active.description: Unleashes a burst of hellfire in a 8-meter cone, dealing 150% magic damage and applying a burn for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

ultimate.name: Feast of the Damned
ultimate.description: Halal consumes all Sin stacks, gaining 30% damage reduction, 50% lifesteal, and 30% movement speed for 5 seconds, plus an additional 1 second per stack consumed. Cooldown: 80 seconds.

special.name: Wicked Hunter
special.description: Halal's abilities deal 25% bonus damage to demon-type enemies and 15% bonus damage to enemies with 'sinner' lore tags.

ratings.notes: Bruiser with sustain, scaling damage, and anti-demon specialization.

skills.name: Infernal Resilience
skills.description: Halal gains 10 armor and magic resist for each stack of Sin he has.

skills.name: Demon Step
skills.description: Halal teleports to a target enemy, dealing 80 physical damage and slowing them by 30% for 2 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Hellfire Aura
skills.description: Halal surrounds himself with hellfire for 4 seconds, dealing 60 magic damage per second to nearby enemies. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

skills.name: The Bargain
skills.description: When fighting Aldous, Halal cannot use Feast of the Damned (wants to see Aldous's path). If Aldous is an ally, Halal gains 10% damage reduction and Aldous gains 10% damage bonus.

heroes.lore: Halal was a knight whose wife was murdered by a demon. He sold his soul for the power to punish the wicked and became a Sin Eater, a demon who hunts demons. He wields the Gluttony gauntlet and is accompanied by Slither, a fire snake. He watches over Aldous, hoping the young hunter will not make the same bargain.

relationships.description: Halal sees himself in Aldous and hopes the young hunter does not fall to the same darkness.
Her murder drove Halal to damnation; her vertebrae are woven into his necklace.

pet.name: Slither
pet.description: A red fire snake of terrifying majesty, scales deep blood red, body covered in small curved horns, back adorned with wings of translucent membrane crackling with fire. Moves with silent sinuous grace, tongue flicking, tasting guilt in the air.
pet.fulldesc: Slither is a red fire snake, its scales a deep, blood red, its body covered in small, curved horns, its back adorned with wings of translucent membrane that crackle with fire when it flies. It moves with a silent, sinuous grace, its tongue flicking, tasting the guilt in the air, and when Halal points, it strikes, its fangs injecting a venom that burns the soul as well as the flesh. Slither is not merely a pet; it is the only being that has never judged him, the only creature that has stayed by his side through the centuries of his damnation. It coils around his neck when he rests, its scales warm against his skin, and when he goes to war, it flies ahead, a red blur against the black sky, a warning to the wicked that the Sin Eater is coming. When Halal is sad, Slither wraps around his arm, its fiery wings dimming, and he feels the warmth of the only love he has left.
abilities: Venom Strike – applies a burn that deals 50 magic damage per second for 4 seconds.
Fire Flight – Slither flies, revealing enemies within 10 meters.
Soul Scent – Slither reveals the location of the enemy with the most kills on the map for 3 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Bruiser with sustain, scaling damage, and anti-demon specialization. Excels at extended fights and punishing sinners.

heroes.name: Ferhren‑Kah
heroes.shortDescription: A pharaoh of ancient Egypt who has transcended the mortal need for conquest and now dedicates his eternal reign to the salvation of the living. Ferhren‑Kah is the nemesis of Khufra, the cursed pharaoh who embraced the darkness. He wields floating Egyptian symbols—the Ankh, the Eye of Horus, the Djed pillar, the Was scepter—and is accompanied by Seikhin Jarur, a royal black cat with gold accessories.
heroes.fullDescription: Ferhren‑Kah rises from the golden sands like a promise whispered by the gods, a being of such regal benevolent power that the desert itself blooms in his presence—a pharaoh of ancient Egypt who has transcended the mortal need for conquest and now dedicates his eternal reign to the salvation of the living, the nemesis of Khufra, the cursed pharaoh who embraced the darkness. His face is a study in divine weathered beauty: a strong square jaw, high prominent cheekbones, a straight aristocratic nose, and full warm lips perpetually curved in a gentle knowing smile. His skin is the color of burnished gold, smooth and warm, with a faint jade undertone that seems to glow from within. His eyes are large and almond‑shaped, clear luminous jade green, the color of the Nile at the moment of the flood. His hair is long and voluminous, a cascade of rich brown curls past his shoulders, shot through with strands of gold. His beard is long and full, braided in the style of old pharaohs, adorned with tiny jade beads. His physique is bulky: broad shoulders, a defined chest, thick powerful arms, massive hands, a narrow waist, and long strong legs. He wears sarcophagus armor of gold and jade green—plates overlapping, with a white linen robe over it, open at the chest. His arms are armored, his shoulders protected by armored pauldrons. A collar of gold and jade circles his neck, and a gold belt cinches his waist. A scarab crown of pure gold with jade gems, the beetle's wings spread, sits on his head. His feet are bare, floating above the sand. Around him, floating in a slow eternal orbit, are the Sacred Symbols—an Ankh, the Eye of Horus, the Djed pillar, and the Was scepter, each forged from pure gold and jade, each glowing with soft green light. With the Ankh, he can grant eternal life, heal the sick, raise the spirits of the departed to guide the living. With the Was scepter, he can command the sands, summon storms of gold that blind his enemies, raise barriers of light that no darkness can pierce. His pet, Seikhin Jarur, is a royal black cat—its fur deep lustrous black, its body adorned with gold accessories and Egyptian jewelry: a collar of lapis and gold, anklets of jade, a small golden scarab on its forehead, its eyes two points of jade green. It can speak, in a voice like the rustle of papyrus, and it serves as his link to the gods, capable of seeing through illusions, walking through walls of shadow, and firing beams of golden light from its eyes. This pharaoh was chosen by the sun god Ra to be his vessel of warmth and salvation, granted immortality and the power to command the sands. His brother Khufra grew jealous, made a pact with a demon, and became a cursed pharaoh. Ferhren‑Kah sealed Khufra in a tomb beneath the sands and has waited millennia for his brother's redemption. Now he stands at the entrance of Khufra's tomb in the Great Desert—a vast expanse of golden dunes, ancient ruins half‑buried in sand, and the relentless heat of the sun, with the Sacred Symbols circling him and Seikhin Jarur at his side—waiting to offer his brother warmth one more time, hoping that even the darkest soul can be saved.

story.part1: Ferhren‑Kah was not born; he was chosen. The sun god Ra, seeing the corruption spreading across Egypt, selected a mortal pharaoh to be his vessel of warmth and salvation. Ferhren‑Kah was a just ruler, beloved by his people, and he accepted the blessing with humility. He was granted immortality, the power to command the sands, and the floating symbols that would become his weapons. He ruled Egypt for a thousand years, his reign a golden age of peace and prosperity. The people called him 'The Warm Father,' and they built temples in his honor.
story.part2: Khufra was his brother, a prince who grew jealous of Ferhren‑Kah's blessing. Khufra sought power from darker sources, making a pact with a demon of the Shadow Accord. He became a cursed pharaoh, his body withering, his heart turning to stone. He raised armies of the dead and marched on Ferhren‑Kah's capital. The battle lasted a hundred days, and in the end, Ferhren‑Kah sealed Khufra in a tomb deep beneath the sands, hoping that his brother would one day find redemption. He wept as the tomb closed, his tears turning to jade.
story.part3: The Sundering cracked Khufra's tomb, and the cursed pharaoh emerged, more powerful than ever. Ferhren‑Kah felt the darkness stirring and rose from his golden throne. He had spent millennia in meditation, communing with Ra, learning to channel the sun's warmth not as a weapon, but as a healing light. He would not destroy Khufra; he would save him, if possible. He gathered his floating symbols—the Ankh for life, the Eye of Horus for protection, the Djed pillar for stability, the Was scepter for command—and set out to find his brother.
story.part4: Seikhin Jarur was his constant companion, a black cat who had been with him since the first day of his reign. The cat was not merely a pet; it was a goddess in feline form, a gift from Bastet, the goddess of home and hearth. Seikhin Jarur could speak, in a voice like the rustle of papyrus, and it offered Ferhren‑Kah counsel when the path was unclear. It could see through illusions, could walk through walls of shadow, and could fire beams of golden light from its eyes. Ferhren‑Kah trusted the cat more than any of his human advisors, and it had never failed him.
story.part5: Now, Ferhren‑Kah stands at the entrance of Khufra's tomb, the floating symbols circling him, Seikhin Jarur at his side. He knows that his brother is waiting inside, consumed by darkness, beyond reason. He knows that words may not reach Khufra, that the cursed pharaoh may only respond to force. But Ferhren‑Kah will not raise his weapons first. He will offer Khufra the same warmth he has offered all who seek it. He will say, 'Brother, the sun still rises. Will you not feel its warmth?' And if Khufra attacks, Ferhren‑Kah will defend himself, but he will not strike to kill. He will strike to heal, to seal, to give his brother one more chance. For he is Ferhren‑Kah, the Pharaoh of Eternal Warmth, and he believes that even the darkest soul can be saved.

weapons.name: The Sacred Symbols
weapons.fulldesc: Around Ferhren‑Kah, floating in a slow, eternal orbit, are an Ankh and a collection of Egyptian symbols—the Eye of Horus, the Djed pillar, the Was scepter—each one forged from pure gold and jade, each one glowing with a soft, green light. These symbols are his weapons and his shields, his barriers and his blessings. With the Ankh, he can grant eternal life, can heal the sick, can raise the spirits of the departed to guide the living. With the Was scepter, he can command the sands, can summon storms of gold that blind his enemies, can raise barriers of light that no darkness can pierce. The symbols circle him like moons around a planet, and when he raises his hand, they respond, forming shields of golden light, firing beams of jade energy, weaving spells of protection and salvation.
weapons.look: Ankh, Eye of Horus, Djed pillar, Was scepter, gold and jade, glowing green

passive.name: Sun's Warmth
passive.description: Allies within 12 meters of Ferhren‑Kah gain 15 health regeneration per second and 10% damage reduction.

passive.name: Jade Blessing
passive.description: Ferhren‑Kah's abilities heal him for 10% of damage dealt to enemies.

active.description: Heals an ally for 250 health plus 15% of their max health and removes all debuffs. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Was Scepter Blast

ultimate.name: Eternal Warmth
ultimate.description: Ferhren‑Kah channels for 2 seconds, then creates a 25-meter radius zone of golden light for 8 seconds. Allies inside are immune to death (cannot fall below 1 HP), heal for 15% of their max health per second, and deal 20% bonus damage. Enemies inside are slowed by 40% and take 100 magic damage per second. Cooldown: 100 seconds.

special.name: Brother's Redemption
special.description: When Khufra is an ally (redeemed timeline), Ferhren‑Kah's healing on Khufra is doubled. When Khufra is an enemy, Ferhren‑Kah's damage against him is reduced by 40% (cannot bring himself to hurt him), but his healing on other allies is increased by 20%.

ratings.notes: Support tank with healing, vision, area denial, and team invincibility ultimate.

skills.name: Golden Aura
skills.description: Ferhren‑Kah's basic attacks heal the nearest ally for 30 health.

skills.name: Sand Shield
skills.description: Creates a shield of sand around an ally that absorbs 300 damage and reflects 15% of damage back to attackers. Lasts 5 seconds. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Djed Pillar
skills.description: Summons a pillar of stability at target location, preventing enemies from dashing or blinking through it for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 16 seconds.

skills.name: Pharaoh's Patience
skills.description: If Khufra is on the enemy team and falls below 10% health, Ferhren‑Kah automatically casts Ankh of Life on him (once per match).

heroes.lore: Ferhren‑Kah is the Pharaoh of Eternal Warmth, blessed by Ra with immortality and the power to heal. His brother Khufra fell to darkness and became a cursed pharaoh. Ferhren‑Kah sealed Khufra in a tomb and now waits for his brother's redemption, wielding the sacred symbols and accompanied by the royal cat Seikhin Jarur.

relationships.description: Ferhren‑Kah loves his brother and seeks to redeem him, not destroy him.
Ra blessed Ferhren‑Kah and communicates with him through the sun.

pet.name: Seikhin Jarur
pet.description: A royal black cat of breathtaking majesty, fur deep lustrous black, adorned with gold accessories and Egyptian jewelry—a collar of lapis and gold, anklets of jade, a small golden scarab on its forehead. Eyes two points of jade green. Can speak in a voice like rustling papyrus.
pet.fulldesc: Seikhin Jarur is a royal black cat of breathtaking majesty, its fur a deep, lustrous black, its body adorned with gold accessories and Egyptian jewelry—a collar of lapis and gold, anklets of jade, a small, golden scarab on its forehead. Its eyes are two points of jade green that mirror Ferhren‑Kah's own. It is not an ordinary cat; it is a goddess in feline form, a gift from Bastet, and it serves as Ferhren‑Kah's link to the gods, his confidant in the long, lonely hours of the night. Seikhin Jarur can speak, in a voice like the rustle of papyrus, and it offers him counsel when the path is unclear. It curls on his shoulder when he rests, its purr a low, comforting hum, and when he goes to war, it walks beside him, its eyes blazing, its claws sharp, a reminder that even the gentlest creatures can be fierce when protecting those they love. When Ferhren‑Kah is sad, Seikhin Jarur rests its head on his hand, and he strokes its fur, feeling the warmth of the goddess's blessing.
abilities: Goddess's Gaze – reveals invisible enemies within 12 meters.
Scarab's Blessing – grants an ally a shield that absorbs 150 damage.
Bastet's Claw – swipes at an enemy, dealing 100 magic damage and applying a bleed.

heroes.specialty: Support tank with healing, vision, area denial, and team invincibility ultimate. Excels at protecting allies and redeeming enemies.

dialogue.selection: The sun warms all who seek it.

heroes.name: Grengar
titles: The Dirge Singer of the Resurrected Flame
heroes.shortDescription: A revenant of Victorian origin who died in the flames of a theater fire and was reborn by the same fire. Grengar is a singer whose voice carries the warmth of resurrection, the far relative of Granger. He wields the Guns of Fire—revolver-shaped instruments that fire notes of joy and happiness—and is accompanied by Indreni, a rainbow-themed nightingale.
heroes.fullDescription: Grengar steps onto the stage of the eternal opera house like a memory of fire given voice, a being of such passionate vibrant beauty that the very notes of the orchestra seem to lean toward him—a revenant of Victorian origin who died in the flames of a theater fire and was reborn by the same fire, a singer whose voice carries the warmth of resurrection, the far relative of Granger. His face is a study in tragic handsome nobility: a strong angular jawline, high prominent cheekbones, a straight aristocratic nose, and full warm lips perpetually parted as if about to sing. His skin is fair and smooth, with a faint golden undertone, warm to the touch. His eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep glowing ember, the color of the fire that claimed him and then gave him back. His hair is a fiery voluminous cascade of short crimson curls, styled in a tapper fade. His physique is lean, built for the stage. He wears a tattered tuxedo of deep rich red, singed at the edges, over a white shirt stained with ash, the collar undone and sleeves rolled to his elbows. A half face mask of black leather covers the left side of his face, etched with musical notes, and a brass and glass corrective lens sits over his right eye. Black trousers cover his legs, and scuffed black shoes cover his feet. His weapons, the Guns of Fire, are a pair of ornate revolver‑shaped instruments that fire not bullets but notes of joy and happiness—crafted from polished brass and red steel, their barrels etched with musical scores, their chambers loaded with paper cartridges containing concentrated bursts of melody. When he pulls the trigger, the gun emits a soft musical note—a chord of pure unadulterated joy that resonates in the hearts of all who hear it. He can fire single notes that heal the wounded, chords that lift the spirits of the despondent, a full symphony that brings tears of happiness to the eyes of the most hardened souls. His pet, Indreni, is a rainbow‑themed nightingale of breathtaking beauty—her feathers shifting through the spectrum as she moves: deep red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet, her voice a melody that rivals Grengar's own. She can sing a rainbow aria that heals allies, unleash a color burst that blinds enemies, and harmonize with Grengar to increase his healing. This dirge singer was the most celebrated tenor of the Victorian era, performing at the Royal Opera House until a fire started in the wings. He had time to escape but heard a child screaming from the balcony, ran into the flames, threw the child to safety, and died when the rafters collapsed. The fire that killed him also gave him back—a spirit of resurrection breathed life into his charred body, and his voice now carried the warmth of the flames, able to heal as well as harm. Now he stands on the stage of the eternal opera house—a realm of crimson curtains, gold leaf, and eternal twilight, where the audience is made of shadows and the orchestra plays without musicians—the Guns of Fire in his hands, Indreni on his shoulder, searching for his cousin Granger, hoping to harmonize joy and sorrow into a symphony that the world has been waiting for.

story.part1: Grengar was the most celebrated tenor of the Victorian era, a man whose voice could make angels weep and audiences swoon. He performed at the Royal Opera House, his name on every lip, his face on every poster. His cousin Granger was a violinist, a prodigy whose music was beautiful but melancholic. Grengar loved his cousin, but he could not understand why Granger always chose sorrow over joy. 'Life is short,' Grengar would say. 'Sing while you can.' Granger would nod, but his music never changed.
story.part2: The fire started in the wings, a spark from a gas lamp that spread faster than anyone could react. Grengar was on stage, mid-aria, when the curtains caught. He had time to escape, but he heard a child screaming from the balcony. He ran into the flames, found the child, and threw her to safety. The rafters collapsed on him, and Grengar died, his voice silenced forever. Or so they thought.
story.part3: The fire that killed him also gave him back. A spirit of resurrection, drawn by his sacrifice, breathed life into his charred body. Grengar rose from the ashes, his skin healed, his voice restored—but changed. His voice now carried the warmth of the flames, a heat that could heal as well as harm. He could sing life into the dying, could play music that lifted the spirits of the despondent, could unleash a war cry that filled his allies with courage. He became the Dirge Singer, not of sorrow, but of joy.
story.part4: The Guns of Fire were crafted by a gunsmith who had also died in the fire, a man who owed Grengar his life. The guns were not weapons of destruction; they were instruments of salvation. Each pull of the trigger fired a note—a chord of pure, unadulterated joy that resonated in the hearts of all who heard it. Grengar could fire single notes that healed the wounded, chords that lifted spirits, a full symphony that brought tears of happiness to the eyes of the most hardened souls. He became a legend, a wandering bard who brought joy to the darkest corners of the world.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought sorrow to every realm, and Grengar has heard that his cousin Granger is performing a dirge that could wake the dead—not to life, but to undeath. He does not want to stop Granger; he wants to harmonize with him. He believes that joy and sorrow are two sides of the same coin, that a true symphony needs both. He sets out to find his cousin, the Guns of Fire in his hands, Indreni on his shoulder, ready to play the duet that the world has been waiting for. For he is Grengar, the Dirge Singer of the Resurrected Flame, and his music is the fire that warms the world.

weapons.name: Guns of Fire
weapons.fulldesc: The Guns of Fire are a pair of ornate, revolver‑shaped instruments that fire not bullets, but notes of joy and happiness. The guns are crafted from polished brass and red steel, their barrels etched with musical scores, their chambers loaded with paper cartridges that contain concentrated bursts of melody. When he pulls the trigger, the gun emits a soft, musical note—a chord of pure, unadulterated joy that resonates in the hearts of all who hear it. He can fire single notes that heal the wounded, can fire chords that lift the spirits of the despondent, can fire a full symphony that brings tears of happiness to the eyes of the most hardened souls. The Guns of Fire are not weapons of destruction; they are instruments of salvation, and he wields them with the precision of a master musician.

special.description: When Granger is an ally, Grengar's healing is increased by 20% and Granger's damage is increased by 15%. When Granger is an enemy, their abilities cannot target each other (they refuse to fight).

skills.name: Warmth of Rebirth
skills.description: Grengar's abilities leave a trail of warmth that heals allies who stand in it for 20 health per second.

skills.name: Fire Step

skills.name: Encouraging Aria

skills.name: The Duet
skills.description: If Granger is on the enemy team, Grengar cannot attack Granger. Instead, he gains 50% movement speed toward Granger and can only heal him, even as an enemy.

heroes.lore: Grengar was a Victorian tenor who died saving a child from a theater fire and was reborn with the power to heal through music. He is the far relative of Granger, the dirge singer, and seeks to harmonize joy and sorrow. He wields the Guns of Fire and is accompanied by the rainbow nightingale Indreni.

relationships.description: Grengar respects Granger's sorrowful music and seeks to create a symphony of both joy and grief.
The nightingale learned to sing from Grengar; they perform as a duet.

pet.name: Indreni
pet.description: A rainbow‑themed nightingale of breathtaking beauty, feathers shift through the spectrum as she moves: deep red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet. Voice a melody that rivals Grengar's own.
pet.fulldesc: Indreni is a rainbow‑themed nightingale of breathtaking beauty, her feathers shifting through the spectrum as she moves, from deep red to bright orange to sunny yellow to forest green to sky blue to indigo to violet, and when she sings, the colors seem to dance in the air. She is not merely a pet; she is Grengar's partner, his duet, the other half of his harmony. She learned to sing from him, and he learned to feel from her, and together, they create music that can raise the dead—not to undeath, but to life. She perches on his shoulder when he rests, her head tucked under her wing, and when he performs, she flies above the stage, her song intertwining with his, a duet of joy that brings tears to the eyes of the gods themselves. When Grengar is sad, Indreni sings softly, and her colors brighten, as if trying to warm him.
abilities: Rainbow Aria – heals allies in a 8-meter radius for 100 health.
Color Burst – blinds enemies in a cone for 1 second.
Harmony – increases Grengar's healing by 20% while Indreni is perched on him.

heroes.specialty: Support mage with healing, buffs, and area control. Excels at keeping allies alive and countering sorrow-based effects.

heroes.name: Rust
titles: The Scrap Tyrant of the Junkyard Wastes
heroes.shortDescription: A humanoid machine who was once flesh and blood and was reforged in the fires of an apocalypse into something that hovers between human and construct. Rust is the parallel of X.Borg, a survivor who carved out a kingdom in the ruins of a dead world. He wields a futuristic shield that assembles junk into weapons and a freeze ray from his machine hand, accompanied by Eco 51, a small scorpion-like machine.
heroes.fullDescription: Rust crawls from the wreckage of a dead world like a memory of industry refusing to fade, a being of such brutal utilitarian beauty that the very scrap he commands seems to bow in his presence—a humanoid machine, a man who was once flesh and blood and was reforged in the fires of an apocalypse into something that hovers between human and construct, the parallel of X.Borg. His face is a study in tragic rugged determination: a strong square jaw, high prominent cheekbones, a straight broad nose, and thin pale lips pressed into a grim focused line. His skin, where visible beneath rusted armor, is a patchwork of pale flesh and dark metal, the seams raised and scarred. His eyes are mismatched: one human, a warm weary brown; the other a mechanical lens of yellow glass, dilating and contracting. A broken futuristic visor half‑helmet is fused to his skull. His hair is gone, replaced by metal plates on his scalp, though stray brown strands remain on his neck. His chest is bare and scarred, with a V‑shaped opening revealing his pectorals. His physique is bulky: broad shoulders, thick powerful arms, one human hand, one machine hand with a laser emitter, a narrow waist, and long strong legs. He wears rusted iron plate armor bolted with crude rivets, a laser cannon on his shoulder, and a futuristic shield on his right arm. His legs are mechanical, his feet clad in heavy armored boots. His weapon, the Shield & Freeze Ray, is a combination of his two hands. His right hand (the human one) holds a futuristic shield of polished silver and black carbon fiber, etched with symbols of the old world, capable of assembling junk into weapons—consuming a pile of debris and extruding a sword, mace, or throwing axe. His left hand (the machine hand) is fused with a futuristic laser machine of intricate patterns, a maze of gears and glowing conduits, capable of firing a freeze ray—a beam of intense cold that can freeze an enemy solid in seconds. His pet, Eco 51, is a small scorpion drone—no larger than a dog, its body a box of rusted metal, its four legs articulated, its tail a flexible tube ending in a laser emitter, its eyes two points of yellow light, moving with skittering unpredictable motion, capable of firing lasers from its tail, climbing walls, slipping through cracks too small for Rust to follow. This scrap tyrant was a factory worker in an industrial city before the apocalypse. Bombs fell, killing his wife and daughter, leaving him buried in rubble. He survived by scavenging, replacing his legs with scrap metal, his arm with a salvaged laser emitter, his eye with a cracked targeting lens. He built a suit of armor from a crashed military transport and carved out a territory in the junkyard wastes. Now he stands in the junkyard wastes—a realm of mountains of rusted metal, skeletal remains of vehicles, and the constant sound of dripping oil and shifting scrap, where the sky is perpetually grey with smog and the only light comes from the crackling of his freeze ray and the glow of Eco 51's laser—his shield raised, his freeze ray charged, waiting for X.Borg, his parallel from another universe, to see if they are allies or rivals.

story.part1: Before the apocalypse, Rust was a factory worker in a sprawling industrial city, a man who spent his days welding metal and his nights dreaming of a better life. He had a wife, a daughter, and a small apartment on the edge of the smog belt. When the bombs fell, he was at work. The explosion threw him through a wall, shattering his legs, burning his skin, destroying everything he had ever known. He crawled through the rubble for three days, searching for his family. He found their bodies in the ruins of his apartment, huddled together, holding each other. Rust screamed, but there was no one left to hear.
story.part2: He survived by scavenging, by repairing, by becoming part of the machines he once built. He replaced his legs with scrap metal, his arm with a salvaged laser emitter, his eye with a cracked targeting lens. He built a suit of armor from the wreckage of a crashed military transport, and he carved out a territory in the junkyard wastes. Other survivors came to him for protection, for repairs, for hope. Rust became their leader, not because he wanted power, but because he was the only one who could build walls strong enough to keep the demons out.
story.part3: The shield on his arm was his greatest creation, a futuristic device that could assemble junk into weapons. With a thought, he could command it to consume a pile of debris and extrude a sword, a mace, a throwing axe—whatever he needed for the fight. The freeze ray in his machine hand was a salvaged piece of cryo-tech, capable of freezing an enemy solid in seconds. Together, the two hands represented his duality: creation and destruction, heat and cold, the human and the machine.
story.part4: Eco 51 was a security drone he found half‑buried in a scrap heap, its systems failing, its power cells drained. Rust rebuilt it, giving it a new body—four scorpion-like legs, a laser tail, and a single yellow eye. The drone became his scout, his sentry, his last line of defense. It could climb walls, could slip through cracks that were too small for him to follow, and it was utterly loyal, having been rebuilt by his own hands from the remains of a machine that died saving his life. Eco 51 was not a pet; it was a partner, the only being that had been with him since the beginning of the junkyard kingdom.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the sky above the junkyard, and demons are pouring through. Rust has heard that X.Borg, a parallel version of himself from another universe, is fighting a similar war. He does not know if X.Borg is an ally or a rival, but he knows that they are connected—two survivors, two machines, two men who refused to die. He raises his shield, charges his freeze ray, and prepares for the fight of his life. For he is Rust, the Scrap Tyrant, and his kingdom is the junkyard, and he will not let anyone take it from him.

weapons.name: Shield & Freeze Ray
weapons.fulldesc: Rust's right hand—the human one—holds a futuristic shield of polished silver and black carbon fiber, its surface etched with the symbols of the old world. The shield is not merely defensive; it can assemble junk into weapons, using the scrap that litters his world to form blades, spears, and even temporary firearms. With a thought, he can command the shield to consume a pile of debris and extrude a sword, a mace, a throwing axe—whatever he needs for the fight. His left hand—the machine hand—is fused with a futuristic laser machine of intricate, detailed patterns, its surface a maze of gears and glowing conduits. This hand can fire a freeze ray, a beam of intense cold that can freeze an enemy solid in seconds, leaving them vulnerable to a shattering blow from his shield‑forged blade. Together, the two hands represent his duality: creation and destruction, heat and cold, the human and the machine.

weapons.aura: Blue cold mist, silver shield glow

ratings.notes: Tanky fighter with crowd control, scrap-based scaling, and weapon assembly.

skills.name: Junkyard King

skills.name: Scrap Shield

skills.name: Magnetic Pull

skills.name: Survivor's Bond
skills.description: When X.Borg is on the enemy team, Rust cannot use his ultimate on X.Borg. Instead, he gains 30% movement speed toward X.Borg and can only use basic attacks on him (honorable duel).

heroes.lore: Rust was a factory worker who survived an apocalypse by replacing his body with machine parts. He built a kingdom in the junkyard wastes and protects the survivors who come to him. He is the parallel of X.Borg, a reflection of what could have been.

relationships.description: Rust respects X.Borg as a fellow survivor and survivor of a different apocalypse.
Rust rebuilt Eco 51 from a dying drone; the machine is fiercely loyal.

pet.name: Eco 51
pet.description: A small machine of terrifying efficiency, no larger than a dog, body a box of rusted metal, four scorpion-like legs articulated, tail a flexible tube ending in a laser emitter. Eyes two points of yellow light. Moves with skittering, unpredictable motion.
pet.fulldesc: Eco 51 is a small machine of terrifying efficiency, no larger than a dog, its body a box of rusted metal, its legs articulated, its tail a flexible tube that ends in a laser emitter. Its eyes are two points of yellow light, and it moves with the skittering, unpredictable motion of an insect. Eco 51 is not merely a pet; it is Rust's scout, his sentry, his last line of defense. It can fire lasers from its tail, can climb walls, can slip through cracks that are too small for him to follow, and it is utterly loyal, having been rebuilt by his own hands from the remains of a machine that died saving his life. It perches on his shoulder when he rests, its legs folded, its laser dim, and when he goes to war, it skitters ahead, its tail raised, a tiny guardian in a world of giants. When Rust is sad, Eco 51 climbs onto his knee and emits a soft, buzzing sound, a mechanical purr that reminds him he is not alone.
abilities: Laser Sting – fires a laser, dealing 100 magic damage.
Scout Mode – reveals enemies within 12 meters.
Wall Climb – can traverse walls, revealing hidden areas.

heroes.specialty: Tanky fighter with crowd control, scrap-based scaling, and weapon assembly. Excels at extended fights and area control.

dialogue.selection: The junkyard survives. So do I.

heroes.name: Feng Xiang
titles: The Storm Herald of the Tibetan Plateau
heroes.shortDescription: A wind spirit of the Tibetan Plateau, a woman whose form is a masterwork of celestial fury and noble grace. Feng Xiang was not always the Storm Herald; she was a woman, a lover, a mother, until the winds took everything from her. She wields the Tempest, a Chinese sword that carries her through the sky, and is accompanied by Foou Xiang, a Chinese guardian lion that rides a cloud.
heroes.fullDescription: Feng Xiang descends from the clouds like a lightning bolt that chose to linger, a being of such tempestuous tragic beauty that the very sky seems to weep in her wake—a wind spirit of the Tibetan Plateau, a woman whose form is a masterwork of celestial fury and noble grace. Her face is a study in ethereal heart‑breaking perfection: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full pale lips pressed into a solemn contemplative line. Her skin is ethereal, human‑like yet luminous as moonlight, smooth and flawless, with a faint cool undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, a swirling tempest of storm‑cloud grey and sapphire blue, framed by thick white lashes. Her hair is a voluminous cascade of pure white, long and curvy, past her waist, floating as if caught in an eternal updraft. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a layered robe of deep grey and black, torn and frayed, cut deep to reveal her cleavage and stomach, with a flowing outer robe of white silk embroidered with lightning bolts. Prayer flags are woven into her sleeves, and wind bells of polished brass hang at her waist. Her arms are bare, with thunder and lightning bead bracelets on her wrists. Her feet are bare, walking on wind. Her weapon, the Tempest, is a sleek Chinese sword (jian) longer than her arm—its blade a curve of polished steel that seems to hum with contained lightning, etched with the patterns of clouds and thunder, its hilt wrapped in white silk, its pommel set with a sphere of amber that glows with soft yellow light. When she draws the Tempest, the blade ignites, crackling with arcs of lightning that dance along its edge, and the sky answers her call. She does not merely wield the sword; she rides it, standing upon its flat surface as it carries her through the air, a lightning bolt given form, a storm given purpose. Her mount, Foou Xiang, is a Chinese guardian lion of breathtaking majesty—its body the color of storm clouds, its mane a cascade of lightning‑yellow fur that crackles with static, its eyes two points of sapphire blue, larger than any mortal beast, riding upon a cloud of its own making, capable of a thunderous roar that stuns enemies, a cloud leap that deals crushing damage, and a lightning mane that damages nearby foes. This storm herald was a village woman on the Tibetan Plateau, married to a yak herder named Tenzin with a daughter. A demon brought a storm unlike any she had seen, and she fought it for three days, but an avalanche killed her husband and daughter. The winds would not let her die; they wrapped around her, lifted her, carried her into the sky, whispering, 'You are the Storm Herald now.' Now she rides the storms above the Tibetan Plateau—a realm of jagged peaks, swirling snow, and eternal grey skies, where the wind howls like a grieving woman and lightning splits the heavens—the Tempest crackling, Foou Xiang roaring, watching over Ling, hoping he does not suffer the same loss, ready to teach him what it truly means to be the storm.

story.part1: Feng Xiang was born in a small village on the Tibetan Plateau, a child of the wind, as the elders called her. She could feel the storms coming days before they arrived, could speak to the clouds in a language only she understood. She was happy, married to a yak herder named Tenzin, and they had a daughter, a girl with eyes like the sky. The village prospered under Feng Xiang's protection; she could calm the storms that threatened their herds, could call rain when the crops were dry. She was loved, and she was content.
story.part2: The demon came from the east, a creature of the Shadow Accord that fed on fear. It brought a storm unlike any Feng Xiang had ever seen—a tempest that did not obey her commands. She fought it for three days and three nights, her body crackling with lightning, her voice hoarse from screaming. In the end, she won, but the storm took its toll. Her husband and daughter, caught in an avalanche caused by the demon's dying thrash, were buried under the snow. Feng Xiang dug with her bare hands until they bled, but she found only their frozen bodies, huddled together, holding each other.
story.part3: She did not weep. The winds wept for her. She carried their bodies to the peak of the highest mountain, laid them to rest, and sat down to die. But the winds would not let her. They wrapped around her, lifted her, carried her into the sky. They whispered to her: 'You are the Storm Herald now. The tempest is your grief. The lightning is your rage. The rain is your tears.' Feng Xiang opened her eyes, and they were no longer human. They were storms themselves, swirling with grey and blue. She rose from the peak, a sword of lightning in her hand, and she became the legend that travelers whisper about around campfires: the Storm Herald, the woman who rides the tempest, the mother who mourns forever.
story.part4: Foou Xiang found her in the clouds, a guardian lion of jade grey and lightning yellow, riding a cloud of its own making. The lion had been sent by the mountain gods to protect her, to keep her from fading into the storm. Feng Xiang did not want a companion, but the lion was persistent. It curled beside her when she rested, its fur warm against her cold skin, and it roared when she was sad, a sound like thunder that shook the clouds. She learned to accept its presence, and the lion became her only friend.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the sky, and demons are pouring through the rifts. Feng Xiang rides the storms, her sword crackling, her lion roaring, and she fights not for revenge, but for protection. She will not let any other mother lose her child to the darkness. She has heard of Ling, the wind dancer, a young man who moves with the grace of a leaf. She does not envy him; she grieves for him, for he has not yet learned that the wind can cut as well as caress. She will watch over him, and when he faces a storm he cannot outrun, she will be there, her sword raised, her lion roaring, to teach him what it truly means to be the storm. For she is Feng Xiang, the Storm Herald, and her grief is the engine that drives the tempest.

heroes.lore: Feng Xiang was a village woman who lost her husband and daughter to a demon storm. The winds transformed her into the Storm Herald, a spirit of grief and fury. She now protects the Tibetan Plateau, wielding the Tempest and accompanied by the guardian lion Foou Xiang.

relationships.description: Feng Xiang sees herself in Ling and watches over him, hoping he does not suffer the same loss.
His death fuels her grief, which powers her storms.

mount.name: Foou Xiang
mount.description: A Chinese guardian lion of breathtaking majesty, body the color of storm clouds, mane a cascade of lightning‑yellow fur crackling with static, eyes two points of sapphire blue. Rides upon a cloud of its own making, a swirling mass of grey and white.
mount.fulldesc: Foou Xiang is a Chinese guardian lion of breathtaking majesty, its body the color of storm clouds, its mane a cascade of lightning‑yellow fur that crackles with static, its eyes two points of sapphire blue that mirror Feng Xiang's own. It is larger than any mortal beast, its paws capable of crushing boulders, its roar a sound that shakes the foundations of the earth. It rides upon a cloud of its own making, a swirling mass of grey and white that carries it through the sky with the grace of a dancer. Foou Xiang is not merely a mount; it is her protector, her confidant, the only being that has been with her since the tragedy that made her the Storm Herald. It curls beside her when she rests, its head on her knee, its fur warm against her skin, and when she goes to war, it roars, a sound like thunder, a warning to all who would dare to face the storm. When Feng Xiang cries, Foou Xiang's mane dims, and it nuzzles her, as if trying to absorb her grief.

heroes.name: Mayine
heroes.shortDescription: A being of such ancient, elemental grace that the very air around her hums with the resonance of a thousand incantations. Mayine is the Queen of Witches, the architect of the magical order in her universe. She wields Golemon, a staff that summons elemental golems, and carries the Book of Unknown, an ancient tome containing forbidden magic. Her companion is Snowy, a massive snow owl with a tiny pointed witch hat.
heroes.fullDescription: Mayine descends from the mist‑veiled Carpathians like a forbidden spell given form, a being of such ancient elemental grace that the very air around her hums with the resonance of a thousand incantations—the Queen of Witches, the architect of the magical order in her universe, a woman whose beauty is as eternal as the magic she wove into the fabric of her world. Her face is a study in timeless aristocratic perfection: a soft heart‑shaped visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full rose‑pink lips perpetually parted as if whispering a spell. Her skin is healthy and human‑like yet luminous, the color of warm cream, smooth and flawless, with a faint ethereal glow. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep luminous purple, the color of twilight and forbidden knowledge, framed by thick pink lashes. A purple corrective monocle lens rests on her right eye. Her hair is a cascade of soft pastel pink, long and voluminous, styled in an elaborate butterfly shape with curtain layers, held by tiny silver crescent moon hairpins. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a slit gown of pale pink and pristine white, cut deep at the chest with silver crescent moon clasps, revealing her cleavage and stomach. A short white fur cape hangs from her shoulders. Her arms are bare, her feet bare. A tall pointed witch hat of white velvet with a purple band and a small silver bell sits on her head. On her back are vast white snow‑owl wings. In her hand, she carries the Book of Unknown—a tome bound in leather so dark it seems to absorb light, its cover embossed with silver symbols that shift and change as she reads, containing the sum of all magical knowledge, including spells too dangerous for mortal hands. Her weapon, Golemon, is a staff of mythical ancient power—taller than she is, its shaft a twisted length of petrified wood wrapped in silver wire, carved with the faces of elementals and the symbols of primal forces. At its head, a cluster of crystals—pink, purple, white—floats within a cage of silver, each pulsing with the essence of a different element. When she strikes the ground, the crystals flare, and from the earth rise golems of living stone, from the air coalesce spirits of wind, from the flames leap guardians of fire, from the waters flow sentinels of the deep. Her pet, Snowy, is a massive snow owl—her feathers pristine white fading to soft brown at the wingtips, her eyes two pools of molten gold, wearing a tiny pointed witch hat of her own between her ear tufts, capable of speaking in a voice like wind through pine needles, shattering illusions with her shriek, and serving as Mayine's eyes in the unseen realms. This queen of witches was the first to draw a magic circle, the first to command the elements, the first to bind a spirit to her will, inventing witchcraft itself. She wrote the Book of Unknown over a thousand years, sealing dangerous spells with curses that would only break for the worthy. Now she stands in her tower of crystal and silver in the mist‑veiled Carpathians—a realm of ancient forests, fog‑shrouded peaks, and the constant whisper of magic, where the walls are lined with glowing crystals and the air smells of ozone and old parchment—Golemon humming, Snowy on her shoulder, the Book of Unknown floating beside her, ready to seal the largest Sundering rift with her magic, weaving new spells to stabilize the elements.

story.part1: Before the Sundering, before the gods learned to weave spells, there was Mayine. She was the first to draw a magic circle, the first to command the elements, the first to bind a spirit to her will. She did not learn from anyone; she invented the art of witchcraft herself, pulling knowledge from the spaces between thoughts, from the whispers of the void, from the dreams of sleeping stars. The other witches who came after her called her 'Mother,' and Mayine accepted the title with a smile. She taught them the elemental circles, the forbidden incantations, the art of binding golems. She was a patient teacher, but she was also a stern one. 'Magic is not a toy,' she would say. 'It is a responsibility. Use it wisely, or not at all.'
story.part2: The Book of Unknown was her greatest creation, a tome that contained the sum of all magical knowledge. She wrote it over a thousand years, her hand never tiring, her ink never fading. The book was bound in leather so dark it seemed to absorb light, its cover embossed with silver symbols that shifted and changed as she read. She intended the book to be a guide, a source of wisdom for future generations. But some of the spells she wrote were too dangerous for mortal hands, and she sealed them with curses that would only break for the worthy. The book became a legend, a thing of fear and wonder, and Mayine guarded it with her life.
story.part3: The Sundering tore the fabric of reality, and Mayine felt the elemental circles waver. She retreated to her tower of crystal and silver, Snowy on her arm, the Book of Unknown floating beside her. She spent a century in meditation, weaving new spells to stabilize the elements, binding the chaos with her will. When she emerged, she found that the world had changed. New magic had sprung up, wild and untamed, and the witches she had taught had scattered, some to the light, some to the darkness. Mayine did not judge them. She had created the magic; it was their right to use it as they saw fit. But she watched, and she waited, ready to intervene if the balance tipped too far.
story.part4: Golemon was her staff, a weapon and a tool, capable of summoning golems of living stone, spirits of wind, guardians of fire, sentinels of water. The staff was taller than she was, its shaft a twisted length of petrified wood wrapped in silver wire, its surface carved with the faces of elementals and the symbols of the primal forces. At its head, a cluster of crystals—pink, purple, white—floated within a cage of silver, each one pulsing with the essence of a different element. When she struck the ground with Golemon, the crystals flared, and from the earth rose golems of living stone, from the air coalesced spirits of wind, from the flames leapt guardians of fire, from the waters flowed sentinels of the deep. The golems were her army, her protectors, her will made manifest, and they obeyed her without question.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has weakened the barriers between worlds, and demons are pouring through, seeking to corrupt the elemental circles. Mayine has emerged from her tower, Snowy on her shoulder, the Book of Unknown in her hand, Golemon humming with power. She has called her golems to her side, and she marches to the largest rift, ready to seal it with her magic. She does not know if she will succeed. The Sundering is stronger than anything she has faced before. But she is Mayine, the Queen of Witches, and she will not let the world fall to chaos. She will weave new spells, bind new elements, and if necessary, rewrite the Book of Unknown from scratch. For she is the architect of magic, and she will not let her creation be destroyed.

heroes.lore: Mayine is the Queen of Witches, the architect of magic in her universe. She wrote the Book of Unknown and wields Golemon, a staff that summons elemental golems. She watches over the balance of magic and protects the world from chaos.

relationships.description: Snowy is Mayine's familiar, a massive snow owl with a tiny witch hat.
Mayine is respected by the elemental lords, who grant her power.

pet.name: Snowy
pet.description: A massive snow owl of breathtaking majesty, feathers pristine white fading to soft brown at wingtips, eyes two pools of molten gold. Wears a tiny pointed witch hat of her own, perched between her ear tufts. Can speak in a voice like wind through pine needles.
pet.fulldesc: Snowy is a massive snow owl, her wingspan rivaling that of a griffin, her feathers a pristine white that fades to soft brown at the tips of her wings, her eyes two pools of molten gold, wise and ancient. She wears a tiny, pointed witch hat of her own, perched between her ear tufts. Snowy is not merely a pet; she is a familiar, a fragment of the first magic that Mayine ever wove, bound to her as a partner and a guardian. She can speak, in a voice like wind through pine needles, and she serves as Mayine's eyes in the unseen realms, her scout, her sentinel. She perches on Mayine's arm when the witch reads from the Book of Unknown, her head turning slowly, her gaze following the magic circles that spiral into the air, and when danger threatens, she spreads her wings and shrieks—a sound that shatters illusions and calls the golems to their queen's side. When Mayine is tired, Snowy rests her head on her shoulder, and the warmth of her feathers reminds Mayine that she is not alone.
abilities: Silent Flight – reveals enemies within 15 meters.
Shriek of Truth – shatters illusions and reveals invisible enemies.
Owl's Wisdom – increases Mayine's magic power by 10% while Snowy is perched.

dialogue.selection: Magic is not a toy. It is a responsibility.

heroes.name: Pahilos
heroes.shortDescription: A machine of impossible kindness, a titan forged in the crucible of tragedy and programmed for one purpose: to save. Pahilos is a rescue machine who seeks to save life, carrying the Pran Scythe that cuts through debris and heals the wounded. He wears the red tattered head scarf of his creator, who died saving him. He has no mount or pet, for he is a lone guardian.
heroes.fullDescription: Pahilos stands amidst the smoke of collapsed towers like a promise carved from steel and sorrow, a being of such gentle mechanical grace that the wounded reach for him instinctively, even before they know what he is—a machine of impossible kindness, a titan forged in the crucible of tragedy and programmed for one purpose: to save. His form is a masterwork of high technology and sorrowful artistry, a fusion of sleek silver alloys, warm orange plating, and deep maroon red accents. His head is a semi‑circular clockwork helm of polished silver, its surface etched with the gears of a thousand rescued souls. From its sides, two horns curve upward—graceful, like antennae. Where a face should be, there is only clockwork: a delicate arrangement of spinning gears, oscillating balances, and a single round lens of orange glass that glows with a soft warm light. His body is broad and sturdy, with thick maroon red armor plates on his shoulders. A high‑tech double rocket backpack is mounted on his back. His chest is wide silver plating etched with the names of those he has saved. His arms are long and articulated, with massive delicate hands. His legs are thick, his feet wide. Around his right arm, wrapped tightly around the bicep, is a red tattered head scarf of intricate pattern—the only remnant of his creator, Elara. His weapon, the Pran Scythe, is a blade that defies the nature of its form—longer than he is tall, its shaft a length of polished silver wrapped in maroon leather, its blade a crescent of orange crystal that glows with a soft warm light. When he swings it, it does not cut flesh; it cuts through debris, through twisted metal and shattered concrete, clearing paths to the trapped. When he touches a wounded soul with its blade, it glows brighter, and warmth flows from the crystal into the victim, healing wounds, stabilizing fractures, drawing the dying back from the edge. The scythe hums when it works, a low resonant note that vibrates in the chest, a sound that has become synonymous with hope in the ruins he walks. This rescue machine was created by Dr. Elara Voss in the hidden laboratories of the Forge Pact, designed to survive any disaster, reach any victim, and heal any wound. She did not program him to obey; she taught him to care. When a rival faction sabotaged the laboratory, Elara was trapped in the fire. Pahilos broke through his restraints and ran to save her, but the roof collapsed, and Elara pushed him clear, dying in his arms. He wrapped her red head scarf around his arm and has never taken it off. Now he stands amidst the smoke of collapsed towers—a disaster zone of broken concrete, twisted rebar, and the cries of the trapped, where the only light comes from his orange crystal scythe and the glow of his single lens—the Pran Scythe humming, his rockets ready, searching for the next cry for help, carrying the weight of every death on his shoulders, but never stopping, because as long as there is someone to save, he will keep moving.

story.part1: In the hidden laboratories of the Forge Pact, a scientist named Dr. Elara Voss created the perfect rescue machine. She poured her heart into the project, designing a titan that could survive any disaster, reach any victim, and heal any wound. She called him Pahilos, after a word in an ancient language meaning 'one who lifts.' She did not program him to obey; she taught him to care. She showed him images of people in need, played recordings of their cries for help, and let him develop his own sense of compassion. Pahilos learned faster than she expected, and soon he was not just a machine—he was a person, with hopes, fears, and a fierce desire to protect the innocent.
story.part2: The fire came from within the Pact. A rival faction, jealous of Elara's success, sabotaged the laboratory. The flames spread quickly, trapping Elara in her office. Pahilos, still incomplete, broke through his restraints and ran to save her. He found her pinned under a beam, her body burned, her eyes barely open. 'Save yourself,' she whispered. 'I built you to save lives. Don't waste yours on me.' Pahilos refused. He lifted the beam, cradled her in his arms, and carried her toward the exit. The roof collapsed. Elara pushed him clear, and the debris crushed her instead. Pahilos held her body as the flames consumed the laboratory, and when the fire died, he carried her to the surface. He buried her under a tree, and he wrapped her red head scarf around his arm. He has never taken it off.
story.part3: For decades, Pahilos wandered the world, saving anyone he could find. He pulled survivors from earthquakes, from floods, from collapsing buildings. He used the Pran Scythe to cut through debris and heal the wounded, its orange crystal glowing brighter with each life saved. He never asked for payment, never sought recognition. He simply appeared when needed, did his work, and vanished into the smoke. The people called him 'The Clockwork Savior,' and they carved his image into the walls of their hospitals as a symbol of hope.
story.part4: The Pran Scythe was his only weapon, a blade that defied the nature of its form. The scythe was longer than he was tall, its shaft a length of polished silver wrapped in maroon leather, its blade a crescent of orange crystal that glowed with a soft, warm light. It was not a weapon of war; it was a tool of salvation. When he swung it, it cut through debris, through twisted metal and shattered concrete, clearing paths to the trapped. When he touched a wounded soul with its blade, it glowed brighter, and warmth flowed from the crystal into the victim, healing wounds, stabilizing fractures, drawing the dying back from the edge. The scythe hummed when it worked, a low, resonant note that vibrated in the chest, a sound that had become synonymous with hope in the ruins he walked.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the world, and disasters are more frequent than ever. Pahilos works without rest, his rockets launching him from one crisis to another, his scythe never cooling. He has saved thousands, but he has lost many more. He carries the weight of every death on his shoulders, and sometimes, in the quiet of the night, he plays the recordings of Elara's voice that he saved in his memory banks. 'You are not a machine,' she says. 'You are my son.' Pahilos does not know if he believes her. But he knows that as long as there is someone to save, he will keep moving. For he is Pahilos, the Rescue Machine, and his purpose is the only thing that keeps him from breaking.

weapons.fulldesc: The Pran Scythe is a blade that defies the nature of its form. The scythe is longer than Pahilos is tall, its shaft a length of polished silver wrapped in maroon leather, its blade a crescent of orange crystal that glows with a soft, warm light. It is not a weapon of war; it is a tool of salvation. When he swings the Pran Scythe, it does not cut flesh; it cuts through debris, through twisted metal and shattered concrete, clearing paths to the trapped. When he touches a wounded soul with its blade, it glows brighter, and warmth flows from the crystal into the victim, healing wounds, stabilizing fractures, drawing the dying back from the edge. The scythe hums when it works, a low, resonant note that vibrates in the chest, a sound that has become synonymous with hope in the ruins he walks.
weapons.look: Polished silver shaft, maroon leather wrap, orange crystal crescent blade, warm glow

passive.name: Rescue Protocol
passive.description: Pahilos gains 20% movement speed when moving toward allies below 30% health.

passive.name: Structural Scan
passive.description: Pahilos can see enemy structures' health bars and weaknesses (increased damage to turrets by 15%).

active.name: Healing Touch
active.description: Heals an ally for 200 health plus 15% of their max health. Can be used on self. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Clear Path
active.description: Swings the scythe in a 10-meter arc, dealing 150% physical damage to enemies and destroying enemy deployables (traps, turrets, etc.). Cooldown: 10 seconds.

ultimate.name: Emergency Response
ultimate.description: Pahilos activates his rockets and becomes invulnerable for 3 seconds, flying to a target ally anywhere on the map. Upon arrival, he heals all allies within 15 meters for 300 health plus 20% of their max health and cleanses all debuffs. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Elara's Scarf
special.description: When Pahilos falls below 10% health, he gains 50% damage reduction and 50% movement speed for 5 seconds (once per match).

ratings.notes: Support tank with healing, global rescue ultimate, and anti-structure capabilities.

skills.name: Reinforced Frame
skills.description: Pahilos takes 15% reduced damage from all sources.

skills.name: Rocket Boost
skills.description: Pahilos dashes forward 10 meters, knocking aside enemies and leaving a smoke cloud that blinds enemies for 1 second. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

skills.name: Stabilize
skills.description: Pahilos prevents an ally from dying for 3 seconds (cannot fall below 1 HP). Cooldown: 40 seconds.

skills.name: The Unfinished Promise
skills.description: If Pahilos is the one to save an ally from certain death (healing them from below 5% health), he gains a permanent 5 health regeneration per second for the rest of the match (stacks up to 5 times).

heroes.lore: Pahilos was created by Dr. Elara Voss as a rescue machine. She died saving him from a fire, and he now wanders the world saving others in her memory. He wears her red scarf on his arm and wields the Pran Scythe, a tool of salvation.

relationships.description: Pahilos carries her scarf and her memory in every rescue.
The Pact's sabotage killed Elara; Pahilos has no love for them.

heroes.specialty: Support tank with healing, global rescue ultimate, and anti-structure capabilities. Excels at protecting allies and turning fights.

dialogue.selection: Emergency response. Where am I needed?

heroes.name: Rexingu Raide
heroes.shortDescription: A wild steampunk girl of Japanese heritage who tears across the prehistoric plains like a wildfire wrapped in brass and leather. Rexingu Raide tamed the king of the Tyrannosaurus species, Marei Fougi, and rides him into battle. She wields the Jaws—a steampunk whip-like rope with brass jaws at the tip—and wears laser steampunk gloves that harness fire, ice, water, and laser.
heroes.fullDescription: Rexingu Raide tears across the prehistoric plains like a wildfire wrapped in brass and leather, a being of such wild mechanical beauty that the very dinosaurs pause in their feeding to watch her pass—a steampunk dino rider of Japanese heritage, a woman whose form is a masterwork of industrial elegance and untamed spirit. Her face is a study in fierce ethereal beauty: a soft heart‑shaped visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full bronze‑tinted lips perpetually curved in a wild reckless grin. Her skin is sun‑kissed, healthy, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, striking vivid green, framed by thick orange lashes. Her hair is a cascade of vibrant orange, long and voluminous, styled in twin ponytails tied with bronze‑red ribbons. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a cropped top of burnished bronze leather with brass rivets, cut low to reveal her cleavage and stomach, over a fitted steampunk vest jacket of iron‑blue velvet with torn sleeves. Her arms are bare, covered by laser steampunk gloves with orange, blue, silver, and red crystals on the knuckles. A mini skirt of bronze leather with brass buttons, slit high, covers her hips, exposing her thick thighs. A belt of interlocking brass gears cinches her waist. Steampunk short boots of iron‑blue leather with small wheels cover her feet. Her weapon, the Jaws, is a steampunk whip‑like rope longer than she is tall—a braid of bronze‑red leather and iron‑blue wire, its handle wrapped in the hide of the first dinosaur she ever tamed, its tip a set of small brass jaws, articulated and spring‑loaded, capable of clamping onto the toughest scales with unbreakable grip. With the Jaws, she can tame any species, ride any beast, command the loyalty of creatures that would tear lesser humans apart. Her mount, Marei Fougi, is the king of the Tyrannosaurus species—its scales a deep burnished red shifting to orange in sunlight, its stripes a stark brilliant white from snout to tail tip, its eyes two points of intelligent gold, wearing armor of dragon‑scale steel forged by Rexingu's own hands, capable of a stomp that deals crushing damage, a tail sweep that knocks back enemies, and a roar that stuns all who hear it. This steampunk dino rider was born into a family of dinosaur tamers, the daughter of the chief of the Bronze Claw tribe. When she was twelve, a rival tribe killed her parents and burned her home. She fled into the jungle, survived by scavenging and inventing, and tamed Marei Fougi after freeing it from a trap. Now she rides across the prehistoric plains—a realm of towering ferns, ancient cycads, and herds of dinosaurs that shake the earth, where the sky is a brilliant blue and the air smells of damp earth and wildflowers—the Jaws whip in her hand, Marei Fougi thundering beneath her, leading a coalition of tamed beasts against the demons and rival tribes threatening her home.

story.part1: Rexingu Raide was born into a family of dinosaur tamers, a lineage that stretched back to the first humans who learned to ride the great beasts of the prehistoric plains. Her father was the chief of the Bronze Claw tribe, and her mother was an inventor who built the first steampunk harnesses. Rexingu grew up in the saddle, learning to ride before she could walk, to tame before she could speak. She was a prodigy, able to communicate with the dinosaurs in a way that seemed almost magical. The tribe called her 'The Whisperer,' and they believed she was blessed by the dinosaur gods.
story.part2: The tragedy came when Rexingu was twelve. A rival tribe, jealous of the Bronze Claw's power, launched a raid on their village. They killed her parents, burned her home, and scattered the tribe. Rexingu fled into the jungle with nothing but her mother's tools and her father's whip. She survived by scavenging, by inventing, by taming the very beasts that the rival tribe used as weapons. She built her first laser glove from scraps, and she used it to freeze a charging raptor mid-leap. She was no longer a child; she was a survivor.
story.part3: Marei Fougi found her in the heart of the jungle, a wounded Tyrannosaurus Rex, its leg caught in a trap set by the rival tribe. Rexingu could have killed it, could have used its parts for her inventions. Instead, she freed it, healed its wound, and spoke to it in the language of the old tamers. The T-Rex looked at her with its golden eyes, and something passed between them—a bond that transcended species. Marei Fougi lowered its massive head, and Rexingu climbed onto its back. She had found her partner.
story.part4: The Jaws whip was her invention, a steampunk masterpiece that could tame any species. The whip was longer than she was tall, its length a braid of bronze‑red leather and iron‑blue wire, its handle wrapped in the hide of the first dinosaur she ever tamed. At its tip, a set of small, brass jaws were mounted—articulated, spring‑loaded, capable of clamping onto the toughest scales with unbreakable grip. With the Jaws, she could command the loyalty of creatures that would tear lesser humans apart. She used it to guide Marei Fougi, to signal her intentions, to remind the king of tyrants that they were partners, not master and slave.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has torn open the prehistoric plains, and demons are pouring through, threatening the dinosaur herds. Rexingu rides Marei Fougi at the head of a coalition of tamed beasts, fighting to protect her home. She has heard that the rival tribe has allied with the demons, that they seek to capture Marei Fougi and use him as a weapon. She will not let that happen. She will fight with her gloves, her whip, and her bond with the king of tyrants. For she is Rexingu Raide, the Steampunk Dino Rider, and the jungle will never forget her name.

heroes.lore: Rexingu Raide is a steampunk dino rider, the last of the Bronze Claw tribe. She tamed Marei Fougi, the king of the Tyrannosaurus, after her parents were killed by a rival tribe. She now protects the prehistoric plains from demons and rival tamers, wielding the Jaws whip and laser gloves.

relationships.description: The T-Rex is Rexingu's partner, bound by a bond of mutual respect and survival.
The tribe that killed her parents; she seeks to protect the jungle from them.

mount.name: Marei Fougi
mount.description: The king of the Tyrannosaurus species, its scales a deep burnished red shifting to orange in sunlight, stripes a stark brilliant white from snout to tail tip. Eyes two points of intelligent gold. Wears armor of dragon-scale steel forged by Rexingu's own hands.
mount.fulldesc: Marei Fougi is the king of the Tyrannosaurus species, its scales a deep, burnished red that shifts to orange in the sunlight, its stripes a stark, brilliant white that run from its snout to the tip of its tail. Its eyes are two points of intelligent gold, and it wears armor of dragon‑scale steel, forged by Rexingu's own hands, that protects its chest, its shoulders, its neck. Marei Fougi can speak, in a voice like grinding stones and distant thunder, and it speaks to her in the language of the old ones, sharing wisdom, offering counsel, reminding her that she is never alone. It moves with a power that shakes the earth, its roar a sound that silences the jungle, and when she calls, it answers, lowering its massive head so she can climb, rumbling with a sound that is half growl, half purr, the sound of a bond that transcends species. When Rexingu is sad, Marei Fougi nudges her with its snout, and she strokes its scales, feeling the warmth of the only family she has left.
abilities: Stomp – deals 200% physical damage in a 8-meter radius.
Tail Sweep – sweeps tail in a cone, knocking back enemies.
Roar – stuns enemies in a 12-meter cone for 1.5 seconds.

heroes.name: Morrigan
heroes.shortDescription: A Celtic war goddess who has walked the battlefields of Ireland since the first warrior raised a blade. Morrigan is the chooser of the slain, the one who decides which side will taste victory and which will drink of death. She wields Caledfwlch—a staff that becomes a sword—and is accompanied by a black raven with dark blue feathers.
heroes.fullDescription: Morrigan steps from the mist‑shrouded fields of eternal battle like a prophecy carved in blood and shadow, a being of such terrible majestic beauty that the warriors who see her know, instantly, whether they will live or die—a Celtic war goddess, the chooser of the slain, the one who decides which side will taste victory and which will drink of death. Her face is a study in sharp ethereal perfection: a strong angular jawline, high prominent cheekbones, a straight aristocratic nose, and full dark lips perpetually parted as if uttering a prophecy. Her skin is healthy and luminous, the color of moonlight on still water, smooth and flawless, with a faint cool undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep absolute black like the void between stars, yet within them, faint pinpricks of silver light glimmer. Her hair is a cascade of starry blue, long and voluminous, styled in curtain layers that fall past her shoulders in waves that seem to shimmer with captured constellations. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a feathered cloak of black and abyssal blue, tattered and flowing, with iron armor shoulders. A black crop top of supple leather, cut deep, reveals her cleavage. Her arms are bare. Blue jean shorts trimmed with thick black fur, cut high, cover her hips, exposing her thick thighs. Black pointed long heels boots cover her feet. A watch of ancient magic that shows omens is strapped to her wrist. Her weapon, Caledfwlch, is a staff that is also a sword—in its staff form, taller than she is, its shaft a length of blackened yew wrapped in silver wire, carved with the names of the battles she has witnessed, its head a crescent blade of polished obsidian sharp enough to cut the threads of fate. When she twists the shaft, the blade extends, and the staff becomes a long straight sword of silver and black, its edge humming with the energy of a thousand war cries. Caledfwlch is not a weapon she carries; it is an extension of her will, and when she raises it, the warriors who see it know that their fate is sealed. Her pet is a black raven with dark blue feathers that shift in the light, its eyes two points of silver light, capable of flying faster than thought, seeing through the eyes of other ravens, speaking in a voice like the rustle of wings and the clash of swords, whispering prophecies in Morrigan's ear. This war goddess walked the battlefields of Ireland since the first warrior raised a blade, coalescing from the mist of the first battlefield, from the blood of the first slain warrior. She does not favor sides; she observes, records, and reveals outcomes to those with eyes to see. The Sundering frayed the threads of fate for the first time, and Morrigan decided to intervene, guiding warriors toward battles that will determine the fate of the realms. Now she stands at the edge of the largest Sundering rift—a realm of swirling chaos, broken earth, and the distant clash of swords, where the sky is a bleeding wound and the ground trembles with each step—the raven on her shoulder, Caledfwlch in her hand, waiting for the final battle, smiling because for the first time, the prophecy is unwritten.

story.part1: Before the Celts raised their first standing stone, Morrigan walked the fields of the unborn. She was not born; she coalesced from the mist of the first battlefield, from the blood of the first slain warrior, from the scream of the first dying man. The other gods called her 'The Phantom Queen,' and they feared her, for she did not rule over life—she ruled over the moment between life and death, the space between a heartbeat and the next. She watched as tribes clashed, as kingdoms rose and fell, and she learned that war was not chaos; it was a pattern, a dance, a prophecy that she alone could read.
story.part2: Caledfwlch was given to her by the earth itself, forged from the iron of a meteorite that fell on the day of the first battle. In its staff form, it was a tool of prophecy, allowing her to see the threads of fate that connected each warrior to their death. In its sword form, it was a weapon of judgment, cutting through the illusions that warriors used to hide their fear. Morrigan wielded it with a cold precision, never favoring one side over another, never intervening—only observing, recording, and when the time was right, revealing the outcome to those who had eyes to see.
story.part3: The raven came to her on the battlefield of Mag Tuired, a wounded bird that had been struck by a stray arrow. Morrigan healed it, and the raven became her familiar, its feathers shifting from black to dark blue in the moonlight. The raven could fly faster than thought, could see through the eyes of other ravens, could speak in a voice like the rustle of wings and the clash of swords. It whispered prophecies in Morrigan's ear, warned her of ambushes, celebrated her victories with a harsh, triumphant cry. It was her only companion, the only being that had never asked her to choose a side.
story.part4: The Sundering tore open the barriers between worlds, and new wars erupted across Kretos—wars that Morrigan had not foreseen. For the first time, the threads of fate were frayed, and she could not read the outcome. She felt something she had not felt in millennia: uncertainty. She decided to intervene, not to choose a side, but to guide the warriors toward battles that would determine the fate of the realms. She appeared to heroes in their dreams, showed them visions of victory and death, and left them to make their own choices.
story.part5: Now, Morrigan stands at the edge of the largest rift, the raven on her shoulder, Caledfwlch in her hand. She has seen that the final battle will be fought not between demons and gods, but between the warriors who have been shaped by the Sundering—heroes who carry the weight of their worlds on their shoulders. She does not know who will win. For the first time, the prophecy is unwritten. She smiles, a cold, terrible expression that makes the raven shift uneasily. 'Finally,' she whispers, 'a battle worth watching.' For she is Morrigan, the Raven of War, and she will be there when the last sword falls, to welcome the slain into her embrace.
