weapons.name: Caledfwlch
weapons.fulldesc: Caledfwlch is a staff that is also a sword, a weapon of legendary power that brings victory or death depending on the will of its wielder. In its staff form, it is taller than Morrigan, its shaft a length of blackened yew wrapped in silver wire, its surface carved with the names of the battles she has witnessed. At its head, a crescent blade of polished obsidian curves inward, sharp enough to cut the threads of fate. When she twists the shaft, the blade extends, and the staff becomes a sword—a long, straight blade of silver and black, its edge humming with the energy of a thousand war cries. Caledfwlch is not a weapon she carries; it is an extension of her will, and when she raises it, the warriors who see it know that their fate is sealed. Victory or death—there is no other outcome.

heroes.lore: Morrigan is the Celtic war goddess, the chooser of the slain. She walks the battlefields of Kretos, reading the threads of fate and guiding warriors toward their destinies. She wields Caledfwlch and is accompanied by her raven familiar.

relationships.description: The raven is her companion, her scout, and her link to the battlefield.
She is respected by the other Celtic gods but walks her own path.

pet.name: Black Raven
pet.description: A black raven with dark blue feathers that shift in the light, eyes two points of silver light. Can fly faster than thought, can see through the eyes of other ravens, can speak in a voice like the rustle of wings and the clash of swords.
pet.fulldesc: The black raven is Morrigan's constant companion, its feathers a deep, abyssal blue that shifts to black in the shadows, its eyes two points of silver light that mirror her own. The raven is not merely a pet; it is a fragment of her power, a messenger, a scout, a spy. It can fly faster than thought, can see through the eyes of other ravens, can speak in a voice like the rustle of wings and the clash of swords. It whispers prophecies in her ear, warns her of ambushes, celebrates her victories with a harsh, triumphant cry. It perches on her shoulder when she rests, its head tucked under its wing, and when she goes to war, it flies ahead, a dark star against the grey sky, a promise of the bloodshed to come. When Morrigan is uncertain, the raven caws, and she listens, for the raven has never been wrong.
abilities: Prophetic Whisper – reveals a random enemy's location every 20 seconds.
Raven Scout – reveals a 15-meter area around the raven.
War Cry – the raven's cry increases Morrigan's critical strike chance by 10% for 5 seconds.

heroes.specialty: Hybrid assassin/support with prophecy mechanics, crowd control, and execution potential. Excels at reading the flow of battle and finishing low-health targets.

dialogue.selection: I see the threads. Do you?

heroes.name: Kael
heroes.shortDescription: A storm‑touched human of Nepali heritage, a man whose body was forged in the crucible of a tempest and emerged transformed. Kael's hands are forged with thunder, his martial arts infused with lightning chi. He wears an eyepatch over his thunder eye, which glows with constant violet aura. He has no mount or pet, for he walks the mountains alone, the storm following him.
heroes.fullDescription: Kael walks the ridge lines of the Himalayas like a thundercloud that has learned to breathe, a being of such raw contained fury that the very air around him crackles with the promise of lightning—a storm‑touched human of Nepali heritage, a man whose body was forged in the crucible of a tempest and emerged transformed. His face is a study in rugged elemental beauty: a strong square jaw, high prominent cheekbones, a straight broad nose, and thin pale lips pressed into a focused meditative line. His skin is warm and sun‑kissed, the color of the earth after rain, smooth and healthy, yet across his temples and down his neck, faint purple veins pulse with the lightning that courses through him. His eyes are mismatched: the left a deep piercing purple that shifts to silver when his power rises; the right hidden beneath an eye patch of worn dark leather etched with a silver thunderbolt—beneath the patch, his thunder eye glows with a constant violet aura. His hair is short and spiky, a shock of blonde with silver tips that stands on end as if constantly charged with static. His physique is lean, built for explosive movement, with a bare chest covered in branching purple lightning scars, broad shoulders, thick powerful arms, large calloused hands crackling with violet sparks, a narrow waist, and long strong legs. He wears a dark coat of heavy wool over his bare chest, open, and a simple suit of dark cotton trousers. A simple cord of braided leather with a small leather pouch containing the ashes of his home is tied around his waist. His feet are bare, calloused and tough. His weapon is his body—his hands are forged with thunder, the palms etched with branching patterns, the fingers crackling with violet lightning when he flexes them. He does not need a blade or a staff; when he raises his hand, the sky answers. He can summon thunder bolts that split the air, throw lightning bolts that arc from his fingertips, and channel the power of the storm into his fists to deliver blows that shatter stone. His martial arts are infused with lightning chi, each strike carrying the charge of a thundercloud, each block creating a shockwave that sends enemies flying. This storm‑touched man was a humble farmer and martial artist in the high valleys of the Himalayas, living with his wife and daughter. A storm of purple lightning struck him directly, rewiring his nervous system, turning his cells into conduits for electricity. He found his wife and daughter dead under the rubble of their home. He made a vow: he would learn to control the power that had taken everything from him and use it to ensure no other family suffered the same fate. Now he walks the ridge lines of the Himalayas—a realm of jagged peaks, deep valleys, and the constant rumble of distant thunder, where the air is thin and cold and the only light comes from the crackling of his hands and the glow of his thunder eye—the ashes pouch warm against his skin, his hands crackling, searching for Geigei Fuks, the storm mage of the north, hoping to learn from her.

story.part1: Kael was a humble man, a farmer in the high valleys of the Himalayas, a practitioner of martial arts who sought only peace. He lived with his wife and daughter in a small village, tending yaks and praying to the mountain gods. He was not a hero; he was not a warrior. He was a father who taught his daughter to meditate, a husband who held his wife's hand as they watched the sunset. The storm came without warning, a tempest of purple lightning that had never been seen in those peaks. The villagers called it 'The Wrath of the Sky,' and they fled to their homes. Kael stayed outside, trying to calm the family's animals. The lightning struck him directly, a bolt of pure, celestial electricity that should have turned him to ash.
story.part2: He did not die. The lightning rewired his nervous system, turning his cells into conduits for electricity. He rose from the ground, his eyes crackling, his hands sparking. He found his village in ruins, his wife and daughter gone, buried under the rubble of their home. Kael screamed, and the lightning screamed with him. He dug through the debris with his bare hands, the electricity arcing from his fingers, and he found their bodies. He held them as the storm raged, and he made a vow: he would learn to control the power that had taken everything from him, and he would use it to ensure that no other family suffered the same fate.
story.part3: The lightning eye was the source of his power and his curse. The eye that had been struck by the lightning glowed constantly, a violet aura that could not be dimmed. Kael wore an eyepatch to contain it, but the light still escaped around the edges, dancing across his cheekbone and into his hair. He learned to channel the lightning through his fists, to throw bolts from his fingertips, to move with the speed of a thunderbolt. He became a myth in the Himalayas, a figure that appeared in times of crisis, struck down demons with purple lightning, and vanished into the clouds.
story.part4: The ashes in the pouch around his waist were all that remained of his home—the hut that had been struck by lightning, the place where his family had lived. He carried them everywhere, a reminder of what he had lost and why he fought. He never spoke of his wife or daughter, but the pouch was always there, warm against his skin, pulsing with a faint, electric charge. Sometimes, in the quiet of the night, he would open the pouch and let the ashes drift into the wind, whispering their names. The wind would carry them to the peaks, and Kael would feel a moment of peace before the storm returned.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought demons to the Himalayas, and Kael fights them alone, his hands crackling, his eye blazing. He has heard of a storm mage named Geigei Fuks, a woman who commands lightning from the north. He does not know if she is an ally or a rival, but he knows that they are connected—two souls touched by the storm. He will find her, not to challenge her, but to learn from her. For he is Kael, the Storm Touched, and his grief is the lightning that fuels his fists.

weapons.name: Lightning Chi (Unarmed)
weapons.fulldesc: Kael's body is his weapon. His hands are forged with thunder, the palms etched with the same branching patterns that cover his chest, the fingers crackling with violet lightning when he flexes them. He does not need a blade or a staff; when he raises his hand, the sky answers. He can summon thunder bolts that split the air, can throw lightning bolts that arc from his fingertips, can channel the power of the storm into his fists and deliver blows that shatter stone. His martial arts are infused with lightning chi, each strike carrying the charge of a thundercloud, each block creating a shockwave that sends enemies flying. He is a living weapon, a human lightning rod, and he has not yet met an opponent who could withstand his full fury.
weapons.look: Hands crackling with violet lightning, purple arcs from fingertips

heroes.lore: Kael was a Himalayan farmer and martial artist who was struck by a magical lightning bolt, losing his family and gaining the power of the storm. He now wanders the mountains, fighting demons and protecting villages, his thunder eye glowing, his hands crackling with lightning.

relationships.description: Kael has heard of the Storm Mage and seeks to learn from her.
Their ashes are in the pouch he carries; their memory drives him.

heroes.specialty: Mobile fighter with lightning damage, crowd control, and scaling passive. Excels at dueling and chasing.

dialogue.selection: The storm inside me never sleeps.

heroes.name: Nyx
heroes.shortDescription: The Primordial Night, a woman whose form is the canvas upon which darkness first learned to be beautiful. Nyx existed before the first star ignited, and she will be there when the last star dies. She wields the Night, a mythical staff of condensed shadow, and is accompanied by Ulele, a black panther with gold skeletal jewelry and silver armor.
heroes.fullDescription: Nyx drifts at the edge of existence like the silence before the first star ignited, a being of such profound ancient beauty that the universe itself holds its breath in her presence—the Primordial Night, a woman whose form is the canvas upon which darkness first learned to be beautiful. Her face is a study in eternal cosmic perfection: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full silver‑tinted lips perpetually parted as if whispering the secrets of the void. Her skin is ethereal, human‑like yet luminous beyond mortal comprehension, the color of the space between galaxies—a deep warm cream shot through with veins of silver that pulse with the rhythm of distant stars. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, a swirling galaxy of deep purple, silver, and absolute black, with pinpricks of light that flare and fade like distant supernovae. Her hair is a cascade of pure silver, long and voluminous, falling past her waist in waves that seem to float as if underwater, each strand catching the faint light of distant stars and shimmering with an inner luminescence. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears a robe woven from captured starlight and condensed darkness, cut deep at the chest, with long silky strands of deep purple and silver drifting from her shoulders and waist. Her arms are bare, her feet bare and floating. A crown of darkness—a circlet of solidified shadow with nebula patterns—sits on her head. Her weapon, the Night, is a mythical staff of condensed shadow—taller than she is, its shaft a length of condensed darkness that seems to absorb all light that touches it, its surface etched with the names of the stars that were born from her darkness. At its head, a sphere of absolute black floats within a cage of silver filaments, and within that sphere, the faint light of distant galaxies can be seen, trapped, waiting. When she raises the Night, the sphere pulses, and darkness flows from it like a tide, spreading across the land, swallowing light, bringing the night. With the Night, she can summon shadows that move at her command, draw the light from a room, blanket the world in darkness that no flame can pierce. Her pet, Ulele, is a black panther of breathtaking majesty—its fur the color of absolute darkness, so deep that it seems to swallow the light around it, its eyes two points of silver fire, wearing gold skeletal jewelry (a collar of interlocking gold vertebrae, anklets of gold bones) and silver armor plates on its chest and shoulders, capable of a shadow pounce that stuns enemies, a silver glow that reveals invisible foes, and skeletal protection that reduces damage taken by Nyx. This primordial night existed before the first star ignited, the darkness her body, the silence her voice. She watched as the universe took shape, as the first gods emerged from the light. The Sundering was a wound she could not ignore, and she fought the demons of the void with her staff. Now she drifts at the edge of existence—a realm of absolute darkness punctuated by distant stars, where time has no meaning and the only sound is the silence—the Night staff in her hand, Ulele padding beside her, protecting the stars not because she loves them, but because without them, there would be no shadows for her to rule.

story.part1: Before the first star ignited, before the void learned to hunger, there was Nyx. She was not born; she simply was. The darkness was her body, the silence her voice, the cold her embrace. She existed for eons alone, watching as the universe took shape from the chaos, as the first gods emerged from the light. They feared her, for she was the end of all things—the darkness that would consume their creations when the light faded. But Nyx did not hate them; she pitied them. They were so young, so desperate to be eternal. She knew that nothing was eternal. She was patient.
story.part2: The first star was born from her shadow. She did not intend to create it; it simply happened, a spark that escaped her darkness and blossomed into light. The other gods celebrated, but Nyx watched with sorrow. The star would die, as all things died, and the darkness would reclaim it. She did not interfere; she observed. The star burned for a billion years, and when it finally died, its light returned to her, a faint echo of the warmth it had once held. Nyx felt something she had not expected: loss. She began to understand why the gods feared the end.
story.part3: The Sundering was a wound in reality that even Nyx could not ignore. The chaos spilled into her domain, corrupting the darkness, turning it into something hungry and mad. Nyx fought the demons of the void with her staff, the Night, summoning shadows that moved at her command, drawing the light from the rifts, blanketing the worlds in darkness to starve the demons. The other gods called her cruel, but Nyx did not care. She was protecting them, even if they could not see it.
story.part4: Ulele was the first creature she created, a black panther born from the first shadow that ever fell. The panther's fur was the color of absolute darkness, its eyes two points of silver fire. Nyx adorned it with gold skeletal jewelry—a collar of interlocking gold vertebrae, anklets of gold bones—and silver armor plates etched with the patterns of her crown. Ulele was not a pet; she was a fragment of the first shadow, the creature that was born from Nyx's first exhale, and she had followed her through the eons with the devotion of a sister. When Nyx rested, Ulele curled at her feet; when Nyx went to war, Ulele moved beside her, a shadow among shadows.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has weakened the barriers between worlds, and the demons of the void are growing bolder. Nyx has emerged from her domain, the Night staff in her hand, Ulele padding beside her. She does not fight for the gods; she fights for the balance, for the darkness that gives meaning to light. She has sensed that the demon lord who seeks to unmake reality is targeting the stars, hoping to extinguish them and plunge the universe into a chaos that even she cannot control. She will not allow it. She is Nyx, the Primordial Night, and she will protect the stars—not because she loves them, but because without them, there would be no shadows for her to rule.

weapons.name: The Night
weapons.fulldesc: The Night is a mythical staff that has become the symbol of Nyx's power over the darkness. The staff is taller than she is, its shaft a length of condensed shadow that seems to absorb all light that touches it, its surface etched with the names of the stars that were born from her darkness. At its head, a sphere of absolute black floats within a cage of silver filaments, and within that sphere, the faint light of distant galaxies can be seen, trapped, waiting. When she raises the Night, the sphere pulses, and darkness flows from it like a tide, spreading across the land, swallowing light, bringing the night. With the Night, she can summon shadows that move at her command, can draw the light from a room, can blanket the world in darkness that no flame can pierce. She has wielded it since the moment she was born from the void, and it has never failed her.
weapons.look: Condensed shadow shaft, silver cage, sphere of absolute black with trapped galaxies

passive.name: Primordial Darkness

active.description: Summons tendrils of darkness in a 12-meter radius, dealing 150% magic damage to all enemies and slowing them by 40% for 3 seconds. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

active.name: Extinguish Light
active.description: Target an area, creating a zone of absolute darkness for 5 seconds. Enemies inside are blinded (cannot see beyond 3 meters) and silenced. Cooldown: 14 seconds.

ultimate.name: Eternal Night
ultimate.description: Nyx blankets the entire map in darkness for 8 seconds. All enemies' vision range is reduced to 5 meters, and all allies gain 30% movement speed and become invisible to enemies outside of 5 meters. Cooldown: 120 seconds.

special.name: Mother of Shadows
special.description: Nyx's abilities deal 15% bonus damage to light-aligned enemies and she takes 20% reduced damage from them.

ratings.notes: Control mage with global vision manipulation, crowd control, and anti-light specialization.

skills.name: The Eternal Void
skills.description: If the match timer reaches 40 minutes, Nyx's ultimate becomes permanent (global darkness until the match ends).

heroes.lore: Nyx is the Primordial Night, the darkness that existed before the first star. She has watched over the universe for eons, protecting the balance between light and shadow. She wields the Night staff and is accompanied by Ulele, a black panther born from the first shadow.

relationships.description: Nyx respects Lunox's duality of light and dark, as she embodies the darkness that gives light meaning.
They are two of the oldest beings; they coexist in mutual silence.

pet.name: Ulele
pet.description: A black panther of breathtaking majesty, fur the color of absolute darkness, eyes two points of silver fire. Wears gold skeletal jewelry—a collar of interlocking gold vertebrae, anklets of gold bones—and silver armor plates on chest and shoulders.
pet.fulldesc: Ulele is a black panther of breathtaking majesty, its fur the color of absolute darkness, so deep that it seems to swallow the light around it, but its eyes are two points of silver fire that glow with the same light as Nyx's staff. It wears gold skeletal jewelry—a collar of interlocking gold vertebrae that wrap around its neck, anklets of gold bones that chime softly as it moves—and silver armor plates that cover its chest and shoulders, etched with the same patterns as Nyx's crown. Ulele is not merely a mount; it is a fragment of the first shadow, the creature that was born from Nyx's first exhale, and it has followed her through the eons with the devotion of a sister. It moves with a silent, liquid grace, its paws making no sound on any surface, its breath a soft, warm mist in the cold darkness. When Nyx rests, Ulele curls at her feet, its golden eyes watching, its silver armor gleaming faintly; when Nyx goes to war, Ulele moves beside her, a shadow among shadows, a guardian that no light can reveal. When Nyx is sad, Ulele rests its head on her knee, and she strokes its fur, feeling the warmth of the first creature she ever loved.
abilities: Shadow Pounce – leaps to an enemy, dealing 150% physical damage and stunning for 0.5 seconds.
Silver Glow – reveals invisible enemies within 12 meters.
Skeletal Protection – reduces damage taken by Nyx by 15% while Ulele is within 5 meters.

heroes.specialty: Control mage with global vision manipulation, crowd control, and anti-light specialization. Excels at controlling fights and denying vision.

heroes.name: Márquez
heroes.shortDescription: The Heavenly Ring Boxing Champion, a man whose form is a masterwork of athletic perfection and divine blessing. Márquez has never lost a fight, wielding his fists wrapped in blue leather gloves that pulse with jade-green aura. He wears a tribal skeletal jade necklace and is accompanied by a corgi dressed in a miniature boxing outfit.
heroes.fullDescription: Márquez stands in the center of the celestial ring like a monument carved from ambition and jade, a being of such focused radiant power that the very air around him seems to ripple with the force of his presence—the Heavenly Ring Boxing Champion, a man whose form is a masterwork of athletic perfection and divine blessing. His face is a study in sharp handsome symmetry: a strong square jaw, high prominent cheekbones, a straight broad nose flattened slightly from a hundred fights, and full determined lips pressed into a focused unwavering line. His skin is a rich warm brown, the color of fertile earth under a harvest sun, smooth and flawless, with a faint sheen of sweat that catches the jade glow of his aura. His eyes are large and deep‑set, a warm rich brown that holds the fire of a thousand victories. His hair is cropped close to his scalp in a precise buzzcut, brunette strands neat and clean. A faint silvery scar traces the line of his left eyebrow. His physique is a testament to the art of boxing: immense shoulders capped with thick sculpted deltoids, a broad defined chest, thick powerful arms, large calloused hands wrapped in blue leather, a narrow ridged waist, and thick powerful legs. He wears half‑white boxing shorts of pristine silk with 'Márquez' embroidered in golden thread. His chest is bare. His hands are encased in deep royal blue boxing gloves that pulse with jade aura, the gloves etched with golden patterns. Blue boxing shoes cover his feet. Around his neck hangs a tribal skeletal jade necklace—a grinning skull carved from a single piece of luminous green jade, its eyes set with tiny diamonds. A jade bead on a leather cord is tied around his right arm. His weapon is his hands, his gloves, his body honed to perfection—his signature technique, the Heavenly Jade Punch, channels the full power of his jade aura into a single devastating blow, a strike that glows with green light, resonates with the force of a thunderclap, and has never failed to end a fight. He can throw a hundred‑fold punch, a blur of motion that seems to land a hundred blows in the space of a heartbeat. His pet, Champ, is a corgi of impossible cuteness—its fur a warm golden brown, its face a mask of cheerful determination, dressed in a miniature white boxing jersey with 'Márquez' printed across the back, a tiny jade bead on a leather cord around its neck, and miniature blue booties on its paws, capable of an encouraging bark that heals Márquez, a distraction that causes enemies to miss, and a lucky charm that increases critical strike chance. This boxing champion was born in the slums of Mekhanika, a boy with nothing but a pair of worn boxing gloves and a dream. He trained from the age of five, lost his first fight at twelve, and swore he would never lose again. A mysterious old man gave him the jade necklace, saying it would amplify what was already there. Márquez became the Heavenly Ring Champion, defeating champions from other realms—demons, angels, machines, and monsters. Now he stands in the center of the celestial ring—a realm of floating platforms, golden light, and the roar of an invisible crowd, where the ropes are made of starlight and the canvas is woven from clouds—his jade aura blazing, Champ at ringside, waiting for demon challengers who seek to claim the championship for themselves.

story.part1: Márquez was born in the slums of Mekhanika, a boy with nothing but a pair of worn boxing gloves and a dream. His father was a fighter who had lost his chance at glory, and he poured all his hopes into his son. Márquez trained from the age of five, waking before dawn to run through the smog-choked streets, shadowboxing in the empty lots, sparring with anyone who would face him. He had talent, but more than that, he had hunger. He lost his first fight at twelve, and the loss burned in his chest for years. He swore he would never lose again. He never did.
story.part2: The jade necklace was a gift from a mysterious old man who appeared at Márquez's gym one night. The man said he had watched Márquez fight and saw potential—not just to be a champion, but to be something more. He gave Márquez the necklace, a grinning skull carved from a single piece of luminous green jade, its eyes set with tiny diamonds. 'This will give you strength,' the old man said. 'But remember: the jade does not fight for you. It only amplifies what is already there.' Márquez wore the necklace, and his punches began to glow with a faint green aura. He knocked out opponents with single blows, his speed doubled, his endurance tripled. He became a legend, and the old man vanished, never to be seen again.
story.part3: The Heavenly Ring was not a physical place; it was a state of being, a tournament held in the dreams of the greatest fighters. Márquez was invited after his hundredth victory, and he fought against champions from other realms—demons, angels, machines, and monsters. He defeated them all, his jade aura blazing, his fists unstoppable. The tournament judges declared him the Heavenly Ring Champion, and they gave him a belt of woven starlight as his prize. Márquez wore it once, then hung it on his wall. He did not fight for glory; he fought because it was the only thing that made him feel alive.
story.part4: The corgi found him after a fight, a tiny ball of fur with a miniature boxing jersey tied around its neck. Márquez tried to shoo it away, but the corgi followed him home, climbed onto his bed, and refused to leave. He named it 'Champ' and dressed it in a miniature version of his own outfit. The corgi became his mascot, his good‑luck charm, the only creature that had been with him since he was a hungry fighter fighting for scraps. It sat at ringside during his matches, its ears perked, its eyes fixed on the ring, and when he won—which was always—it trotted out to meet him, its tail wagging, its tiny face split in a doggy grin.
story.part5: Now, the Sundering has brought new challengers to the Heavenly Ring—demons who seek to claim the championship for themselves. Márquez has accepted their challenges, not because he wants to fight, but because he cannot let the demons win. He stands in the center of the ring, his gloves crackling with jade energy, the corgi at ringside, the necklace pulsing with his heartbeat. He has never lost a fight, and he does not intend to start now. For he is Márquez, the Heavenly Ring Champion, and his fists are the law.

weapons.name: Jade Fists
weapons.fulldesc: Márquez's hands are his weapons, wrapped in the blue leather of his gloves, and they carry the force of his divine power. With them, he can unleash an uppercut that sends opponents flying through the air, can throw a counterpunch that shatters defenses, can deliver a smash punch that cracks the very stones of the ring. His signature technique, the Heavenly Jade Punch, channels the full power of his jade aura into a single, devastating blow—a strike that glows with green light, that resonates with the force of a thunderclap, that has never failed to end a fight. He can throw a hundred‑fold punch, a blur of motion that seems to land a hundred blows in the space of a heartbeat, and he can stand in the center of the ring, absorbing the fury of his opponents, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.

heroes.lore: Márquez is the Heavenly Ring Boxing Champion, a fighter who has never lost a match. He was given a jade necklace by a mysterious old man, granting him a divine aura. He fights to protect his title from demon challengers, accompanied by his corgi mascot Champ.

relationships.description: The corgi is his good‑luck charm and constant companion.
The old man gave him the jade necklace and vanished; Márquez seeks to find him.

pet.name: Champ
pet.description: A corgi of impossible cuteness, fur warm golden brown, face a mask of cheerful determination. Dressed in a miniature white boxing jersey with 'Márquez' printed across the back, a tiny jade bead on a leather cord around its neck, and miniature blue booties on its paws.
pet.fulldesc: Champ is a corgi of impossible cuteness, its fur a warm, golden brown, its face a mask of cheerful determination, and it wears a tiny white boxing jersey with 'Márquez' printed across the back in miniature letters. Around its neck, a small jade bead hangs from a leather cord, a match to the one on its master's arm, and its paws are wrapped in miniature blue booties that squeak softly when it walks. The corgi is not merely a pet; it is his mascot, his good‑luck charm, the only creature that has been with him since he was a hungry fighter fighting for scraps. It sits at ringside during his matches, its ears perked, its eyes fixed on the ring, and when he wins—which is always—it trots out to meet him, its tail wagging, its tiny face split in a doggy grin. When Márquez is tired, Champ licks his hand, and the warmth of the little dog reminds him why he fights.
abilities: Encouraging Bark – heals Márquez for 20 health per second while nearby.
Distraction – Champ barks, causing an enemy's next attack to miss.
Lucky Charm – increases Márquez's critical strike chance by 5% while Champ is within 10 meters.

heroes.specialty: Melee fighter with high single-target damage, sustain, and crowd control immunity. Excels at dueling and finishing enemies.

heroes.name: Victtoria
heroes.shortDescription: A zealot who hunts those she deems sinners, the blade that cleanses the impure, the hand of the Holy Church made flesh. Victtoria wields the Inquisition rapier, which ignites with holy fire, and is accompanied by Solas, a husky of remarkable intelligence and wary grace. She walks the streets with the certainty of an archangel, her judgment absolute.
heroes.fullDescription: Victtoria strides through the cobblestone streets of the renaissance city like a judgment carved from marble and fire, a being of such terrible beautiful certainty that the shadows themselves seem to shrink from her path—the Blade of the Inquisition, a zealot who hunts those she deems sinners, the hand of the Holy Church made flesh. Her face is a study in sharp aristocratic perfection: a strong angular jawline, high prominent cheekbones, a straight refined nose, and full crimson lips pressed into a thin judgmental line. Her skin is fair and luminous, the color of freshly fallen snow, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone—the mark of one blessed by the holy fire she wields. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, piercing crystalline blue, the color of a winter sky at the moment of execution. Her hair is a cascade of pure blonde silk, long and voluminous, falling past her shoulders in waves that catch the torchlight and shimmer like a halo, worn loose. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a full G‑cup bust. She wears white minimal armor of polished steel and white leather, cut deep to reveal her cleavage and stomach, with golden filigree. Her arms are bare, with golden pauldrons shaped like angel wings guarding her shoulders. A red velvet skirt slit high over thigh‑long black stockings covers her hips. White heels cover her feet. A long gold silky cape with a red cross embroidered on it hangs from her shoulders. A white police cap with a stiff brim and a red cross on the crown sits on her head. Her weapon, the Inquisition, is a rapier longer than her arm—its blade shimmering silver that seems to glow with an inner light, its edge sharp enough to cut through the lies of the damned. The hilt is wrapped in white leather, its guard shaped like a cross, its pommel set with a ruby that pulses with the fire of judgment. When she draws the Inquisition, the blade ignites with holy golden flame—a fire that does not burn the righteous but consumes the impure, turning heresy to ash and demons to cinders. She wields it with the precision of a master duelist, her thrusts swift and sure, her cuts deliberate and final. Her pet, Solas, is a husky of remarkable intelligence and wary grace—its fur a mix of pure white and deep charcoal, its mask sharp, its eyes a pale piercing blue, moving with stealth, capable of tracking a heretic through the densest crowds, sensing the fear that gives away the guilty, and warning Victtoria of ambushes with a low rumbling growl. This inquisitor was raised by the Holy Church of the Celestial Order from childhood, trained to be a weapon against heresy. At sixteen, she conducted her first inquisition, purging a village of demon‑worshippers without flinching. She has wielded the Inquisition rapier in a hundred purges, each strike a prayer, each thrust a judgment. Now she strides through the cobblestone streets of a renaissance city—a place of narrow alleys, gas lamps, and the looming spires of cathedrals, where the fog rolls in from the river and the only sounds are church bells and the footsteps of the faithful—the Inquisition rapier in her hand, Solas at her side, hunting a demon lord who was once an angel, her judgment absolute.

story.part1: Victtoria was not born; she was forged. The Holy Church of the Celestial Order took orphaned children and raised them in the faith, training them to be weapons against heresy. Victtoria was the finest of her generation—pious, ruthless, and utterly without doubt. She memorized the scriptures, mastered the blade, and learned to see sin in the hearts of men. At sixteen, she conducted her first inquisition, purging a village of demon-worshippers. She did not flinch when they begged for mercy. She did not weep when she saw the children among the condemned. She believed, with every fiber of her being, that she was doing the will of the gods.
story.part2: The Inquisition rapier was blessed by the High Pontiff himself, forged from silver that had been quenched in holy water and tempered with prayers. When Victtoria draws it, the blade ignites with golden flame—a fire that does not burn the righteous but consumes the impure, turning heresy to ash and demons to cinders. She has wielded it in a hundred purges, and each strike is a prayer, each thrust a judgment. The rapier has never failed her, and she has never doubted its purpose.
story.part3: Solas was a gift from the Church, a husky trained to sniff out heresy. The dog's fur is a mix of pure white and deep charcoal, its mask sharp, its eyes a pale, piercing blue that mirrors Victtoria's own. Solas can track a heretic through the densest crowds, can sense the fear that gives away the guilty, can warn her of ambushes with a low, rumbling growl. It wears no collar, no adornment—its loyalty is its only badge, its vigilance its only armor. Victtoria trusts Solas more than any human, for the dog has never lied to her.
story.part4: The Sundering brought chaos to the Church, and Victtoria was dispatched to the mortal realms to hunt the demons that poured through the rifts. She found that the lines between heretic and innocent had blurred, that the demons often wore the faces of the suffering. She did not hesitate. She judged them all, and those she deemed impure, she purged. The people of the cities learned to fear the white cap and the golden flame, and they whispered that the Inquisition had sent an angel—or a devil—to cleanse their streets.
story.part5: Now, Victtoria walks the cobblestone streets of a city torn by the Sundering, Solas at her side, the Inquisition rapier in her hand. She has heard whispers of a demon lord who preys on the innocent, and she has sworn to find him. She does not know that the demon lord was once an angel, that he was cast out for questioning the very Church she serves. She does not care. She is the Blade of the Inquisition, and her judgment is absolute. For she is Victtoria, and she will purge the darkness—or die trying.

weapons.name: Inquisition
weapons.fulldesc: The Inquisition is a rapier that has become the symbol of Victtoria's holy purpose. The blade is longer than her arm, its steel a shimmering silver that seems to glow with an inner light, its edge sharp enough to cut through the lies of the damned. The hilt is wrapped in white leather, its guard shaped like a cross, its pommel set with a ruby that pulses with the fire of judgment. When she draws the Inquisition, the blade ignites with holy fire—a golden flame that does not burn the righteous but consumes the impure, turning heresy to ash and demons to cinders. She wields it with the precision of a master duelist, her thrusts swift and sure, her cuts deliberate and final. The Inquisition does not merely kill; it purifies, stripping away the corruption of the soul and leaving only the truth.
weapons.look: Silver blade with golden flame, cross-shaped guard, white leather hilt, ruby pommel

passive.name: Holy Flame
passive.description: Basic attacks deal bonus magic damage equal to 3% of the target's max health against demon or undead enemies.

passive.name: Judgment
passive.description: Victtoria gains 10% damage bonus against enemies with 'sinner' or 'heretic' lore tags.

active.name: Purifying Thrust
active.description: Victtoria dashes forward 8 meters, dealing 160% physical damage to the first enemy hit and silencing them for 1.5 seconds. Cooldown: 8 seconds.

active.name: Holy Barrier
active.description: Creates a barrier of holy light around Victtoria for 3 seconds, absorbing 300 damage and reflecting 20% of damage back to attackers. Cooldown: 12 seconds.

ultimate.name: Cleansing Flame
ultimate.description: Victtoria raises the Inquisition, unleashing a wave of holy fire in a 20-meter radius. Enemies take 350% magic damage and are stunned for 2 seconds. Allies are healed for 300 health plus 15% of their max health and have all debuffs removed. Cooldown: 85 seconds.

special.name: Zealot's Fury
special.description: Victtoria's abilities deal 15% increased damage to enemies who have killed an ally in the last 10 seconds.

ratings.notes: Hybrid fighter/assassin with holy damage, crowd control, and anti-demon specialization.

skills.name: Unwavering Faith
skills.description: Victtoria is immune to fear and silence effects.

skills.name: Righteous Dash
skills.description: Victtoria dashes 10 meters, leaving a trail of holy fire that deals 60 magic damage per second to enemies. Cooldown: 10 seconds.

skills.name: Mark of the Inquisition
skills.description: Victtoria marks an enemy for 5 seconds. Marked enemies take 20% increased damage from her and are revealed on the minimap. Cooldown: 15 seconds.

skills.name: The Purge
skills.description: If Victtoria kills a demon-type enemy with her ultimate, the ultimate's cooldown is reduced by 50%.

heroes.lore: Victtoria is the Blade of the Inquisition, a zealot who hunts heretics and demons for the Holy Church. She wields the Inquisition rapier and is accompanied by Solas, a husky that can sniff out heresy. She judges all she encounters without mercy, believing her actions are the will of the gods.

relationships.description: Victtoria has been dispatched to hunt Argus, the fallen angel, whom she sees as the ultimate heretic.
The husky is her partner, trained to track heretics and warn of danger.

pet.name: Solas
pet.description: A husky of remarkable intelligence and wary grace, fur a mix of pure white and deep charcoal, mask sharp, eyes a pale piercing blue. Moves with stealth, ears constantly swiveling, nose testing the air for the scent of corruption.
pet.fulldesc: Solas is a husky of remarkable intelligence and wary grace, its fur a mix of pure white and deep charcoal, its mask sharp, its eyes a pale, piercing blue that mirrors Victtoria's own. It moves with a stealth that belies its size, its ears constantly swiveling, its nose testing the air for the scent of corruption. Solas is not merely a pet; it is her partner, her scout, her second set of eyes in the hunt. It can track a heretic through the densest crowds, can sense the fear that gives away the guilty, can warn her of ambushes with a low, rumbling growl. It wears no collar, no adornment—its loyalty is its only badge, its vigilance its only armor. When Victtoria rests, it curls at her feet, its head on its paws, its eyes never fully closing; when she hunts, it moves beside her, a silent, deadly shadow, the perfect complement to the Blade of the Inquisition. When Victtoria doubts, Solas nudges her hand, and she feels the warmth of the only creature that has never judged her.
abilities: Scent of Heresy – reveals the location of the enemy with the most kills on the minimap every 20 seconds.
Wary Growl – warns Victtoria of incoming attacks, granting her 10% dodge chance for 3 seconds.
Silent Step – Solas makes no sound, ideal for scouting.

heroes.specialty: Hybrid fighter/assassin with holy damage, crowd control, and anti-demon specialization. Excels at dueling and purging heretics.

heroes.name: Xian Ju
heroes.shortDescription: The Celestial Judge, a woman whose form is a masterwork of heavenly perfection and balanced power. Xian Ju descends from the jade gates of the heavenly court like a breath of divine order, weighing the souls of emperors and peasants alike. She wields the Scales of Heaven, a feather‑like weapon that delivers divine punishment or mercy, and is accompanied by Whei Bijou, a monstrous yet majestic beast with the body of a lion and the legs of a dragon, wreathed in blue jade flames.
heroes.fullDescription: Xian Ju descends from the jade gates of the heavenly court like a breath of divine order, a being of such serene absolute authority that the very chaos of the mortal realm stills in her presence—the Celestial Judge, a woman whose form is a masterwork of heavenly perfection and balanced power, the arbiter of fate, the keeper of scales. Her face is a study in ethereal timeless beauty: a soft oval visage with a delicate jawline, a small straight nose, and full rose‑pink lips perpetually pressed into a serene contemplative smile. Her skin is healthy and luminous, the color of warm ivory, smooth and flawless, with a faint golden undertone. Her eyes are large and almond‑shaped, deep clear blue, the color of the sky at the moment justice is served. Her hair is a cascade of long black silk, falling past her waist in a straight shimmering curtain, with soft side‑swept bangs that brush her brows, worn loose. Her physique is a classic hourglass: a slender waist, full rounded hips, thick powerful thighs, and a balanced bust. She wears a pristine white silk robe with gold embroidery of dragons and phoenixes, high‑collared, with flowing layers. Her arms are covered by long sleeves. A golden crown of delicate filigree with sun‑ray points and blue sapphires sits on her head. A belt of interlocking jade pendants cinches her waist, and a jade blue flower is tucked above her left ear. Her feet are bare, floating above the ground. Her weapon, the Scales of Heaven, is a feather‑like staff that appears as a delicate white wand with tiny golden scales hanging from a thread of starlight—no longer than her forearm. The scales are perfectly balanced when at rest, but when she passes judgment, they tip, and the weight of a soul is measured. The weapon does not cut or burn; it weighs, and in that weighing, it delivers justice. With the Scales, she can summon divine punishment that strikes the guilty with the force of a falling star, or grant mercy that lifts the burden of the innocent. Her mount, Whei Bijou, is a monstrous yet majestic beast with the body of a lion, massive and muscular, its fur deep creamy white with gold strands in its mane, its legs scaled like a dragon, its claws wreathed in blue jade flames that do not burn but purify, its eyes two points of golden light, wearing minimal golden armor—a breastplate of gold filigree, a helm on its brow, a saddle of woven starlight—capable of a jade flame breath that purifies buffs, a thunderous roar that stuns enemies, and celestial speed. This celestial judge was appointed by the Jade Emperor, created from a feather of a celestial crane, a drop of water from the river of time, and a spark of justice. She weighs the souls of the living and the dead, her judgments never questioned. The Sundering brought chaos, and she was dispatched to the mortal realms to judge the demons who poured through the rifts. Now she stands at the edge of the largest rift—a realm of swirling chaos, broken earth, and the faint glow of distant stars, where the ground trembles and the sky is torn—the Scales of Heaven in her hand, Whei Bijou at her side, waiting for the final battle, ready to pass judgment on heroes and demons alike, favoring neither, only balance.

story.part1: Xian Ju was not born; she was appointed. The Jade Emperor, seeing that the mortal realms were descending into chaos, created a being of pure balance to judge the souls of the living and the dead. He took a feather from the wing of a celestial crane, a drop of water from the river of time, and a spark of justice from his own heart, and he wove them into a woman. He named her Xian Ju, and he gave her the Scales of Heaven. 'Weigh every action,' he said. 'Let the punishment fit the crime. Let the mercy match the plea.' Xian Ju bowed, and she began her eternal work.
story.part2: The Scales of Heaven were her only tool, a feather‑like staff that appeared as a delicate white wand with tiny golden scales hanging from a thread of starlight. The scales were perfectly balanced when at rest, but when she passed judgment, they tipped—one side rising, the other falling—and the weight of a soul was measured. The weapon did not cut or burn; it weighed, and in that weighing, it delivered justice. With the Scales, she could summon divine punishment that struck the guilty with the force of a falling star, or grant mercy that lifted the burden of the innocent. She has used it for millennia, and her judgments have never been questioned.
story.part3: Whei Bijou was given to her by the Jade Emperor as a mount and a guardian. The beast had the body of a lion, massive and muscular, its fur a deep, creamy white that rippled like snowdrifts. Its mane was voluminous, shot through with strands of gold. Its legs were scaled, like those of a dragon, and its claws were wreathed in blue jade flames that did not burn but purified, consuming corruption and leaving only truth. Its eyes were two points of golden light, wise and ancient. Whei Bijou could move with the speed of thought, and when Xian Ju declared a soul guilty, it roared—a sound like thunder and the crumbling of mountains—and the blue jade flames leaped forward, consuming the corruption of the damned. When she declared mercy, it purred, a sound like wind through bamboo, and the flames dimmed, becoming a gentle warmth that soothed the innocent.
story.part4: The Sundering brought chaos to the heavenly court, and Xian Ju was dispatched to the mortal realms to judge the demons who poured through the rifts. She found that the Sundering had blurred the lines between good and evil, that many of the 'demons' were corrupted souls who had once been innocent. She did not judge them all as guilty; she weighed their actions, their intentions, their regrets. Some she purified, sending their cleansed souls back to the heavens. Others she punished, sealing them in the void for eternity. The other gods called her merciful; the demons called her cruel. Xian Ju did not care. She was balance, and balance was neither mercy nor cruelty—it was truth.
story.part5: Now, Xian Ju stands at the edge of the largest rift, the Scales of Heaven in her hand, Whei Bijou at her side. She has seen that the final battle will determine the fate of Kretos, and she has come to judge the outcome. She does not favor the heroes or the demons; she favors balance. If the heroes are righteous, she will aid them. If they have strayed, she will punish them. She waits, patient as stone, for the moment when the scales will tip. For she is Xian Ju, the Celestial Judge, and her justice is as eternal as the stars.

weapons.name: Scales of Heaven
weapons.fulldesc: The Scales of Heaven are a feather‑like weapon that appears as a small wizard staff of delicate white wood, its length no longer than Xian Ju's forearm, its tip adorned with a pair of tiny, golden scales that hang from a thread of starlight. The scales are perfectly balanced when at rest, but when she passes judgment, they tip—one side rising, the other falling—and the weight of a soul is measured. The weapon does not cut or burn; it weighs, and in that weighing, it delivers justice. With the Scales, she can summon divine punishment that strikes the guilty with the force of a falling star, or grant mercy that lifts the burden of the innocent. The scales glow with a soft, blue light when she uses them, and the air around her fills with the scent of incense and the sound of distant, chanting monks.
weapons.look: White wood staff, tiny golden scales on starlight thread, blue glow

passive.name: Balance of Justice
passive.description: Xian Ju's abilities alternate between Judgment (damage) and Mercy (heal). Each time she uses an ability, the Scales tip.

passive.name: Weigh Soul
passive.description: Xian Ju can see the 'karma' of enemies (visual indicator of how many kills/deaths they have).

active.name: Judgment (Scales tipped to Judgment)
active.description: Deals 200% magic damage to a single enemy and stuns them for 1 second. If the enemy has more kills than allies, damage is doubled. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

active.name: Mercy (Scales tipped to Mercy)
active.description: Heals an ally for 250 health plus 15% of their max health and removes one debuff. If the ally has more deaths than kills, healing is doubled. Cooldown: 6 seconds.

ultimate.name: Final Verdict
ultimate.description: Xian Ju passes judgment on all enemies within a 25-meter radius. Enemies are dealt 350% magic damage and stunned for 2 seconds. Allies are healed for 400 health plus 20% of their max health. The effect is doubled for enemies with more kills than allies and allies with more deaths than kills. Cooldown: 90 seconds.

special.name: Impartial Judge
special.description: Xian Ju cannot be affected by crowd control effects that would force her to act against her will (charm, taunt, fear).

ratings.notes: Support mage with healing, damage, and scaling based on match performance.

skills.name: Celestial Aura
skills.description: Allies within 12 meters of Xian Ju gain 10 armor and magic resist.

skills.name: Scale Tip
skills.description: Xian Ju manually tips her Scales, changing her current mode without casting a spell. Cooldown: 2 seconds.

skills.name: Reveal Truth
skills.description: Xian Ju reveals the true nature of an enemy, removing their buffs and revealing their location for 5 seconds. Cooldown: 18 seconds.

skills.name: Eternal Balance
skills.description: If the match timer reaches 25 minutes, Xian Ju's Judgment and Mercy abilities have no cooldown but deal/heal 50% less (the scales balance over time).

heroes.lore: Xian Ju is the Celestial Judge, appointed by the Jade Emperor to weigh the souls of the living and the dead. She wields the Scales of Heaven and is accompanied by Whei Bijou, a lion-dragon beast wreathed in blue jade flames. She judges all without bias, delivering punishment and mercy in equal measure.

relationships.description: Xian Ju serves the Jade Emperor faithfully, executing his will with perfect balance.
The lion-dragon beast is her companion, executing her judgments with purifying flames.

mount.name: Whei Bijou
mount.description: A monstrous yet majestic beast with the body of a lion, massive and muscular, fur deep creamy white with gold strands in mane. Legs scaled like a dragon, claws wreathed in blue jade flames that do not burn but purify. Eyes two points of golden light. Wears minimal golden armor—a breastplate of gold filigree, a helm on its brow, a saddle of woven starlight.
mount.fulldesc: Whei Bijou is a monstrous yet majestic beast, its body that of a lion, massive and muscular, its fur a deep, creamy white that ripples like snowdrifts. Its mane is voluminous, flowing around its head like a lion's, but the fur is shot through with strands of gold that shimmer in the light. Its legs are scaled, like those of a dragon, and its claws are wreathed in blue jade flames that do not burn but purify, consuming corruption and leaving only truth. Its eyes are two points of golden light, wise and ancient, and it has no wings—it does not need them, for it moves with the speed of thought. Its body is adorned with unique, minimal golden armor of the heavens: a breastplate of gold filigree, a helm that covers its brow, and a saddle of woven starlight that floats just above its back. Whei Bijou is not merely a mount; it is her partner, her enforcer, the beast that carries out the physical aspect of her judgments. When she declares a soul guilty, Whei Bijou roars—a sound like thunder and the crumbling of mountains—and the blue jade flames on its claws leap forward, consuming the corruption of the damned. When she declares mercy, it purrs, a sound like wind through bamboo, and the flames dim, becoming a gentle warmth that soothes the innocent. When Xian Ju is tired, Whei Bijou rests its massive head on her shoulder, and she strokes its mane, feeling the warmth of the only creature that has never questioned her judgments.
abilities: Jade Flame Breath – exhales blue jade flames in a cone, dealing 200% magic damage and purifying buffs.
Thunderous Roar – stuns all enemies within 15 meters for 1.5 seconds.
Celestial Speed – moves at 50% increased speed while mounted.

heroes.specialty: Support mage with healing, damage, and scaling based on match performance. Excels at balancing team fights and executing fair judgments.
